title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
v0.8.7: Normalization Engine - Tag/Rules Engine Redesign & Testing

This release completes the normalization engine with a major architectural redesign separating tags from rules, followed by comprehensive testing.

## Part 1: Tag/Rules Engine Redesign (5 child epics, 22 tasks)
Separates the current normalization system into two distinct subsystems:

**Tag Engine** - Vocabulary/data management
- TagGroup and Tag models for organizing strings into groups
- Built-in tag groups: Quality, Country, Timezone, League, Network
- Tag management UI with bulk import

**Rules Engine** - Enhanced logic layer
- New 'tag_group' condition type (match ANY tag from a group)
- Position-aware matching (prefix/suffix/contains)
- If/Then/Else conditional logic
- Enhanced rule editor UI

### Phases:
1. Phase 1: Tag Engine Backend (models, migration, API, built-in tags)
2. Phase 2: Rules Engine Enhancement (tag_group condition, else logic)
3. Phase 3: Tag Engine Frontend (TagEngineSection component)
4. Phase 4: Rules Engine Frontend (enhanced rule editor)
5. Phase 5: Migration & Testing
6. Phase 6: Add Normalization to Channel Creation (optional normalize flag, preview, settings)

## Part 2: Testing (7 tasks)
- Unit tests for compound condition matching
- Integration tests for normalization rules API
- Frontend unit tests for normalization components
- E2E tests for normalization engine
- Documentation updates

- Superscript pattern support in rules (paste ᴿᴬᵂ, matches RAW)

Backend:

- normalization_engine.py: multi-pass, superscript conversion, legacy tag support

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Apply normalization when normalize flag is set in bulk commit operations.

## Changes
In bulk_commit_operations() Phase 2 (createChannel handling):
- Check if op.normalize is True
- If true, import normalization engine and normalize the channel name
- Use normalized name in channel_data

## Code Pattern
elif op.type == 'createChannel':
    name = op.name
    if getattr(op, 'normalize', False):
        from normalization_engine import get_normalization_engine
        engine = get_normalization_engine(session)
        result = engine.normalize(name)
        name = result.normalized
    channel_data = {'name': name, ...}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bulk channel creation respects normalize flag
- Normalization only applied when flag is True
- Works with existing normalization rules

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Apply normalization when normalize flag is set in single channel create endpoint.

## Changes
In create_channel() endpoint (@app.post('/api/channels')):
- Check if request.normalize is True
- If true, get normalization engine and normalize the name
- Use normalized name in channel data

## Code Pattern
@app.post('/api/channels')
async def create_channel(request: CreateChannelRequest):
    data = {'name': request.name, ...}
    if request.normalize:
        from normalization_engine import get_normalization_engine
        session = get_session()
        try:
            engine = get_normalization_engine(session)
            result = engine.normalize(request.name)
            data['name'] = result.normalized
        finally:
            session.close()
    result = await client.create_channel(data)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single channel creation respects normalize flag
- Normalization only applied when flag is True

title: Bug: MCP bulk_add_streams_to_channel times out (~4 min) on Raspberry Pi for 9-10 streams (gh #223)
description: GH #223 (reporter Koalamanx, Pi 5 / Docker Compose). bulk_add_streams_to_channel with 9-10 stream IDs times out after ~4 min on a Pi; single add_stream_to_channel calls are reliable. The MCP 'bulk' tool (mcp-server/tools/channels.py:266-296) fakes a bulk op by looping POST /api/channels/{id}/add-stream (one HTTP request per stream); there is no real backend bulk-add-streams endpoint (routes: /add-stream, /remove-stream, /reorder-streams, /bulk-commit). On slow hardware each add-stream Dispatcharr roundtrip is slow; 9-10 sequential × per-call latency exceeds the MCP tool's httpx timeout. Fix candidates: (a) raise the MCP tool's httpx timeout for bulk ops; (b) add a real backend bulk-add-streams endpoint (one Dispatcharr roundtrip / fewer); (c) chunk the batch; (d) document the loop-single workaround. Needs profiling of the add-stream endpoint cost. Severity: P3 — workaround exists; slow-hardware edge case.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.16.0-0079 (PR #233) — new POST /api/channels/{id}/add-streams (one Dispatcharr roundtrip), MCP tool rewired w/ 120s timeout. GH #223.

title: Remediate py/path-injection in backend/routers/backup.py (CodeQL alerts 1416-1419)
description:
## Context
GitHub CodeQL Default Setup (enabled via PR #82's workflow additions) flagged 4 HIGH-severity py/path-injection errors in backend/routers/backup.py:
- Alert 1416 — line 1165 (Path.exists() in download_saved_backup)
- Alert 1417 — line 1167 (Path.read_text() in download_saved_backup)
- Alert 1418 — line 1181 (Path.exists() in delete_saved_backup)
- Alert 1419 — line 1183 (Path.unlink() in delete_saved_backup)

CWE-22/23/36/73/99. Source: 'filename' URL path parameter. Sink: Path operations under BACKUPS_DIR (= CONFIG_DIR/'backups').

## Exploitability Assessment — effective risk LOW
1. Both endpoints gated by RequireAdminIfEnabled (admin-only when auth is enabled)
2. Existing sanitization: strict regex allowlist at lines 1162/1178:
     _BACKUP_FILENAME_RE = re.compile(r'^ecm-backup-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}_\d{6}\.yaml$')
   This anchored regex permits only: literal 'ecm-backup-' prefix, digits/hyphens/underscore in a fixed YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSS shape, literal '.yaml' suffix. No '/', no '..', no backslash, no null bytes, no absolute paths.
3. CodeQL flags this because its dataflow tracker cannot symbolically prove the regex constrains the value; this is a known SAST limitation with anchored-regex validators.
4. No concrete exploit path exists with current code. Confirmed false-positive modulo regex bypass (none identified).

Rating: CVSS-adapted Low (1.8) — auth-gated + hardened allowlist, no path traversal possible, theoretical-only risk.

## Remediation (fix shape — do NOT rush; get it right)
Belt-and-suspenders canonicalization + containment. Match the pattern already established in commit a591105c ('Harden zip backup path validation with canonicalization (OWASP A03)'):

  import os
  safe_root = BACKUPS_DIR.resolve()
  candidate = (BACKUPS_DIR / filename).resolve()
  try:
      candidate.relative_to(safe_root)
  except ValueError:
      raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Invalid filename')

This closes the CodeQL finding by making containment explicit and defense-in-depth against future regex regressions. Apply to BOTH download_saved_backup (lines 1159-1172) AND delete_saved_backup (lines 1175-1185).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Canonicalization + containment check added to download_saved_backup and delete_saved_backup
- [ ] CodeQL alerts 1416, 1417, 1418, 1419 closed on next scan (or dismissed-with-justification and 'won't fix' dismissal comment citing regex + auth if team prefers; preferred path is FIX not DISMISS)
- [ ] Unit tests in backend/tests/routers/test_backup.py cover:
      * '../' traversal attempt returns 400
      * Absolute path attempt ('/etc/passwd') returns 400
      * Symlink-escape attempt (if feasible in test fixture) returns 400
      * Valid filename still works end-to-end
- [ ] docs/security/threat_model_dbas_import.md updated with a note on backup.py path-handling controls (short addendum)
- [ ] No regression in existing backup tests (2447+ BE tests stay green)

## Related
- Existing hardening precedent: commit a591105c ('Harden zip backup path validation with canonicalization')
- Threat model: docs/security/threat_model_dbas_import.md (backup.py is DBAS-adjacent)
- Triage bead: enhancedchannelmanager-rbmkt
- PR #82 surfaced these findings (CodeQL Default Setup enablement)
- Similar patterns exist in backend/routers/export.py (lines 397/408/433/444) and backend/export_manager.py (lines 87/89) — filed separately if prioritized.

Labels: security, codeql, path-traversal

title: BE test drift: 3 tests cover removed/unimplemented code (surfaced by tp681)
description:
Surfaced by bead tp681 (flake audit, 2026-04-20). These 3 BE tests are NOT flaky — they fail deterministically in the prod-like ecm-ecm-1 container because they reference code that is no longer present (or test an environment variant that CI does not exercise).

1. tests/integration/test_api_tasks.py::TestRunTaskWithSchedule::test_run_task_with_schedule_id
   - Expects POST /api/tasks/{task_id}/schedules/{schedule_id}/run — this route was removed from routers/tasks.py. Test returns 405. Delete the test or re-add the endpoint (coordinate with product).

2. tests/integration/test_router_registration.py::TestRoutePrefixes::test_all_routes_under_api
   - SPA fallback route /{full_path:path} registers only when backend/static/ exists. Exists in the container, not in CI. Add 'serve_spa' (or the path) to the NON_API_ROUTES exclusion set.

3. tests/unit/test_ffmpeg_execution.py::TestExecutionSafety::test_validates_output_path_writable
   - Test expects ffmpeg_builder/execution.py to raise on unwritable output path. Docstring promises the check; code does not implement it. Either implement the check or remove the test (coordinate with security-engineer — this was likely part of the OWASP hardening scope that shipped incomplete).

Acceptance:
- Each test either passes in the container OR has been deleted with a replacement that covers behavior that still exists.
- Re-run 3x cadence (tp681 protocol) in container produces 0 failures without any --deselect.
acceptance_criteria: 3 BE tests either pass or are removed; pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e is green in ecm-ecm-1 without deselections
close_reason: Merged via PR #165 — test drift cleared (3 tests per original scope + dead ffmpeg_builder.execution removed per PO Option C scope expansion).

title: Auto-creation rule analyzer: lint + structural findings (Phase 1)
description:
## Why

Users build broken auto-creation rules and have no way to find out short of
running them and reading the dry-run logs. The 2026-04-28 debug bundle
review surfaced four real-world bugs in one user's rules.yaml that the
existing regex_lint catches none of:

  - `UK|`, `US|` saved under "Matches (Regex)" — valid regex, matches every
    group because of the trailing empty alternation.
  - `^4K` saved under "Contains" — looks like regex but is being substring-
    matched, so it matches nothing.
  - `^\^4k` saved under "Matches (Regex)" — typo from double-escaping.
  - OR-connector precedence: ungrouped ORs after a `normalized_name_in_group`
    constraint silently drop the guard for the OR'd conditions.

## What shipped

1. **Three new advisory lint codes** in `backend/regex_lint.py`:
   - `REGEX_TRIVIALLY_MATCHES_ALL` — empty alternation in a top-level `|`.
   - `OPERATOR_VALUE_LOOKS_LIKE_REGEX` — Contains-operator value with regex-
     like substrings (`^foo`, `foo$`, `.*`, `\b`, etc.). Bare `|` not flagged
     (legitimate for M3U group substrings).
   - `REGEX_REDUNDANT_ESCAPE_CARET` — `^\^` prefix.
   New `severity` field on `LintViolation` (default `error` for back-compat).
   New `lint_pattern_advisory` and `lint_conditions_json_advisory` so the
   advisory codes never come back from the strict save-time path that
   raises 422.

2. **New module** `backend/auto_creation_rule_analyzer.py`:
   - Reuses the AND/OR grouping algorithm from
     `auto_creation_evaluator.evaluate_conditions` (duplicated, not
     imported, to keep the analyzer off the runtime hot path —
     `split_or_groups` + `test_users_sports_rule_grouping` lock the
     contract).
   - Emits structural findings: `ANDOR_DROPS_GUARD`,
     `MERGE_STREAMS_NO_TARGET_CHANNELS`,
     `RULE_HAS_NO_HOPE_OF_MATCHING`. All severity `warning`; saves never
     blocked.

3. **Two new endpoints** in `backend/routers/auto_creation.py` (declared
   before `/rules/{rule_id}` so the static segment isn't captured by the
   path parameter):
   - `POST /api/auto-creation/rules/analyze` — analyze rules currently
     in the DB.
   - `POST /api/auto-creation/rules/analyze/from-bundle` — multipart
     upload of a debug bundle tar.gz; parses `rules.yaml` and (when
     present) `channel_groups_diagnostic.json` for the structural
     checks. Never touches the DB — safe to point at any user's bundle.

4. **New MCP tool** `analyze_auto_creation_rules(bundle_path=None)` in
   `mcp-server/tools/auto_creation.py`. Calls live endpoint when
   `bundle_path` is None; otherwise streams the bundle to `/from-bundle`.
   Returns a markdown report with one section per rule. New
   `post_multipart` helper added to `ecm_client.py`.

## Acceptance

- 25 new unit tests in `test_regex_lint.py` covering each new code,
  severity plumbing, and the advisory-doesn't-block-strict path.
- 30 new unit tests in `test_auto_creation_rule_analyzer.py` covering
  every finding code (positive + negative).
- 9 new router tests in `test_auto_creation.py` covering live-mode
  happy path, bundle-mode happy path, bundle missing rules.yaml → 400,
  non-tar.gz → 400, invalid YAML → 400.
- 4 new MCP tests in `test_tools.py` covering live mode, clean-result
  message, multipart upload, and missing bundle file.
- 10 acceptance tests in `test_bd_0gntx_user_bundle.py` against the
  saved 2026-04-28 user fixture — every finding called out in the
  diagnosis reproduces.
- Backend full-suite: 3294 pass. MCP full-suite: 26 pass. Coverage
  61.5% (gate is 56%).

## Docs

- New `docs/auto_creation_rule_analyzer.md` documents each finding
  code with example, why-it-matters, and remediation. Linked from
  `CLAUDE.md` reference table.

## Note on MERGE_STREAMS_NO_TARGET_CHANNELS

The bead originally listed an execution-history-based path for this
finding. The shipped Phase 1 only implements the simpler path: explicit
`target_group_id` on the rule + the bundle's `channel_groups_diagnostic.json`
showing 0 channels in that group. The execution-history path is deferred
to Phase 2 along with live group-corpus matching and per-rule dry-run
replay.

title: v0.18.0 DBAS absorption: Backup + Restore (full round-trip, 13 categories)
description:
Absorb DBAS (Dispatcharr Backup And Sync) into ECM as a first-class backup/restore subsystem. Source of truth: docs/adr/ADR-008-dbas-absorption-approach.md (Accepted 2026-04-21). Supersedes stale 42-bead plan gb5r5 (retired 2026-04-21).

Scope: Phase 0 (security prerequisites) + Phase 1 (backup + cloud upload) + Phase 2 (restore, all 13 categories). Sync (Phase 3) is v0.18.1, separate epic.

Estimate: 27-46 engineer-days single-engineer.

Nine PO-level decisions captured in ADR-008:
- D1 Artifact format: ZIP-wrapped YAML + binary subtree + SHA256 + schema_version mandatory day one.
- D2 Phase-1 scope: v0.18.0 MUST ship backup AND restore (backup without restore is pointless).
- D3 Credential storage: Fernet symmetric via existing backend/cloud_storage/crypto.py; no KMS for MVP.
- D4 Outbound URL policy: first-run SSRF wizard (LAN-friendly default) + always-on denylist + DNS-rebinding mitigations.
- D5 Progress transport: HTTP polling (reuse task_scheduler + NotificationCenter); WebSocket NOT ported.
- D6 Release sequencing: v0.18.0 then v0.18.1 sequential per ADR-004 ONE-major-thing discipline.
- D7 Dry-run engine: counts-only; full diff tree deferred to v0.19.x.
- D8 Logo memory: streaming upload pattern, not CumulativeMemoryTracker port.
- D9 Logo-miss severity: WARN log + aggregate count + prominent red banner on restore-complete (not silent DEBUG).

Five Open Questions all resolved inline in ADR-008 (scope=full round-trip, 27-46 day estimate accepted, SSRF default=LAN-friendly, dry-run=counts-only, logo-miss=prominent banner).

13 Dispatcharr categories covered: M3U accounts, EPG sources, channel groups, channel profiles, stream profiles, user agents, core settings, plugins, DVR rules, comskip config, users, channels (streams embedded), logos.

User value: enables Dispatcharr operators to back up full configuration + restore end-to-end (cross-instance migration, disaster recovery). Retires standalone DBAS companion product.

Success signal: v0.18.0 ships with full 13-category round-trip; first user-driven full restore on a clean Dispatcharr succeeds without manual cleanup; logo-miss banner fires correctly when logos are absent.

title: Phase 0: Redact secrets in ZIP export path (journal.db + settings.json)
description:
Fix backend/routers/backup.py:100-109 (ZIP path) to match YAML path's redaction at backup.py:319-327. Redact settings.json via _gather_settings shape. Produce a filtered/scrubbed journal.db (either table-subset export or VACUUM INTO with sensitive columns nulled).

Source: ADR-008 Phase 0 + Security Mandatory #4. Blocks ALL cloud-upload work — shipping cloud upload without this redaction would broadcast secrets to every user's cloud destination.

User value: admin's Dispatcharr credentials + ECM settings secrets no longer leak to cloud-storage destinations when backups are uploaded. Today's ZIP path zips these raw; the YAML path already redacts. This closes the parity gap.

Success signal: ZIP artifact contents diff against YAML artifact contents show no raw credential strings; unit test proves round-trip redaction on both paths.
close_reason: Rescoped to enhancedchannelmanager-l0nhi (standalone P0, uncoupled from v0.18.0 DBAS). The redaction parity fix will ship in the next release cut to clear G1a, rather than waiting for v0.18.0. Side effect: 17 v0.18.0 dependent beads (.4, .5, .6, .8, .10, .14, .15, .18, plus P2 .9, .11, .12, .13, .16, .17, .19, .20, .7) are unblocked from this dependency — they remain blocked on their other Phase 0/1 prerequisites.

title: Phase 2: M3U accounts importer (4-way group matching + deferred auto-sync)
description:
Restore M3U accounts with the patterns proven by DBAS:
- 4-way group matching (by ID, by name, by URL, by export-key).
- Deferred auto-sync pattern (extract settings on import; apply AFTER Channels + Logos finish — protects logo import from an auto-sync race on the Dispatcharr side).
- 2-stage refresh polling: status endpoint + stream-count-stable heuristic.
- is_active toggle workaround for the Dispatcharr quirk where newly-imported M3Us do not always trigger stream fetch.

Shape of the deferred settings return: deferredAutoSyncSettings: list[{m3u_account_id: int, settings: dict}] (per ADR-008 deferred M3U auto-sync settings contract).

Size: 4-6 days. First entity to restore (blocks EPG, Channel Groups, User Agents, Channels). No missing Dispatcharr client methods.

Source: ADR-008 per-entity sizing + sequencing-hard requirement (M3U BEFORE EPG BEFORE Channels BEFORE Logos).

User value: Dispatcharr M3U accounts restore cleanly with their upstream refresh settings intact; no manual per-account fix-up after restore.

Success signal: restore of 20 M3U accounts with mixed group states (new, existing, renamed) hits 100% match via the 4-way logic; deferred settings apply correctly after Channels + Logos finish; refresh polling terminates when stream count stabilizes.

title: Phase 2: EPG sources importer
description:
Restore EPG sources after M3U completes. Waits for Dispatcharr's EPG-data download to finish before reporting success (otherwise downstream Channels importer matches against an empty EPG).

Size: 1-2 days. Verify existing Dispatcharr client methods cover create/update/delete EPG source.

Source: ADR-008 per-entity sizing + sequencing-hard requirement (M3U BEFORE EPG).

User value: EPG sources restore with their URLs and refresh schedules; users do not manually re-add each source.

Success signal: EPG sources restore successfully; EPG-data download completes before Channels import begins (verified by polling or synchronous wait).

title: Phase 2: Bulk importer for channel groups + channel profiles + stream profiles
description:
Restore three related entities that feed Channels. ECM already has partial equivalents:
- backup.py:1051-1076 (channel groups)
- backup.py:1079-1097 (channel profiles)
- backup.py:1100-1122 (stream profiles)

Extend each to match ADR-008 scope and produce an ID-remap table (source-export ID -> destination ID) for downstream consumers (Channels, DVR Rules, Core Settings).

Size: ~3 days combined (Channel Groups: 1 day, Channel Profiles: 1 day, Stream Profiles: 1 day per ADR-008 per-entity table).

Source: ADR-008 per-entity sizing + ID-remap tables contract.

User value: Channel groups, profiles, and stream profiles restore before Channels import so foreign keys resolve on the first pass.

Success signal: remap tables correctly rewrite FKs when Channels importer runs; ECM's existing upsert-by-name logic for channel groups continues to work.

title: Phase 2: Bulk importer — user agents + core settings + plugins + DVR rules + comskip + users
description:
Restore the 6 net-new entity types (not previously in ECM's backup scope):
- User agents: ~4 missing Dispatcharr client methods (get/create/update/delete), 1-2 days.
- Core settings: ~2 missing methods (get/update), 1-2 days; consumes remap tables from upstream entities.
- Plugins: ~2 missing methods (get/create), 0.5-1 day; straight POST.
- DVR rules: ~3 missing methods (list/get/create), 0.5-1 day; requires channel ID remap.
- Comskip config: ~2 missing methods, 1 day; YAML/JSON format quirks.
- Users: ~1 missing method (create_user), 1-2 days; username uniqueness + password hash handling.

Size: ~5-7 days combined. All net-new in ECM.

Source: ADR-008 per-entity sizing table.

User value: users do not have to manually re-create user agents, adjust core settings, reinstall plugin configs, recreate DVR rules, or recreate admin users after a restore.

Success signal: each entity round-trips via backup/restore; core settings correctly consume remap tables so FK references point at the restored IDs, not the source-export IDs.

title: Phase 2: Channels importer with 4-tier stream matching + custom-stream fallback
description:
Channels is the most complex restore entity. Streams are embedded (not standalone). Implement the 4-tier stream-matching fallback chain (documented in sub-spike output) + custom-stream fallback (synthesizes a custom-stream M3U account for orphaned streams, with a WARN log on fallback path so operators see when the heuristic fires).

Missing Dispatcharr client method: create_stream (must be added). Verified present: update_profile_channel at dispatcharr_client.py:950.

Size: 4-5.5 days (Channels) + 1.5-2.5 days (embedded Streams) = ~5.5-8 days.

Source: ADR-008 Channels row + per-entity sizing.

User value: channels restore end-to-end with their correct stream attachments; orphaned streams do not vanish — they land in a custom-stream account where the user can triage them.

Success signal: 4-tier match rate >99% on a test archive with mixed stream states; custom-stream fallback fires with a WARN log and correctly attributes the orphans; update_profile_channel correctly reattaches channels to profiles post-create.

title: Phase 2: Logos importer — 3-tier match + bulk-delete + streaming upload + validation
description:
Logos has the largest memory/bandwidth profile in the restore. Implement per ADR-008 D8:
- Streaming upload pattern: read each logo file -> decode base64 -> pass bytes to upload_logo_file -> release before reading the next file. NOT a CumulativeMemoryTracker port (D8 leans streaming; re-evaluable at kickoff per implementation-time decisions).
- 3-tier match per logoMap contract: src:{source_id} (primary) -> name:{lowercased_original_name} (fallback 1) -> file:{sanitized_filename} (fallback 2).
- Bulk-delete pre-step to clear existing logos before upload.

Missing Dispatcharr client methods (must be added): bulk_delete_logos, get_all_logos_paginated.

Security validation (part of the 17-item STRIDE checklist + new addenda):
- 50MB per-logo cap.
- MIME magic-byte validation (reject mismatched-extension / disguised binaries).
- Path-traversal guard on ZIP extract (reject ../ or absolute paths in archive entries).
- Filename-basename validation (strip directory components).

Math: 50MB cap x 2000 logos = 100GB policy ceiling; streaming design must survive this without OOM.

Size: 4-6 days.

Source: ADR-008 D8 + Logos contract + Security Mandatory/STRIDE items.

User value: logos restore correctly at large archive sizes without OOMing the container; disguised binaries or path-traversal-crafted archives are rejected before extraction.

Success signal: 2000-logo archive restore completes under the streaming pattern without OOM; MIME + path-traversal attack corpus rejected; 3-tier match rate >95% on real backup data.

title: Phase 2: Dry-run engine (counts-only, default-ON for Dispatcharr restores)
description:
Aggregate per-entity, per-action counts: would-create, would-update, would-skip. Scope is counts-only; full entity-level diff tree deferred to v0.19.x per D7.

Default-ON for Dispatcharr restores — destructive-on-live-data risk is real; counts-only dry-run is a cheap guardrail. User can opt in to apply (not opt out of dry-run).

Size: 1-2 days.

Source: ADR-008 D7 + Alternative A-rejection (full diff tree too expensive for v0.18.0).

User value: before applying a restore, user sees 'would create 14 channels, update 203, skip 2 orphans, attach 187 logos (12 missing)' — answers the 'am I about to wreck my setup?' question with almost zero engineering cost vs. a full diff tree.

Success signal: dry-run produces correct counts on a known test archive; apply-after-dry-run result matches the counts dry-run predicted; toggle defaults to ON every time the restore UI opens.

title: Phase 2: Schema version validation on restore
description:
Validate the schema_version field in the restore archive's manifest. Refuse unknown versions with user-facing message 'Unsupported backup version'; full version detail is logged server-side only (no schema-internals leakage in UI or HTTP error body — per D1 + S4).

ECM already has ecm_export.version infrastructure; extend to the DBAS-absorbed path.

Size: 0.5-1 day.

Source: ADR-008 D1 + S4.

User value: a user restoring a v0.19 archive on a v0.18 ECM sees 'Unsupported backup version' immediately instead of a silent partial restore that corrupts data. Closes the silent-drift failure mode that DBAS's current format has (no schema_version today).

Success signal: restore of a crafted archive with a higher schema_version than the running build is refused with the correct user-facing message; server log contains the actual version detail for operator troubleshooting.

title: Phase 2: Pre-flight validation + compensating-delete rollback
description:
Dispatcharr has no database transactions. Best-effort consistency is:
1. Pre-flight validation — validate the entire import plan (FK references resolvable, names unique where required, counts within reasonable bounds) BEFORE applying any changes.
2. Compensating-delete rollback — if an entity's apply step fails mid-run, track everything created up to that point and issue compensating DELETEs to restore the pre-restore state.

The restore-summary response surfaces partial state explicitly (which entities succeeded, which failed, which were rolled back) rather than reporting success when the state is actually mixed.

Size: 1-2 days.

Source: ADR-008 per-entity transactional safety row + Risks: 'Post-restore state drift on partial failure'.

User value: a restore that hits a Dispatcharr API error mid-run does not leave the user with a half-applied archive; either the restore completes or the rollback returns them to the starting state. Partial failures are visible, not silent.

Success signal: fault-injection test (simulated 500 from Dispatcharr at the midpoint of Channels import) triggers full compensating-delete rollback; restore summary correctly reports 'failed — rolled back' not 'success'.

title: Phase 2: Restore-complete UX — logo-miss red banner
description:
On the restore-complete screen, when channels_without_logo > 0, display a prominent RED banner (PO-specified UX treatment, 2026-04-21). Banner text names the count and links to a detail view listing the affected channels so the user can fix them one-off.

Source: ADR-008 D9 updated + Open Question 5 resolution.

User value: a restored channel with a missing logo looks identical to a correctly-restored channel at the API level (DBAS's silent-DEBUG pattern hides this). The red banner makes the problem unmissable at the moment the user sees the restore complete, when they can act on it. Closes the silent-data-quality bug DBAS ships today.

Success signal: banner fires on a test restore with 1+ logo miss; banner does NOT fire when all logos attached; detail view lists the correct affected channels with edit links that land on Dispatcharr's channel page.

title: Phase 0: Fernet key bootstrap integrity check (mode 0600 + ownership)
description:
On container boot, verify /config/.export_key is mode 0600 and owned by the app user. If either constraint is violated, regenerate rather than proceed with a weakened key.

Source: ADR-008 Security Mandatory #1.

User value: credential encryption key cannot be silently degraded by a filesystem permission regression — the container either boots with a correct key or bootstraps a fresh one, never runs with a world-readable or wrong-owned key.

Success signal: bootstrap logs show 'key integrity verified' or 'key regenerated due to <reason>' on every container start; unit test confirms bad-mode and bad-owner scenarios both trigger regeneration.

title: Phase 2: Restore-complete UX — overall summary + per-entity counts
description:
Render the aggregate-count response from the dry-run engine + real restore. For each entity show: created, updated, skipped (with reasons), failed (with reasons). For rollback scenarios, clearly label the state as 'restore failed — state rolled back' not 'success'.

Size: 1 day.

Source: ADR-008 D9 three-tier surfacing + Sequencing Phase 2.

User value: user sees exactly what changed and what did not at the end of a restore, not a generic 'restore complete' toast. Mirrors the dry-run preview so apply-vs-preview discrepancies are visible.

Success signal: UI renders the full per-entity breakdown on restore complete; rollback state is labeled correctly; mirrors the dry-run preview shape so users recognize it.

title: Phase 0: STRIDE addenda — export artifact redaction + outbound destinations
description:
Extend docs/security/threat_model_dbas_import.md with two addenda sections:
1. Export artifact redaction — documents the ZIP-path redaction requirement that matches the existing YAML-path redaction (closes Security Mandatory #4 + #6).
2. Outbound destinations — documents SyncTarget + cloud-destination credentials + SSRF validators on all outbound URLs (feeds Phase 1 SSRF wizard design).

Source: ADR-008 Sequencing Phase 0 + Two STRIDE addenda required as Phase 1 prerequisites.

User value: the shipped STRIDE model stays the authoritative security reference for DBAS-absorbed code; future reviewers have one document, not a stale model plus a scattered ADR.

Success signal: threat_model_dbas_import.md contains both addenda with threat rows, mitigations, and residual-risk assessments; the 17-item hardening checklist is extended to cover the new items.

title: Phase 1: SyncTarget + CloudTarget models with Fernet-encrypted credentials
description:
New SQLAlchemy models:
- SyncTarget — Dispatcharr-B URL + encrypted credentials (reserved for v0.18.1 sync; defined in v0.18.0 so the schema lands once).
- CloudTarget — cloud destination tokens (S3, WebDAV, OneDrive, Dropbox, GDrive).

Reuse backend/cloud_storage/crypto.py Fernet primitive (no new crypto surface). Add credential_version + token_revoked_at columns for scheduled-op credential-freshness binding (Security Mandatory #5).

Source: ADR-008 D3 + Security Mandatory #1, #5.

User value: users can configure cloud backup destinations with credentials encrypted at rest; schedules that fire after credentials are rotated or revoked no longer silently use stale tokens.

Success signal: CRUD for both models round-trips encrypted credentials correctly; Alembic migration runs cleanly; freshness-check blocks a scheduled op when credential_version increments.

title: Phase 1: First-run SSRF wizard + always-on denylist + DNS rebinding mitigations
description:
First-run wizard choice: LAN-friendly (RFC1918 private IPs allowed; DEFAULT) vs. public-only (private IPs blocked). Always-on denylist regardless of choice: link-local 169.254.0.0/16, IMDS 169.254.169.254, CGNAT 100.64.0.0/10, IPv6 loopback ::1, IPv6 ULA fc00::/7, IPv6 link-local fe80::/10, IPv6 site-local fec0::/10, IPv4-mapped IPv6 ::ffff:0:0/96, wildcard 0.0.0.0/8, non-http(s) schemes. TLS verify=True default; per-target insecure=true flag requires audit-log entry on every outbound request.

DNS rebinding mitigations (mandatory):
- Resolve-then-connect-by-IP: resolve hostname once, validate returned IP against denylist, connect by IP with hostname as SNI/Host header.
- Block HTTP redirects (3xx) to previously-unvalidated hosts OR re-run full denylist check on every redirect target before following.

Extends bead zbt74 validator pattern with IPv6 / CGNAT / DNS-rebinding coverage if not already present.

Source: ADR-008 D4 + Security Mandatory #2, #3.

User value: ECM cannot be tricked into hitting cloud metadata endpoints, internal infrastructure, or unicast-scanning via user-configurable destinations — protects the deployment environment from an authenticated-admin-induced SSRF.

Success signal: regression test corpus covers unicode, IPv6-mapped, redirect chains, and DNS-rebinding (two-IP response) cases; wizard appears on first run and records the choice; per-request audit log fires on insecure=true targets.

title: Phase 1: Scheduled + manual backup invocation via task_scheduler
description:
Register DBAS backup task in backend/task_scheduler.py / task_registry.py. Support cron and interval schedules. Expose a manual-trigger endpoint for on-demand backups. Each scheduled task carries an owning user_id; at fire time, verify owner is still admin + active AND owning target credentials have not been rotated (Security Mandatory #5).

Source: ADR-008 D5 + Sequencing Phase 1.

User value: users can schedule recurring backups (nightly, weekly) without cron on the host; manual trigger supports 'before-upgrade' safety snapshots.

Success signal: scheduled backup fires at the configured time, produces a valid ZIP artifact with schema_version, emits progress via the polling endpoint; orphaned schedule with deactivated owner does NOT fire and logs the skip.

title: Phase 1: Backup ZIP artifact builder with schema_version + SHA256 + manifest
description:
Build the ZIP container: YAML settings file + binary subtree (per-image logo files + metadata.json + url-mappings.json) + SHA256 checksum file alongside the ZIP + manifest entry carrying schema_version.

schema_version is MANDATORY from the first release (ADR-008 D1 + Alternative A7 rejection). Restore on an unknown version refuses with user-facing message 'Unsupported backup version' (full detail server-side logs only, per D1 / S4).

Source: ADR-008 D1 + Sequencing Phase 1.

User value: a v0.18.0 backup is forward-compatible — future ECM versions can detect an older artifact and route to a migrator rather than silently misapplying it. The checksum protects against transport corruption on cloud-storage round-trips.

Success signal: manifest round-trips correctly on local restore; SHA256 validates; unknown schema_version is rejected with the correct user-facing message.

title: Phase 1: Cloud upload wiring (S3 / OneDrive / Dropbox / GDrive / WebDAV)
description:
Extend existing backend/cloud_storage/ adapters to accept the backup ZIP artifact + checksum. Add WebDAV adapter if missing. Per-destination audit log entry on every outbound upload call (Security Mandatory #3, #8).

Credential masking in outbound request logs — Authorization headers, bearer tokens, S3 access keys, OneDrive tokens, signed-URL query strings all masked in log lines (Security Mandatory #8).

Source: ADR-008 D4 + Sequencing Phase 1. Blocks on bead 0i2vt.1 (redaction — without it the uploaded artifact contains raw secrets) and 0i2vt.5 (SSRF — without it the destination URL can hit internal infra).

User value: backups land at a user-configured durable destination (off-host storage) so host failure does not lose the backup.

Success signal: all five destination types upload a v0.18.0 artifact successfully; audit log records each upload with masked credentials; failure modes (bad creds, network error, 507 out-of-space) surface a user-visible error.

title: Phase 1: Backup retention policy (last-N + time-based)
description:
Retention policy for backup artifacts (local + cloud): configurable last-N (keep last N successful backups) and time-based (keep backups newer than X days). Deletion runs after a successful new backup completes, never before — never leave a user with zero backups.

Aligns with ADR-007 precedent (retention/rollup policy) once filed by Stats v2 initiative.

Source: ADR-008 Sequencing Phase 1.

User value: cloud storage bills stay bounded; old backups aged out automatically so users do not manually prune.

Success signal: after N+1 backups, the oldest is gone; time-based pruning honors the threshold; audit log records each deletion with the reason (last-N or age-based).

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Onboarding finding (YELLOW, UX resilience). No React ErrorBoundary anywhere in frontend. A single uncaught render error white-screens the SPA mid-edit-session, losing unsaved edit-mode state (which useChangeHistory/useEditMode carefully preserve). Fix: add top-level boundary in App.tsx and per-tab boundaries so a broken Stats tab doesn't kill Channel Manager. Flagged by UX Designer during /onboard 2026-04-19.

title: Replace unicode/text icons with Material Icons in HistoryToolbar, EditModeToggle, and EditModeBanner
close_reason: Added Material Icons CDN to index.html, replaced unicode characters with Material Icons in HistoryToolbar (undo, redo, bookmark_add, close, fiber_manual_record), EditModeToggle (edit, check), and EditModeBanner (edit). Updated CSS for proper icon sizing.

title: Frontend: Two-pane layout with channels (left) and streams (right)
description:
Create the main UI layout:
- Left pane: Channels list
  - Display channel name, number, logo
  - Show count of assigned streams per channel
  - Clicking a channel shows its assigned streams (expandable or inline)
  - Scrollable list with virtualization for performance
- Right pane: Streams list  
  - Display stream name, logo, provider info
  - Visual indicator if stream is already assigned to selected channel
  - Scrollable list with virtualization for performance
- Responsive layout that works on various screen sizes
- Loading states and error handling

title: Backend API endpoints for frontend consumption
description:
Create FastAPI endpoints that proxy/aggregate Dispatcharr data:
- GET /api/channels - list channels with filtering support
- GET /api/channels/{id}/streams - get streams assigned to a channel
- GET /api/streams - list all streams with filtering support
- GET /api/channel-groups - list channel groups (for filter dropdowns)
- GET /api/stream-groups - list stream groups (for filter dropdowns)
- GET /api/providers - list M3U accounts/providers (for filter dropdowns)
- POST /api/channels/{id}/add-stream - add stream to channel
- POST /api/channels/{id}/remove-stream - remove stream from channel
- Query params for filtering: channel_group, m3u_account, search/name

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Added channel profile selection to bulk channel creation modal.

Features:
- New 'Channel Profiles' collapsible section in BulkCreateModal
- Multi-select checkboxes to select which profiles to add new channels to
- Profile assignments are applied after channels are committed to Dispatcharr
- Uses channel number range to find newly created channels and assign them

Implementation:
- Added channelProfiles prop to StreamsPane and BulkCreateModal UI
- Added bulkCreateSelectedProfiles state for tracking selected profiles
- Added profileIds parameter to onBulkCreateFromGroup callback
- Added pendingProfileAssignmentsRef to track assignments across commit
- Updated onCommitComplete to apply profile assignments after channels load
- Added CSS styling for profile selection section

Files modified:
- StreamsPane.tsx - Added UI and state for profile selection
- StreamsPane.css - Added profile section styling
- ChannelManagerTab.tsx - Pass channelProfiles to StreamsPane
- App.tsx - Handle profile assignments after commit

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed button styling in M3ULinkedAccountsModal edit-actions section. The Cancel and Create Group buttons were missing proper styles because they were in .edit-actions instead of .modal-footer. Added explicit button styles for .edit-actions to match the rest of the UI.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Scope investigation for SEC-104-1 finding from issue #104 team review. Determine whether user-submitted regex exposure is limited to normalization engine or codebase-wide, and which fix pattern (narrow / shared utility / library swap) is correct. Blocks Decision 3 item #4 of the normalization-parity epic.
close_reason: Spike complete. Recommendation: shared safe_regex utility (unanimous across 5 personas). Implementation tracked in bd-eio04.5-.8. Evidence: ~20 user-input regex callsites across 6 features; narrow fix leaves 5 features exposed; library swap affects 120 hardcoded patterns unnecessarily.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Epic: Full task management via MCP. Covers schedules, cancel, history, engine status. Enables 'Schedule M3U refresh every 6 hours', 'Cancel the running probe', 'Show task history' workflows.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/tasks/{id}/schedules. Create, update, and delete task schedules (cron-based).

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: POST /api/tasks/{id}/cancel. Cancel a currently running task.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: GET /api/tasks/{id}/history and GET /api/tasks/history/all. View past task runs with status and duration.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Dispatcharr stream API doesn't return channel_id. Built reverse map from existing channels to populate stream_ctx.channel_id so set_stream_priority (and remove_from_channel) can find channel associations. All 343 matched streams were being skipped with 'Stream not assigned to any channel'.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Fixed EPG matching to properly balance HD preference with call sign quality.

Issues fixed:
- US | 5050 - FYI: now prefers FYIChannelHD(FYIHD).us over FYI(FYISD).us
- US | 5051 - GET TV: now prefers getHD(GETHD).us over get(GETTVS).us
- US | 5079 - Lifetime Movie: now prefers LMNHD(LMNHD).us
- US | 5086 - MGM+: now prefers MGM+HD(MGMHD).us over MGM+West(MGMW).us
- US | 5092 - Ovation: now prefers OvationHD(OVATNHD).us over Ovation(OVATION).us
- US | 5097 - Pursuit: now prefers PursuitChannelHD(PURSTHD).us over PursuitUP(PRSTUP).us
- US | 5102 - Reelz: now prefers ReelzChannelHD(REELZHD).us over REELZ(VIZREEL).us
- US | 5113 - Tastemade Travel: now prefers TastemadeTravel(TMTRAVEL).us (actually this one should still work from v65)
- US | 5116 - TCM: now prefers TurnerClassicMoviesHD(TCMHD).us over TCM.us
- US | 5125 - TV Land: now prefers TVLandHD(TVLNDHD).us over TVLand(TVLAND).us

Root cause:
Previous logic (v65) prioritized call sign match quality over HD preference. This caused issues where non-HD variants with slightly better call sign matches (e.g., TVLAND exact match) were preferred over HD variants with good-but-not-exact call signs (e.g., TVLNDHD).

Solution:
Combined HD preference with call sign scoring. Now:
- When comparing HD vs non-HD, prefer HD UNLESS:
  - The non-HD has a MUCH better call sign (score difference >= 2)
  - AND the HD variant has a poor call sign (score <= 1)
- This means NOSEY (score 3) still beats VIZNOSE-HD (score 0)
- But TVLNDHD (score 2) now beats TVLAND (score 3) because diff is only 1

title: EPG Match modal sort order reversed (Z-A instead of A-Z)
description: EPG matching modal is sorting results in reverse alphabetical order, should be A-Z
notes: Fixed by adding sortByChannelName function in BulkEPGAssignModal.tsx. The autoMatched, conflicts, and unmatched arrays are now sorted alphabetically A-Z by channel name using localeCompare().

title: Debug channel groups not all displaying
description: Some channel groups like 'National Sports Networks' and 'Local Channels' are not showing in the UI. Need to investigate why groups are missing.

title: Auto-creation: country prefixes (DE:/AT:) and quality suffixes (3840P) no longer stripped from channel names
description: User reports that auto-creation rules that previously normalized 'DE: RTL 3840P' down to 'RTL' have stopped working. Channels are landing with full prefix+suffix. Same family as a fix shipped last week. Need to inspect the debug bundle in ~/ecm to determine whether it's a rule misconfiguration or a logic regression.

title: Move Upload Image button inline with URL input
description: Move the Upload Image button to be on the same line as the Add logo from URL input, removing the 'or' divider for a more consistent layout

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Added deparen and word-prefix matching sub-steps to merge_streams core-name fallback. Also fixed pre-existing bug where max_streams_per_provider was never enforced for existing channel streams because the API returns stream IDs (ints) not dicts — replaced broken init seeding with lazy per-channel fetch via get_channel_streams().

title: Frontend: Create NormalizationEngineTab in Settings
description: Create new Settings section or sub-tab for normalization engine. Layout: RulesList on left (with add rule button), RuleTestPanel on right. Include rule group management (create/rename/delete groups), import/export rules as JSON, reset to defaults button with confirmation.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fix dry-run vs execute discrepancy: track simulated channels/groups in _created_channels/_created_groups caches during dry-run mode so subsequent streams see them as existing, matching execute-mode behavior. The cache uses normalized channel names as keys.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Added onSourcesChange callback to EPGManagerTab so that when EPG sources are added or deleted, the main App.tsx state is refreshed. This ensures the EPG source list in search dropdowns is updated without requiring a page refresh.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed EPG matching to prioritize country matching above all other criteria. Previously, exact name matches would beat country matching, causing 'Discovery' to match 'DiscoveryHD.be' over 'DiscoveryChannel.us'. Now country matching is the first sort criterion, ensuring US channels prefer US EPG entries. Also added default to US when no country is detected.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fix prober settings not pushing to backend without restart. Added live-push of 9 settings (auto_reorder_after_probe, probe_timeout, bitrate_sample_duration, etc.) in settings save handler.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed M3U Changes table header alignment bug caused by CSS class conflict with M3U Manager. Both tabs defined .list-header with different grid-template-columns. Scoped selectors to parent containers (.m3u-changes-tab .list-header and .m3u-manager-tab .list-header) to prevent CSS cascade conflicts when switching tabs.

title: Fix duplicate channel numbers when using push-down and drag operations in edit mode
Fix EPG Data endpoint path
description: Changed backend endpoint from /api/epg/data/ to /api/epg/epgdata/ to match Dispatcharr API. The EPG Data dropdown in the Edit Channel modal now properly fetches and displays EPG entries.

title: Fix Stream Profile endpoint path
description: Changed backend endpoint from /api/stream_profiles/ to /api/core/streamprofiles/ to match Dispatcharr API. The Stream Profile dropdown in the Edit Channel modal now properly shows all available profiles (ffmpeg, Proxy, Redirect, streamlink, etc.).

title: Add Get from EPG button for TVG-ID field

title: Fix EPG Data API response handling

title: Document infra-side cache invalidation for operators behind nginx/Cloudflare/CDN
description:
Surfaced by SRE runbook spike (2026-04-24, PR #164). docs/runbooks/dep-bump-frontend-regression.md covers browser-cache invalidation (asset hashes, hard reload, service-worker flush) but assumes users hit ECM directly. Operators deploying behind nginx / Cloudflare / Varnish / other CDNs may need infra-side flush steps that the runbook doesn't cover.

Two shapes to consider:
1. Add a section to dep-bump-frontend-regression.md covering common reverse-proxy cache behavior.
2. Create sibling runbook docs/runbooks/infra-cache-invalidation.md that any frontend-touching runbook links.

Option 2 is preferred — the cache concern isn't dep-bump-specific, it applies to any frontend release.

Scope (either option):
- Common reverse-proxy configs: nginx location blocks with proxy_cache, Cloudflare cache rules, Varnish VCL patterns.
- How to flush after a frontend release (purge-by-URL, purge-all, soft-purge).
- How operator verifies cache is flushed (curl + response headers, asset-hash spot-check).
- Link from dep-bump-frontend-regression.md and from any future frontend-release runbook.

Acceptance:
- File or section exists with the three items above.
- Linked from docs/runbooks/README.md index.
close_reason: Merged via PR #167 (c4b8749a) — docs/runbooks/infra-cache-invalidation.md shipped covering nginx/Cloudflare/Varnish/CDN invalidation patterns with verification via asset-hash cross-check.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Add an optional provider diversification step to the stream probe's auto-reorder (Smart Sort) pipeline. After streams are sorted by quality (resolution, bitrate, etc.), optionally interleave them across M3U accounts so failback streams alternate providers instead of clustering on one. This improves failover resilience — if one provider goes down, the next stream in line comes from a different provider.

Implementation approach:
- Add a diversification step after the existing Smart Sort in stream_prober.py _smart_sort_streams()
- Two modes: round-robin (alphabetical rotation across accounts) and priority-weighted (user-assigned account priorities determine order)
- Controlled by a new setting (e.g. stream_sort_diversify: disabled/round_robin/priority_weighted) alongside existing sort criteria
- Could reuse the existing m3u_account_priorities setting already in the prober
- Should be per-schedule configurable like other probe parameters

Inspired by PR #37 (closed — broader feature set was undiscussed and duplicated existing functionality, but this specific concept has value).

title: Spike: session semantics for SLO-6 denominator (ecm_session_starts_total)
description:
Decide how ECM defines a "session" before implementing the ecm_session_starts_total counter. The SLO-6 SLI denominator needs a well-defined session boundary; getting this wrong once and then changing it later invalidates 28 days of SLI history.

## Candidates (all proposed during i6a1m extension work)

**A. One per page load (SPA boot)**
- Pros: simplest to implement; frontend emits once on initial mount; no auth dependency; captures pre-login bootstrap errors.
- Cons: multi-tab inflation — 3 tabs of the same user = 3 "sessions". Also every hard-refresh resets. SLO-6 denominator becomes noisier than actual unique user-sessions.

**B. One per JWT issuance (auth-tied)**
- Pros: aligned with existing auth flow; natural session boundary; single counter bump per login event.
- Cons: excludes anonymous/pre-login bootstrap errors from the SLI denominator — the "bootstrap" error kind in clientErrorReporter.ts can't match against this denominator. Also undercounts: long-lived JWTs mean one "session" can span days of activity.

**C. One per session-cookie lifetime**
- Pros: closest to a true "unique user-session" — SubtleCrypto-generated UUID stored in sessionStorage, counter emitted once on first page load with that session_id. Survives same-tab navigation, resets on tab close. No auth dependency.
- Cons: requires a new frontend bookkeeping surface (session cookie/sessionStorage write); need to decide scope (sessionStorage vs localStorage vs httpOnly cookie); slight privacy surface (though scoped to same-origin SLI use).

## Out of scope for this spike

- Implementation of the counter itself — that's the follow-up bead (see DEPENDS_ON below, blocked on this spike).
- SLO target revision — 99.0% lands with i6a1m; revise only if the spike surfaces that the chosen semantic meaningfully changes the measurable baseline.

## Who to consult

- **SRE** — recommended C during standup for measurement accuracy. Owns SLO evolution.
- **UX Designer** — weigh in on multi-tab semantics and whether sessionStorage write is visible/degrading for users.
- **Security Engineer** — review the session_id surface: is it PII? Does it need rotation? Any LGPD/GDPR implication of adding a per-session identifier?
- **Project Engineer** — sizing + implementation complexity of each candidate.

## Deliverable

One-page decision doc (committed as a retro/spike artifact, OR embedded in a new ADR if the consensus is this needs an architectural decision record). Decision must name the chosen semantic with one-line justification per persona, plus enumerate the implementation steps for the follow-up bead.

Run /spike when ready.

LABELS: spike, sre, slo, observability
close_reason: Spike complete. Decision: candidate C (cookie/sessionStorage lifetime). All three personas (SRE, Security, UX) converged on C from independent rationales. Decision doc merged to dev: docs/sre/spike-slo-6-session-semantics.md (PR #192, merge SHA b4e033b87682aace36bb57a1143d1e76ccd304b1). Implementation steps for arp3o documented in §6 of the doc; arp3o is now actionable.

title: Promote CLAUDE.md + docs/css_guidelines.md rules into docs/style_guide.md
description:
**Why:** Style rules are currently scattered across CLAUDE.md (agent instructions), docs/css_guidelines.md, and tacit conventions. The ESLint 10 flat-config migration (bd-5x6n7) will rename/remove/restructure rules; without a persistent rationale doc, the zero-lint floor cannot be preserved intentionally across that migration. Also needed for the DBAS Python port (bd-gb5r5.3) — 'name-based conflict resolution' semantics and API conventions need a style reference so the TS port does not leak camelCase / class-heavy idioms into the Python side.

**Scope:** Consolidate into docs/style_guide.md:
- TypeScript / React naming, file structure, hook patterns
- Python naming (PEP 8 + project-specific exceptions)
- API contract conventions (REST + WebSocket message envelope {type, phase, progress, data, meta})
- Error envelope for HTTP responses
- Test naming & structure (reference docs/pytest_conventions.md rather than duplicate)
- Comment & docstring policy
- Import ordering
- When // eslint-disable-next-line / # noqa is acceptable (reason required)
- Link from CLAUDE.md Reference Guides table
- Update docs/frontend_lint.md (from PR #76) to defer to the style guide where they overlap

**Acceptance:**
- docs/style_guide.md exists and covers both TypeScript and Python
- CLAUDE.md Reference Guides table references it
- No style rule lives in CLAUDE.md only — each rule is either migrated to the style guide or cross-linked
- docs/frontend_lint.md defers to the style guide on overlapping rules

**Out of scope:** Retroactive style enforcement on existing code — the zero-lint floor already enforces going forward.

**Blocks:** bd-5x6n7 (eslint 10 flat-config migration) — rule rationale needs a home before rules get renamed/restructured.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #158. New docs/style_guide.md (8 sections: Naming Conventions, Module Organization, Comments and Docstrings, Regex, Error Handling and Logging, CSS Conventions, Frontend Lint Policy, Test Conventions). Sources drained from CLAUDE.md (root + frontend/) and existing dev docs (css_guidelines.md, frontend_lint.md, pytest_conventions.md). 5 files changed, +434/-24. CSS + Lint + Test sections summarize and defer to existing authoritative docs. 3 conflicts surfaced for PO decision (state-class naming consistency .is-/.active mix; --accent-50 vs theme-flip rule; pytest_conventions.md scope-vs-name).

title: CI: doc-only PRs blocked by required CodeQL checks (paths-ignore + branch protection chicken-and-egg)
description:
Surfaced by PR #139 (bd-pf82j). .github/workflows/build.yml and test.yml use paths-ignore: '**.md', so doc-only PRs trigger zero CI. But dev branch protection requires 5 checks (Backend Tests, Frontend Tests, CodeQL Analysis (python), CodeQL Analysis (javascript-typescript), Semgrep Lint). Result: doc-only PRs are unmergeable without admin override or hacky code-touch.

Approaches (engineer picks based on what's smallest + safest):
(a) Remove '**.md' from paths-ignore — full CI on every doc PR; correct but slow
(b) Add a sentinel 'docs-only-pass' workflow that triggers on paths: '**.md' and emits check-runs matching the required names with success — preserves CI economy; requires careful name matching across multiple jobs (RECOMMENDED)
(c) dorny/paths-filter inside existing workflows to no-op the heavy steps for doc-only PRs

Constraint: must not silently mask real CI failures on mixed PRs (any non-docs path in a PR must trigger real CI). Approach (b) needs paths/paths-ignore to be mutually exclusive between the sentinel workflow and the main workflows.

Verification: open a test PR with only a CHANGELOG.md edit and confirm it merges cleanly with all 5 required checks satisfied. Then verify a mixed PR (docs + .py) still gets the real checks, not the sentinel.

Directly blocks PR #139 today; affects every future doc-only PR (api.md updates, runbook additions, CHANGELOG ceremony, ADR drafts).

Effort: ~1-2 hours including verification on real test PRs.

User value (DX): doc improvements ship without ceremony or workarounds.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #141. New .github/workflows/docs-only-pass.yml emits the 5 required check-runs (Backend Tests, Frontend Tests, Semgrep Lint, CodeQL Analysis (python+javascript-typescript) via matrix) for doc-only PRs matching paths: ['**.md', '.beads/**']. Path filter is the strict complement of build.yml/test.yml's paths-ignore. Mixed-PR safety verified live: PR #141 itself triggered real CI (workflow file is not a doc path), confirming sentinel does not mask real failures. Unblocks PR #139 (api.md backfill from bd-pf82j) once its branch updates pick up the sentinel.

E2E in CI: Dispatcharr mock sidecar OR self-hosted runner
description:
Reframed 2026-04-19 after engineer evaluation (Path C chosen).

ORIGINAL SCOPE (obsolete): seed the ECM container for modals.spec.ts conditional skips.

REAL PROBLEM — architectural:
- ECM is a proxy. ~15 of 21 conditional skips in modals.spec.ts require Dispatcharr-owned entities (channels, channel_groups, M3U accounts, profiles, logos, EPG sources). No amount of ECM-side seeding produces them.
- CI currently points DISPATCHARR_URL at localhost:9999 (a black hole). There is no Dispatcharr instance in CI.
- Only ~6 of 21 skips could be covered by ECM-native seeding (dummy EPG, auto-creation rules, orphan-scan state).
- This means the E2E job as originally designed in t8xw3 can never be a real gate on GitHub-hosted runners.

TWO REAL PATHS (pick when scheduled):
1. **Mock Dispatcharr sidecar.** Extend backend/tests/fixtures/mock_dispatcharr.py into a standalone FastAPI container; docker-compose alongside ECM in CI; point DISPATCHARR_URL at it. Effort ~10-14h. Risk: mock drifts from real Dispatcharr contract → false-positive passes.
2. **Self-hosted GitHub runner with real Dispatcharr.** Run CI on a local machine that has the full integration environment (Dispatcharr + Postgres + mock M3U sources). Highest fidelity; biggest infra lift. Requires managing runner uptime, security boundaries, data hygiene.

DECISION POSTPONED:
PO chose Path C from the 2026-04-19 evaluation: pull E2E from t8xw3 entirely and treat E2E-in-CI as a separate future initiative. This bead now tracks THAT initiative.

SCOPE FOR THIS BEAD (when picked up):
- Evaluate mock vs. self-hosted runner vs. a hybrid.
- Implement chosen approach.
- Add e2e job back to .github/workflows/test.yml (gated on PR-to-main + nightly + workflow_dispatch per original t8xw3 design).
- Ensure all 21 modals.spec.ts skips either run cleanly or are explicitly marked fixme with reason.
- Required checks on main include the e2e job (companion bead 8w33i needs updating once this lands).

OUT OF SCOPE:
- Hygiene cleanup of test.skip() → test.fixme() in modals.spec.ts. That's a separate small bead so the silent-green antipattern is killed immediately, not blocked on E2E infrastructure work.

Status: P2, no immediate priority. Sits in backlog until PO chooses to invest.
design:
Evaluation in progress (research phase). See final brief in conversation — do not implement until PO greenlights.

Key architectural finding: Most spec.ts state requirements route via Dispatcharr (external service). In CI today, DISPATCHARR_URL=http://localhost:9999 is a black hole — no Dispatcharr runs. ECM-native data is limited to: auto-creation rules, dummy EPG profiles, FFmpeg profiles, normalization rules, tags, alerts, scheduled tasks, journal, and notifications. The 21 skips' required entities (orphaned groups, channels, M3U accounts/profiles, logos, LCN-capable channels, M3U filters) are ALL Dispatcharr-owned.

Implication: Seeding via ECM's REST API (option 1) is insufficient by itself — those API routes forward to Dispatcharr. Options must be reframed:
- Option 1 as written (API-driven seed via ECM): only works for auto-creation + dummy EPG. Does NOT cover the majority of the 21 skips.
- Option 2 (SQL-direct to /config/ecm.db): also only covers ECM-native entities. The real data is in Dispatcharr's Postgres, unreachable from CI without standing up a Dispatcharr service.
- Option 3 (/api/testing/seed endpoint): same constraint — endpoint lives in ECM, can't write to Dispatcharr.

Missing option (option 4): Mock the Dispatcharr backend. Add a test-mode env flag that makes DispatcharrClient return a deterministic fixture from a JSON file instead of hitting the real service. Already have partial infra: backend/tests/fixtures/mock_dispatcharr.py uses respx for unit tests; that pattern could be extended to an in-process Dispatcharr shim served via docker-compose in the E2E job.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
Closed as won't-fix per PO 2026-04-25.

Rationale: E2E in CI requires either (a) a standalone Dispatcharr mock sidecar Docker image built from backend/tests/fixtures/mock_dispatcharr.py, OR (b) a self-hosted runner with a real Dispatcharr instance. Neither environment is initiable in our setup, so the work is structurally blocked rather than calendar-blocked.

The 656-line respx-based mock_dispatcharr fixture remains useful for unit/integration tests inside the existing test suite — that doesn't change. Only the CI E2E lane is being declined.

The decomposition recipe (decision spike, Path 1 build, CI compose stack, workflow re-introduction, modals.spec.ts skip audit, branch-protection update) is preserved in this bead's prior comment if circumstances change.

Related: bd-t8xw3 (original E2E removal from CI) and bd-8w33i (branch-protection required-checks) — neither blocked by this closure.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
User feedback: Two matching gaps in merge_streams logic:

1. 'Bravo East' does not match 'US Bravo (East)' — after country prefix strip we get 'Bravo (East)' but the channel is 'Bravo East'. The parentheses around directional words (East/West) prevent matching.

2. 'Fox News Channel' does not match 'US Fox News' — after country prefix strip we get 'Fox News' but the channel is 'Fox News Channel'. The shorter provider name is a substring of the channel name but not an exact match.

Possible approaches:
- For case 1: normalize parenthetical directions in core-name comparison, e.g. 'Bravo (East)' -> 'Bravo East'
- For case 2: needs careful thought. Cannot just strip 'Channel' since providers may also use it. Could try a 'channel name contains provider name' fallback tier, but risk of false positives. May need scoring/confidence thresholds.

Low priority — user says 'not a big deal' and overall matching is working well.

title: Per-provider stream stats: buffering / time / bitrate over time (GH-59)
description:
GitHub issue #59, filed 2026-04-13. User priority: Low.

## Ask
Enhance stats page to enable analysis of upstream provider usage and performance:
- Buffering events by provider
- Time spent watching each provider
- Channels-by-provider heat map
- Bitrate by channel/provider over time

## Goal
Help operator decide which providers to keep vs. replace.

## Scope considerations
- Requires sustained per-stream telemetry (buffering events, bitrate sampling, duration) — verify ECM captures this
- Data model: time-series per provider
- UI: Stats tab extension

## Related
GH #62 (User stats) — overlapping scope; consider consolidating into single Stats v2 initiative

## External ref
https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/issues/59
notes: 2026-04-21: Deferred from v0.17.0 per team-plan consensus. Linked to Stats v2 epic enhancedchannelmanager-skqln as v0.18.0+ scope. Requires new ingest (provider tagging, buffering events, bitrate sampling) — GH-59 impl beads will be filed when v0.18.0 planning happens.

comments.author: Claude

Impl lives under skqln epic as children: skqln.14 (provider resolver), skqln.15 (buffer-event ingest), skqln.16 (4 read APIs), skqln.17 (chart primitives/heatmap), skqln.18 (Providers panel). This tracker closes when skqln.16 + skqln.18 ship. Grooming 2026-04-23 was 6/6 Stay Deferred; PO override accepted the risks (see skqln epic body for documented risk acceptance).

title: Prefer exact call sign match over suffix-stripped match

comments.author: lecaptainc
comments.text:
Fixed FYI matching to prefer exact call sign match over suffix-stripped match.

Issue: 'FYI' was matching 'FYI(FYISD).us' over 'FYI(FYI).us' because both call signs matched after stripping the SD suffix.

Fix: Added priority levels for call sign matching:
1. Exact call sign match (FYI = fyi) - highest priority
2. Base call sign match after stripping suffix (FYIHD → fyi) - second priority  
3. No match - lowest priority

Now 'FYI(FYI).us' (exact match) is correctly preferred over 'FYI(FYISD).us' (base match).

Version: 0.2.30063

title: Phase 2 automation: mechanical G1a + G1b + G5 + G6 + G7 verification on release-cut PRs (depends on bd in CI for G1a)
description:
Automate the manually-verified pre-cut gate items from ADR-004 §3 via a GitHub Action that runs on release-cut PRs (PRs whose title matches the regex ^Release v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ AND whose head branch matches ^release/v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$).

## Mechanically-verifiable gates

| Gate | Mechanism | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| G1a | Run 'bd list --status open --priority 0' and 'bd list --status open --priority 1' against the cut SHA — both must be empty, OR every open item must be cited by ID in the PR description's 'G1a Justifications' section | **Requires 'bd' available in CI** — the dependency. Either a 'bd' container image, a Go binary fetched in the action step, or the beads project's published action if one exists. |
| G1b | 'gh api repos/:owner/:repo/code-scanning/alerts --paginate | jq …' — must return 0 OR every open alert is cited by alert number + dismissal category in the PR description's 'G1b Waivers' section per docs/shipping.md G1b 'formally waived' semantics | Easier than G1a; 'gh' is already available in actions. The PR-description parsing is the harder half. |
| G5 | Diff CHANGELOG.md between the PR head and 'main'; assert (a) a [Unreleased] section is present + empty, (b) a [X.Y.Z] section (matching the PR title's version) is present with today's date | Pure file diff + regex; no external state. |
| G6 | Parse 'frontend/package.json' on the PR head; assert version == X.Y.Z (extracted from PR title / branch name) | Trivial. |
| G7 | 'gh pr list --base main --state open --json number,title' — must return only the PR being checked (or that PR + a hotfix PR with the hotfix-priority tiebreaker handled) | Trivial; runs at merge-time, not PR-open-time, to avoid race windows. |

G2, G3, G4 are already mechanically enforced via branch protection required checks + Code Scanning merge protection rule (per ADR-005). No new automation needed for those.

## Implementation sketch

- New workflow: '.github/workflows/release-cut-gate.yml'
- Trigger: 'pull_request' (opened, synchronize, edited) AND 'pull_request_review' if needed for the merge-time G7 re-check.
- Job: 'release-cut-gate' — only runs when 'github.event.pull_request.title' matches the release-cut regex.
- Steps: install 'bd' (G1a), run each gate check, post a check-run with per-gate pass/fail, optionally post a sticky PR comment summarizing which gates pass and which require justification.
- Block merge: register the 'release-cut-gate' check as a required status check on 'main' branch protection (additive to ADR-005's CodeQL checks). Hotfix PRs are exempt because their branch name does not match 'release/v…'.

## Out of scope

- Hotfix-PR gate automation (different scope subset; if needed, file a separate follow-on).
- CHANGELOG content quality lint (ADR-004 §Implementation Sketch §3 lists this as 'separate docs-tooling bead if desired'; not bundled here).
- Auto-creating the release-cut PR or auto-promoting CHANGELOG (still PO-authored).

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED ON: 'bd' available in CI runner. If beads doesn't yet ship a GitHub Action or installable binary suitable for CI, file a sub-bead to address that first.
- Soft dependency on bd-tvlxz item 5 (G1b 'formally waived' semantics) — already landed in docs/shipping.md; the automation needs to parse PR description per that semantics.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Release-cut-gate workflow lives at '.github/workflows/release-cut-gate.yml'.
- [ ] All five gates (G1a, G1b, G5, G6, G7) report individual pass/fail status in the PR check-runs.
- [ ] At least one synthetic-PR test demonstrates each gate failing and then passing.
- [ ] 'release-cut-gate' added to 'main' branch protection required checks (in addition to ADR-005's existing CodeQL + tests checks).
- [ ] docs/shipping.md updated to note that Phase 2 enforcement is now active and the manual checklist is now a redundant safety net (kept for the cut-authorizer to read, no longer the primary gate).

## References

- ADR-004 §4 ("Implementation Sketch" / "Out of scope" — Phase 2 automation explicitly punted to follow-on bead)
- ADR-004 §Sequencing & Rollout item 5 ("Month 2+ — Phase 2 automation (separate bead)")
- bd-tvlxz item 6 (this bead's parent task)
- docs/shipping.md §Pre-Cut Gate Checklist (semantics being automated)
- docs/shipping.md §G1b 'formally waived' semantics (parsing target for G1b automation)

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Fixed 500 error when creating new group during bulk channel creation.

Root cause: The code was calling api.createChannelGroup() directly when the user selected 'Create new group' in the bulk create modal. This called the Dispatcharr API immediately, but the Dispatcharr API returned a 400 Bad Request (possibly due to the group name already existing). The backend caught this and returned a 500 Internal Server Error.

Solution: Stage the group creation along with the channel creation, and only create the group in Dispatcharr when committing all staged changes.

Changes:
1. Added 'newGroupName' field to createChannel API call spec
2. Updated stageCreateChannel to accept newGroupName parameter  
3. Updated commit logic to:
   - Collect all unique new group names from staged createChannel operations
   - Create those groups first before creating channels
   - Map group names to their new IDs
   - Use the mapped ID when creating channels
4. Updated App.tsx handleBulkCreateFromGroup to pass newGroupName instead of calling API directly

Now when the user selects 'Create new group':
- The group creation is staged along with the channels
- The group is only created in Dispatcharr when the user clicks 'Done' (commits)
- If the user discards, no orphaned group is left in Dispatcharr
