Feature allows users to select specific channel groups to probe. Only groups with channels containing streams are shown. Empty selection means probe all groups with streams.

title: CodeQL config: exclude py/unused-global-variable for backend/alembic/versions/** (framework false-positive)
description: Every new Alembic migration triggers 4 note-severity py/unused-global-variable alerts for revision, down_revision, branch_labels, depends_on — framework identifiers Alembic reads by module introspection. Dismissed 4 on PR #110 (alerts 1466-1469) as false-positive. Fix: add a narrow per-path, per-query exclusion in .github/codeql/codeql-config.yml so future migrations don't re-trigger. Scope the exclusion to the rule id py/unused-global-variable on path backend/alembic/versions/** only — do NOT exclude the whole directory from CodeQL (real security findings like SQL injection in migrations must still surface).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #137 (v0.16.0-0053). Added query-filters exclusion of py/unused-global-variable scoped to backend/alembic/versions/** in .github/codeql/codeql-config.yml. False-positive root cause: Alembic introspects depends_on/down_revision/branch_labels at runtime via getattr; CodeQL static analysis can't see the framework reading them. Other CodeQL queries still apply to migration files.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Fixed pagination bug in /api/channel-groups/with-streams endpoint.

The endpoint was only fetching 1 channel per group (page_size=1), which meant it would miss groups where only the 2nd, 3rd, or later channels had streams. This caused no groups to appear in the probe channel group selection UI.

Changed logic to:
- Fetch all channels in each group using pagination (page_size=500)
- Check all channels in the group, not just the first one
- Stop checking as soon as we find one channel with streams (optimization)

This fixes the issue where users saw 'No groups with streams available' even when groups existed.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Fixed /api/channel-groups/with-streams endpoint by rewriting the logic.

The previous fix (v0.7.20039) used channel_group_id as a filter parameter when fetching channels, but this parameter doesn't exist in the Dispatcharr API. This caused the endpoint to not work correctly.

New approach:
1. Fetch all channel groups and build a lookup map (group_id -> group)
2. Fetch all channels in a single pass
3. For each channel that has streams, record its channel_group ID in a set
4. Return only the groups whose IDs are in that set

This is simpler, more efficient (one pass through channels instead of N passes), and doesn't rely on unsupported API parameters. Added detailed logging to track progress.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Added channel group selection feature for stream probing with modal UI.

Implemented:
- v0.7.20038-20043: Backend channel group filtering infrastructure
  - Added probe_channel_groups config setting
  - Created /api/channel-groups/with-streams endpoint
  - Modified stream_prober.py to filter by selected groups
  - Fixed field name bug (channel_group vs channel_group_id)
- v0.7.20044: Made 'Probe all streams now' button respect group selection
  - Added ProbeAllRequest model with optional channel_groups filter
  - Modified probe endpoint to accept group filter parameter
  - Updated frontend to pass currently selected groups
- v0.7.20045: Moved group selection to modal dialog
  - Created modal with checkbox list for group selection
  - Added button showing selection count (e.g., '3 groups selected')
  - Improved UI by decluttering settings page

User can now:
- Select specific channel groups to probe (both manual and scheduled)
- Use modal dialog for clean selection interface
- See selection count at a glance without opening modal
- Leave all unchecked to probe all groups with streams

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Trigger lazy load when dragging unexpanded stream groups. When a group's drag handle is grabbed and the group has no streams loaded, onGroupExpand is called to fetch them. Also handles multi-group drag by loading all selected groups that need streams.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Add video bitrate capture and audio codec badge

Backend changes:
- models.py: Added video_bitrate field to StreamStats model (BigInteger, bits per second)
- stream_prober.py: Extract video bitrate from ffprobe video stream data (video_stream.bit_rate)
- Updated to_dict() to include video_bitrate in API responses

Frontend changes:
- types/index.ts: Added video_bitrate to StreamStats interface
- ChannelsPane.tsx: Added formatBitrate() helper and display video bitrate in stream metadata tags (e.g., "5.2 Mbps")
- StatsTab.tsx: Added audio codec badge display in channel card header
- StatsTab.css: Added styling for audio-codec-badge (purple background, white text)

The video bitrate is now captured from ffprobe for each stream and displayed alongside resolution, fps, and codec info in the Channels pane. Audio codec is shown as a badge in the live Stats tab.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Fixed httpx.Timeout configuration error in bitrate measurement

The _measure_stream_bitrate() method was using httpx.Timeout with only connect and read parameters. httpx requires either a single default timeout OR all four parameters (connect, read, write, pool) to be set explicitly.

Updated timeout configuration to include all four parameters:
- connect: 10.0s (connection establishment)
- read: BITRATE_SAMPLE_DURATION + 5.0s (data reading)  
- write: 10.0s (data writing)
- pool: 10.0s (connection pool timeout)

This fixes the error: 'httpx.Timeout must either include a default, or set all four parameters explicitly.'

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Added optional setting to deprioritize failed streams in smart sort. When enabled, streams with probe_status of 'failed', 'timeout', or 'pending' are automatically sorted to the bottom of the stream list during smart sort operations, ensuring working streams are prioritized for playback. Changes: Added 'deprioritize_failed_streams' config setting (backend/config.py, defaults to true), updated ChannelDefaults interface (frontend StreamsPane.tsx), modified createMultiCriteriaSortComparator to check probe_status first when setting enabled (frontend ChannelsPane.tsx), added checkbox in Settings > Stream Sort Priority section (frontend SettingsTab.tsx), updated TypeScript types (frontend api.ts, App.tsx).

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Added setting to skip recently probed streams to prevent excessive probing. New setting 'Skip recently probed streams (hours)' allows users to configure a time window (0-168 hours) to skip streams that were successfully probed within that period. Set to 0 to always probe all streams. This prevents hammering the probe service when running multiple checks in succession. Changes: Added skip_recently_probed_hours config setting (backend config.py, defaults to 0), updated StreamProber class to accept parameter and filter recently probed streams (backend stream_prober.py), queries StreamStats for successful probes within threshold and skips them, added UI input in Settings > Stream Probe section (frontend SettingsTab.tsx), updated TypeScript types (frontend api.ts). Version: 0.7.20074

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Fix stats page M3U counters showing 0/x despite active streams

Issue: Stats page showed channels as active but M3U counters displayed 0/x instead of actual usage
Root cause: Code was counting connections by m3u_profile_id but looking up counts by account.id
Fix: Added profileToAccountMap to properly map profile IDs to account IDs before counting

Changes made in frontend/src/components/tabs/StatsTab.tsx:
- Built map of profile ID -> account ID from M3U accounts
- When counting active connections, map each profile ID to its parent account ID
- Increment counters using account ID instead of profile ID
- Updated debug logging to show mapping for troubleshooting

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Implement re-run for failed streams only instead of all streams

Issue: In the failed streams modal, the 'Re-run All Streams' button was re-probing ALL streams instead of just the failed ones shown in the modal. There was even a TODO comment in the code indicating this needed to be implemented.

Fix: Implemented proper failed streams re-probe functionality using the existing probeBulkStreams API

Changes made in frontend/src/components/tabs/SettingsTab.tsx:
- Updated handleRerunFailed to extract stream IDs from failed_streams array
- Call api.probeBulkStreams with only the failed stream IDs
- Show progress indicator and result message
- Refresh probe history after completion to show updated results
- Changed button text from 'Re-run All Streams' to 'Re-probe Failed Streams' for clarity
- Added proper null checks for TypeScript safety

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Renamed section header from 'Stream Sort Priority' to 'Smart Sort Priority' for better clarity. Fixed priority number visibility in sort criteria circles by adding fallback colors and improved text styling. The circles now properly display priority numbers (1, 2, 3) in white text on colored backgrounds. Changes: Renamed header in SettingsTab.tsx line 1371, improved circle styling with fallback CSS colors and explicit text color at lines 109-114.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Add semantic version tagging to GitHub Actions container builds. Extract version from frontend/package.json and tag Docker images with semver format (e.g., 0.7.20080-dev) in addition to existing branch/SHA tags. This enables version-based container deployment and better release tracking.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Added 'Hide EPG URLs' and 'Hide M3U URLs' settings to Display Options in Settings tab. When enabled, these settings hide sensitive URLs in the EPG Manager and M3U Manager tabs respectively. Updated: api.ts (added hide_epg_urls and hide_m3u_urls to SettingsResponse and saveSettings), SettingsTab.tsx (added state, load/save logic, and UI checkboxes), App.tsx (added state and prop passing), EPGManagerTab.tsx (conditionally hide EPG URLs and copy button), M3UManagerTab.tsx (conditionally hide M3U server URLs). Version bumped to 0.7.20087.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Added Gracenote ID conflict resolution with modal and settings. Created GracenoteConflictModal component to show conflicts when assigning IDs. Added gracenote_conflict_mode setting (ask/skip/overwrite) in Settings > Display Options. Modified ChannelsPane.handleBulkLCNAssign to detect conflicts and handle based on user preference. Files: GracenoteConflictModal.tsx/.css (new), api.ts (added GracenoteConflictMode type + setting), SettingsTab.tsx (added UI dropdown), ChannelsPane.tsx (conflict detection logic), ChannelManagerTab.tsx (prop passing), App.tsx (state management). Version: 0.7.20088.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed Hide EPG URLs, Hide M3U URLs, and Gracenote Conflict Mode settings not persisting. The backend config.py was missing these three fields: hide_epg_urls (default false), hide_m3u_urls (default false), and gracenote_conflict_mode (default 'ask'). Added all three fields to backend/config.py appearance settings section. Version: 0.7.20089.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed settings persistence bug where hide_epg_urls, hide_m3u_urls, gracenote_conflict_mode, skip_recently_probed_hours, and deprioritize_failed_streams were not being saved. Root cause: These 5 fields existed in backend/config.py but were missing from SettingsRequest and SettingsResponse models in backend/main.py, and were not being passed through in update_settings or get_current_settings functions. Added all 5 fields to both models and both API endpoint handlers.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed Gracenote conflict modal not appearing when assigning IDs to channels with existing IDs. Root cause: BulkLCNFetchModal.handleAssign() and selectedCount only iterated over 'found' array, excluding channels in 'alreadyHas' category. Even if user manually checked a channel that already had a Gracenote ID, it wouldn't be included in assignments, so conflict detection never ran. Fixed by changing both functions to iterate over ALL results instead of just 'found' array.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed Gracenote ID lookup to only search the channel's assigned EPG source instead of all sources. Previously, the backend would search ALL EPG sources and return the first match, which could give incorrect results if multiple EPG sources had the same TVG-ID. Now each channel specifies its EPG source ID, and the backend only searches that specific source. Backend groups requests by EPG source for efficiency. Falls back to searching all sources if channel has no EPG source assigned.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed DAST workflow failing silently when application doesn't start. The wait loop would check for health endpoint but never fail if app didn't become ready, allowing ZAP scan to run against non-existent service. Added proper failure handling, better logging of container status and logs, and health verification step before scan runs.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Added comprehensive preflight checks to container startup with visual feedback using colored output and checkmarks. Checks: Python environment and packages, filesystem permissions and directories, network port availability, application module loading. Shows green checkmarks for success, red X for failures, yellow warnings. Exits early with detailed error messages if any check fails. Makes debugging container startup issues much easier.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed Python SyntaxError in backend/main.py at line 2037. The except blocks in the Gracenote batch lookup code were incorrectly indented (4 spaces too far, nested inside else block instead of aligned with try block). Dedented exception handlers to proper alignment. Verified with py_compile.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Updated CLAUDE.md to explicitly require syntax validation in quality gates step. Added mandatory Python syntax check (py_compile) for backend changes and TypeScript compilation for frontend changes. Created quality-gates.sh (Linux/Mac) and quality-gates.bat (Windows) scripts to run all checks at once. This prevents committing code with syntax errors like the one introduced in v0.7.3-0092.

title: Testing: Add frontend tests for sort criteria and probe schedules
description: Add Vitest tests for: sort criteria drag-drop with new criteria, ProbeScheduleManager component, AddEditProbeScheduleModal form validation, API service functions for probe schedules. Mock API responses.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Added margin-right: 0.25rem to .checkbox-option input[type="checkbox"] in StreamsPane.css to increase spacing between checkbox and label text

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Issue #27: _tag_group_cache in normalization_engine.py is never cleared when tags are added/removed/updated via the API. Add invalidate_tag_cache() and call it from tag mutation endpoints.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Bump version to 0.16.0 in main.py and package.json. Update changelog. Final quality gates before release.

title: Require edit mode for stream operations

Stream drag-drop onto channels, stream removal, and stream reordering now require edit mode to be active. Changes made:
- handleStreamDrop in ChannelsPane.tsx only allows drops in edit mode
- handleStreamDragOver only shows visual feedback in edit mode
- handleRemoveStream only works in edit mode
- handleStreamDragEnd (reorder) only works in edit mode
- handleStreamDropOnChannel and handleBulkStreamDropOnChannel in App.tsx now require edit mode
- SortableStreamItem component now accepts isEditMode prop and disables drag handles and hides remove button when not in edit mode
- Added CSS for disabled stream-drag-handle state

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Update getStreamQualityPriority to handle any resolution ending in 'p' (e.g., 476p, 540p, 576p, 1440p). Parse the number and sort by value (larger = higher quality).

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Fixed bulk commit validation bug where client.get_channels() and client.get_streams() were being iterated directly as lists when they actually return paginated dicts. Changed the validation code to properly handle pagination by:
1. Extracting results from response.get('results', [])
2. Fetching all pages by checking response.get('next')
3. Using page_size=500 for efficient fetching

This fixes the 'string indices must be integers, not str' error that occurred when deleting channel groups with many channels.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Add debug-level logging with tags like [BULK-APPLY], [BULK-VALIDATE], [BULK-GROUP], [BULK-CHANNEL] to track each step of the bulk commit process for debugging.

title: MCP Tool Efficiency: Add bulk/missing API tools
description: The MCP server is missing several backend API endpoints that would dramatically reduce tool call counts for common operations. This epic adds 5 high-impact tools: streams-by-ids, channels bulk-commit, probe-bulk, delete-orphaned-groups, and FFmpeg domain coverage.

title: MCP: Add get_streams_by_ids tool
description: Add MCP tool wrapping POST /streams/by-ids to fetch stream details by ID list. Eliminates N+1 search pattern when looking up multiple streams.

title: MCP: Add bulk_commit_channels tool
description: Add MCP tool wrapping POST /channels/bulk-commit to batch create/update/delete channels in one call instead of individual operations.

title: MCP: Add probe_bulk_streams tool
description: Add MCP tool wrapping POST /stream-stats/probe/bulk to probe specific streams by ID list. Currently MCP only supports probe-all or probe-one.

title: MCP: Add delete_orphaned_groups tool
description: Add MCP tool wrapping DELETE /channel-groups/orphaned for one-call cleanup of channel groups with no channels.

title: MCP: Add FFmpeg domain tools
description: Add MCP tools covering the FFmpeg domain (18 backend endpoints): capabilities, configs, profiles, jobs, queue management. Currently the entire transcoding capability is invisible to MCP.

Files affected: ModalBase.css, StreamsPane.css, SettingsTab.css, NotificationCenter.css, AlertMethodSettings.css, StatsTab.css, EPGManagerTab.css, LogoManagerTab.css, M3UManagerTab.css, JournalTab.css, GuideTab.css, ChannelsPane.css, TaskEditorModal.css

BLOCKERS: None

title: Channel groups as tree with drag-and-drop channel assignment
description:
Channel groups displayed as a tree structure in the left pane:

Structure:

Drag-and-drop operations:

- Drag channel out of group (to root or another group) to reassign
- Drag channel between groups to move it
- API call to update channel's channel_group_id on drop

Group management (TBD - UI style pending):
- Create new channel groups
- Rename groups
- Delete groups (move channels to ungrouped or another group)
- Reorder groups

Visual:
- Expand/collapse icons for groups
- Channel count badge per group
- Indentation for hierarchy
- Drag handles or full-row draggable

Note: Detailed UI styling to be determined later

title: enhancedchannelmanager

title: enhancedchannelmanager

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed channel visibility filter in ChannelsPane.tsx that hid auto-created channels when their group was no longer in autoSyncRelatedGroups. When auto-channel-sync is turned off in Dispatcharr, channels still exist but were being hidden by a return false in the filter. Changed to return true so channels remain visible.

Cover GET (list), POST (create), PATCH (partial update — config-only is now used by SettingsTab post PR #163), DELETE. Note method_type uniqueness is not enforced. title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Settings notification improvements:

1. Restart notification removal: When a service restart is triggered (from toast or NotificationCenter), the 'Restart Required' notification is now automatically removed from both the toast and NotificationCenter.

2. Settings saved toast: Added 'Settings saved successfully' toast notification to all settings save handlers (AlertMethodSettings, TaskEditorModal, SettingsModal, M3U Digest settings).

4. Toast/notification coordination: Added custom event 'services-restarted' to dismiss the restart toast when restart is triggered from NotificationCenter.

5. Backend endpoint: Added DELETE /api/notifications/by-source endpoint to delete notifications by source and source_id.

Files changed:

- frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.tsx (success toast)
- frontend/src/components/ScheduledTasksSection.tsx (show_notifications check for toasts)

title: Docs: add Notifications & Alert Methods section to user_guide
description: Add a section to docs/user_guide/ covering Settings → Notification Settings: SMTP (Send Test Email), Email Alert Recipients UI (added in PR #163), Discord, Telegram, and how scheduled tasks dispatch external alerts. Pre-existing user-guide gap. Flagged by Tech Writer in PR #163 review (not blocking that PR).
close_reason: Merged via PR #202. User-guide Notifications & Alert Methods section shipped to dev as 0.16.0-0064.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Companion bead to enhancedchannelmanager-t8xw3.

STATUS (2026-04-19): Branch protection CONFIGURED on main via `gh api PUT /repos/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/branches/main/protection`. Verified state:
- required_status_checks.contexts = ["Backend Tests","Frontend Tests"] (strict=true)
- enforce_admins.enabled = false
- allow_force_pushes.enabled = false
- allow_deletions.enabled = false
- required_conversation_resolution.enabled = true
- required_linear_history.enabled = false
- required_signatures.enabled = false
- required_pull_request_reviews = null (disabled — solo maintainer)

Docs PR #67 opens against dev to document the config in docs/shipping.md.

Chicken-and-egg note: GitHub accepted the context names on the PUT even though Backend Tests / Frontend Tests had never actually run on main (they only ran on dev in PR #64). No workaround needed. The next PR merged into main will be the first real exercise of the required-checks gate.

Known surprise: docs-only PRs won't trigger the Tests workflow due to `paths-ignore: ['**.md']`, so if a pure-docs PR ever targets main, the required checks will stay in a pending state and the PR can't merge without admin override. PR #67 targets dev so it's unaffected, but note this for future.

KEEP OPEN until PR #67 merges.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Added mass renumber feature to Channels Pane. When in edit mode with channels selected, a new 'Renumber' button appears in the toolbar. Clicking it opens a modal to set a new starting number. The feature includes: collision detection showing which channels will be displaced, automatic shifting of conflicting channels, preview of changes, and respects the auto-rename setting for channel number prefixes in names.

title: Add auto-sync groups to selection when filter is re-enabled
description: When Show Auto Channel Groups filter is checked, auto-sync groups should be added to the dropdown selection.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed normalization engine processing order. Custom tags are now applied before quality suffixes (HD, FHD, 4K) are stripped, ensuring correct transformations when tags contain quality indicators.

title: WebSocket integration for real-time EPG progress updates
description:
## Feature Request
Add WebSocket integration with Dispatcharr to show real-time EPG refresh progress like the Dispatcharr UI does.

## Current State
ECM polls the REST API every 2 seconds during refresh, but can only see:
- status: 'fetching' or 'parsing'
- last_message: Only updated when operation completes

## Desired State
Show real-time progress messages like Dispatcharr's UI:
- 'Downloading' with speed/progress
- 'Extracting' for compressed files
- 'Parsing channels' with batch progress
- 'Parsing programs' with percentage

## Technical Details
- Dispatcharr WebSocket endpoint: ws://host:port/ws/
- Message type: 'update' with 'data' containing progress info
- Actions: 'downloading', 'extracting', 'parsing_channels', 'parsing_programs'
- Updates sent via channel_layer.group_send() to 'updates' group

## Implementation Notes
1. Create WebSocket client to connect to Dispatcharr
2. Subscribe to EPG progress updates
3. Update EPG Manager UI with real-time progress messages
4. Handle reconnection on disconnect

title: Stream priority reordering within channels
description:
Allow reordering streams within a channel to set priority:
- When viewing a channel's assigned streams, allow drag-and-drop reorder
- First stream = highest priority (used for playback)
- Visual drag handles for stream items
- API call to update channel's streams array order
- Optimistic UI with rollback on error
- Visual indication of priority order (numbers or position)

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed entrypoint.sh paths to match Dockerfile structure. Dockerfile copies backend/ to /app/, so main.py is at /app/main.py not /app/backend/main.py. Updated three locations: check_application() path check, cd command in check, and final cd before uvicorn. Also updated frontend build check from /app/frontend/dist to /app/static to match Dockerfile COPY location.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed Gracenote conflict detection in BulkLCNFetchModal categorization logic. Previously, any channel with an existing Gracenote ID was placed in 'alreadyHas' category without checking if EPG has a different ID. Now compares channel.tvc_guide_stationid with result.lcn - if different, adds to 'found' category which triggers the conflict modal. This allows users to see and resolve conflicts between existing and EPG Gracenote IDs.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed button colors in BulkLCNFetchModal and GracenoteConflictModal. Both modals were using hardcoded 'color: white' and 'background-color: var(--accent-primary)' instead of the CSS variables 'var(--button-primary-bg)' and 'var(--button-primary-text)'. This caused white-on-white text in certain theme configurations. Changed to match all other modals in the codebase.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Enhanced channel probing workflow with three major improvements: (1) Refresh M3Us before probe - triggers M3U refresh for all accounts and waits before probing to ensure latest stream data. (2) Channel group filtering - probes only streams in selected groups (already implemented, now documented). (3) Auto-reorder after probe - automatically reorders streams in channels using smart sort based on resolution/bitrate/framerate, with failed streams deprioritized. Tracks reordered channels in probe history. Added new settings refresh_m3us_before_probe (default true) and auto_reorder_after_probe (default false) to backend and frontend. Updated StreamProber to support these workflows.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Enhanced channel probing workflow with M3U refresh, auto-reorder, and change reporting. Added two new settings: refresh_m3us_before_probe (default: true) and auto_reorder_after_probe (default: false). Implemented smart sort for stream reordering with failed stream deprioritization. CRITICAL FIX: Added missing fields to SettingsRequest model that was preventing ALL settings from saving in v0.7.3-0099.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fix settings persistence by adding refresh_m3us_before_probe and auto_reorder_after_probe fields to DispatcharrSettings model in config.py. The fields were added to SettingsRequest in v0.7.3-0100 but missed in the actual settings model, causing Pydantic validation failures when saving.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Added automatic detection of M3U refresh status in M3U Manager tab. When probe triggers M3U refresh, the M3U Manager now detects accounts in 'fetching' or 'parsing' status and automatically starts polling to show refresh progress. Fixes UI disconnect where probe-initiated refreshes weren't visible in M3U Manager tab.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed EPG guide timeout with large channel counts (~2000+). Added time range filtering (1 hour ago to 24 hours ahead) to EPG requests, reducing data size. Backend now passes start/end query parameters to Dispatcharr. Improved error handling to catch both client and server timeouts with helpful messages.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Fixed auto-reorder streams feature not working after probe. The issue was that paginated channel list doesn't include full stream arrays, so the filter was excluding all channels. Now we fetch full channel details via get_channel() to get the actual stream IDs before reordering.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: PR #99 emits a single journal entry for bulk-update with entity_id=0, entity_name='bulk', first-5-rule-names description, and no batch_id. This doesn't match the established pattern in backend/routers/channels.py:914 (bulk channel renumber) which emits one entry per entity with a shared batch_id uuid. For forensics ('what did bulk-update do to rule 47 at 14:32?') we need per-rule entries with before/after diffs. Implement: (1) generate batch_id = uuid4().hex, (2) capture scalar field before-values during _apply_rule_scalar_updates (return the diff), (3) emit one journal entry per mutated rule with entity_id=rule.id, action_type='bulk_update', batch_id, and the diff. Flagged in original R1 review as non-blocking follow-up; tracking here.

comments.author: user
comments.text: Fixed EPG match sorting to prefer matches where the channel name is a prefix of the EPG name. Now 'ebonytv' will prefer 'ebonytvbylionsgate' (where channel is prefix) over 'e' (where EPG is prefix of channel). Added startsWith check before the length difference comparison.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
CPU usage investigation from log analysis.

## Issues Found:

### 1. Massive IN clause queries (HIGH PRIORITY)
- `get_stats_by_stream_ids` generates SQL with 1900+ parameters in IN clause
- This is extremely inefficient and causes DB overhead
- FIX: Batch queries into chunks of 500 IDs

### 2. Authentication race condition (MEDIUM PRIORITY)  
- Multiple concurrent API requests all trigger authentication separately
- Logs show 5-6 'Successfully authenticated' within 50ms
- FIX: Add asyncio.Lock to _ensure_authenticated

### 3. Slow upstream API responses (INFO)
- /api/providers: 2530ms
- /api/epg/data: 3796ms (12.7MB response)
- /api/channel-groups/with-streams: 2036ms
- These are mostly upstream Dispatcharr API latency

Files to modify:
- backend/stream_prober.py: get_stats_by_stream_ids() - add batching
- backend/dispatcharr_client.py: _ensure_authenticated() - add locking

title: Add SLO-3 calibration-risk note to slos.md (aspirational until Prometheus ships)
description:
Surfaced by SRE runbook spike (2026-04-24, PR #164). SLO-3 (5xx error rate < 1%) has a burn-rate alert defined in docs/sre/prometheus_rules.yaml, but ECM ships no Prometheus scrape target — alerts only fire for operators who have self-provisioned scraping. 'Alert did not fire before users reported' is the normal case today, not an anomaly.

Small doc edit:
- Add a 'Calibration Notice' section to docs/sre/slos.md at the top of SLO-3 (or wherever SLO catalog lives).
- Explicit statement: SLO-3 burn-rate alerting is aspirational until a Prometheus scrape target ships with ECM (see companion bead).
- Link to the companion Prometheus-shipping bead.

This is pure transparency — the numbers are credible targets, the ALERTING on those numbers is operator-provisioned.

Acceptance:
- Note added to slos.md.
- Links to the Prometheus-shipping bead.
- Resolves automatically (bead closes) when the Prometheus-shipping bead lands and operators have default scraping.
close_reason: Absorbed by enhancedchannelmanager-5uxwh per PO decision (2026-04-24) to go with option 2 (document operator-provisioned). The alerting-posture section in slos.md will cover all SLOs uniformly including the SLO-3 calibration concern — scoping as a targeted SLO-3 note was no longer the right shape.

title: Triage react-hooks/preserve-manual-memoization (10 sites) - eslint 7.1 - bd-me2lt followup
description: Spun out of bd-me2lt. 10 violations when react-hooks/preserve-manual-memoization re-enabled (React Compiler 'Existing memoization could not be preserved'). Concentrated in: ChannelsPane.tsx (8 sites at 3429, 3447, 3530, 3541, 3567, 3615 x2, 3618), StreamsPane.tsx:1137, StatsTab.tsx:647. Documented fix in docs/frontend_lint.md: optional-chain property deps (e.g., displayInfo?.externalUrl) confuse the compiler - replace with the whole object dep (displayInfo). Single-file work in 3 components. Small-Medium scope. Currently 'react-hooks/preserve-manual-memoization': 'off' in frontend/eslint.config.mjs.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: Closed 2026-04-25 as won't-fix-without-evidence-of-harm per PO value-gate review. Rule violations are a static-analysis concern (React Compiler optimization preference), not a correctness or user-impact issue. App ships, 1177 tests pass, users unaffected. Cost of fix (refactor + verification + regression risk) outweighs speculative benefit. Rule remains disabled in frontend/eslint.config.mjs with rationale. Reopen if (a) actual perf issue traced to this pattern, or (b) future React/Compiler bump forces the fix.

title: Stats tab broken with React error 185 (infinite re-render) after heatmap work
description:
ROOT CAUSE: recharts 3.x is incompatible with React 19. Recharts 3.x uses react-redux + @reduxjs/toolkit internally. Under React 19's stricter concurrent rendering, recharts' YAxis -> RenderedTicksReporter useEffect dispatches setRenderedTicks with a fresh ticks array reference every render, causing an infinite re-render loop.

TIMELINE:
- 2026-04-16 (62ea5c5f): recharts 3.7.0 -> 3.8.1 (introduced RenderedTicksReporter)
- 2026-04-24 (ca6e5019): React 18.3.1 -> 19.2.5 <- actual trigger; broke Stats tab
- 2026-04-25 (571b2e85): heatmap commit (purely additive, NOT the cause)

FIX: pin recharts to 2.15.4 (latest 2.x; supports React 19 in peerDeps; pre-dates the redux refactor).

VERIFICATION: 110 stats panel tests pass; tsc clean; container browser test confirms 0 console errors and both charts render.

PR: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/208

title: Structured logging + /metrics endpoint (observability baseline)
description:
## Why

No observability platform today. Deep health (`/api/health/ready`, bd-w0iyw) just landed — a signal nobody listens to. DBAS import (bd-gb5r5.3) is a long-running destructive operation whose 14-category partial-failure states are undebuggable without correlation IDs and structured logs. DBAS sync (bd-gb5r5.4) compounds this with two-instance failure modes. Rollback today (v0.16.0 / bd-vgm4l) left no metric trace. Flagged during grooming 2026-04-20 as blocker for v0.17.0 DBAS epics.

## Scope

**Structured logging:**
- JSON formatter on the root logger
- Per-request correlation ID via middleware (trace-id from header if present, else generated)
- Log fields: `ts`, `level`, `logger`, `msg`, `trace_id`, plus structured context (`restore_id`, `sync_id`, `phase`, etc.)
- Python `structlog` or stdlib `logging.Formatter` subclass — PE picks

**/metrics endpoint:**
- Prometheus-compatible text exposition via `prometheus_client`
- Minimum metric set:
  - `ecm_http_requests_total{method,path,status}`
  - `ecm_http_request_duration_seconds{method,path}`
  - `ecm_health_ready_ok`
  - `ecm_health_ready_check_duration_seconds{check}`
- Cardinality rules enforced: bounded labels only (no user_id, no trace_id, no unbounded paths)

**No platform required:** expose the endpoint only. Prometheus/Grafana wiring is a separate future bead. Curl-scrape-able is enough for v0.17.0 blocker-clear.

**Instrumentation standards:**
- Update `docs/backend_architecture.md` with logging + metrics patterns (metric naming: `<namespace>_<subsystem>_<name>_<unit>`, structured log schema, cardinality rules)
- Update the SRE persona SKILL.md reference if needed so new code adds metrics as a matter of course

## Acceptance

- `/metrics` returns Prometheus text for the minimum metric set
- Every request produces one JSON log line with a `trace_id`
- `docs/backend_architecture.md` updated with logging + metrics patterns
- Instrumentation standards documented (metric naming, cardinality rules, required log fields)
- Correlation ID propagates from HTTP header → log context → response header

## Out of Scope

- SLOs (separate bead — needs baseline data first)
- Alertmanager / alert rules (separate bead)
- Distributed tracing (Jaeger / Tempo / OTel exporters) — separate bead
- Prometheus / Grafana / Loki deployment — separate bead

This is the substrate those beads will stand on.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: PR #80 opened (https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/80). CI: Backend Tests pass 1m33s, Frontend Tests pass 2m13s. Substrate delivered: JSON logging with trace_id contextvar + X-Request-ID correlation, /metrics Prometheus endpoint exposing the four required series (http_requests_total, http_request_duration_seconds, health_ready_ok, health_ready_check_duration_seconds), /api/health/ready instrumented with gauge + per-check histograms. Cardinality discipline enforced: HTTP metrics labeled by route pattern, not raw path. Auth decision documented: /metrics unauthenticated (trusted LAN / reverse proxy assumption), IP allowlist / bearer token deferred to future bead. 23 new tests (12 unit + 11 middleware), full suite 2472 pass. Container smoke confirmed end-to-end. Not closing bead - await merge.

title: Add ecm_session_starts_total counter for SLO-6 denominator
description:
Implement the counter that closes out SLO-6's SLI formula. Today the SLO-6 entry in docs/sre/slos.md references this counter but the counter does not exist, so the SLI falls back to log-aggregation (slow, requires log aggregator we don't have) and the two alert rules in docs/sre/prometheus_rules.yaml fire on absolute error rate (noisier proxy — fires on traffic spikes even when error ratio is flat).

## Blocked by

~~Upstream spike bead decides session semantics (A page-load / B JWT / C cookie-lifetime). This bead is intentionally small-scope implementation once the spike decides.~~

**RESOLVED** by enhancedchannelmanager-1tl01. Spike merged 2026-04-24 (PR #192, merge SHA b4e033b87682aace36bb57a1143d1e76ccd304b1).

## Chosen semantic: C — cookie/sessionStorage lifetime

SRE, Security Engineer, and UX Designer personas converged independently. Frontend generates a SubtleCrypto-backed UUIDv4 `session_id`, persists it in `sessionStorage` (NOT localStorage, NOT a server-set cookie), emits `POST /api/session-start` once with that `session_id`. Backend deduplicates via short-TTL (24h) in-memory set before incrementing `ecm_session_starts_total`.

**Security constraints** (non-negotiable, from spike §3.2):

1. `session_id` MUST be a SubtleCrypto-generated v4 UUID, NOT a hash of UA / IP / user identifiers.
2. Stored in `sessionStorage` ONLY — never `localStorage`, never a persistent cookie.
3. Never logged by the backend, never returned in API responses, never indexed in the database. The dedup set is in-memory only.

**Fail-open contract** (UX, from spike §3.3): if `sessionStorage.setItem` throws (private mode, strict-privacy browser), skip silently — the user is excluded from the SLO denominator, NOT blocked from the app.

Full implementation steps in [docs/sre/spike-slo-6-session-semantics.md](../docs/sre/spike-slo-6-session-semantics.md) §6.

## Acceptance (refined per spike outcome)

1. New counter `ecm_session_starts_total` (no labels) registered in backend/observability.py alongside the existing ecm_* families. Also a gauge `ecm_session_dedup_set_size` so SRE can spot pruner leaks.
2. Frontend: new `installSessionTracker()` (or equivalent in `clientErrorReporter.ts` adjacent module) reads `sessionStorage.getItem('ecm_session_id')`, generates UUIDv4 + persists + POSTs if absent, no-ops if present. Wraps the entire block in try/catch — fail-open if sessionStorage unavailable. Honors `VITE_ECM_ERROR_TELEMETRY_ENABLED` and runtime `telemetry_client_errors_enabled` settings.
3. Backend: new `POST /api/session-start` endpoint with Pydantic schema `{ session_id: str }`. UUID v4 regex validation; reject non-matching with 422. In-memory dedup set with 24h TTL, lazy-prune at request time. Increment counter only on first sighting. Sit behind same auth posture as `/api/client-errors` (defer the pre-auth question to PO during implementation review).
4. SLO-6 SLI expression in docs/sre/slos.md updated to PromQL-native form:
   ```
   1 - (sum(rate(ecm_client_errors_total{kind="boundary"}[28d]))
        / sum(rate(ecm_session_starts_total[28d])))
   ```
5. Alert rules in docs/sre/prometheus_rules.yaml updated from absolute-rate alerts to ratio-based alerts so they match SLO-6 semantics (e.g., ECMClientErrorRatioElevated: fire when ratio > 0.5% sustained 10m).
6. Tests:
   - Unit: `sessionStorage.setItem` failure → no POST, no exception bubbles.
   - Unit: dedup set rejects re-submitted UUID within TTL; accepts after expiry.
   - Integration: full session lifecycle bumps `ecm_session_starts_total` exactly once across N navigations within the same tab.
7. Smoke in ecm-ecm-1: open one browser session, observe `/metrics` shows `ecm_session_starts_total` increased by exactly 1.

## Follow-ups

- docs/runbooks/frontend_error_rate.md (separate bead already filed) will need the ratio-alert language once this lands.
- The pre-auth coverage question (ADR-006 §1 vs UX rationale for C) may warrant an ADR-006 amendment — file as a separate bead during implementation review if the PO chooses to expand coverage.
close_reason: Implemented and merged: PR #196 (merge SHA 5c16c14d95a8862cd523a458e1dd2cc84a305567). Backend counter ecm_session_starts_total + gauge ecm_session_dedup_set_size registered in observability.py, new POST /api/session-start endpoint with UUIDv4 dedup (24h TTL). Frontend installSessionTracker() wired in main.tsx with sessionStorage + crypto.randomUUID + fail-open per spike Option C. SLO-6 SLI in slos.md promoted to PromQL-native; prometheus_rules.yaml ECMClientErrorRate* alerts swapped to ratio-based ECMClientErrorRatio*. 18 backend tests + 8 frontend tests added (3174 + 1185 total green). Smoke verified in ecm-ecm-1: counter goes 0→1 on first POST, dedup returns true on resubmit. Runbook update tracked under follow-up bead enhancedchannelmanager-06jyv per spike §6.7.

title: Reconcile docs/database_migrations.md with Alembic baseline (PR #81)
description:
Why:
Tech Writer flagged docs/database_migrations.md RED in 2026-04-20 standup: risk that the next migration runs from stale instructions. PR #81 (commit f996ec9b) landed Alembic on 2026-04-20 and the accompanying doc was authored as part of that same commit, so while there is no 'predecessor' to delete, the doc has specific inaccuracies that will mislead the next engineer running a migration.

What is stale/wrong (as of doc introduced in f996ec9b):
- Section '4. Test locally' step uses 'rm -f /tmp/mig_test.db && CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/mig_test alembic upgrade head'. env.py + database.get_database_url() resolve the DB URL from CONFIG_DIR as '$CONFIG_DIR/journal.db', so the actual DB lands at /tmp/mig_test/journal.db — the preceding 'rm' targets the wrong path and CONFIG_DIR is a directory, not a file. Command will silently produce stale state on re-run.
- 'Authoring a migration' does not call out that the container-side Alembic tree is /app/alembic/ (not /app/backend/alembic/); the backend deploys flat to /app per backend/CLAUDE.md. New engineers copying from backend/CLAUDE.md will otherwise cp to the wrong path.
- The doc does not explicitly note that the baseline has not been exercised with a real up/down cycle (DBE YELLOW in 2026-04-20 standup) — silent acceptance that revision 0001 is 'proven' is a trap.
- Minor: 'stamp head' path is described in runtime behaviour but not explicitly as a knob for manual recovery (operators coming in cold need to know the recovery story).

What the current Alembic-based flow actually is:
- Config: backend/alembic.ini (script_location = %(here)s/alembic, filename template YYYYMMDD_HHMM_<rev>_<slug>, DB URL falls through env.py to ALEMBIC_DATABASE_URL or database.get_database_url()).
- env.py: imports database + models + export_models + ffmpeg_builder.persistence so Base.metadata is fully populated; runs online by default, offline supported; compare_type + compare_server_default both True.
- Runtime bootstrap (database._bootstrap_alembic): fresh install → upgrade head; pre-Alembic install (tables exist, no alembic_version) → stamp head; at head → no-op; behind head → upgrade.
- Baseline revision: backend/alembic/versions/20260420_2034_0001_baseline_initial_schema.py (944 lines, 36 tables).
- Drift guard: backend/tests/unit/test_alembic_baseline.py (test_baseline_matches_metadata_no_drift).
- Operator visibility: GET /api/health/schema returns current_revision, head_revision, up_to_date, foreign_keys_enabled, journal_mode.

Acceptance criteria:
- docs/database_migrations.md reflects the actual Alembic flow with no command that silently fails or targets the wrong path.
- No contradictory instructions remain between docs/database_migrations.md, backend/alembic/env.py docstring, and backend/CLAUDE.md deploy rules.
- A DBE joining the project can onboard from this doc alone to: (a) create a new migration, (b) run up/down, (c) understand baseline semantics, (d) know the SQLite batch-mode rule, (e) know where to look when an upgrade fails at boot.
- Doc calls out that baseline (0001) has not yet been exercised with a real up/down cycle so engineers do not mistake 'passes drift test' for 'proven reversible'.

Out of scope:
- Actually running a baseline up/down smoke test. That is a separate follow-up bead.
- Retroactive migrations for historical ad-hoc _add_* helpers in database.py.

Labels: database, documentation, migrations

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: GH #58: Failed Stream Ordering wasn't persisting. Four backend gaps: (1) SettingsRequest Pydantic model lacked failed_stream_sort_order so FastAPI dropped it from POST body; (2) update_settings POST handler never copied the field into DispatcharrSettings; (3) SettingsResponse + GET handler omitted the field so refresh always returned the default; (4) StreamProber._smart_sort_streams had a positional-arg shift bug — passed channel_name (a string) into smart_sort_streams' failed_stream_sort_order slot, corrupting the rank map. Fixed all four in routers/settings.py + stream_prober.py + main.py. Also extended StreamProber.__init__ and update_sort_settings to accept and live-update the field. Switched _smart_sort_streams to keyword args to prevent future positional drift.

title: Improve regional variant detection for WestCoastFeed etc

comments.author: lecaptainc
comments.text:
Fixed regional variant detection to catch more patterns like WestCoastFeed.

Issue: 'MotorTrend' was matching 'MotorTrend(WestCoastFeed)(MTPHD).us' over 'MOTORTRENDHD(MTHD).us' because 'WestCoastFeed' wasn't being detected as a regional variant.

Fix: Expanded the regional pattern to match more variants:
- Old: /\((Pacific|East|West|Central|Mountain)\)/i
- New: /\((Pacific|East(?:ern)?(?:Feed)?|West(?:ern)?(?:Coast)?(?:Feed)?|Central|Mountain)\)/i

Now matches:
- Pacific
- East, Eastern, EastFeed, EasternFeed
- West, Western, WestCoast, WestCoastFeed, WesternFeed
- Central
- Mountain

Since 'MotorTrend' has no region hint, it defaults to preferring East, and the WestCoastFeed variant is now correctly deprioritized.

Version: 0.2.30064

ADR-006: Frontend error telemetry (Phase 1 local-sink architecture, OTel migration target)
description:
Prereq for enhancedchannelmanager-i6a1m (Phase 1 frontend error telemetry implementation).

Surfaced by spike on 2026-04-24 with SRE + security-engineer + it-architect reporting concurrently. PO accepted the Phase 1 shape; IT-architect's guidance: write ADR before implementation, keep i6a1m open until ADR lands.

The ADR must codify:

**Decision**: Ship local-sink frontend error telemetry at `POST /api/client-errors` for Phase 1. OTel-log-record-schema for payload. No external egress by default. Defer SaaS opt-in, SQLite admin view, and OTel collector export to Phase 2.

**Scope**:
- Endpoint: `POST /api/client-errors`, JWT-required (same auth as other /api/* routes).
- Rate limit: token bucket per session, 10 events/minute + 8 KB/request cap; 429 on overflow.
- Payload schema (OTel-log-record compatible):
  ```
  { kind: 'boundary'|'unhandled_rejection'|'chunk_load'|'resource',
    message: str (<=512, scrubbed),
    stack: str (<=4096, basename-stripped),
    release: str (Vite build env),
    route: str (pathname only, no query string),
    user_agent_hash: str (sha256),
    ts: iso8601 }
  ```
- Field allowlist (security-engineer): YES stack/message/pathname/viewport/UA-major-version; NO query strings/referrer/cookies/localStorage/user-typed input.
- Scrubber: extend backend/obfuscate.py regexes (IP/hostname/credential redaction) to the frontend payload path, applied both client-side before send and server-side before log write (belt-and-suspenders).
- Server-side landing: structlog line with `logger: 'ecm.client_error'` + Prometheus counter `ecm_client_errors_total{kind,release}` + histogram `ecm_client_error_reports_bytes` on existing /metrics.
- Label cardinality: `kind` fixed enum; `release` capped to current + prior 2 builds.
- Capture sites: React ErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch (exists) + window.onerror + window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection') + Vite vite:preloadError + dynamic-import .catch + pre-bundle inline script in index.html (~20 lines via navigator.sendBeacon for pre-mount crashes).
- Transport: navigator.sendBeacon with fetch fallback; reporter MUST swallow its own errors (never break UX).
- Opt-out: telemetry.client_errors_enabled setting, default ON. Data never leaves the container; privacy-neutral by default. Any future external sink is opt-IN with operator-owned DSN.
- SLO-6 (in docs/sre/slos.md): error-free session rate >= 99% on 7d window; gated at `sessions >= 50/day` for evaluation. Target marked 'to-be-calibrated after 30d of data' (insufficient-data handling consistent with other uncalibrated SLOs per 5uxwh posture).
- Alerting rule added to docs/sre/prometheus_rules.yaml. Operator-provisioned per 5uxwh.

**Phase 2 (out of scope for ADR-006; note as migration target)**:
- OTel migration path: OTLP-compatible payload means future collector swap is drop-in.
- Opt-in external SaaS sink (operator-owned DSN).
- SQLite-backed admin 'recent errors' view (only if operator demand surfaces).

**Risks codified in ADR**:
- Crash-loop error storms (mitigated by rate limit + circuit breaker).
- Label cardinality blow-up (mitigated by bounded vocab).
- PII/credential leakage (mitigated by scrubber + allowlist).
- Sink unreachable must not break UX (mitigated by try/catch + sendBeacon).
- Base-rate blindness on low-traffic LAN instances (mitigated by insufficient-data gate).

Acceptance:
- docs/adr/ADR-006-frontend-error-telemetry.md exists, follows existing ADR format (ADR-001, ADR-004, ADR-005).
- Status: accepted.
- Links: i6a1m, 5uxwh (alerting posture), SRE/security-engineer/it-architect spike findings.
- PR merged to dev.

Size: Small (doc-only, encodes a decision already made).
close_reason: Merged via PR #168 — docs/adr/ADR-006-frontend-error-telemetry.md landed, encoding PO-accepted Phase 1 architecture. Unblocks i6a1m implementation.

title: [BUG] Auto-create engine _smart_sort_streams has same within-bucket bug as sw883
description:
Follow-up to bd-sw883 / GitHub #73.

The sw883 fix corrects the within-bucket sort in backend/stream_prober.py — the comparator previously appended a tuple of zeros for the primary criteria on deprioritized streams, collapsing the within-bucket order to insertion order.

The same pattern is mirrored in backend/auto_creation_engine.py lines 2177, 2182, 2187 (_smart_sort_streams). It is out of scope for sw883 per PM intake (bugfix-sprint scope limited to the reporter's [STREAM-PROBE-SORT] code path), but deserves its own ticket so it is not forgotten. The fix is the analogue of the sw883 fix: compute the active-criteria values for deprioritized streams instead of emitting zeros.

Reproduction: same shape as sw883 test — within the same bucket assert that configured primary criteria order the streams.

See: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/issues/73 (parent issue for reference)
notes:
PR: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/88 (targets dev).
Root cause: composite sort key for deprioritized streams in auto_creation_engine._smart_sort_streams was (1, rank) + (0,)*len(active_criteria) — same pattern as bd-sw883 in stream_prober.py. All streams inside a bucket collided on the key, so Python's stable sort preserved insertion order instead of applying configured primary criteria.
Fix: mirrored the sw883 fix — extracted a local compute_criteria_values() helper and called it on all three deprioritized paths (failed, black_screen, low_fps) and the successful path so the within-bucket tiebreaker matches the successful-stream comparator. Minimum-scope per bugfix-sprint rule — no refactor beyond the fix, no touch to stream_prober.py.
Tests: 4 new regression tests in TestAutoCreateSmartSortWithinBucketPrimaryCriteria (3 RED pre-fix, GREEN post-fix; 1 cross-bucket invariant guard that was already green). Full backend suite 2483 passing (6 pre-existing alembic-env failures unchanged — unrelated; local venv missing alembic package).
Container smoke: docker cp auto_creation_engine.py to ecm-ecm-1, restart, ran condensed ferteque scenario (3 BS + 3 failed) in-container — result [102, 101, 103, 202, 203, 201]. 1920x1080 leads each bucket as expected (matches sw883 smoke output).
Version: 0.16.0-0040 -> 0.16.0-0041.
Status: left in_progress — PR CI in flight; per PO memory do not close while verification is running. Close after dev merge.

title: Investigate CodeQL scan configuration: custom vs. Default Setup (clarify single-source-of-truth)
description:
Architect investigation. The PR #82 dispatch context claimed the repo runs TWO CodeQL scans that disagree (custom .github/workflows/build.yml AND GitHub Default Setup), with Default Setup producing the 4 new path-injection errors while custom passes. Evidence on the PR itself contradicts this:

1. All 847 historical CodeQL alerts in the repo (including the 4 new path-injection alerts IDs 1416-1419) have analysis_key = '.github/workflows/build.yml:codeql-analysis' — meaning they come from the CUSTOM workflow.
2. PR #82's individual 'CodeQL Analysis (python)' and 'CodeQL Analysis (javascript-typescript)' jobs both PASS (they exit zero because they successfully UPLOAD SARIF).
3. The failing check named 'CodeQL' is owned by app 'GitHub Advanced Security' (app_slug: github-advanced-security) — it is the AGGREGATE PR check that compares SARIF delta vs. the base branch and fails when new HIGH alerts are introduced. It consumes output from the SAME custom workflow.

In other words: there is almost certainly ONE CodeQL scan (the custom one), and the perceived disagreement is between (a) the individual scan jobs 'pass' because SARIF upload succeeded and (b) the aggregate GHAS PR check 'fail' because new HIGH findings were introduced. That is correct behavior, not config drift.

Task: verify this hypothesis definitively. If confirmed, update the PR #82 dispatch framing. If there genuinely IS a Default Setup also enabled (visible at Settings > Code security > Code scanning > Default setup), recommend disabling it to avoid dual-scan drift and unify on the custom workflow + codeql-config.yml query filters (currently excludes py/log-injection).

Output: short architect note + config change recommendation if needed. Close the bead with evidence.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #156. Drift verdict: NO DRIFT — gh api code-scanning/default-setup returns state=not-configured; last 100 analyses all from .github/workflows/build.yml:codeql-analysis (single source); alerts from one tool entry (CodeQL 2.25.2). Source-of-truth split documented: build.yml (codeql-analysis job) owns WHEN; .github/codeql/codeql-config.yml owns WHAT. New docs/security/codeql-config.md (operational reference: SoT table, add/remove rule procedure, drift verification commands). ADR-005 cross-link added; CLAUDE.md Reference Guides updated.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Create a fast-running visual smoke test script for quick feedback during CSS development. Full E2E suite takes too long for iterative CSS work.

**SCRIPT: scripts/css-smoke-test.sh (or .bat for Windows)**

Purpose: Quick visual sanity check after CSS changes (~30 seconds vs full suite)

**KEY PAGES TO SCREENSHOT:**
1. Channels tab - main view with groups expanded
2. Streams pane - with selection active
3. EPG Manager tab - source list view
4. Settings tab - with sections expanded
5. A modal - e.g., M3U Account modal open
6. Dark mode variant of #1

**IMPLEMENTATION:**
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Quick CSS smoke test - run after CSS changes

echo "Running CSS visual smoke tests..."

# Run only the visual smoke test spec
npx playwright test e2e/css-smoke.spec.ts --reporter=list

# Show results
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "✓ Visual smoke tests passed"
else
  echo "✗ Visual differences detected!"
  echo "Run 'npx playwright show-report' to see differences"
  exit 1
fi
```

**TEST FILE: e2e/css-smoke.spec.ts**
```typescript
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';

test.describe('CSS Visual Smoke Tests', () => {
  test('channels tab', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('/');
    await page.waitForSelector('.channels-pane');
    await expect(page).toHaveScreenshot('channels-tab.png', { 
      maxDiffPixelRatio: 0.01 
    });
  });
  
  // ... similar for other key views
});
```

**USAGE DURING DEVELOPMENT:**
1. Make CSS change
2. Run: ./scripts/css-smoke-test.sh
3. If pass → continue or commit
4. If fail → review diff, fix or update baseline

**NPM SCRIPT:**
Add to package.json: "test:css-smoke": "playwright test e2e/css-smoke.spec.ts"

PREDECESSORS: enhancedchannelmanager-5h15i (Visual regression testing strategy)
BLOCKERS: None

title: Fix Stream Probe task error and add Run Now feedback
Introduce Alembic + schema baseline (blocks DBAS restore/sync)
description:
Why:
DBAS import/restore (bd-gb5r5.3) needs schema versioning. Without Alembic, a DBAS ZIP backup taken on schema v1 restored against schema v2 silently corrupts. DBA flagged as hard blocker during grooming 2026-04-20. Also precondition for bd-gb5r5.4 (sync reuses import path).

Scope:
- Add alembic to backend deps; initial 'alembic init'
- Baseline the current SQLite schema as revision 0001 (autogenerate + hand-review)
- Wire 'alembic upgrade head' into container startup per docs/backend_architecture.md
- Document per-connection PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON enforcement (SQLite default is OFF; quiet FK violations possible today)
- Document PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL verification
- Add schema-version field that restore engine can gate on
- Document migration authoring conventions in docs/backend_architecture.md or a new docs/database_migrations.md

TDD:
- A test that asserts the baseline revision matches the live schema (guard against drift)

Out of scope:
- Retroactive migrations for legacy schema changes; just baseline current state.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'alembic upgrade head' runs cleanly on a fresh container
- Schema version is readable via API or known-path query
- FK enforcement verified via test that attempts an invalid FK insert and sees rejection
- No existing test regressions
notes: PR https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/81 opened 2026-04-20 21:06 UTC, CI green (Backend 1m33s, Frontend 2m15s). Awaiting PO review; do not close.

title: Update ADR-001: remove per-week dep-bump cadence limit
description:
ADR-001 currently caps major dependency bumps to one-per-7-day-window merged to dev. The cadence rule was anchored in human review/observation time — operators needed days to spot latent regressions before the next bump landed.

PO call (2026-04-25): the rule is structurally wrong for AI-era work. The actual bottleneck is CI verification cycles (minutes), not human attention (days). Six parallel agents can verify and ship six bumps in less time than the cadence rule permits one.

## Required updates to ADR-001

1. Excise the "one major bump per 7-day window" cadence rule.
2. Preserve the rest of ADR-001 (whatever other rules it codifies).
3. If blast-radius isolation was a separate concern (and it was — bisectability of a regression), note that branch protection's serialized merges + CI delta-zero already provide that, in compressed time.
4. Add a Status / Revision history entry noting the change with date and rationale.

## Acceptance

- ADR-001 §Cadence (or equivalent) is removed or rewritten
- Other ADR-001 sections preserved
- Revision history entry added
- Any other doc references to "ADR-001 7-day cadence" updated (grep CLAUDE.md, docs/shipping.md, docs/runbooks/, bead bodies)

## Out of scope

- Whether to add a NEW cadence rule of any kind (the PO said remove entirely; if you find yourself wanting to add a replacement, file a separate bead and don't slip it in)
- Other ADRs

title: Debug bundle generation fails with generic 'Failed to generate debug bundle'
description: Debug bundle generation timed out for large catalogs (~15K channels). Root cause: GET /api/auto-creation/debug-bundle ran sequentially inside the request — 150 page fetches + 150 stream-detail batches blew the 30s ECM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS budget. Fix: replaced GET with POST/{job_id} 202+poll pattern (matches bd-enfsy), parallelized page + stream-batch fetches via asyncio.gather (concurrency cap 8), in-memory job dict with 30-min TTL pruning. Frontend uses new startDebugBundle/pollDebugBundle helpers; both Settings and Auto-Creation tabs updated. 7 new backend tests + 6 new frontend tests; container smoke test confirmed enqueue in 1.8ms, 571-channel bundle delivered in 531ms.

title: Write ASGI rollback runbook (docs/runbooks/dep-bump-backend-asgi-regression.md) — PR #170 merge gate
description:
Merge gate for PR #170 (ASGI triplet: starlette 1.0 + fastapi 0.136.1 + uvicorn 0.46).

SRE identified gap during standup 2026-04-24: existing docs/runbooks/dep-bump-smoke-test.md is generic; does NOT cover ASGI-triplet regression modes. PR #170 is a major starlette bump touching the middleware chain that emits all four ecm_http_* / ecm_health_ready_* SLI families. Silent SLI degradation is the real risk — container boots, serves 200s, and emits 3 of 4 metric families while SLOs burn undetected.

Runbook must document:

1. Pre-merge walkthrough (dry-run the rollback):
   - git revert <#170 merge SHA> → docker build → docker restart ecm-ecm-1
   - Confirm previous image tag is pinned before merge
   - Verify /metrics emits all four ecm_* families under synthetic traffic BEFORE merge baseline capture

2. Post-deploy verification (non-negotiable):
   curl /metrics | grep -E '^ecm_(http_requests_total|http_request_duration_seconds|health_ready_ok|health_ready_check_duration_seconds)'
   Must return non-zero samples for ALL four families, not just container-up.

3. Regression modes specific to starlette 1.0:
   - Middleware ordering / BaseHTTPMiddleware semantics changes
   - ASGI scope mutation changes
   - Exception propagation / handler contract changes
   - uvicorn 0.46 event-loop behavior

4. Rollback triggers:
   - Any ecm_* family stops emitting post-deploy
   - p95 latency delta > 10% from pre-merge baseline
   - 5xx rate delta > baseline + 0.1%
   - vjlzf/pvq6s test count regresses post-merge (should be stable or improving)

Acceptance: runbook exists, rollback dry-run logged in PR #170, reverse link added from dep-bump-smoke-test.md.

LABELS: observability, runbook, sre, roadmap:v0.16.0
notes: 2026-04-24 SRE: Pre-merge dry-run gate landed on branch docs/runbook-asgi-regression-d0pbr (commit bba33094), pushed to origin. Augmented the existing post-merge rollback runbook at docs/runbooks/dep-bump-backend-asgi-regression.md with: (1) Pre-merge dry-run gate (PREV_DEV_SHA capture, ecm_* four-family baseline under synthetic traffic, local git-revert dry-run via smoke_test_dev_container.sh, compose/registry pinning, 5-box pre-merge checklist); (2) numeric Rollback Triggers table (ecm_* family drop / p95 +10% / 5xx +0.1pp / vjlzf+pvq6s known-red growth / readiness flap / restart loop); (3) Starlette-1.0 specific regression-modes isolation table (BaseHTTPMiddleware exception propagation, 5-middleware-stack ordering, ASGI scope mutation, request.state, uvicorn 0.46 wait_for cancellation into 504 path, lifespan protocol, StreamingResponse bytes-only); (4) Verify block extended to diff post-rollback /metrics against pre-merge baseline (all four ecm_* family roots must match); (5) Notify step pointing at discord_release_notes.md. Reverse link added from dep-bump-smoke-test.md. Leaving bead OPEN pending PR #170 merge per task instructions.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #171 (https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/171), merged to dev as 1eb8cfff. Content augmented onto the existing docs/runbooks/dep-bump-backend-asgi-regression.md runbook (originally authored under bd-eqmop / PR #164) rather than replacing it — added pre-merge dry-run gate, rollback trigger thresholds, 7-row starlette 1.0 regression-modes table, /metrics diff step, notify step, and reverse-link from dep-bump-smoke-test.md. Closes gate 2 of PR #170.

title: Integration test for auto-creation bulk-update flow (real DB, no mocked journal) + Hypothesis ReDoS fuzz
description:
QA's standup finding (2026-04-23): every bulk-update test in backend/tests/routers/test_auto_creation.py:171-548 mocks the journal via patch("routers.auto_creation.journal") at lines 180/218/253/278/460/499/537. The batch_id assertions at lines 445-484 verify CALL KWARGS, not real DB rows. Three bulk-related PRs landed in 24h (bd-bh1hh single-fetch + bd-gjoe5 reject conditions/actions + bd-91mcq per-entity audit) — they share the _apply_rule_scalar_updates code path; their interactions are untested.

Scope (~80 lines, one test file):
1. Add ONE integration test in backend/tests/integration/test_api_auto_creation.py using existing async_client + test_session fixtures (no new infra). POSTs a mixed scalar-only payload to ~50 real rules with the REAL journal, then asserts:
   - 50 journal rows exist after the bulk update
   - All share one batch_id
   - Each row's diff matches the actual before/after
   - A payload containing rejected 'conditions' returns 400 AND writes zero journal rows (rollback works)
2. Add a Hypothesis-based ReDoS fuzz test against the bulk-update sort_regex lint path (bd-k41e0). Modeled on existing backend/tests/unit/test_auto_creation_safe_regex_migration.py:40-54 adversarial pattern. Catches the security regression class for bd-ltjyx/bd-3u6p0 going forward.

Dependencies / sequencing:
- The Hypothesis fuzz test requires the 'hypothesis' package (tracked in bd-mzm3j). Engineer can either: (a) wait for bd-mzm3j to land and add only the integration test in this PR, or (b) install hypothesis as part of this PR's diff and obviate bd-mzm3j. Engineer's call based on what's smaller.

Cross-persona alignment:
- DBA's per-entity journal volume concern: integration test verifies real write-and-readback under load
- Security's ReDoS concern: fuzz test extends bd-ltjyx/bd-3u6p0 protection class
- Engineer's 'no E2E for bulk flow' gap: closed

User value: bulk-update interactions cannot silently break on the next merge; ReDoS regression class detected at write-time, not exploit-time.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #140. backend/tests/integration/test_api_auto_creation.py +314 lines, no production code changed. Three tests: (1) 50-rule bulk-update against real DB asserts 50 journal rows + shared batch_id + correct diffs; (2) rejected-conditions payload returns 4xx and writes zero journal rows (rollback); (3) Hypothesis ReDoS fuzz over nested-quantifier shapes with derandomize=True for CI stability and 1.5s wall-clock budget. Engineer chose option (b): hypothesis was already in backend/requirements.txt (added by bd-eio04.5), so bd-mzm3j was effectively already resolved. 3079 backend tests pass overall (was 3076).

title: ADR-003: WebSocket introduction & long-running job pattern
description:
bd-gb5r5.4 (two-instance sync) requires WebSocket-based progress. If WS is not already established in ECM, this epic introduces the pattern — and the pattern will calcify fast (CR warning) so schema discipline is required up front.

Decisions required:
(a) Authn/authz for WS connections — session cookie, bearer token, per-message auth?
(b) Reconnect semantics — resume on last-seen sequence, replay buffer size, idempotency.
(c) Backpressure — slow client handling, server-side drop policy, queue bounds.
(d) Progress envelope — versioned schema, e.g. {type, phase, progress, data, meta}. Naming and versioning discipline locked before first consumer ships.
(e) Scope — WS progress as a cross-cutting pattern (reusable by import, black-screen scan, probe, restore) vs. sync-specific. Recommendation: cross-cutting.

Soft-couples to ADR-002 — bd-gb5r5.3's restore progress UI likely wants the same envelope.

Stub bead. Architect + engineer fill later. Blocks bd-gb5r5.4 (hard) and bd-gb5r5.3 (soft, via 'related').
close_reason: Retired 2026-04-21 by PO directive. The original gb5r5 DBAS migration plan was filed 2026-02-18 and the team-plan archaeology (agent a4ca4a38) found it significantly overlapping with already-shipped ECM work (backup.py YAML restore, task_scheduler.py, dispatcharr_client.py, notifications infra). 26 of the 37 tree beads had not been updated in 67 days and lacked acceptance criteria. Rather than salvage, PO chose to kill the existing scope entirely so fresh DBAS planning can start from user need (not from the standalone DBAS product's original shape). Fresh plan to be authored in a future /team-plan or /onboard session. Do NOT reopen these beads — if DBAS work resumes, file new beads with current framing.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Bandwidth tracker treated Dispatcharr total_bytes as already including fan-out to all clients. In reality total_bytes is the source stream bytes only. Outbound should be channel_bytes_delta * client_count, inbound should be channel_bytes_delta. The bitrate calculation was already correct (multiplied by client_count) but bytes were not. Production showed near 1:1 in/out ratio despite 6 viewers.

title: Define SLOs + alert rules on /metrics baseline
description:
## Why

PR #80 (bd-ak1db, merged 2026-04-20) shipped the observability baseline — structured JSON logging + Prometheus `/metrics` exposition with four series:

- `ecm_http_requests_total{method,path,status}` (Counter)
- `ecm_http_request_duration_seconds{method,path}` (Histogram)
- `ecm_health_ready_ok` (Gauge, 0/1)
- `ecm_health_ready_check_duration_seconds{check}` (Histogram)

ak1db explicitly deferred SLOs ("needs baseline data first") and Alertmanager/alert rules ("separate bead"). PO uplifted SRE YELLOW into sprint scope today: **signal without thresholds** — on-call has metrics but no way to alert, no commitment on what "good" looks like, and no runbook path from alert to mitigation. This bead resolves that YELLOW by turning signal into actionable policy.

## Scope

Define initial SLOs for the 4 baseline metrics and ship Prometheus alert rules that match:

1. **Availability SLO** — derived from `ecm_health_ready_ok`. Target conservative (99% 30-day to start; calibrate once we have real traffic data).
2. **Latency SLO** — derived from `ecm_http_request_duration_seconds` p95. Target p95 < 500ms against the histogram buckets ak1db shipped (5ms–10s, tuned for local/LAN).
3. **Error-rate SLO** — derived from `ecm_http_requests_total` grouped by status. Target < 1% 5xx rate over rolling 30d (excluding `/metrics` which is self-skipped by middleware).
4. **Readiness sub-check latency** (stretch) — `ecm_health_ready_check_duration_seconds` per `check` label; informational dashboard signal, alert threshold optional for v1.

Corresponding Prometheus alert rules written as `rules-as-code` YAML. Burn-rate alerts (multi-window fast+slow) where feasible; single-window acceptable for scaffold.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] SLO document exists (`docs/sre/slos.md`) covering: SLI definition, SLO target, error budget, error-budget policy (what triggers when budget burns).
- [ ] Prometheus alert rules committed as YAML (`docs/sre/prometheus_rules.yaml` or equivalent) with severity labels and runbook annotation links.
- [ ] Runbook stubs linked from alert rules — expected path `docs/runbooks/<alert-name>.md` (coordinate with sibling bead bwly4 which scaffolds `docs/runbooks/`; reference the path even if the directory lands later).
- [ ] `docs/backend_architecture.md` §Observability → Out of scope updated to cross-link the new SLO doc (no longer out of scope).
- [ ] Commit on dev, PR opened, CI green.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- Deploying Prometheus/Alertmanager (rules-as-code only; deployment is a future bead).
- Grafana dashboards (future bead).
- Distributed tracing (ak1db deferred; separate bead).
- SLO calibration against real traffic (targets are initial — must be re-tuned once 30 days of metrics exist).

## Labels

sre, observability, slo

## Cross-references

- Parent baseline: bd-ak1db (closed, PR #80) — https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/80
- Sibling: bd-bwly4 (in-flight) — scaffolds `docs/runbooks/` directory + template

description: Create ProbeSchedule SQLAlchemy model extending TaskSchedule concept for probes. Fields: id, name, enabled, schedule_type, schedule_time, timezone, days_of_week, interval_seconds, channel_groups (JSON array of group names), batch_size, timeout, max_concurrent, next_run_at, last_run_at, created_at. Each schedule can target specific groups.

title: ADR-006: Two-instance sync credential & trust model
description:
bd-gb5r5.4 requires ECM to hold outbound credentials to a second Dispatcharr instance. This is a new trust boundary and credential surface; requires Security sign-off on threat model.

Decisions required:
(a) Credential storage — encrypted at rest (key source: env secret, KMS, sealed secret?). Never plaintext in env vars or DB columns. Masked in all UI surfaces and logs.
(b) TLS enforcement — verify=True default for all outbound calls to the peer instance. No verify=False unless per-instance opt-in flag is set AND the opt-in is audit-logged each use.
(c) Trust model — is the destination assumed to be a trusted peer within the same org, or must we design for arbitrary third-party Dispatcharr endpoints? Affects rate limiting, data minimization, and error-handling assumptions.
(d) Payload scoping — user passwords and API tokens inside the sync payload excluded by default. Explicit allow-list for what syncs, deny-list never overridable.
(e) Threat model — spoofed destination, MITM, credential theft at rest, replay. Paired review with Security.

Stub bead. Architect + Security fill content. Blocks bd-gb5r5.4.
close_reason: Retired 2026-04-21 by PO directive. The original gb5r5 DBAS migration plan was filed 2026-02-18 and the team-plan archaeology (agent a4ca4a38) found it significantly overlapping with already-shipped ECM work (backup.py YAML restore, task_scheduler.py, dispatcharr_client.py, notifications infra). 26 of the 37 tree beads had not been updated in 67 days and lacked acceptance criteria. Rather than salvage, PO chose to kill the existing scope entirely so fresh DBAS planning can start from user need (not from the standalone DBAS product's original shape). Fresh plan to be authored in a future /team-plan or /onboard session. Do NOT reopen these beads — if DBAS work resumes, file new beads with current framing.

title: Journal table retention + missing batch_id/entity indexes (post bd-91mcq)
description:
Per DBA standup finding 2026-04-23: bd-91mcq added per-entity journal rows with shared batch_id, but journal_entries has zero index on batch_id and no composite (entity_type, entity_id, timestamp DESC). Also no retention policy in backend/tasks/cleanup.py for this table; bulk operations now amplify row growth N-fold with no upper bound.

Confirmed gaps:
- backend/models.py:35-37 — JournalEntry has indexes only on timestamp DESC, category, action_type
- backend/alembic/versions/20260420_2034_0001_baseline_initial_schema.py:248,252-254 — baseline DDL confirms missing indexes
- backend/tasks/cleanup.py — no journal retention defined

Scope:
1. Alembic migration adding idx_journal_batch_id and idx_journal_entity (entity_type, entity_id, timestamp DESC)
2. Define retention policy (proposal: 90d hot retention, then archive-or-drop) wired into backend/tasks/cleanup.py
3. Backfill plan if production journal_entries is already large (concurrent CREATE INDEX, batched DELETE)

User value: forensic audit queries ("show me batch X" / "history for rule N") stay sub-second as journal volume grows; bulk operations don't silently bloat the table without bound.

Success signal: explain analyze on representative batch-id and entity-history queries shows index usage; cleanup.py retention test proves rows older than threshold are pruned; migration runs cleanly on 5M-row test fixture.

Priority: P1 — confirmed correctness/perf bug shipped in last 24h.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #133 (v0.16.0-0053). Alembic 0004 added idx_journal_batch_id and idx_journal_entity (category, entity_id, timestamp DESC). CleanupTask.journal_days raised 30 -> 90 to align with journal.purge_old_entries default. Model + migration agree (drift test). CHANGELOG entry under Fixed.
