title: Add bulk commit endpoint for batch operations
description:
## Summary
Added bulk commit endpoint for batch operations to fix Apply Changes button becoming unclickable with 1000+ changes.

## Changes Made

### Backend (main.py)
- Added Pydantic models for bulk operations: BulkUpdateChannelOp, BulkAddStreamOp, BulkRemoveStreamOp, BulkReorderStreamsOp, BulkAssignNumbersOp, BulkCreateChannelOp, BulkDeleteChannelOp, BulkCreateGroupOp, BulkDeleteGroupOp
- Added BulkCommitRequest and BulkCommitResponse models
- Created POST /api/channels/bulk-commit endpoint that:
  - Accepts all operations in a single HTTP request
  - Creates groups first (Phase 1)
  - Processes operations sequentially (Phase 2)
  - Tracks temp ID -> real ID mappings for newly created channels
  - Handles logo URL resolution for new channels
  - Returns success status, operation counts, and ID mappings

### Frontend (api.ts)
- Added BulkOperation, BulkCommitRequest, BulkCommitResponse interfaces
- Added bulkCommit() function to call the bulk endpoint

### Frontend (useEditMode.ts)
- Refactored commit() to use bulk commit instead of sequential API calls
- Converts consolidated operations to bulk format
- Makes single API call instead of 1000+ individual calls
- Simplified progress tracking (2 steps: bulk commit + fetch)
- Removed unused logoCache variable and Logo import

## Impact
- Eliminates 1000+ network round-trips for large change sets
- Single HTTP request for all operations
- Progress bar now shows 2 steps instead of N operations
- Major performance improvement for bulk operations

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Public OSS project on GHCR with external contributions visible in git log (e.g., PR #60 from krog/fix/ffmpeg-metadata-parsing). No CONTRIBUTING.md exists.

Scope:

- Cover: branching strategy (dev → main), PR process (squash-merge to dev, release tags cut from main), required local checks before pushing (lint, typecheck, tests), code of conduct pointer or brief statement, where to file issues (GitHub issues + beads for ECM maintainers), how to run locally (docker compose up), where to find architecture docs.
- Distinguish HUMAN contributor guidance from AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md (which target AI agents). CONTRIBUTING.md is for people.
- Reference docs/ for deeper reading: architecture.md (or project_architecture.md — Technical Writer previously noted these overlap; pick one as canonical), backend_architecture.md, testing.md, shipping.md, css_guidelines.md.
- Keep it short. ~100 lines is plenty. Don't replicate README content; link to it.

Out of scope:
- Adding a CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (separate concern; file if PO wants it).
- CLA/DCO configuration (project doesn't use one currently).

Flagged by Technical Writer during /onboard 2026-04-19.

Did NOT resolve the overlap between docs/architecture.md, docs/backend_architecture.md, and docs/project_architecture.md in this session — scoped out. Recommend filing a follow-up bead to consolidate: project_architecture.md as canonical, fold unique content from the other two into it, then delete or redirect. backend_architecture.md is only 13 lines so may already be obsolete.

title: security: add RequireAdminIfEnabled to all normalization router endpoints
description:
Code Reviewer flagged during PR #107 review (GH-104 fix, bd-u9odj): backend/routers/normalization.py has **zero** endpoints with RequireAdminIfEnabled, despite several being destructive:

- POST /api/normalization/groups — creates new rule groups
- DELETE /api/normalization/rules/{id} — deletes rules
- POST /api/normalization/migration/run — runs migrations
- POST /api/normalization/import — imports rule config (arbitrary data ingest)
- POST /api/normalization/apply-to-channels — **rename + delete channels** (NEW in PR #107)

Pre-existing systemic gap; not PR-introduced. CR chose not to block PR #107 on this since adding auth to the whole router is scope creep on a bug-fix PR.

## Scope
Add RequireAdminIfEnabled to every mutating endpoint in backend/routers/normalization.py. Audit read endpoints (GET /groups, GET /rules) — likely OK without, but confirm.

## Acceptance
- All POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE endpoints in normalization.py carry RequireAdminIfEnabled
- Tests added for 401/403 on non-admin caller for each endpoint
- No regression on admin flow

## Priority rationale
P2 (not P1) because:
- Auth is enforced at the router middleware level typically anyway
- Destructive endpoints are unlikely to be discovered + exploited by unauthenticated users
- Pre-existing, not urgent
- Should land soon after PR #107 but doesn't block it

## Source of finding
Code Reviewer subagent first-pass review of PR #107, 2026-04-22.
close_reason: Folded into bd-eio04.12 as single PR per PO decision 2026-04-22. Admin gate on /api/normalization/apply-to-channels ships with re-normalize UI in one change.

title: Normalization parity: Test Rules and Auto Create share one path
description:
Collapse the Test Rules preview path and the Auto Create execution path onto a single shared NormalizationPolicy so they cannot diverge. Replaces iterative symptom fixes on GH #104 (bd-u9odj, bd-yui1k, bd-yj5yi) after third user report of the same bug class.

Scope:
- Unify the two paths onto one policy object (drop the preserve_superscripts boolean divergence).
- Drop the superscript-numeric-preservation carve-out entirely — all superscripts convert to ASCII on both paths. PO decision 2026-04-22.
- Add parity test and shared unicode fixture bank so this bug class is regression-locked.
- Ship user-requested 'Re-normalize existing channels' UI (both reporters asked explicitly).
- Fold in Security finding SEC-104-2 (NFKC Unicode normalization) — SEC-104-1 (ReDoS) expanded into 4 child beads after spike.
- Add normalization observability and documentation.

Source: team-review on GH #104. User acknowledgment posted at https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/issues/104#issuecomment-4296922069. Ships as dev update, not full release cut.

title: Spike: redos-detector library vs hand-rolled AST check for pattern linting
description:
Blocks bd-eio04.7 from starting. Benchmark both approaches against the existing production rule corpus (normalization_rules, auto_creation_rules, dummy_epg_rules tables) to measure false-positive rate on real user patterns.

Question: does redos-detector (third-party pkg) or a ~50-line hand-rolled AST walker over compiled regex produce fewer false positives on the real rule corpus?

Deliverables:
- Prototype both detectors.
- Seed a dev DB with the exported rule corpus (or anonymized subset).
- Run both detectors against every pattern; record (false-positive-rate, catch-rate-on-known-ReDoS).
- Pick winner + document in bd-eio04.7 scope.

Success: one chosen detector with measured false-positive rate < 5% on the corpus.

Size: 0.5d.
close_reason: Spike complete. Winner: hand-rolled AST walker. Report + reference code in comments.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
# bd-eio04.11 — ReDoS Detector Spike Report

**Decision:** Use the hand-rolled AST walker. Do not adopt `redos-detector` (npm-only)
or `regexploit` (PyPI, stale).

## 1. Corpus

**Sources:** `/config/journal.db` in `ecm-ecm-1` (8 normalization rules, 3 auto-creation
rules, 1 dummy-EPG profile) + regex-literal patterns harvested from `backend/tests/**`.

**Size:** 32 unique patterns (user-input syntax, i.e. pre `(?<name>...)` → `(?P<name>...)`
conversion). The real production rule DB is sparse — the meaningful regex surface area
today is almost entirely the dummy-EPG `title_pattern` / `time_pattern` / `date_pattern`
fields.

**Example real-production patterns:**
- `(?<channel>.+?):\s+(?<event>(?:(?<sport>.+?)\s+\|\s+(?<team1>.+?)\s+vs\s+(?<team2>.+?))|(?:.+?))\s+@\s+`
- `(?<hour>\d{1,2})(?:\s*:\s*(?<minute>\d{2}))?\s*(?<ampm>[AaPp][Mm])(?:\s+(?<timezone>[A-Z]{1,5}))?`
- `(?<month>Jan|Feb|...|\d{1,2})[/\s]\s*(?<day>\d{1,2})(?:[/,\s]+(?<year>\d{2,4}))?`

Full list: `/tmp/bd-eio04.11-rule-corpus.txt`

## 2. Library landscape

| Candidate | PyPI? | Last release | Notes |
|-|-|-|-|
| `redos-detector` | **No — npm only** | 2026-02-14 (npm) | Not usable from Python without shelling out to Node. Brief assumed a Python pkg; there isn't one with that name. |
| `regexploit` (doyensec) | Yes | **2021-03-11** (5 years stale) | Only maintained Python ReDoS AST-analyzer on PyPI. Models `re.sre` but with limitations noted below. |
| Hand-rolled on `re._parser` | n/a | Stdlib (always current) | ~60 LoC, zero deps, already matches how our engine actually parses. |

Given `redos-detector` is npm-only, the binary choice in the brief is effectively
`regexploit` vs hand-rolled.

## 3. Results

### Run setup
- Both detectors receive the user-syntax pattern and apply our own
  `_js_to_python_named_groups` conversion (same helper used at runtime in
  `backend/dummy_epg_engine.py`).
- `regexploit` was run at its **most-sensitive** setting (any finding = flag; default
  CLI uses `starriness > 2`). This gives it every chance.
- Hand-rolled walker only flags **nested unbounded quantifier with a trailing
  killer** — this precisely matches the shape Python's `re` engine is actually
  vulnerable to. Bare `(a*)*` and similar are not flagged because Python's engine
  optimizes them away (verified empirically below).

### Numbers

| Set | n | Hand-rolled flagged | `regexploit` flagged | Notes |
|-|-|-|-|-|
| **Real corpus (benign — must not flag)** | 32 | **0 (0 FP)** | **1 (1 FP)** | `regexploit` flagged the B1G production `title_pattern`. |
| **Known-evil with killer (must flag)** | 7 | **7/7** | 7/7 | Both detectors equal. |
| **Ambiguous no-killer (Python optimizes — should not flag)** | 3 | 0 | 0 | Both detectors equal. |
| **Total time for 42 patterns** | | 0.97 ms | 4.05 ms | Hand-rolled ~4× faster. |

### The one `regexploit` false positive

```
(?<channel>.+?):\s+(?<event>(?:(?<sport>.+?)\s+\|\s+(?<team1>.+?)\s+vs\s+(?<team2>.+?))|(?:.+?))\s+@\s+
```
(Dummy-EPG profile #1, `B1G Advanced EPG` — currently in production.)

`regexploit` reports `starriness=3`. Empirical verification on stdlib `re`:

| Input | Length | Match time |
|-|-|-|
| `ESPN: Football \| Lions vs Tigers @ 10pm` | 39 ch | 0.1 ms |
| 200× `a` (no `@` terminator) | 200 ch | 0.1 ms |
| Adversarial `x`s with spurious `: vs vs vs …` | 393 ch | 2.6 ms |

Not exploitable. `regexploit` misses that the inner `.+?` repeats are **lazy**; it
over-counts their contribution to `starriness`. This is the specific false positive
shape the bead brief called out as a "one-way door" — rejecting a pattern that has
worked in production.

### Known-evil patterns both detectors flag (TPs at parity)

All of these are **confirmed exploitable** in stdlib `re` — verified with 5 s timeouts:

| Pattern | Observed on 22–30 chars |
|-|-|
| `(a+)+b` | 195 ms |
| `(a*)*b` | HANG (> 5 s) |
| `(.*)*X` | HANG |
| `(a+)*b` | 1553 ms |
| `(.+)+X` | 3099 ms |
| `^(\w+\s?)*$` | HANG at 25 ch |
| `(\w+)+$` | HANG |

Both detectors catch all seven. Hand-rolled reports `nested-unbounded-repeat-with-killer`;
`regexploit` reports `starriness=11` on each.

### Patterns intentionally NOT flagged (shared correct behavior)

Verified with Python's `re` engine — these do **not** ReDoS in production because
Python's engine either optimizes or factors them:

| Pattern | Python behavior |
|-|-|
| `(a*)*` | No backtracking; optimized to O(n). |
| `(.*)*` | Same. |
| `(a\|a)+$` | Python `re._parser` factors `(a\|a)` to `a(\|)` — not ReDoS. |

Both detectors correctly pass these. Any detector that flagged them would produce
spurious FPs.

## 4. Recommendation

### Winner: Hand-rolled AST walker

**Why:**
1. **Zero false positives on the real corpus.** `regexploit` produced one — on a
   production-in-use pattern. For a save-time linter, that's the ticket-escalation
   scenario the bead called out as the critical failure mode.
2. **Identical true-positive coverage.** 7/7 on known-evil-with-killer controls,
   matched by regexploit.
3. **No external dependency.** `regexploit` hasn't had a release since 2021; adding
   a 5-year-stale dependency for ~60 LoC of logic is not a trade we'd justify to the
   code reviewer.
4. **~4× faster per pattern** (marginal at this scale, but relevant if the linter
   runs on every pattern write).
5. **Model matches our engine.** The hand-rolled walker uses `re._parser.parse` —
   exactly what `re.compile` calls. Any behavior it models IS how our engine behaves.
   `regexploit` ships its own parser that can drift.
6. **Maintenance locus.** We own it. If the model ever over- or under-flags, we edit
   the file; we don't file an issue on a dormant GitHub project.

**Caveats:**
- **Uses `re._parser`, which is a private stdlib module.** CPython has been stable on
  this API for years but it is technically private. Python 3.11 renamed the module
  from `sre_parse` to `re._parser`; the import should `try: import re._parser` then
  `except ImportError: import sre_parse`. The eval harness does exactly this.
- **Depends on `re._constants` for opcodes.** Same private-but-stable caveat. If
  this becomes a concern, the codes are small enums we could inline by name.
- **Does not detect ambiguous alternation** (e.g. `(aa|ab)+X`) as a distinct shape.
  However, Python's `re` engine's common-prefix factoring makes most realistic
  ambiguous-alternation patterns non-ReDoS in our engine; and regexploit at default
  threshold also misses them. If we need this coverage, it's ~15 extra LoC.
- **Does not detect exponential patterns that don't involve nested repeats** (rare
  in real life — e.g. `.*.*.*`-style in some engines). Python's `re` handles these
  fine via its NFA-with-memoization in 3.11+ so it's moot for us.

### Reference implementation (for bd-eio04.7 to lift)

The working reference is `/tmp/bd-eio04.11-eval.py`. The essential routine is:

```python
import re
try:
    import re._parser as sre_parse
    from re._constants import (
        MAX_REPEAT, MIN_REPEAT, SUBPATTERN, BRANCH, IN,
        LITERAL, NOT_LITERAL, CATEGORY, AT, ASSERT, ASSERT_NOT, MAXREPEAT,
    )
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover  (Python < 3.11)
    import sre_parse
    from sre_constants import (
        MAX_REPEAT, MIN_REPEAT, SUBPATTERN, BRANCH, IN,
        LITERAL, NOT_LITERAL, CATEGORY, AT, ASSERT, ASSERT_NOT, MAXREPEAT,
    )

_REPEAT_OPS = {MAX_REPEAT, MIN_REPEAT}


def _js_to_python_named_groups(pattern: str) -> str:
    """Match backend/dummy_epg_engine.py — handle (?<name>…) → (?P<name>…)."""
    if not pattern:
        return pattern
    return re.sub(r"\(\?<(?!\=|\!)", "(?P<", pattern)


def _is_unbounded(repeat_args) -> bool:
    _min, _max, _body = repeat_args
    return _max == MAXREPEAT


def _contains_unbounded_repeat(parsed) -> bool:
    for op, args in parsed:
        if op in _REPEAT_OPS and _is_unbounded(args):
            return True
        if op is SUBPATTERN:
            _gid, _a, _b, subp = args
            if _contains_unbounded_repeat(subp):
                return True
        elif op is BRANCH:
            _none, branches = args
            if any(_contains_unbounded_repeat(b) for b in branches):
                return True
        elif op in (ASSERT, ASSERT_NOT):
            _dir, subp = args
            if _contains_unbounded_repeat(subp):
                return True
    return False


def _has_post_killer(subp_tail) -> bool:
    """Anything after an unbounded repeat that can fail the match."""
    for op, args in subp_tail:
        if op in (LITERAL, NOT_LITERAL, IN, CATEGORY, AT):
            return True
        if op is SUBPATTERN:
            _gid, _a, _b, inner = args
            if _has_post_killer(inner):
                return True
        if op in _REPEAT_OPS:
            _min, _max, body = args
            if _min >= 1 and _has_post_killer(body):
                return True
    return False


def detect_redos(pattern: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Return (is_vulnerable, reason). False + "" means safe to accept.

    Flags ONLY nested unbounded quantifiers followed by a killer element
    that forces backtracking — the shape Python's re engine is actually
    vulnerable to.
    """
    try:
        converted = _js_to_python_named_groups(pattern)
        parsed = sre_parse.parse(converted)
    except re.error as e:
        return (False, f"syntax-error:{e}")  # let the caller handle syntax separately

    def walk(subp, outer_suffix_has_killer: bool) -> str | None:
        for idx, (op, args) in enumerate(subp):
            remainder = subp[idx + 1 :]
            local_killer = _has_post_killer(remainder) or outer_suffix_has_killer

            if op in _REPEAT_OPS:
                _min, _max, body = args
                if _is_unbounded((_min, _max, body)) \
                   and _contains_unbounded_repeat(body) \
                   and local_killer:
                    return "nested-unbounded-repeat-with-killer"
                r = walk(body, local_killer)
                if r:
                    return r
            elif op is SUBPATTERN:
                _gid, _a, _b, subp2 = args
                r = walk(subp2, local_killer)
                if r:
                    return r
            elif op is BRANCH:
                _none, branches = args
                for b in branches:
                    r = walk(b, local_killer)
                    if r:
                        return r
            elif op in (ASSERT, ASSERT_NOT):
                _dir, subp2 = args
                r = walk(subp2, False)  # lookarounds don't propagate outer killer
                if r:
                    return r
        return None

    reason = walk(parsed, outer_suffix_has_killer=False)
    return (reason is not None, reason or "")
```

**Integration note for bd-eio04.7:** Call `detect_redos` from the router save handler
for dummy-EPG profiles, normalization rules (when `condition_type == "regex"`), and
auto-creation `sort_regex`. Reject with HTTP 400 and a message pointing to the
documentation when `is_vulnerable` is `True`. Syntax errors are handled separately
by the existing `re.compile` path.

**Tests to include in bd-eio04.7:**
- All 7 known-evil-with-killer patterns → must reject.
- All 32 real-corpus patterns → must accept.
- All 3 ambiguous-no-killer patterns → must accept (Python optimizes).
- Empty / whitespace / None → accept.
- Malformed syntax → returns `syntax-error:` reason (caller decides handling — the
  existing compile step will catch these anyway).

## 5. One-liner

Hand-rolled: 0 FPs, 7/7 TPs, 60 LoC, zero deps.
`regexploit`: 1 FP (on a production pattern), 7/7 TPs, 5-year-stale dependency.
`redos-detector` (npm): not applicable to a Python backend.

title: Shared unicode fixture bank for normalization tests
description:
Create backend/tests/fixtures/unicode_fixtures.py with a shared bank of Unicode edge-case inputs used across every normalization test. Every existing test file that invents its own Unicode strings should be migrated to import from here.

Fixture categories:
- Letter superscripts (ᴿᴬᵂ, ᴴᴰ, ᶠᴴᴰ, ᵁᴴᴰ, ˢᴰ) at prefix/suffix/mid-string positions.
- Numeric superscripts (⁰¹²³⁴⁵⁶⁷⁸⁹) at various positions including framerate patterns.
- Mixed letter+numeric (US: ESPN ᴴᴰ ⁶⁰fps).
- NFC vs NFD decomposition variants of the same grapheme.
- Zero-width joiners and spaces (U+200B, U+200D, U+FEFF).
- Combining diacritics.
- Cyrillic/Latin homoglyphs.
- RTL markers.

Unblocks parity test and NFKC work. Supersedes bd-2kb79.

title: NFC Unicode normalization in _match_single_condition and convert_superscripts
description:
Apply unicodedata.normalize('NFKC', text) at the top of _match_single_condition and convert_superscripts so NFC/NFD decomposition variants, zero-width joiners, and homoglyph variants collapse to canonical form before matching.

Security finding SEC-104-2. Also addresses the langtd19 'weird placements' complaint — mid-string superscript failures are often NFD-decomposition gaps, not code path divergence.

Changes:
- NFKC-normalize both the pattern and the text in backend/normalization_engine.py at _match_single_condition (~L511) and convert_superscripts (~L137).
- Optionally strip Cf/Cc category code points (control/format chars) before matching.
- Regression test using fixtures from the unicode fixture bank (depends on bd-eio04.3).

Supersedes bd-86t7f security finding 2.

Grooming outcomes 2026-04-22:
- **Use NFC, not NFKC** (PO decision 5a). Narrower fix — fixes NFD decomposition (SEC-104-2) without touching ligatures/fullwidth/Roman-numeral compatibility. Safer behavior-change profile.
- Bundled into bd-eio04.1's PR (PO decision 12). NFC lives inside the unified NormalizationPolicy.
- Apply at top of _match_single_condition and convert_superscripts (backend/normalization_engine.py). Both pattern and text normalized.

title: backend/safe_regex.py — shared safe-regex utility with ReDoS timeout
description:
New module backend/safe_regex.py providing search/match/sub/compile wrapping the 'regex' library (not stdlib 're') with per-call ReDoS protection.

API:
  safe_regex.search(pattern, text, *, flags=0, timeout_ms=100, max_pattern_len=500)
  safe_regex.match(pattern, text, *, flags=0, timeout_ms=100, max_pattern_len=500)
  safe_regex.sub(pattern, repl, text, *, flags=0, timeout_ms=100, max_pattern_len=500)
  safe_regex.compile(pattern, *, flags=0, max_pattern_len=500)
  class RegexTimeoutError(Exception)

Behavior:
- 100ms default timeout via regex library's timeout kwarg (best-effort in worker threads, which is acceptable given task-level budgets above).
- 500-char pattern length cap, matching existing template_engine.py MAX_REGEX_LEN precedent.
- On timeout or compilation error: log at WARNING with [SAFE_REGEX] prefix and return no-match sentinel (None for search/match, original text for sub). This matches the existing try/except re.error fallthrough in NormalizationEngine.
- Expose RegexTimeoutError for callers wanting strict mode.

Add 'regex' to backend dependencies. Follows existing shared-utility convention from backend/log_utils.py and backend/obfuscate.py.

Supersedes bd-tcegl.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #114 (merge 32067fee). backend/safe_regex.py live; regex library pinned at 2026.4.4.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
Grooming outcomes 2026-04-22:
- Docstring must call out that regex library timeout is BEST-EFFORT — devs will assume hard-timeout otherwise.
- Log pattern_sha256 + truncated pattern excerpt, not full pattern at WARNING (security note from security-engineer persona: pattern may contain attacker-controlled text).
- RegexTimeoutError inherits from module-level SafeRegexError (base class for catch-all).
- compile() with pattern > max_len RAISES (not sentinel) — compile is authoring-time, raising is correct contract.
- Pin 'regex' library version in backend/requirements.txt.
- Tests must include adversarial fixture (a+)+b against 'a'*30 + '!' with 500ms wall-clock ceiling (5x budget) to absorb CI jitter.
- Ships without feature flag (PO decision 10: .6 ships straight, no flag).
- NO dependency on bd-w3z4h for this bead — scaffolding is additive.

title: Write-time pattern linting at persistence boundaries
description:
Reject pathological regex patterns at save time — cheaper than runtime timeout and catches issues at authoring.

Persistence boundaries:
- POST/PUT /api/normalization/rules — normalization rule condition_value and action value
- POST/PUT /api/auto-creation/rules — condition value, action pattern, sort_regex, name_transform_pattern
- POST/PUT /api/dummy-epg/rules — title_pattern, time_pattern, date_pattern, substitution find pairs

Validation:
- Reject if pattern length > 500 chars.
- Reject if static analyzer detects nested quantifier (e.g., (a+)+, (a*)*, (a|a)+). Use redos-detector library or small AST check.
- Reject if pattern fails to compile via safe_regex.compile.

One-time migration: scan existing rows in normalization_rules, auto_creation_rules, dummy_epg_rules for pre-lint rows that would now fail validation; flag in UI for user review. Do not auto-delete user data.

title: Semgrep CI rule + style guide: prohibit bare re.* on user input
description:
Prevent regression of the ReDoS bug class by blocking dynamic-pattern re.* calls at review time.

Deliverables:
1. .semgrep.yml with rule 'no-bare-re-on-dynamic-pattern' that flags re.search/match/sub/compile when the first arg is not a raw string literal. Excludes tests/ and safe_regex.py itself.
2. Wire Semgrep into .github/workflows/test.yml as a required job.
3. Create docs/style_guide.md with a 'Regex' section:
   'User-supplied regex must use safe_regex, not re. Any regex where the pattern originates from the database, request body, settings, template, or any user-editable source must be evaluated through safe_regex. The re module is reserved for module-level constants compiled from hard-coded literals (convention: _UPPER_SNAKE_RE = re.compile(r"...")). safe_regex enforces a 500-character pattern cap and a 100ms evaluation timeout to defeat ReDoS. On timeout or invalid pattern it logs [SAFE_REGEX] at WARNING and returns the no-match sentinel — callers should treat this as did not match and continue.'
4. Rename the kwarg preserve_superscripts → preserve_numeric_superscripts (if any remains post-policy refactor) in same PR as the style guide lands.

title: Normalization observability: decision log + metrics + SLO
description:
Eliminate the 'debug by attached tar.gz bundle' pattern that produced three consecutive regressions on GH #104 with zero telemetry detection.

Instrumentation:
- Promote normalization decisions from DEBUG to INFO with structured fields: {input, matched_rule_ids, output, applied, preserve_numeric_superscripts}. Bounded sampling to avoid log blowup.

Metrics (backend/observability.py):
- ecm_normalization_rule_matches_total{rule_id} (Counter)
- ecm_normalization_no_change_total (Counter — 'ran but output == input')
- ecm_auto_creation_channels_created_total{normalized='true|false|skipped'} (Counter)
- ecm_normalization_duration_seconds (Histogram)

SLO (docs/sre/slos.md):
- SLO-4: Normalization Correctness. SLI = 1 - (bug reports containing 'normaliz' / 30d) / (auto-creations / 30d). Conservative target until baseline.

Canary harness: nightly CI job runs a golden set of 50 fixtures through both POST /api/normalization/test AND the auto-create executor; fails on any divergence. Alerts Slack on failure.

Supersedes bd-iaaau.

title: Auto-creation /run endpoints → background-task pattern (202 + poll)
description:
Current state: POST /api/auto-creation/run and POST /api/auto-creation/rules/{id}/run execute the pipeline synchronously inside the request coroutine. On large catalogs this can take minutes, which is why we just exempted /api/auto-creation/ from the 30s request-timeout middleware (bd-zv6pi hotfix). That exemption removes defense-in-depth on all auto-creation endpoints.

Goal: convert the run endpoints to fire-and-poll:
- POST returns 202 with { execution_id } immediately after enqueuing work to a background task / asyncio.create_task / task_engine.
- Frontend (frontend/src/hooks/useAutoCreationExecution.ts runPipeline) polls GET /api/auto-creation/executions/{id} until status is terminal (completed/failed/rolled_back), updating the execution list incrementally.
- Once converted, remove /api/auto-creation/ from _TIMEOUT_EXEMPT_PREFIXES in backend/main.py so the 30s budget protects CRUD + the now-fast enqueue handler.

Also address: useAutoCreationExecution.runPipeline currently only calls fetchExecutions() inside the try block (line ~129); on any POST error the execution list is stale until browser refresh. Move fetchExecutions() to a finally so the UI always refetches post-attempt. That cleanup pairs well with the polling change but can be shipped standalone too.

Related: bd-w3z4h (introduced the middleware), bd-zv6pi (narrow hotfix: exempt prefix).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #134 (v0.16.0-0053). /run endpoints converted to 202+poll background pattern with supervised asyncio.create_task; 30s timeout middleware re-armed by removing /api/auto-creation/ from _TIMEOUT_EXEMPT_PREFIXES. Frontend hook polls every 1s with AbortSignal-aware 30-min cap. CHANGELOG entry under Changed.

title: Runbook: dep-bump backend ASGI regression (fastapi/starlette/uvicorn rollback)
description:
Blocks PR1 of v0.16.0 dep-bump epic (backend ASGI triplet bundled: j9xrz + 6rrl5.1 + 6rrl5.2). SRE spike (2026-04-24) flagged this as P1 prerequisite — the triplet has no existing rollback runbook and is the highest blast-radius bump in the epic (request-path middleware contract + ASGI lifespan).

Scope:
- Detection: what signals (ecm_http_request_duration_seconds p95, 5xx burst, readiness flaps, worker-restart rate) indicate ASGI regression. Reference docs/sre/slos.md SLOs.
- Triage: is this starlette contract change, fastapi dependency-injection change, or uvicorn worker behavior? How to isolate.
- Rollback: git revert merge commit; GHCR retag previous SHA; docker pull + run; verify readiness.
- Post-rollback: bead creation, pinning requirements.txt back, re-cut process.
- Out-of-hours: can an operator roll back with no engineer available? The answer must be yes.

Style/template: follow docs/runbooks/v0.16.0-rollback.md + docs/runbooks/dep-bump-smoke-test.md conventions. Link from docs/runbooks/README.md.

Acceptance:
- File at docs/runbooks/dep-bump-backend-asgi-regression.md
- Linked from docs/runbooks/README.md index
- PR merged to dev
close_reason: Merged via PR #164 — docs/runbooks/dep-bump-backend-asgi-regression.md shipped. Operator detection (Prom + no-metrics paths), triage decision tree (starlette / fastapi / uvicorn / pydantic / asgiref-h11-httptools), GHCR image-swap + rebuild rollback modes, verification block.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Add rate limiting to /api/auth/login and /api/auth/dispatcharr/login using slowapi library. In-memory backend (no Redis needed — single-process Docker app). Apply to login endpoints only, not globally. Suggested limit: 5 attempts/minute per IP.

Steps:
1. Add slowapi to requirements.txt
2. Initialize Limiter in auth/routes.py with default key_func (client IP from X-Forwarded-For)
3. Add @limiter.limit() decorator to login() and dispatcharr_login()
4. Register SlowAPIMiddleware and 429 exception handler in main.py
5. Tests for rate limit behavior

title: Add safe security headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy)
description:
Add middleware to main.py setting three risk-free security headers on all responses:

- X-Frame-Options: DENY (prevents clickjacking)
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff (prevents MIME sniffing)
- Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin (prevents URL leakage)

These are unconditionally safe — no risk of breaking the SPA or LAN access.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Replace detail=str(e) with generic messages on HTTP 500 responses only (~30 instances across m3u.py, dummy_epg.py, export.py, backup.py, stream_preview.py, tags.py, normalization.py, epg.py). These leak internal details (file paths, DB errors, service URLs).

Leave 400/404/409 responses alone — those contain user-meaningful feedback (validation errors, not-found IDs, duplicate names, YAML parse errors) that the UI needs.

Pattern: replace detail=str(e) with a generic message (e.g., 'Operation failed') and ensure the exception is already logged via logger.error/warning/exception above the raise.

title: enhancedchannelmanager

Split from es2x6.2 which covers the safe headers.

title: Fix react-hooks/purity violation - Date.now() in NotificationCenter.tsx render - bd-me2lt followup

Trivial Small fix - move Date.now() into a useState initializer or a useMemo, or compute in the relevant effect. After this single-site fix, react-hooks/purity can be re-enabled in frontend/eslint.config.mjs (currently 'off' there alongside the other 4 react-hooks 7.1 rules).
close_reason: Fixed Date.now() in render at NotificationCenter.tsx:126 via ticking now state (10s interval). Re-enabled react-hooks/purity rule in eslint config. PR #195 merged into dev as 2ac9cbae.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
After moving Dispatcharr+ECM to a new server and restoring an ECM ZIP backup, Channel Manager showed static groups (e.g. My Teams) as empty/wrong-count while the Guide tab worked. Two independent bugs:

1. backend/routers/channel_groups.py get_channel_groups() filtered hidden groups by numeric ID only. hidden_channel_groups records (carried over in journal.db from the old server) collided with unrelated groups on the new Dispatcharr instance, hiding the user's static groups. Fix: validate stored group_name against current Dispatcharr group at the same ID; auto-prune stale records.

2. backend/routers/backup.py _restore_channel_groups() (YAML restore) deleted all Dispatcharr groups and recreated them by name, orphaning all channel/stream FK references. Fix: upsert by name only.

Tests added for both. Both deployed to ecm-ecm-1.

title: Investigate max_concurrent_probes limit not being respected
description:
Fixed two issues with stream probing:

1. **Misleading parallel probe count display**: The progress display was showing the number of queued tasks (waiting at semaphore) instead of actual concurrent probes. Fixed to show:
   - '[N parallel]' when N probes are actually running inside the semaphore
   - '[N queued]' when tasks are queued but only 0-1 are actually running
   This accurately reflects max_concurrent_probes being respected.

2. **No progress when re-probing failed streams**: The 'Re-probe Failed Streams' button used probeBulkStreams which had no progress tracking. Changed to use probeAllStreams with a new stream_ids filter parameter, which shows real-time progress during re-probe.

Files changed:
- backend/stream_prober.py: Fixed progress display (lines 1297-1308), added stream_ids_filter support (lines 985-1102)
- backend/main.py: Added stream_ids to ProbeAllRequest model (line 4681), passed filter to prober (line 4758)
- frontend/src/services/api.ts: Added streamIds parameter to probeAllStreams (lines 1376-1389)
- frontend/src/components/tabs/SettingsTab.tsx: Changed handleRerunFailed to use probeAllStreams with progress tracking (lines 801-835)

title: Pre-Alembic install bootstrap stamps at HEAD instead of baseline — skips all post-baseline migrations
description:
User reports 'no such column: auto_creation_rules.match_scope_target_group' breaking Auto Creation with 500s.

Root cause: backend/database.py:_bootstrap_alembic lines 144-151. When ECM detects a pre-Alembic install (user_tables exist, alembic_version table missing), it stamps at HEAD and returns before running upgrade head. The intent was to avoid duplicate-DDL on existing schemas — but stamping at HEAD means Alembic believes ALL post-baseline migrations are already applied, even though the schema is physically at the baseline.

Affected: any user whose journal.db was created before the Alembic baseline (revision 0001, ~2026-04-20) and who has since pulled an image with new columns/indexes added by migrations 0002, 0003, 0004.

Migrations skipped:
- 0002: auto_creation_rules.match_scope_target_group (the column the user is hitting — a SELECT * fails because SQLAlchemy includes it in queries)
- 0003: rule_lint_findings
- 0004: journal batch_id + entity composite indexes

Fix: change line 150 from command.stamp(alembic_cfg, 'head') to command.stamp(alembic_cfg, '0001'), drop the return, so upgrade head runs after stamping at baseline. The baseline IS the pre-Alembic schema by design.

Recovery for already-affected users (no code change required):
docker exec ecm-ecm-1 sqlite3 /config/journal.db "UPDATE alembic_version SET version_num='0001';"
docker restart ecm-ecm-1

This re-stamps the user at baseline; bootstrap then upgrades to head (0004), applying the 3 missing migrations.

Considerations:
- The fix MUST also include a self-heal: detect any schema/version mismatch on startup and recover. Otherwise users already stamped-at-head are stuck without the manual SQL.
- Alternatively, file as separate bead: ship the bootstrap fix now (prevents future cases), follow up with self-heal logic.

Priority: P0 — production users hitting 500s, blocks Auto Creation entirely for affected DBs.
close_reason: Merged via PR #201 (commit b7d69c40). Pre-Alembic stamping bug fixed + self-heal for stamped-at-head DBs shipped to dev as 0.16.0-0063. Affected users pull the new container image and the bug auto-recovers on first restart.

   - Implement tag matching with caching
   - Add else branch execution logic

3. Update existing normalization API to include new fields

If/Then/Else Logic:
- IF condition matches: apply action_type/action_value
- ELSE (optional): apply else_action_type/else_action_value

New Condition Type 'tag_group':
- Matches if text contains ANY tag from specified group
- Position-aware: prefix, suffix, or anywhere (contains)

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Epic: Add M3U playlist and XMLTV EPG export generation from managed channels, with cloud storage distribution (S3, Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox). Includes playlist profiles for curated selection, local file generation, cloud adapter framework, publish pipeline with scheduling, and full frontend UI. Generate-and-distribute model, not live hosting. 7 phases, 55 leaf tasks. TDD approach: write unit/integration/E2E tests FIRST in each phase before implementation code.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Foundation phase. TDD: write tests (.1.7) FIRST, then implement. New DB tables: playlist_profiles (named configs with channel/group selection, stream URL mode, output options), cloud_storage_targets (provider configs with encrypted credentials), publish_configurations (profile+target+trigger linking), publish_history (audit log). New modules: backend/m3u_generator.py (EXTM3U with EXTINF entries, tvg-id/logo/chno/group-title, direct vs proxy URLs), backend/xmltv_generator.py (XMLTV with channel/programme elements filtered to profile), backend/export_manager.py (orchestrates generation, writes to /config/exports/<profile_id>/). 7 leaf tasks.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Add playlist_profiles table to backend/database.py. Fields: id (PK), name (unique), description, selection_mode (enum: all/groups/channels), selected_groups (JSON array), selected_channels (JSON array), stream_url_mode (enum: direct/proxy), include_logos (bool), include_epg_ids (bool), include_channel_numbers (bool), sort_order (enum: name/number/group), filename_prefix (str), created_at, updated_at. Add SQLAlchemy model and Pydantic schemas.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Add cloud_storage_targets table to backend/database.py. Fields: id (PK), name (unique), provider_type (enum: s3/gdrive/onedrive/dropbox), credentials (encrypted JSON), upload_path (str), enabled (bool), created_at, updated_at. Credentials field stores provider-specific config (bucket/keys for S3, service account JSON for GDrive, etc). Use Fernet symmetric encryption for credentials at rest.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Add two tables. publish_configurations: id (PK), name, profile_id (FK->playlist_profiles), target_id (FK->cloud_storage_targets, nullable for local-only), schedule_type (enum: manual/cron/event), cron_expression (str, nullable), event_triggers (JSON array of trigger types), enabled (bool), created_at, updated_at. publish_history: id (PK), config_id (FK->publish_configurations), started_at, completed_at, status (enum: running/success/failed), channels_count, file_size_bytes, error_message, details (JSON).

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/m3u_generator.py. Function generate_m3u(channels, profile) -> str. Output format: #EXTM3U header, then per channel: #EXTINF:-1 tvg-id="epg_id" tvg-name="name" tvg-logo="logo_url" tvg-chno="channel_number" group-title="group_name",Channel Name followed by stream URL line. Respect profile settings: include/exclude logos, EPG IDs, channel numbers. Support direct vs proxy URL modes. Handle edge cases: missing EPG, no logo, special characters in names.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/xmltv_generator.py. Function generate_xmltv(channels, epg_data, profile) -> str. Output valid XMLTV format: <?xml> header, <tv> root with generator-info, <channel> elements (id, display-name, icon), <programme> elements (start/stop/channel attrs, title, desc, category, icon). Filter EPG data to only channels in the profile. Use xml.etree.ElementTree for generation. Handle timezone formatting (YYYYMMDDHHMMSS +HHMM).

title: enhancedchannelmanager

Class ExportManager with methods: generate(profile_id) orchestrates full generation - loads profile, fetches channel/stream/EPG data from Dispatcharr API, calls m3u_generator and xmltv_generator, writes files to /config/exports/<profile_id>/playlist.m3u and guide.xml. get_export_path(profile_id) returns the export directory. cleanup(profile_id) removes old exports. Ensure directory creation and atomic writes (write to temp then rename).

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Plugin architecture in backend/cloud_storage/. TDD: write tests (.3.9) FIRST, then implement adapters. base.py with CloudStorageAdapter ABC (upload, test_connection, delete). Adapters: s3_adapter.py (S3/MinIO/Backblaze B2 via boto3), gdrive_adapter.py (Google Drive via service account), onedrive_adapter.py (Microsoft Graph API via httpx), dropbox_adapter.py (Dropbox SDK). Cloud target CRUD API + test connection endpoint. New deps: google-api-python-client, google-auth, dropbox. 9 leaf tasks. Depends on Phase 1, parallel with Phase 2.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/cloud_storage/__init__.py and backend/cloud_storage/base.py. Define CloudStorageAdapter ABC with methods: upload(local_path, remote_path) -> UploadResult, test_connection() -> ConnectionTestResult, delete(remote_path) -> bool, list_files(remote_path) -> list[str]. UploadResult dataclass: success, remote_url, file_size, duration_ms, error. ConnectionTestResult dataclass: success, message, provider_info. Factory function get_adapter(target) -> CloudStorageAdapter that instantiates the correct adapter based on provider_type.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/cloud_storage/s3_adapter.py. Implements CloudStorageAdapter for S3-compatible storage (AWS S3, MinIO, Backblaze B2). Config fields: endpoint_url (optional, for non-AWS), bucket_name, access_key_id, secret_access_key, region (optional). Uses boto3 (already in project). upload() uses put_object with content-type. test_connection() does head_bucket. Support custom endpoints for MinIO/B2 compatibility. Handle common errors: access denied, bucket not found, network timeout.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/cloud_storage/gdrive_adapter.py. Implements CloudStorageAdapter for Google Drive via service account. Config fields: service_account_json (full JSON key), folder_id (target folder). Uses google-api-python-client and google-auth. upload() creates/updates file in specified folder using MediaFileUpload. test_connection() verifies service account access to the folder. Handle: folder not found, permission denied, quota exceeded.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/cloud_storage/onedrive_adapter.py. Implements CloudStorageAdapter for OneDrive/SharePoint via Microsoft Graph API. Config fields: client_id, client_secret, tenant_id, drive_id (optional), upload_folder_path. Uses httpx (already available) for Graph API calls. OAuth2 client credentials flow for app-only access. upload() uses PUT to /drives/{id}/items/{path}:/content for files <4MB, resumable upload session for larger. test_connection() lists root folder.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/cloud_storage/dropbox_adapter.py. Implements CloudStorageAdapter for Dropbox. Config fields: access_token (long-lived or refresh token), app_key, app_secret, upload_path. Uses dropbox SDK. upload() uses files_upload for <150MB (our files will be small). test_connection() calls users_get_current_account. Handle: invalid token, path not found, insufficient space.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Add to backend/main.py: GET /api/export/cloud-targets (list targets, mask sensitive credential fields), POST /api/export/cloud-targets (create, encrypt credentials before storage), PATCH /api/export/cloud-targets/{id} (update, re-encrypt if credentials changed, allow partial updates that preserve existing encrypted creds), DELETE /api/export/cloud-targets/{id} (delete if not referenced by active publish configs). Sensitive field masking: show only last 4 chars of keys/tokens in GET responses.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Add POST /api/export/cloud-targets/{id}/test endpoint. Instantiates the appropriate CloudStorageAdapter, calls test_connection(), returns result. Also support POST /api/export/cloud-targets/test with inline credentials (for testing before saving). Set reasonable timeout (10 seconds). Return structured response: success bool, message, provider-specific info (bucket name, folder path, account email, etc).

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/cloud_storage/crypto.py. Functions: generate_key() creates Fernet key stored in /config/.export_key, encrypt_credentials(data: dict) -> str, decrypt_credentials(encrypted: str) -> dict. Key management: auto-generate on first use, load from file on subsequent uses. Use cryptography.fernet.Fernet. Add cryptography to dependencies if not present. Handle key rotation: re-encrypt all targets when key changes.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/tests/test_cloud_storage.py. Test adapter factory returns correct adapter type. Test upload success/failure, test_connection success/failure, error handling. Test credential encryption round-trip. Test sensitive field masking in API responses. Test cloud target CRUD operations.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Pipeline in backend/publish_pipeline.py. TDD: write integration test (.4.8) FIRST, then implement pipeline. Steps: load config+profile+target, fetch channels/streams/EPG from Dispatcharr, generate M3U/XMLTV via generators, upload to cloud target, record history+notify. Scheduling via export_publish task with cron-based auto-publish. Event triggers after M3U refresh, channel edit, EPG refresh with 5-second debounce. 8 leaf tasks. Depends on Phases 1, 2, 3.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/publish_pipeline.py. Class PublishPipeline with method execute(config_id) -> PublishResult. Steps: 1) Load publish_configuration + profile + target from DB, 2) Fetch channels/streams/EPG data from Dispatcharr API based on profile selection, 3) Call m3u_generator and xmltv_generator, 4) Write files locally via ExportManager, 5) If target configured, upload via CloudStorageAdapter, 6) Record publish_history entry. PublishResult dataclass: success, channels_count, m3u_size, xmltv_size, upload_result, duration_ms, error.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Create backend/tasks/export_publish.py. Register export_publish task with existing task scheduler. On scheduler tick, check all publish_configurations with schedule_type='cron' and enabled=True. Evaluate cron expressions against current time. Queue PublishPipeline.execute() for matching configs. Track last_run per config to prevent duplicate runs. Use the same task infrastructure as existing M3U refresh tasks.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Add event hooks in backend/main.py for auto-publishing. Trigger events: after_m3u_refresh, after_channel_edit, after_epg_refresh. When event fires, find all publish_configurations with matching event_triggers and enabled=True. Apply 5-second debounce to batch rapid changes (e.g. bulk channel edits). Queue PublishPipeline.execute() for each matching config. Use asyncio debounce pattern with per-config timers.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Add endpoints: GET /api/export/publish-configs (list with profile/target names), POST /api/export/publish-configs (create, validate profile_id and target_id exist, validate cron expression), PATCH /api/export/publish-configs/{id} (update), DELETE /api/export/publish-configs/{id}. title: enhancedchannelmanager

title: enhancedchannelmanager

title: enhancedchannelmanager

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Black screen detection for stream probing (#46). Shipped in v0.14.0-0016.

Changes:
- Added opt-in ffmpeg blackdetect pass after successful probe
- New is_black_screen field on StreamStats model + DB migration
- Settings: checkbox toggle + sample duration (3-30s) under Maintenance > Stream Probing
- Purple videocam_off badge on streams detected as black screen
- Black screen streams deprioritized in Smart Sort (same tier as failed/timeout)
- 13 new tests covering detection, sort, persistence, and constructor

Testing requested:
1. Go to Settings > Maintenance > Stream Probing
2. Enable 'Black screen detection', set sample duration (default 5s is fine)
3. Save settings
4. Probe a stream (ideally one known to show black content)
5. Verify is_black_screen appears in probe API response
6. If a black screen stream exists, verify the purple videocam_off icon shows in stream list
7. Verify black screen streams sort to bottom in Smart Sort

close_reason: Retired 2026-04-21 by PO directive. The original gb5r5 DBAS migration plan was filed 2026-02-18 and the team-plan archaeology (agent a4ca4a38) found it significantly overlapping with already-shipped ECM work (backup.py YAML restore, task_scheduler.py, dispatcharr_client.py, notifications infra). 26 of the 37 tree beads had not been updated in 67 days and lacked acceptance criteria. Rather than salvage, PO chose to kill the existing scope entirely so fresh DBAS planning can start from user need (not from the standalone DBAS product's original shape). Fresh plan to be authored in a future /team-plan or /onboard session. Do NOT reopen these beads — if DBAS work resumes, file new beads with current framing.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Port DBAS importService.ts to Python. Upload and restore from ZIP. Ordered restore sequence: M3U → EPG sources → profiles → groups → stream profiles → logos → channels → user agents → settings → plugins → DVR → comskip → users → refresh triggers. Name-based conflict resolution with ID remapping. Stream matching fallback chain (tvg_id → station ID → normalized name → hash). Logo matching (source ID → name → sanitized filename). Dry-run/inspect mode. Per-category selective restore.
notes:
Security hardening criteria from bd-qmuij (STRIDE threat model, docs/security/threat_model_dbas_import.md):
(1) Admin-only endpoint + DBAS paths absent from AUTH_EXEMPT_PATHS
(2) Per-category opt-in flags for users + plugins (default false)
(3) Current authenticated admin row never overwritten/deleted/demoted by restore
(4) Zip Slip hardening: reject absolute/.. paths, symlink entries, >32-segment paths, >255-byte names
(5) Zip bomb caps: max 256MB upload, 10k entries, 1GB uncompressed, 100x per-entry ratio (PO-tunable)
(6) Streaming upload (shutil.copyfileobj to NamedTemporaryFile) — no await file.read()
(7) SHA-256 manifest per file verified before any extraction outside tempdir
(8) Tempdir isolation + guaranteed cleanup; dry-run must be side-effect free
(9) Secrets-in-logs denylist + _redact() helper (password, token, api_key, smtp_password, M3U creds)
(10) URL scheme re-validation on restore (reuse bd-ppe28.3)
(11) Per-category audit log (journal.log_entry) with request ID; users category logs usernames only, never hashes
(12) Error message sanitisation (no paths/SQL/usernames in HTTPException detail)
(13) Plugin execution gate: written to disk but NOT executed at import; activation via separate admin action
(14) SQLAlchemy transaction per category with rollback on exception
(15) Dispatcharr restore batches capped at 500 items
(16) CSRF: bearer-token required; reject cookie-only requests
(17) Authz denials emit structured WARN logs
Test module layout: backend/tests/security/ (test_dbas_import_{authz,zipbomb,zipslip,manifest,users,secrets,audit,cleanup,url_validation,plugins}.py)
PO decisions required (see threat model §6): A1 plugins code-vs-config; A2 cross-instance restore scope; A3 users-table version compat; A4 ratify DoS caps; A5 journal tamper-evidence; A6 CSRF posture

title: Fix EPG matching for channels with special characters like AMC+

comments.author: lecaptainc
comments.text:
Fixed EPG matching for channels with special characters like AMC+.

Added semantic character conversion before stripping non-alphanumeric:
- '+' is converted to 'plus' (so 'AMC+' becomes 'amcplus')
- '&' is converted to 'and' (so 'A&E' becomes 'aande')

This ensures channels like 'AMC+' properly match TVG-IDs like 'AMC+(AMCPLUS).us' since both normalize to 'amcplus'.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: PR #99 BulkUpdateAutoCreationRulesRequest inherits conditions: Optional[list] and actions: Optional[list] from UpdateAutoCreationRuleRequest. The bulk handler never reads or applies these fields — a caller sending {'rule_ids': [...], 'conditions': [...], 'actions': [...]} gets a 200 OK with the conditions/actions payload silently dropped. Fix: reject these fields at request validation with 400 ('conditions and actions are not supported in bulk-update; use PUT /rules/{id}'). Add a unit test that asserts the rejection.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Recurring pattern (5dug8 latest example, 0gcu9 prior): bead tracks 'failing tests on dev HEAD' but local source already contains the fixes — the actual drift is between origin/dev and what's deployed in ecm-ecm-1. Tests pass once dev HEAD is re-deployed.

Container-first development workflow (per CLAUDE.md) means agents docker cp specific files when iterating. Nobody re-deploys the full backend on PR merges to dev. So when a session opens that runs the test suite, they observe failures that look like 'real drift' but are actually 'someone else's branch+deploy is stale on the shared container.'

Options to evaluate:
1. Document a pre-test-run 'sync container to origin/dev' step (rsync/docker cp from local backend/ tree)
2. Add a make/script target like 'make redeploy-backend-fresh' that maps local backend/ -> /app/
3. Detect the drift earlier: a tiny smoke that fingerprints /app vs git HEAD and warns if mismatched

This keeps surfacing as 'test drift' beads. Would be a small one-time investment to drain the source.

title: Investigate py/path-injection in export.py + export_manager.py (CodeQL alerts 1354-1359)
description:
## Context
Beyond the backup.py path-injection findings (bead -0a1pr) and the export.py follow-ups (bead -[ext]), the current CodeQL Default Setup scan shows 11 additional HIGH-severity errors the Security Engineer flagged during triage for PR #82:

- py/stack-trace-exposure (8): routers/backup.py:686, routers/channels.py:2164, routers/export.py:675/678/693/696, routers/health.py:232, + 1 more
- py/reflective-xss (1): routers/export.py:1243
- py/partial-ssrf (2): cloud_storage/onedrive_adapter.py:45 and :98
- js/regex/missing-regexp-anchor (1 warning)

## Scope
For each finding:
1. Determine if the exposure/SSRF/XSS is real or a CodeQL false-positive
2. Assess risk (is the path authenticated? what can an attacker do?)
3. Decide: fix vs dismiss-with-justification

Stack-trace-exposure in particular tends to appear when code does 'raise HTTPException(detail=str(e))' — which CLAUDE.md's backend guide actually recommends. Need to decide: sanitize stack traces in production but leak details in dev, or dismiss the rule as intentional-by-policy.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Each of the 11 high-severity errors has a documented disposition (fix or justified dismiss)
- [ ] If 'str(e)' leak pattern is retained by policy, add a comment in backend CLAUDE.md documenting the decision
- [ ] Relevant CodeQL dismissal comments added in the UI with reasoning
- [ ] Cloud storage SSRF: verify URL construction in onedrive_adapter.py cannot be redirected to internal endpoints

Labels: security, codeql
notes:
PR #159 opened: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/159

Per-alert verdict: All six alerts (1354-1359) already dismissed in CodeQL UI as 'false positive' with sanitizer-test reference per ADR-005 Phase 1 policy. Per ADR-005 sequencing 2 + bd-0a1pr precedent, also added canonicalize-and-verify defense-in-depth real-fix in export_manager.py and routers/export.py.

Implementation: New _safe_export_path helper in export_manager.py canonicalizes EXPORTS_DIR/str(profile_id)/*parts and verifies containment via Path.resolve().relative_to(). Wired into ExportManager.get_export_path, ExportManager.cleanup, download_m3u, download_xmltv.

Test coverage: 9 new tests in TestExportPathInjection (mirrors TestSavedBackupsPathInjection from bd-0a1pr) — covers traversal, absolute paths, null-bytes, symlink-escape, plus DESTRUCTIVE rmtree sentinel-file regression.

Verification: 3113 backend tests pass; Semgrep clean across .semgrep.yml + p/python + p/security-audit + p/owasp-top-ten.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #159. All 6 alerts (1354-1359) classified false-positive (already dismissed in CodeQL UI per ADR-005 Phase 1 with sanitizer-test references). Why FP: profile_id is FastAPI int path-param (422 at routing); prefix is Pydantic-validated FILENAME_RE allowlist. Defense-in-depth backstop added: new _safe_export_path(profile_id, *parts) helper canonicalizes via Path.resolve() + relative_to() and raises ExportPathError on escape. Wired into ExportManager.get_export_path + cleanup (rmtree short-circuits before destructive op on escape). routers/export.py download_m3u/download_xmltv re-verify file path containment. 9 new tests in TestExportPathInjection mirror bd-0a1pr pattern. Existing tests refactored to patch EXPORTS_DIR. 3113 backend tests pass. Note: backstop introduced new alert #1501 which was dismissed as FP-with-evidence (sanitizer documented in same function, comprehensive test coverage).

title: FLAKE: TestTraceIdMiddleware fails under certain test ordering (tp681 baseline)
description:
Surfaced by tp681 flake audit 2026-04-20. 2 tests fail non-deterministically depending on test ordering:

- tests/routers/test_observability_middleware.py::TestTraceIdMiddleware::test_trace_id_appears_in_log_line
- tests/routers/test_observability_middleware.py::TestTraceIdMiddleware::test_generated_trace_id_matches_uuidv4_format_in_logs

Reproduction: 
- PASS in isolation: pytest tests/routers/test_observability_middleware.py
- PASS with adjacent file: pytest tests/unit/test_observability.py tests/routers/test_observability_middleware.py (either order)
- FAIL when preceded by the second half of tests/integration/ (test_api_tags through test_router_registration): pytest tests/integration/test_api_tags.py ... tests/routers/test_observability_middleware.py

Likely root cause: contextvar/logging-handler state from an integration test leaks. The capture_json_logs fixture in test_observability_middleware.py builds a StringIO handler; the middleware emits via _access_log.info (the 'ecm.access' named logger). One of the integration tests likely:
- Removes handlers from the root logger
- Calls install_json_logging with side effects that stack filters
- Leaks a trace_id contextvar token that makes the request-scoped set_trace_id return the wrong value

Symptom: 'no log record carried trace_id=trace-log-check-42' OR 'no log records carried a non-sentinel trace id'. The middleware's _access_log.info call is not producing a record that makes it into the capture buffer.

Mitigation (temporary): pass --deselect for these two tests when running full BE suite in the container. Documented in docs/testing.md flake-policy section.

Fix approach (ordered cheapest first):
1. Make the capture_json_logs fixture attach the handler to 'ecm.access' directly (not root) — eliminate the propagation dependency.
2. Add an autouse fixture in tests/conftest.py that re-runs observability.install_observability() after each test, so state is always fresh.
3. Audit integration tests that touch logging and scope their changes.

Acceptance:
- Full BE suite (pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e) passes 3 consecutive times in ecm-ecm-1 WITHOUT --deselect for these 2 tests.
- Root cause identified and documented in the test module's docstring.
acceptance_criteria: 3 consecutive BE full-suite greens in ecm-ecm-1 without deselections; root cause documented
close_reason: Resolved by PR #87 (mcnj0). Same root cause as the order-dependent trace_id flake QA identified in tp681 audit: backend/alembic/env.py fileConfig() without disable_existing_loggers=False was disabling ecm.access logger and pinning root level to WARNING. DBA fix scopes fileConfig to standalone alembic CLI case only; embedded invocation from main.py retains JSON handler + trace-id filter + root level. No separate PR needed.

title: Ship Cache-Control defaults on /assets (immutable) and index.html (no-cache) in backend/main.py
description:
Surfaced by 11t6c runbook work (PR #167, 2026-04-24). Infra-cache agent verified that ECM's Starlette StaticFiles mount emits only ETag / Last-Modified — no Cache-Control header on /assets/* or /index.html.

Consequence: every operator inherits whatever their reverse proxy defaults to. The infra-cache-invalidation runbook (PR #167) is therefore needed because ECM doesn't ship cache-safe defaults. A tiny backend change would shift the problem from 'per-operator infra discipline' to 'ECM ships safe defaults.'

Scope:
- Inspect backend/main.py around lines 1020-1036 (the StaticFiles mount).
- Add Cache-Control response headers via a middleware or explicit header wiring:
  - /index.html: 'no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate' OR 'no-cache' (must always revalidate — entry point must never serve stale).
  - /assets/*: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' (content-addressed hash paths from Vite — eternal cache safe).
- Verify behavior with curl -I against a fresh container.
- Confirm no regression in normal page loads.

Acceptance:
- curl -I ecm-ecm-1:8000/index.html shows Cache-Control: no-cache (or equivalent).
- curl -I ecm-ecm-1:8000/assets/index-<hash>.js shows Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable.
- Backend test coverage for the header-emit behavior.
- Reference added from docs/runbooks/infra-cache-invalidation.md noting that ECM now ships safe defaults (reduces scope of operator-side work; runbook still covers pathological cases).

Size: Small.

This is P3 — infrastructure-discipline improvement, not a bug. Operators' existing setups keep working; this just makes ECM a better citizen.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #181 (merge commit ddaadd9e). Branch was rebased clean (cherry-picked bfb7877f onto current origin/dev) after a bad merge silently dropped sister-bead changes. Diff verified to be ONLY the 3 hl603 files: backend/main.py +52, backend/tests/integration/test_static_cache_headers.py +126 (new), docs/runbooks/infra-cache-invalidation.md +7. Backend suite: 3065 passed, 0 failed. All 5 required CI checks SUCCESS.

title: Fix compound OR logic stripping entire channel name in normalization rules
description:
Bug reported in GH issue #217 by swarthyplacebo (ECM 0.16.0-0056, P2).

Symptom: A normalization rule with Logic=OR + cond1=Starts With VariantA (mismatch on the channel) + cond2=Starts With VariantB (match) + action=Strip Prefix produces an empty channel name. The strip applies to the entire string instead of just the matching prefix.

Root cause confirmed in backend/normalization_engine.py:1033-1034 (_match_compound_conditions):

  if i == 0 and match.matched and not cond_negate:
      primary_match = match

When OR logic short-circuits past a non-matching cond1, the i==0 guard prevents primary_match from being captured even though cond2 matched. final_matched=True (via any()), primary_match=None → falls through to RuleMatch(matched=True, match_start=0, match_end=len(text)) on line 1046 → action strips the entire text.

Proposed fix (one-line, from reporter, verified correct):

  if primary_match is None and match.matched and not cond_negate:
      primary_match = match

This captures the first matching, non-negated condition's metadata regardless of index. Also corrects a latent identical bug for AND logic when condition 1 is negated and a later condition isn't.

Plan when resumed:
1. Branch from origin/dev
2. Write a failing pytest that reproduces the OR-cond2-only-matching empty-string symptom (likely in backend/tests/unit/test_normalization_engine.py or similar — check existing _match_compound_conditions coverage)
3. Apply the one-line fix
4. Verify test passes + full backend suite (cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ --tb=short --no-header -p no:warnings 2>&1 | tail -1)
5. Add CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased] Fixed referencing GH #217
6. Open PR to dev, drive through to merge
7. Comment on GH #217 thanking the reporter, confirming their diagnosis, and linking the fix
8. Close this bead after PR merges

Reporter context: first-time submitter, drafted diagnosis with Gemini + Sonnet 4.6 confirm. Acknowledge that in the resolution comment.

GH issue: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/issues/217
close_reason: Fixed in v0.16.0-0076 (PR #230). One-line fix to _match_compound_conditions per the reporter's diagnosis + 3 regression tests + CHANGELOG. GH #217 commented & closed.

title: security: add XSS regression test for print-guide title (CodeQL 1360)
description:
CodeQL alert 1360 flags py/reflected-xss in the /api/export/print-guide endpoint for the channel/guide title field.

Technical status: FALSE POSITIVE — _escape_html is in fact applied at backend/routers/export.py:1235 before the title renders into the HTML response. CodeQL's dataflow does not appear to trace the escape call.

Why a bead exists instead of a direct dismissal:
ADR-005 Phase 1 policy requires a regression test that PROVES the sanitizer fires before dismissing any reflected-XSS alert. We cannot dismiss 1360 without landing the test first.

Task scope:
1. Add regression test to backend/tests/routers/test_export.py:
   - Payload: title containing <script>alert(1)</script> plus quote/angle-bracket variants
   - Assertion: response body contains <script> (or equivalent entity-encoded form) and does NOT contain raw <script>
   - Assertion: _escape_html is called on the title field (can mock/spy or just assert on rendered HTML)
2. After test lands and passes in CI, dismiss alert 1360 as 'false positive' with dismissal comment citing the test file path + line number.

Secondary concern (PO DECISION REQUIRED, NOT IN SCOPE OF THIS BEAD):
/api/export/print-guide endpoint has NO auth dependency — no RequireAdminIfEnabled. Combined with reflected output of user-supplied fields, this creates a CSRF/XSS angle should the sanitizer ever regress (attacker could craft a link that an authenticated admin clicks and exfiltrates session). Recommendation: add RequireAdminIfEnabled to this endpoint. Flagging explicitly; will NOT do this in this bead unless PO approves.

Acceptance criteria:
- New test lands in backend/tests/routers/test_export.py, named e.g. test_print_guide_escapes_title_xss
- Test passes in CI
- CodeQL alert 1360 dismissed with reason=false positive and dismissal comment referencing test path:line
- PO decision on the auth side-item recorded (separate follow-up if approved)

References:
- CodeQL alert 1360 (py/reflected-xss)
- backend/routers/export.py:1235 (sanitizer location)
- ADR-005 Phase 1 policy (test-before-dismiss for XSS)

title: Frontend error telemetry + error-free session rate SLO (no RUM exists today)
description:
PO-accepted Phase 1 shape (2026-04-24) per spike synthesis. Waiting on ADR-006 (enhancedchannelmanager-bong9) to land before implementation.

**Phase 1 decision (will be formally encoded in ADR-006)**:

- Local-sink architecture: POST /api/client-errors → structlog + Prometheus counters on existing /metrics. No new service, no external egress by default.
- JWT-required endpoint, same auth as other /api/* routes.
- Rate limit: 10 events/session/minute, 8 KB/request, 429 on overflow.
- Payload schema: OTel-log-record-compatible (kind, message<=512, stack<=4096 basename-stripped, release, route pathname-only, user_agent_hash sha256, ts iso8601).
- Field allowlist: stack/message/pathname/viewport/UA-major-version only. No query strings, no referrer, no cookies, no localStorage, no user-typed input.
- Scrubber: extend backend/obfuscate.py patterns, applied client-side and server-side.
- Capture sites: React ErrorBoundary + window.onerror + unhandledrejection + Vite vite:preloadError + dynamic-import .catch + pre-bundle inline script in index.html (~20 lines, sendBeacon).
- Transport: navigator.sendBeacon with fetch fallback; reporter swallows its own errors.
- Opt-out toggle: telemetry.client_errors_enabled (default ON, local-only, privacy-neutral).
- SLO-6 (docs/sre/slos.md): error-free session rate >= 99% on 7d, gated at sessions >= 50/day, target marked uncalibrated until 30d data.
- Alerting rule in docs/sre/prometheus_rules.yaml, operator-provisioned per 5uxwh posture.
- Label cardinality bounded: kind fixed enum, release capped to current + prior 2 builds.

**Phase 2 (deferred, not in scope of this bead)**: opt-in external SaaS sink, SQLite admin view, OTel collector export.

**Dependencies**:
- Blocked by enhancedchannelmanager-bong9 (ADR-006). Implementation does not start until ADR lands.

**Spike personas consulted**: SRE, security-engineer, it-architect (all three converged on this shape).

Acceptance:
- ADR-006 merged.
- Backend endpoint + scrubber + rate limit + counters/histogram shipped.
- Frontend reporter + capture sites wired.
- Pre-bundle inline script in index.html.
- SLO-6 added to slos.md with uncalibrated target note.
- Alerting rule added to prometheus_rules.yaml.
- Opt-out toggle documented in docs/user_guide/.
- PR merged to dev.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #173 (merge commit dca1225c). Phase 1 complete: backend /api/client-errors router + Pydantic schema + sliding-window rate limiter + 3 Prom counters, frontend clientErrorReporter with ErrorBoundary wiring + window.onerror + unhandledrejection + vite:preloadError + dynamic-import .catch (App.tsx tabs) + pre-bundle inline script in index.html, settings toggle telemetry_client_errors_enabled (default true, honored on both sides), SLO-6 (error-free session rate) in docs/sre/slos.md, alert rules ECMClientErrorRateElevated/ECMClientErrorRateCritical in docs/sre/prometheus_rules.yaml, operator opt-out doc at docs/user_guide/error-telemetry-opt-out.md. 20+3 backend tests + 28+6 frontend tests all green; CI Backend Tests + Frontend Tests + CodeQL + Semgrep all passed on PR.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
Phase 1 implementation pushed to feat/frontend-error-telemetry-i6a1m.

Backend:

- Pydantic schema with deny-by-default allowlist; server-side stack basename-strip + obfuscate_text scrubbing.
- In-memory sliding-window rate limiter keyed on JWT sub → cookie hash → client IP (10/60s).
- Three new metrics: ecm_client_errors_total{kind,release}, ecm_client_errors_dropped_total{reason}, ecm_client_error_reports_bytes histogram. All bounded label cardinality.
- 20 router tests: happy path, schema, 413, 429, 401, path scrub, UA hash, counter emission.

Frontend:
- services/clientErrorReporter.ts with scrubStack, scrubMessage, scrubRoute, SHA-256 UA hash, ClientRateLimiter, sendBeacon+fetch transport.
- Feature flag VITE_ECM_ERROR_TELEMETRY_ENABLED (default ON).
- Wired via main.tsx: installGlobalErrorHandlers before React mounts + ErrorBoundary onError prop.
- 28 vitest cases (27 unit + 1 render integration).

Tests: backend 3116/3116 passing, frontend 1170/1170 passing, typecheck clean, lint clean.

Smoke test in ecm-ecm-1: POST /api/client-errors with valid JWT -> 204; /metrics -> ecm_client_errors_total{kind="boundary",release="smoke-v1"} 1.0; unauthenticated POST -> 401.

Deferred to follow-up (still on i6a1m acceptance list):

title: Validate normalization_group_ids against NormalizationRuleGroup at rule write-time
description:
Surfaced by bd-pf82j docs work. PUT /api/auto-creation/rules/{id} and POST /api/auto-creation/rules/bulk-update both accept normalization_group_ids as a JSON list, but neither verifies the IDs exist in normalization_rule_groups. A typo, deleted group, or copy-paste from the wrong env stores garbage; failure surfaces later as silent 'no normalization happened' with no clear error.

Scope:
- Add a Pydantic validator (or service-layer check) that queries normalization_rule_groups for submitted IDs and returns HTTP 422 with the offending IDs named if any are missing
- Apply to both PUT /rules/{id} and POST /rules/bulk-update paths
- Check for stale IDs in existing data first; if present, validate on write only (not on read) to avoid breaking already-stored rules
- Tests: valid IDs accepted, missing ID returns 422 with offending ID, mixed valid+invalid returns 422 listing all invalid

User value: clear error at write-time vs silent runtime failure. Better UX. Catches frontend bugs and copy-paste mistakes from other environments.

Effort: ~30-45 min plus stale-ID audit.

Related: bd-pf82j (api.md backfill that flagged this gap as 'doc states honestly that no validation occurs').
notes: PR #145 opened: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/145 — write-time FK validation for normalization_group_ids on PUT /rules/{id} and POST /rules/bulk-update. Branch: feat/bd-i75ax-normalization-group-ids-validation. Worktree: .claude/worktrees/agent-a2727f7a. Stale-data audit: 8 valid groups, 1 rule referencing them, 0 stale IDs — clean. Delta-on-write semantics chosen (only submitted IDs validated; stored values left alone for backward-compat). Backend tests: 3088 passed. 9 new tests added. Awaiting merge before close.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #145. Stale-data audit confirmed clean (0 stale references in production DB). Helper _validate_normalization_group_ids in backend/routers/auto_creation.py: single IN query, deduped, raises 422 with invalid_normalization_group_ids list. Wired into PUT /rules/{id} (between rule lookup and scalar apply — no partial mutations) and POST /rules/bulk-update (after fetch, before mutation loop — single 422 rolls back batch). Delta-on-write semantics chosen as defense-in-depth. POST /rules (create) explicitly out of scope; symmetric coverage tracked as bd-j5p4k. 9 new tests; 3088 backend tests pass.

title: Bug [CRITICAL]: stop_processing action breaks the whole Pass 2 loop — only 1 stream processed (gh #225)
description: GH #225 (reporter Koalamanx). auto_creation_engine.py Pass 2 (the 'for stream, winning_rule, losing_rules, stream_rules_log in sorted_entries' loop, ~line 1225): each iteration executes the winning rule's actions on one stream; if any action is STOP_PROCESSING, stop_processing=True; at end of the per-stream block (~line 1364-1365) 'if stop_processing: break' — this break exits the ENTIRE sorted_entries loop, so the first stream whose winning rule has a stop_processing action ends Pass 2 for all remaining streams → streams_matched=1 every run. CONFIRMED in code; reporter's diagnosis exact. Note: Pass 1 already resolves one winning rule per stream and has its own per-stream short-circuit (rule.stop_on_first_match → break the per-stream rule loop, ~line 1111). The STOP_PROCESSING *action* in Pass 2 has no remaining rules to stop (one winning rule per stream) — so its only legit meaning is 'don't keep processing THIS stream' i.e. continue to next stream, OR it's a Pass-1 concept mis-applied. Fix candidates: (a) break → continue; (b) drop the Pass-2 stop_processing handling entirely (treat STOP_PROCESSING as a Pass-1-only thing); needs a small design call on intended semantics, then a tiny code change + a regression test (rule with stop_processing action + 50 matching streams → all processed). Severity: P1 — niche feature (stop_processing action) silently broken when used; workaround: use rule.stop_on_first_match=True instead. Same reporter as #221-224, #226.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.16.0-0079 (PR #233) — Pass 2 stop_processing break→continue + regression test (12 streams). GH #225 commented & closed below.

title: Apply auto-rename when reordering channels by drag-drop
description:
Fixed auto-rename not applying when channels are reordered via drag-drop.

Changes:
1. Extracted auto-rename logic into reusable helper function 'computeAutoRename' that takes channel name, old number, and new number and returns the new name if a rename should apply.

2. Updated contiguous reorder path (when dragging channels in a sequential group):
   - Now computes auto-rename for each affected channel
   - Stages individual channel updates with both number and name changes
   - Updates local state with renamed names immediately

3. Updated non-contiguous swap path (when dragging channels in a non-sequential group):
   - Computes auto-rename for both swapped channels
   - Includes name changes in the staged updates
   - Updates local state with renamed names immediately

Example: If channels are '110 Fox News', '111 Fox Business', '112 Fox Weather' and you drag Fox News to after Fox Weather, they now become '110 Fox Business', '111 Fox Weather', '112 Fox News' with the numbers in the names updated.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Analyzed multi-select filter patterns in ChannelsPane and StreamsPane.

KEY DIFFERENCES that complicate extraction:
1. ChannelsPane: Works with ChannelGroup objects, selectedGroups is number[] (IDs)
   - Groups have additional data (channel counts, names)
   - Sorting logic includes selected items first
2. StreamsPane: Works with string[] group names directly
   - Simple string array, no object structure
   - Different sorting logic

DUPLICATION EXISTS but contexts differ:
- Search input + clear button (same)
- Select All / Clear All buttons (same logic, different data types)
- Checkbox list with filtering (same pattern, different item types)

ATTEMPTED SOLUTION:
Created useMultiSelectFilter hook with generic types to handle both:
- TItem: the source items (ChannelGroup[] or string[])
- TKey: the selected keys (number[] or string[])
- getKey/getSearchableText callbacks for flexibility

PROBLEM:
The hook becomes complex with generic types and callbacks, making it harder to understand than the inline implementations. The TypeScript types become convoluted (useMultiSelectFilter<ChannelGroup, number> vs useMultiSelectFilter<string, string>).

RECOMMENDATION:
The current inline implementations are ~40 lines each but are clear and easy to understand. A generic hook would save ~30 lines total but add cognitive overhead and indirection. Given that:
1. Only 2 use sites exist
2. Logic is straightforward
3. Contexts are meaningfully different
4. Hook would require complex generics

Suggest DEFERRING this extraction. The cost/benefit doesn't justify it compared to other P2 work.

STATUS: Hook file created but not integrated. Can delete if we agree extraction isn't worth it.
