title: Write docs/runbooks/frontend_error_rate.md — stub runbook for ECMClientErrorRateElevated + ECMClientErrorRateCritical alerts
description:
The two Prometheus alert rules landed via i6a1m (PR #173) in docs/sre/prometheus_rules.yaml reference docs/runbooks/frontend_error_rate.md in their annotations, but the runbook doesn't exist yet. Operators who page on these alerts land on a 404.

Style to match: docs/runbooks/http_error_rate.md (same family — rate-based alert with ticket + page thresholds).

Required sections (minimum):
1. **What it fires on** — definition of ecm_client_errors_total and the two alert predicates (rate5m > X for Y minutes, severity: ticket vs page)
2. **Initial triage** — which error `kind` label is dominant right now (boundary, preload, bootstrap, unhandled, resource)? Check /metrics.
3. **Common causes by kind** — boundary: React render bug; preload: bundle/chunk 404 (deploy cache); bootstrap: index.html inline script caught something before the SPA boot; unhandled: promise rejection regression; resource: static asset 404
4. **Mitigation patterns** — rollback to previous image, force-clear CDN cache (cross-ref infra-cache-invalidation.md), temporary settings toggle: telemetry_client_errors_enabled=false (suppresses noise if it's an alert storm, NOT a fix)
5. **Severity promotion** — when to page vs ticket
6. **Postmortem hook** — link to docs/runbooks/postmortem-template.md if one exists

Acceptance:
- File exists at docs/runbooks/frontend_error_rate.md
- Both alert rules' runbook annotation resolves
- Reverse-link added from docs/sre/slos.md SLO-6 entry
- Style consistent with http_error_rate.md

LABELS: observability, runbook, sre, frontend
close_reason: Merged via PR #177 (merge commit 8a89b434be124c0e3ffda653a7c2abfe0050c05a). Runbook ships at docs/runbooks/frontend_error_rate.md; SLO-6 reverse-link added; alert annotations verified.

title: Runbook: dep-bump frontend regression (React/Vite/TS rollback + browser cache)
description:
Blocks PR5 of v0.16.0 dep-bump epic (React 19 ecosystem — in620). SRE spike (2026-04-24) flagged this as P1 prerequisite — React 19 introduces stricter hydration + string-ref removal; Vite 8 changes chunking behavior. No frontend error telemetry exists (no RUM), so regressions surface as user reports.

Scope:
- Detection: what operator observes (blank page, hydration console errors, chunk load failures, broken drag-reorder). GitHub Issues is the primary channel given no RUM.
- Triage: which bump is responsible (React vs Vite vs TS vs @dnd-kit). Decision tree.
- Rollback: git revert merge commit; rebuild frontend bundle; verify /app/static/assets cleaned before docker cp (per CLAUDE.md); operator-side browser cache invalidation (hard reload, service-worker flush, asset hash verification).
- Browser-cache guidance: why stale chunks cause symptoms even after server-side rollback; how to confirm the user is hitting the rolled-back bundle.
- Post-rollback: bead creation, package.json pin back, re-cut process.

Style/template: follow docs/runbooks/v0.16.0-rollback.md + docs/runbooks/dep-bump-smoke-test.md conventions. Link from docs/runbooks/README.md.

Acceptance:
- File at docs/runbooks/dep-bump-frontend-regression.md
- Linked from docs/runbooks/README.md index
- PR merged to dev
close_reason: Merged via PR #164 — docs/runbooks/dep-bump-frontend-regression.md shipped. Includes DevTools decision tree (React 19 / Vite 8 / TS 6 / @dnd-kit), Mode A/B rollback, browser-cache invalidation ladder, asset-hash verification, and reverse-links to infra-cache-invalidation runbook (11t6c).

title: Bug: MCP list_auto_creation_rules raises 'str object has no attribute get' (gh #222)
description: GH #222 (reporter Koalamanx). The list_auto_creation_rules MCP tool (mcp-server/tools/auto_creation.py:79-89) does 'rules = await client.get("/api/auto-creation/rules")' then 'for r in rules: r.get(...)'. But the backend endpoint returns {"rules": [...]} (backend/routers/auto_creation.py:315), so 'rules' is a dict, 'for r in rules' iterates its keys → r = 'rules' (str) → r.get() → AttributeError. CONFIRMED. Fix: 'rules = (await client.get(...)).get("rules", [])' (the newer /analyze tool already does .get('rules') correctly). Trivial. Severity: P2 — tool is fully broken; workaround: iterate get_auto_creation_rule(rule_id=N).
close_reason: Fixed in v0.16.0-0079 (PR #233) — MCP list_auto_creation_rules unwraps {"rules":[...]}. GH #222.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: GitHub #61: convert_superscripts() in normalization_engine.normalize() unconditionally strips Unicode superscript chars (e.g. ᴴᴰ→HD). This is correct for matching/lookups, but when normalization is applied to the output channel name in _execute_create_channel, it destroys superscripts the user intentionally placed via regex (e.g. ESPN²→ESPN2). Fix: add a preserve_superscripts flag to normalize() so the output path skips convert_superscripts() while matching paths still use it.

title: security: execute bulk dismissal of 12 CodeQL py/path-injection FPs + 1 test-only
description:
Audit-trail bead for the bulk dismissal of 13 CodeQL alerts executed against dev.

Alerts dismissed as 'false positive' (12):
- 1416, 1417, 1418, 1419 (backup.py path-injection — sanitizer _BACKUP_FILENAME_RE + Path.resolve().relative_to containment)
- 1461, 1462 (backup.py — same sanitizer, post-#98 CodeQL re-analysis alert IDs)
- 1354, 1355, 1356, 1357, 1358, 1359 (export.py / export_manager.py path-injection — FastAPI int path-parameter gate on profile_id + Pydantic FILENAME_RE on filename_prefix)

Alert dismissed as 'used in tests' (1):
- 1404 (frontend/src/components/autoCreation/ActionEditor.test.tsx line 237 — unanchored regex /example\.com/ is a React Testing Library getByPlaceholderText matcher, not a runtime URL validator; Vitest test file, not bundled into production frontend)

Per-alert dismissal comments (paste-ready) are attached to each alert via the gh api dismissal operation.

Sanitizer evidence:
- backup.py: _BACKUP_FILENAME_RE (strict filename allowlist) at backend/routers/backup.py:1140, then Path.resolve().relative_to(BACKUPS_DIR.resolve()) for filesystem containment. Tests in backend/tests/routers/test_backup.py::TestSavedBackupsPathInjection.
- export.py: profile_id is FastAPI int path parameter (HTTP 422 at routing for non-int); prefix is PlaylistProfile.filename_prefix, gated by Pydantic FILENAME_RE = ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ at export.py:38-63. Tests in backend/tests/routers/test_export.py (test_invalid_filename_prefix_returns_422 line 123, test_filename_prefix_allows_hyphens_underscores line 429).

NOT dismissed (out of scope for this bead):
- 1360 (print-guide XSS — requires regression test first; see Bead B)
- 1361, 1362 (OneDrive SSRF — requires code fix; see Bead A)

Acceptance criteria:
- All 13 dismissals succeed via gh api PATCH state=dismissed
- Post-verification: dev HIGH/CRITICAL open count = 3 (1360, 1361, 1362 only)
- This bead closed with notes logging the 13 dismissal operations + final open-alert count

Reference: triage report (earlier session, security-engineer persona), ADR-005 Phase 1 CodeQL policy, PR #98 (backup.py remediation prior).
close_reason:
COMPLETED. 13/13 dismissal operations successful.

Dismissed as 'false positive' (12): 1416, 1417, 1418, 1419 (backup.py), 1461, 1462 (backup.py post-PR#98 alert IDs), 1354, 1355, 1356, 1357, 1358, 1359 (export.py/export_manager.py).
Dismissed as 'used in tests' (1): 1404 (ActionEditor.test.tsx Vitest-only).

Post-verification (security_severity_level=high/critical filter):

title: Triage react-hooks/set-state-in-effect (69 sites) - eslint 7.1 - bd-me2lt followup
description: Spun out of bd-me2lt. 69 violations across ~40 files when react-hooks/set-state-in-effect is re-enabled. Mix of: (a) async-fetch-on-mount sites where setState fires in .then() callback - per docs/frontend_lint.md these get inline-disable with reason 'async setState (inside .then), not synchronous'; (b) genuine synchronous setState-in-effect that should be derived state or removed effects (LoginPage:43, ResetPasswordPage:33, FindDuplicatesModal:30 likely candidates); (c) modal isOpen/prop-sync patterns documented as inner/outer-wrapper refactor (DeleteOrphanedGroupsModal/BulkLCNFetchModal pattern). Approach: decide per file whether to inline-disable (treat as documented exception) or refactor. If the inline-disable count exceeds ~10-15 across the codebase, policy rule #4 favors keeping the rule off and documenting rationale. Currently 'react-hooks/set-state-in-effect': 'off' in frontend/eslint.config.mjs. Top sites: App.tsx(4), ChannelsPane.tsx(5), StreamsPane.tsx(7), SettingsTab.tsx(2), various modals/editors/panels (~50 single-site files).

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: Closed 2026-04-25 as won't-fix-without-evidence-of-harm per PO value-gate review. Rule violations are a static-analysis concern (React Compiler optimization preference), not a correctness or user-impact issue. App ships, 1177 tests pass, users unaffected. Cost of fix (refactor + verification + regression risk) outweighs speculative benefit. Rule remains disabled in frontend/eslint.config.mjs with rationale. Reopen if (a) actual perf issue traced to this pattern, or (b) future React/Compiler bump forces the fix.

title: STRIDE threat model: DBAS import/restore endpoint (gb5r5.3)
description:
**Why:** bd-gb5r5.3 restores ZIPs containing credentials (M3U auth, user agents), users table, plugins, settings — each a distinct attack surface. Current bead lacks any security analysis. Security flagged this as privilege-escalation + RCE surface during grooming 2026-04-20.

**Scope — STRIDE analysis of:**
- ZIP upload path (Spoofing archive origin, Tampering with entries, Info disclosure via extracted secrets)
- ZIP extraction (Zip Slip path traversal, symlink escape, zip bombs, entry-count DoS)
- User-table restore (privilege escalation — attacker-supplied archive grants admin)
- Plugin restore (RCE if plugins are executable code; PO clarification needed — are plugins code or config?)
- M3U/API-token restore (credential exfiltration; secrets-in-logs during dry-run)
- Endpoint authz (admin-only? separate 'restore' permission?)
- Audit logging (who restored what, when, per-category counts)

**Deliverables:**
- docs/security/threat_model_dbas_import.md — STRIDE table, hardening decisions, test cases
- Security acceptance criteria added to bd-gb5r5.3 description:
  - Zip Slip hardening (path normalization, symlink rejection, size+ratio+entry-count caps)
  - Admin-only endpoint + opt-in flag for users/plugins categories
  - Current authenticated admin account NEVER overwritten by restore
  - Secrets redacted in all logs including dry-run
  - Full audit log entry per category with request ID
  - Archive integrity check (SHA-256 manifest)
  - Dry-run guaranteed side-effect free (tempdir cleanup verified)

**Out of scope:** implementation (engineer's work); DBAS sync (gb5r5.4) has its own threat model once instance trust questions resolved via ADR-004.
notes: Threat model authored: docs/security/threat_model_dbas_import.md (50 STRIDE rows, 17-item hardening checklist, 6 PO assumptions flagged). Hardening checklist cross-filed on bd-gb5r5.3 notes. CLAUDE.md Reference Guides table updated. PR: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/79 (doc-only, no CI checks run). Bead stays open — PO closes after PR merges.

description:
Added granular debug logging for CPU diagnostics:

1. Request Timing Middleware:
   - Logs every API request with duration in milliseconds
   - Tracks request rate per endpoint over 10-second windows

   - Logs slow requests (>1000ms) at INFO level

2. New Diagnostic Endpoint:
   - GET /api/debug/request-rates - Shows request rate stats for all endpoints
   - Helps identify which endpoints are causing CPU load

3. Enhanced Endpoint Logging:
   - [CHANNELS] - Logs fetch timing, result counts, pagination info
   - [STREAMS] - Logs cache hits/misses, fetch timing, data sizes
   - [TASKS] - Logs task listing duration, running task IDs

Usage to diagnose CPU issues:
1. Enable DEBUG: curl -X POST http://localhost:5001/api/settings -d '{"backend_log_level":"DEBUG"}'
2. Watch logs: docker logs -f <container>
3. Check rates: curl http://localhost:5001/api/debug/request-rates

Changes to backend/main.py only.

title: Fix E! channel matching to E!Entertainment

comments.author: lecaptainc
comments.text:
Fixed E! channel matching to prefer E!EntertainmentTelevisionHD(EHD).us over E.us.

Root cause: 'E!' normalizes to just 'e', and 'E.us' was an exact match for 'e'. The EPG entry 'E!EntertainmentTelevisionHD(EHD).us' normalizes to 'eentertainmenttelevisionhd' which would only match via prefix matching, but prefix matching was skipped when exact matches existed.

Two-part fix:
1. For very short normalized names (1-2 chars), also try prefix matching even when exact matches exist - single letters are too generic to rely on exact matching alone
2. For short names, don't prioritize exact matches over prefix matches in sorting
3. Added special punctuation matching - prefer EPG entries that share special chars like '!' with the original channel name

Now 'E!' will match 'E!Entertainment' because:
- Both 'E.us' (exact) and 'E!EntertainmentTelevisionHD' (prefix) are found
- The '!' in the channel name prefers the EPG entry with '!' in its TVG-ID

Version: 0.2.30060

title: Auto-creation: separate-not-merge option for channels already in another group (GH-92)
description:
GitHub issue #92 (langtd19), filed 2026-04-21. User impact: High.

## Problem
When two auto-creation rules target different channel groups (e.g., rule A targets SPORTS, rule B targets ESPN), running rule B merges channels into the rule A group if channel names already exist there, rather than creating new channels in the rule B target group.

## User workaround (brittle)
Rename channel groups with unicode suffixes to force differentiation. Works for most cases, breaks for some regex/odd-naming cases.

## Suggested implementation
Add an option to scope the existing-channel check to the target channel group only, so a channel with the same name in a different group triggers a new-channel creation rather than a merge.

## Acceptance direction (TBD by engineer)
- New option on rule (or global) restricting duplicate-check to target group scope
- Test: two rules, same channel name, different target groups → two separate channels each in correct group
- Backwards-compatible default (opt-in)

## External ref
https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/issues/92

title: Order streams by quality AND provider for failover
description:
Enhanced sortStreamsByQuality() to order streams by quality AND provider for failover redundancy.

Algorithm:
1. Group streams by quality tier (UHD/4K → FHD/1080p → HD/720p → SD/480p)
2. Within each quality tier, group by provider (m3u_account)
3. Interleave streams round-robin style across providers

Example ordering:
- Provider 1 ESPN FHD
- Provider 2 ESPN FHD
- Provider 1 ESPN HD
- Provider 2 ESPN HD

This ensures that if a provider fails, the next stream in the failover chain is from a different provider at the same quality level.

Files modified:
- frontend/src/services/api.ts - Updated sortStreamsByQuality function

title: Fix A-Z sorting clearing channel names
description: When sorting and renumbering channels, if a channel name doesn't match the auto-rename pattern, computeAutoRename returns undefined. The condition newName \!== channel.name was true when newName was undefined, causing updates.name to be set to undefined and clearing the channel name.
notes: Fixed: Changed condition from 'if (newName \!== channel.name)' to 'if (newName && newName \!== channel.name)' in handleSortRenumberConfirm at line 3187. When computeAutoRename returns undefined (no pattern match), the old condition evaluated to true (undefined \!== 'SomeName') and set updates.name = undefined, clearing the channel name.

title: Add ?batch_id= query parameter to GET /api/journal for bulk-operation forensics
description:
Surfaced by bd-pf82j docs work. The journal API currently filters via 'search' only (ILIKE on entity_name/description). After bd-91mcq, batch_id is the documented correlation primitive — every bulk auto-creation operation writes N rows sharing one 8-char batch_id. Without an API filter, integrators (and the frontend) must hit the DB directly to answer 'what did this batch change?'

Scope:
- Add 'batch_id: str | None = Query(None, description="Filter to a single bulk operation\'s journal rows")' to backend/routers/journal.py GET handler
- Conditionally '.filter(JournalEntry.batch_id == batch_id)' in the query
- Tests: 200 with batch_id present (returns N rows), 200 with batch_id absent (existing behavior unchanged), invalid batch_id format rejected at parameter level
- Update docs/api.md GET /api/journal section to document the new param

Surfaces the idx_journal_batch_id index shipped in bd-dmu8w (currently unreachable via API).

User value: bulk-operation forensics queryable via API; UI can show 'what did this batch change?' without DB shell access.

Effort: ~30 min. Pure additive; no breaking change.

Related: bd-pf82j (api.md backfill that flagged this gap), bd-91mcq (introduced batch_id), bd-dmu8w (added the supporting index).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #143. backend/routers/journal.py + backend/journal.py: added Optional[str] batch_id Query parameter; conditional JournalEntry.batch_id == batch_id filter (hits idx_journal_batch_id from bd-dmu8w). 4 new tests in test_journal.py covering matching/empty/no-param-regression/composes-with-search. docs/api.md updated: bd-pf82j 'future bead' caveat replaced with first-class param documentation. 3083 backend tests pass.

title: CI gap: alembic-dependent tests silently skip if requirements.txt not installed pre-test
description: Surfaced during bd-dmu8w work. The agent had to pip install alembic in the worktree to run alembic-dependent tests because uv refused without a venv. tests/unit/test_alembic_baseline.py and the new bd-dmu8w migration tests would silently skip in CI if requirements.txt is not installed before pytest runs. Verify the CI workflow installs backend requirements.txt (or equivalent) before invoking pytest. Additionally, configure pytest to FAIL (not skip) on missing required deps for tests that should always run — silent skips are how regressions land green. Audit other test files for similar 'skip on import error' patterns that might be hiding test-coverage gaps.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #144. Investigation confirmed install step already exists in test.yml; real gap was downstream importorskip masking. Fixed: removed pytest.importorskip and try/except ImportError for hypothesis in test_regex_lint_property.py and test_dummy_epg_engine.py. Added backend/scripts/verify_test_deps.py fast-fail guard for alembic/hypothesis/pytest/pytest_asyncio/httpx; wired into test.yml between install and pytest. Audit found 3 legitimate skip patterns (E2E container reachability, SPA-vs-API runtime, OS symlink capability) — left untouched. Verified in fresh venv: with deps -> 3022 backend tests pass; without hypothesis -> verify_test_deps.py exits 1 with FATAL message; pytest collection errors instead of silent skip. bd-mzm3j (closed earlier as dup) effectively resolved by this.

title: Testing: Add unit tests for compound condition matching

title: Unicode confusables/skeleton normalization (homoglyph handling)
description:
Backlog — not part of epic bd-eio04. Filed as follow-up after grooming session 2026-04-22.

NFC/NFKC do NOT fold Cyrillic 'а' (U+0430) with Latin 'a' (U+0061) — they look identical but remain distinct code points. Users experiencing rule-match failures across alphabets need Unicode TR39 skeleton/confusables treatment.

Defer until a real user scenario surfaces (currently speculative per security persona). If filed:
- Use a confusables library (e.g., unicode-confusables pkg) or Unicode TR39 skeleton mapping.
- Apply after NFC normalization (bd-eio04.4).
- Opt-in via NormalizationPolicy flag, not default-on (it's aggressive).
- Scope fixtures: Cyrillic/Latin, Greek/Latin, Latin digit/letter confusables (O/0, I/1/l).

title: ADR-005 audit cadence: extend to PO-downgrade detection + allow_force_pushes flip events
description:
Extend ADR-005's monthly/quarterly dismissal-log audit cadence (per ADR-005 Dismiss-With-Comment Policy item 6) to absorb two new audit scopes surfaced by ADR-004 §Interaction with ADR-005:

## Scope 1: PO-downgrade detection (G1a escape-hatch surveillance)

Surface any release-cut PR where a P1 bug was downgraded to P2 (or lower) by the PO within the 30 days prior to the cut SHA. Catches the authorized-but-risky G1a bypass pattern that ADR-004's 'or each justified in PR' escape-hatch permits — a downgrade IS authorized, but a recurring pattern of downgrade-immediately-before-cut is a signal that the gate is being culturally weakened.

Implementation hint: query 'bd' history for status/priority transitions on each P1-bound bug, cross-reference with the cut SHA timestamp on the release-cut PR. May require a small bd-history shell helper if the audit becomes more than ad-hoc.

## Scope 2: allow_force_pushes flip-event audit

Periodically review the GitHub repo audit log for 'allow_force_pushes' flip events on the 'main' branch protection rule (per ADR-004 §Interaction with ADR-005 item 3). Every legitimate flip corresponds to an authorized rollback bead (e.g., the v0.16.0 rollback, bd-vgm4l); anything else is investigated. Cadence: fold into the existing monthly-then-quarterly dismissal audit so we don't add a separate cadence to maintain.

Implementation hint: 'gh api /repos/:owner/:repo/audit-log' or the GitHub Settings → Audit log UI. Filter on action='protected_branch.update' and inspect the diff for allow_force_pushes toggling. Cross-reference each true → false (and false → true) flip with an authorized rollback bead; flag any flips lacking a corresponding bead.

## Rationale

ADR-004 created two new operational surfaces that ADR-005's existing audit cadence does not currently cover. Rather than file a separate audit cadence, extend the existing one — same persona (Security Engineer per ADR-005, with SRE coordination per ADR-004), same cadence (monthly for the first quarter post-Phase-3, then quarterly), same artifact (audit bead per cycle).

## Acceptance

- [ ] Monthly audit checklist (per ADR-005 item 6) extended to include both new scopes.
- [ ] First audit cycle after this bead lands includes both scopes; findings filed as separate beads if any.
- [ ] If 'gh api' audit log access requires an org-admin PAT not currently provisioned, file a sub-bead to obtain it before the next audit cycle.

## References

- ADR-004 §Interaction with ADR-005 (items 3 and 4)
- ADR-005 §Dismiss-With-Comment Policy item 6 (existing audit cadence)
- bd-tvlxz item 4 (this bead's parent task)
- bd-kgjci (ADR-005 implementation; Phase 5 calls for first monthly audit bead)

title: Stats v2 initiative (v0.17.0): Users panel + session_telemetry foundation
description:
Parent tracker for Stats v2 (v0.17.0 scope). Consolidates **both** GH-62 (user watch-time stats) and GH-59 (per-provider stream stats) into a single cohesive "Stats v2 upgrade" release, backed by the unified session_telemetry data foundation.

## Scope decision history

- **2026-04-21 team-plan consensus**: unanimous AMBER, "yes but smaller" — GH-59 deferred to v0.18.0+, GH-62 committed to v0.17.0 via skqln.5 + skqln.6.
- **2026-04-23 grooming (bd show for details)**: team unanimously recommended keeping GH-59 deferred (6/6 personas). **PO override: pull GH-59 into v0.17.0.** Reason: "Statistics feature — Stats v2 ships as one cohesive stats upgrade release." Override acknowledged; release scope expanded from 12 beads → 17 beads (+42%).

## v0.17.0 scope (as of PO override 2026-04-23)

Chosen pattern: **Package A + DBA's single-write approach** (not dual-write, not defer). Rationale: honors DBA's data-integrity concern while landing the forward investment the architect wants.

## User-visible outcome

Two new Stats tab panels:
- **Users panel** (5th panel, GH-62): personal/admin view of watch-time-by-user with daily trend chart, channel table (total minutes, last watched), and 7/30/90-day selector.
- **Providers panel** (6th panel, GH-59): operator view of provider performance with 4 visualizations — buffering events by provider (time-series), time spent per provider (stacked area), channels-by-provider heatmap, bitrate by provider (time-series). Operator uses this to make data-driven provider keep/drop decisions at renewal.

No sub-nav refactor — users still land on Stats tab normally; both new panels sit alongside Overview/Channels/Popularity/History as flat stacked sections. Stats tab pressure toward sub-nav IA redesign is acknowledged and deferred to v0.18.0.

## What ships (17 child beads)

**Foundation (shared):**
1. skqln.1 — ADR-007: retention/rollup policy (must land BEFORE first write). Amendment: retention window now considers per-provider quarterly trend analysis per PO override.
2. skqln.2 — Schema + Alembic migration for session_telemetry. Amendment: add `(provider_id, channel_id, observed_at) INCLUDE (bytes_delta)` covering index for GH-59 heatmap query.
3. skqln.3 — BandwidthTracker single-write refactor + channel_watch_stats_v compat view. Now the backbone for skqln.14 (provider resolver) and skqln.15 (buffer-event ingest).
4. skqln.7 — Privacy 11a: pre-implementation threat model. Amendment: extend data classification to cover provider attribution (potential provider-relationship PII surface).
5. skqln.8 — Privacy 11b: post-implementation privacy sign-off.

**GH-62 impl (Users panel):**
6. skqln.5 — GH-62 backend watch-time-by-user read API.
7. skqln.6 — GH-62 frontend Users panel.

**GH-59 impl (Providers panel) — NEW, added 2026-04-23 via PO override:**
8. skqln.14 — Active-stream → provider resolver in BandwidthTracker (the "schedule-killer" the grooming team flagged).
9. skqln.15 — Buffer-event ingestion: wire Dispatcharr system events into session_telemetry.buffer_event_count.
10. skqln.16 — GH-59 backend: 4 provider-stats read API endpoints.
11. skqln.17 — Chart primitives: heatmap component + colorblind-safe categorical palette.
12. skqln.18 — GH-59 frontend Providers panel (6th panel in Stats tab, 4 visualizations).

**Quality gates:**
13. skqln.10 — Perf baseline + CI benchmark gate. Amendment: gate expands from 1 hot query to 5 queries (GH-62 watch-time + 4 GH-59 provider aggregates).
14. skqln.11 — SLOs, storage-growth alerts, deployment-safety runbook. Amendment: runbook entry for NULL provider_id cascading into "Unknown" provider bucket; per-provider SLIs on read latency.
15. skqln.12 — Observability instrumentation. Amendment: `provider_id` confirmed safe as Prometheus label (<20 providers bounded per SRE pre-clearance); add data-consistency SLI for provider resolution rate.
16. skqln.9 — Stats v2 user guide entry + metric glossary. Scope extends to cover both Users and Providers panels.
17. (Reserved — add if scope adjustment needed.)

## What's still NOT in v0.17.0 (kept deferred)

- Sub-nav refactor for Stats tab (IA redesign; pressure is now higher with 6 panels, accept for v0.17.0).
- Cross-feature Stats IA redesign.

## Key design decisions (from team-plan + PO override)

- **Write pattern**: single-write to session_telemetry; legacy channel_watch_stats exposed via VIEW channel_watch_stats_v for GH-62 read compat. (Not dual-write — DBA argued and won: two write paths would silently drift.)
- **Provider tagging**: BandwidthTracker gains active-stream → provider resolver (skqln.14). NULL provider_id surfaces as "Unknown" bucket in Providers panel; not silently excluded.
- **Buffer event ingestion**: new polling path folded into `_collect_stats` (skqln.15), with idempotency/dedup on event_id or (channel_id, client_id, event_ts).
- **Retention**: keep raw 30 days, rollups forever. ADR-007 scope re-examined for quarterly trend suitability.
- **Cardinality discipline**: user_id and channel_id NEVER become Prometheus metric labels (SRE veto). provider_id IS allowed as a label (bounded <20 providers).
- **Testing**: property-based consistency tests between session_telemetry and legacy tables during cutover; pytest-benchmark gate on 5 hot queries; Playwright E2E on Stats tab if and only if sub-nav ships (not in v0.17.0, so not needed).

## User-facing trackers

- hl2fm (GH-62 User stats) — closes when skqln.5 + skqln.6 ship.
- 39ao6 (GH-59 Per-provider stats) — closes when skqln.16 + skqln.18 ship.

## Risk acceptance (PO override 2026-04-23)

The grooming team unanimously recommended deferring GH-59. The PO overrode on release-cohesion grounds. Risks the team identified and the PO has accepted:

1. **Engineer**: skqln.3 BandwidthTracker refactor becomes 1.5×–2× harder with provider resolver + buffer-event ingest piled on.
2. **DBA**: 30-day empty-panel problem — Providers panel will show limited data until session_telemetry has accumulated 30d+ of history post-deploy. Backfill into channel_watch_stats' provider-free history is impossible. Operators should expect "useful in 30d, fully useful in 90d" messaging.
3. **SRE**: ADR-007 retention policy now pressured to commit to longer raw window or explicit rollup-with-provider-dimension before skqln.1 closes.
4. **UX**: Stats tab IA pressure — 6 stacked panels in a single release degrades Users panel discoverability in its own launch release; sub-nav refactor debt grows.
5. **QA**: test matrix expands 2D → 3D (user + provider consistency); 5M-row fixture rewrites for provider cardinality; benchmark gate 1 → 5 queries.
6. **PM**: commitment-stability reputational cost — reopening unanimous team-plan AMBER 48h after it was made. Mitigation: override is documented here so future decisions can reference the trail.

## Reference

- Team-plan session 2026-04-21 (12 personas, unanimous AMBER).
- Grooming session 2026-04-23 (6/6 Stay Deferred).
- PO override 2026-04-23: full pull-in, release-cohesion reason.

title: ADR-007: Retention & rollup policy for session_telemetry
description:
Architectural decision record defining the retention, rollup, and data-lifecycle policy for the new session_telemetry table.

## Why this must land BEFORE first write
Architect and DBA both asserted this as a hard prerequisite during the 2026-04-21 team-plan. Without a retention policy in place, session_telemetry accumulates unboundedly — DBA projected ~13–26M rows over 3–6 months (~1.5–3GB with indexes) at current load, which is workable for SQLite only with disciplined retention + rollups.

## Decisions the ADR must capture
- Raw retention window (DBA proposed 30 days; SRE wants 30d full + 90d downsampled to 5-min buckets; architect leans 180d)
- Rollup schedule (nightly cron vs. on-demand view)
- Rollup tables vs. views (DBA strong pref for tables — views re-scan 26M rows)
- Per-user watch_time_by_user_daily PK and shape
- Per-provider provider_performance_daily PK and shape
- Pruning job (who runs it, alerting if it falls behind, failure modes)
- When Postgres trigger fires (DBA: >50M rows or multi-writer, not v0.17.0)

## Authorship
SRE owns retention window justification (operational cost + query perf at horizon); Architect owns schema-lifecycle placement; DBA owns enforcement mechanism; Tech Writer owns the ADR write-up consistency and cross-linking to database_migrations.md.

## Blocks
- BandwidthTracker single-write refactor (child bead, TBD) — cannot land before ADR-007

## Acceptance
- ADR file lands at docs/adr/ADR-007-session-telemetry-retention.md
- Format matches ADR-001/004/005 precedent
- PO-accepted with all Open Questions resolved inline

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: 2026-04-23: Scope amended for PO override pulling GH-59 into v0.17.0. Retention policy must now consider per-provider quarterly trend analysis, not only per-user 7/30/90d use case. Evaluate whether 30d raw + rollups-forever still satisfies 'useful provider keep/drop decision over quarterly window' — may need longer raw retention or rollups with provider_id dimension. See skqln epic body for full override trail.

title: Stats v2: performance baseline + CI benchmark gate for hot aggregation query
description:
Performance baseline + automated CI regression gate on the hot watch-time aggregation query. QA + DBA both argued (and won) during team-plan that this is non-negotiable for v0.17.0 — 'cut to 1 day if sprint squeezes, do not cut to zero.'

## Scope
- Seeded 6-month synthetic dataset (deterministic seed) committed as a test fixture — generator script that produces realistic session counts (e.g., ~50 users × 10 sessions/day × 180 days = ~90k rows)
- pytest-benchmark gate in CI on the three hottest aggregation queries (watch_time_by_user_daily, per-user breakdown, date-range filter)
- p95 SLO per query: < 200ms at the 6-month data shape
- Fail CI if p95 regresses by > 20% vs. baseline

## Why not skip this
DBA: 26M-row scale is where aggregation on unindexed columns degrades. Without a CI gate, we discover in prod.
QA: 6-month degradation is a product-trust bug; cheaper to baseline now than chase later.
PM pressure to slip to v0.17.1 should be resisted (surfaced in team-plan).

## Blocked by
- child .5 (GH-62 backend read API) — queries to benchmark must exist
- (implicit) schema + indexes from child .2

## Blocks
- GH-62 release (QA hard ask)

## Acceptance
- Synthetic fixture generator script at backend/tests/fixtures/
- pytest-benchmark config + 3 benchmark tests at backend/tests/performance/
- CI pipeline runs benchmarks and fails on >20% regression
- Baseline numbers documented in test file docstring

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: 2026-04-23: Benchmark gate expanded for PO override pulling GH-59 into v0.17.0. Previous scope: 1 hot query (GH-62 watch-time). New scope: 5 hot queries (GH-62 + 4 GH-59 provider aggregates). Target: p95 < 800ms per endpoint against 5M-row seeded fixture. Fixture must include realistic multi-provider distribution (Zipfian, not uniform) to catch 80/20 skew bugs QA flagged. NULL-provider rows must appear in benchmark fixture (~5% default) to exercise 'Unknown' bucket path.

title: Stats v2: SLOs, storage-growth alerts, and deployment-safety runbook
description:
SRE deliverables for Stats v2 operational readiness. Lands WITH the feature, not after (SRE hard ask from team-plan).

## SLOs (SRE starting point from team-plan)
- Stats tab availability: 99.5% (internal admin tool — not 99.9% revenue-path). ~3.6h/month error budget.
- /api/stats/watch-time p95 < 800ms, p99 < 2s
- Stats tab initial render p95 < 1.5s (API + chart paint)
- Dual-consistency SLI: session_telemetry row count vs. BandwidthTracker session count — alert if divergence > 0.5% over 1h during the transition window (when legacy writes are still on)

## Storage-growth alerts
- P3 warning at 60% of projected 12-month capacity (ticket, plan retention)
- P2 alert at 80% (page during business hours, execute ADR-007 retention)
- P1 alert at 95% (page immediately)
- Pages go to on-call SRE rotation (doesn't exist yet; page routes to curt@lecaptain.org until rotation exists — DOCUMENT this explicitly per SRE)

## Deployment safety
- Feature flag for BandwidthTracker writer (stats_v2_writer_enabled, default off, flip per-env) — matches Architect's refinement
- Frontend feature flag for Users panel (stats_v2_users_enabled, default off, flip per-env)
- Rollback path: docker cp of prior dist/ for frontend; container restart for backend. Keep last 3 bundles on disk.
- Post-deploy smoke test: hit Overview + Channels + Users endpoints, assert 200 + non-empty payload, within 60s of container start
- Update docs/runbooks/ with Stats v2-specific entries (storage-at-capacity, dual-consistency-alert, feature-flag-toggle)

## Cardinality veto (SRE block at review)
- user_id and channel_id MUST NOT become Prometheus metric labels — value in response only
- provider label OK (bounded <20 providers expected)
- buffering event counts go in histograms, not as labels

## Blocked by
- child .3 (BandwidthTracker refactor) — writer must exist to set SLO targets
- (parallel with) child .11 (observability instrumentation) — metrics from instrumentation feed the alerts

## Blocks
- GH-62 release (SRE hard ask: instrumentation lands with feature)

## Acceptance
- SLO doc lands (likely at docs/operations/ or as a runbook entry)
- Prometheus alert rules land in config or wherever alert rules live (TBD)
- Runbook entries for the 3 Stats v2 alert classes
- Deployment runbook updated with Stats v2 smoke test

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: 2026-04-23: SRE scope expanded for PO override pulling GH-59 into v0.17.0. Add per-provider SLIs on read latency (5 endpoints now — GH-62 + 4 GH-59 aggregates). Runbook must cover: (1) NULL provider_id cascading into 'Unknown' bucket — operator-visible degradation signal, not silent failure; (2) Post-deploy behavior — Providers panel shows limited data until session_telemetry accumulates 30d+ history; 'useful in 30d, fully useful in 90d' messaging required in release notes. Alerting scope: dashboards-only for v0.17.0 (per PO grooming Q&A — per-provider buffering alerts NOT in scope).

title: Stats v2: observability instrumentation (metrics, logs, data-consistency SLI)
description:
Instrumentation for Stats v2 — metrics, logs, and the dual-write divergence SLI measurement during the transition window. Implementation counterpart to child .10 (which defines SLOs and alert rules against these metrics).

## Metrics to emit (SRE spec from team-plan, bounded cardinality)

- ecm_stats_query_duration_seconds{endpoint, granularity} — histogram. Labels bounded (~5 endpoints × 3 granularities = ~15 series per bucket)
- ecm_session_telemetry_writes_total{result=success|failure} — counter
- ecm_session_telemetry_write_duration_seconds — histogram
- ecm_bandwidth_tracker_dual_write_divergence — gauge (during transition window; computed by background reconciler)
- ecm_stats_ingest_queue_depth — gauge (if any async ingest path exists)
- ecm_session_telemetry_row_count — gauge (for storage-growth alerts)
- Each rollup table: _last_refresh_seconds_ago gauge (DBA pushback: 'stale refreshes are how GH-59 silently lies to users')

## Logs
- Every session_telemetry write failure: WARN with trace_id, session_id, provider_id
- DO NOT log per-buffering-event — that's metric territory, not log
- Log redaction rules from Privacy 11a apply (no user_id+channel_id pairs leak)

## Data-consistency SLI (transition window)
- Background reconciler runs every N minutes during dual-read feature-flag window
- Compares: SUM(session_telemetry.bytes_delta WHERE channel=X) vs. channel_watch_stats.total_bytes WHERE channel=X for a sample window
- Emits ecm_bandwidth_tracker_dual_write_divergence gauge
- Alert fires if divergence > 0.5% over 1h (per child .10)

## Blocked by
- child .3 (BandwidthTracker refactor) — writer must exist to instrument
- child .5 (GH-62 backend API) — endpoint must exist to time
- child .1 (ADR-007) — indirectly, since rollup tables being monitored depend on retention design

## Blocks
- GH-62 release (SRE: instrumentation ships WITH feature)
- child .10 (SRE SLO/alert config) — alerts need metrics to fire on

## Acceptance
- All metrics above registered with bounded cardinality verified (no user_id/channel_id as labels — Code Reviewer checkpoint)
- Log lines for write-failures emit with correct fields (trace_id, no PII leaks)
- Reconciler implemented and emitting the divergence gauge
- Per-route timing middleware labels don't explode (Code Reviewer checkpoint)

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: 2026-04-23: Observability scope expanded for PO override pulling GH-59 into v0.17.0. provider_id CONFIRMED safe as Prometheus label (<20 providers bounded per SRE pre-clearance). Add data-consistency SLI: 'pct of polls successfully resolving provider_id'; target ≥95% steady state. Instrument the resolver cache hit rate (skqln.14) for observability. user_id and channel_id remain BANNED as labels (SRE veto stands).

title: BandwidthTracker: active-stream → provider resolver for per-poll provider tagging (GH-59)
description:
Add active-stream → provider resolver inside BandwidthTracker so every per-poll `session_telemetry` row gets a non-NULL `provider_id`. This is the write-side companion to the forward-compat column in skqln.2 and the "schedule-killer" the grooming team identified.

## Why this is non-trivial (Engineer's finding)

`backend/bandwidth_tracker.py:272-394` (`_collect_stats`) currently keys only on `channel_id` / `channel_number` / `client_ip`. Dispatcharr exposes `m3u_account` per-stream (`backend/dispatcharr_client.py:282-321`), but **channels have failover across multiple streams across multiple providers**. Per-poll provider attribution requires resolving: "active stream for channel N at time T → that stream's `m3u_account_id`." That resolver does not exist today.

## Scope

1. Extract or extend a resolver: given a channel in `_collect_stats`, determine the currently active stream and return its `m3u_account_id` (→ `provider_id` via M3U-to-provider mapping).
2. Handle NULL resolution gracefully: log a counter when provider cannot be resolved (falls back to `provider_id = NULL` on write, surfaces as "Unknown" in provider panel).
3. Cache resolution results within a single poll cycle to avoid N Dispatcharr round-trips per poll.
4. Emit a `data_consistency_sli` metric: % of rows with non-NULL provider_id per poll (for skqln.12 observability scope).

## Blocks / blocked by

- Blocked by: skqln.2 (schema migration), skqln.3 (BandwidthTracker single-write refactor — this rides on the refactor, not a separate write path)
- Blocks: skqln.14 (buffer-event ingest shares resolver pattern), skqln.15 (read APIs assume provider_id populated)

## Hidden work (pre-flagged)

- Dispatcharr `/proxy/ts/status` payload may not name the active stream directly; may require cross-referencing `/channels/<id>/streams` + client-session correlation.
- Backfill of historical `channel_watch_stats` rows into `session_telemetry` with NULL `provider_id` is acceptable (can't recover history) — provider panel will show "Unknown" bucket for pre-cutover data. Document this in skqln.11 runbook.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Resolver returns `provider_id` (or NULL) for every channel in every poll
- [ ] ≥95% of polls successfully resolve provider_id under steady state (SLI target; tune post-deploy)
- [ ] Unit tests: resolver correctness for single-stream, failover, stream-not-yet-resolved, and no-active-stream cases
- [ ] Integration test: 5-minute poll cycle against seeded fixture with 3 providers produces expected provider_id distribution
- [ ] No p95 latency regression on `_collect_stats` (pytest-benchmark gate from skqln.10 passes)

title: Buffer-event ingestion: wire Dispatcharr system events into session_telemetry.buffer_event_count (GH-59)
description:
Wire Dispatcharr buffering/system events into `session_telemetry.buffer_event_count` so GH-59's "buffering events by provider" chart has real data.

## Why net-new (Engineer's finding)

`backend/dispatcharr_client.py:984-991` (`get_channel_stats_detail`) returns "buffer status" per-client, and `get_system_events` at line 993-1012 emits buffering events. **Neither is called from `BandwidthTracker._collect_stats` today** — it only polls `get_channel_stats`. The `buffer_event_count` column in skqln.2's schema exists but has no writer.

## Scope

1. Decide: fold buffer-event polling into `_collect_stats` (single poll path, keeps skqln.3's single-write invariant) OR add a sibling poller (separate cadence for system-events stream). Default to folding into `_collect_stats` to minimize moving parts.
2. Idempotency / dedup: system events stream may re-deliver events. De-duplicate by event_id or (channel_id, client_id, event_ts) within a poll window.
3. Attribute each buffer event to the correct `provider_id` using skqln.13's resolver.
4. Increment `session_telemetry.buffer_event_count` per (session_id, observed_at) bucket.

## Blocks / blocked by

- Blocked by: skqln.2, skqln.3, skqln.13 (uses the resolver)
- Blocks: skqln.15 (buffering-by-provider endpoint needs populated column)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Buffer events flow from Dispatcharr → session_telemetry within one poll cycle of occurrence
- [ ] Dedup test: same event delivered twice → count increments once
- [ ] Unit test: buffer event on failover stream attributes to the active provider at event time, not poll time
- [ ] No p95 latency regression on poll cycle

title: GH-59: 4 provider-stats read API endpoints (buffering, watch-time, heatmap, bitrate)
description:
Ship 4 read-API endpoints backing the GH-59 Providers panel. All aggregate from `session_telemetry` with `provider_id` grouping.

## Endpoints

1. **`GET /api/stats/providers/buffering`** — time-series of buffer_event_count grouped by (provider_id, time_bucket). Query params: `window` (7d/30d/90d), `bucket` (hour/day).
2. **`GET /api/stats/providers/watch-time`** — total watch time per provider over window. Aggregates `SUM(poll_interval_ms)` grouped by provider_id.
3. **`GET /api/stats/providers/channel-heatmap`** — 2D grid: rows=providers, cols=channels, cell=SUM(bytes_delta). Caps at top-N channels to keep payload bounded (config: N=50 default).
4. **`GET /api/stats/providers/bitrate`** — derived bitrate time-series per provider. Computes `SUM(bytes_delta) / SUM(poll_interval_ms) * 8` per (provider_id, time_bucket).

## Indexes used

Per skqln.2 (amended by this epic): `(provider_id, observed_at)` for queries 1/2/4; `(provider_id, channel_id, observed_at) INCLUDE (bytes_delta)` covering index for heatmap (query 3). DBA flagged ~10–15% write-overhead cost for the additional index on the hot write path — accepted for GH-59.

## Handling NULL provider_id

Rows with NULL provider_id (pre-cutover data, resolver gaps) surface as a synthesized "Unknown" provider bucket. Do not exclude — operators need to see the attribution gap.

## Blocks / blocked by

- Blocked by: skqln.2, skqln.3, skqln.13, skqln.14
- Blocks: skqln.17 (frontend panel)

## Acceptance

- [ ] All 4 endpoints return correct aggregates against a 5M-row seeded fixture with 3 providers
- [ ] Response payloads match frontend contract (documented inline with skqln.17)
- [ ] pytest-benchmark gate (skqln.10, amended) shows p95 < 800ms for each endpoint against seeded fixture
- [ ] Property-based test: SUM of per-provider watch-time = SUM of per-user watch-time (same universe)
- [ ] NULL-provider bucket surfaces as "Unknown" and is not silently excluded
- [ ] Auth: endpoints require authenticated user (follow existing `/api/stats/*` middleware)

title: Chart primitives: heatmap component + colorblind-safe categorical palette for provider series (GH-59)
description:
Build the chart primitives that GH-59 needs but the rest of ECM doesn't have yet. Recharts 3.8.1 (`frontend/package.json`) has no first-class heatmap, and ECM has no categorical color scale for multi-series charts.

## Scope (UX flagged as "chart primitives sub-initiative in disguise")

1. **Heatmap component** — 2D grid visualization for Channels-by-Provider. Options: build on `recharts` `ScatterChart` with cell rectangles, or add a lightweight dep (`@nivo/heatmap` considered too heavy; `visx` rejected — footprint). Lean toward custom SVG component; document decision in ADR if the effort justifies one.
2. **Categorical color scale** — colorblind-safe palette tokens for up to ~15 providers. Adopt Okabe-Ito or Paul Tol's "bright" qualitative palette; define tokens in frontend design-system stylesheet.
3. **Multi-series legend** — reusable legend component that handles N categorical series with truncation and hover emphasis.
4. **Numeric overlay for heatmap cells** — per UX's WCAG 1.4.1 requirement: color must not be the sole channel carrying meaning. Cells render their numeric value on hover + high-contrast label overlay when color contrast is insufficient.

## What this is NOT (explicit de-scoping)

- NOT a general chart-primitive library rewrite. Time-series, line, bar, area stay on recharts. Only add what GH-59 requires.
- NOT a Stats-tab IA redesign. Primitives must work inside the existing flat-stack Stats tab (see UX finding: 8+ sibling sections already).

## Blocks / blocked by

- Independent on backend — can progress in parallel with skqln.13/.14/.15.
- Blocks: skqln.17 (frontend panel consumes these primitives)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Heatmap component renders 2D data (N_providers × N_channels) with hover tooltip + cell value overlay
- [ ] Categorical palette tokens in design-system; Storybook or equivalent demo of 3/5/10/15 provider variants
- [ ] Colorblind simulation check (deuteranopia + protanopia + tritanopia) passes visual review
- [ ] Responsive: heatmap downgrades gracefully to scrollable cells at <1024px viewport
- [ ] No runtime dependency added without ADR justification
close_reason: Merged via PR #204. Heatmap component + Tol-bright colorblind-safe palette shipped to dev as 0.16.0-0066. Stats v2 frontend prep complete; Providers panel (skqln.18) can now consume both primitives.

title: GH-59: Providers panel (6th panel in Stats tab, 4 visualizations)
description:
Ship the GH-59 "Providers" panel as the 6th panel in the Stats tab, delivering all 4 visualizations the reporter asked for.

## User outcome

Operator opens Stats tab → scrolls to Providers section → sees 4 charts answering "which upstream providers are performing well vs poorly" → makes data-driven keep/drop decision at renewal.

## Panel contents (4 visualizations per GH-59)

1. **Buffering events by provider** — multi-series time-series (one line per provider). Data: `/api/stats/providers/buffering` (skqln.15).
2. **Time spent watching each provider** — stacked area chart showing cumulative watch time per provider, with total at top. Data: `/api/stats/providers/watch-time`.
3. **Channels-by-provider heat map** — 2D grid: rows=providers, cols=top-N channels, cell=watch time. Data: `/api/stats/providers/channel-heatmap`. Uses heatmap primitive from skqln.16.
4. **Bitrate by provider over time** — multi-series time-series of computed bitrate (bytes/sec). Data: `/api/stats/providers/bitrate`.

## UI details

- Window selector: 7d / 30d / 90d (shared across all 4 charts in the panel).
- NULL provider_id rows surface as "Unknown" bucket with a tooltip explaining pre-cutover attribution gap.
- Panel placement: 6th stacked section in `frontend/src/components/tabs/StatsTab.tsx`, after the Users panel (skqln.6).
- Stats tab IA stays flat (no sub-nav) — UX flagged this as pressure toward the deferred sub-nav refactor; accept for v0.17.0 and revisit in v0.18.0.

## Blocks / blocked by

- Blocked by: skqln.15 (all 4 APIs), skqln.16 (heatmap + palette primitives)
- Blocks: 39ao6 (user-facing tracker closes when this ships + skqln.15 ships)

## Acceptance

- [ ] All 4 charts render against live session_telemetry data
- [ ] Window selector switches all 4 charts in sync
- [ ] "Unknown" provider bucket is visible and tooltipped
- [ ] Accessibility: all charts meet WCAG 1.4.1 (color not sole channel) — tooltips, patterns, or value overlays carry meaning
- [ ] Responsive: panel degrades gracefully at <1024px (charts stack or scroll, heatmap uses skqln.16's responsive fallback)
- [ ] Vitest component tests: data-mapping correctness for each chart from API payload → rendered series
- [ ] No regression on Users panel (skqln.6) rendering or perf

title: Schema + Alembic migration for session_telemetry (Stats v2)
description:
Create the unified session_telemetry table and ship its Alembic migration. This is the foundation for both GH-62 and (future) GH-59.

## Schema (per DBA's refined design during team-plan)

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| session_id | TEXT (UUID) | indexed |
| observed_at | INTEGER (unix epoch ms) | DBA renamed from 'ts'; mandatory index |
| user_id | INTEGER | FK users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL |
| provider_id | INTEGER | FK providers(id) ON DELETE SET NULL — nullable until provider tagging ships for GH-59 |
| channel_id | INTEGER | FK channels(id) ON DELETE SET NULL |
| bytes_delta | INTEGER NOT NULL | per-poll delta, CHECK >= 0 (DBA renamed from 'bytes' — explicit delta semantics) |
| buffer_event_count | INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 | DBA renamed from 'buffer_events' |
| poll_interval_ms | INTEGER NOT NULL | avoid future assumption of fixed cadence |

NOTE: bitrate_bps dropped — DBA: derivable from bytes_delta / poll_interval_ms; storing derived values invites disagreement.

## Mandatory indexes
- (observed_at) — retention sweeps, time-range queries
- (user_id, observed_at) — GH-62 watch time by user
- (provider_id, observed_at) — GH-59 provider performance (forward-looking)
- (session_id) — session reconstruction

Covering index (user_id, channel_id, observed_at) INCLUDE (bytes_delta) — only if EXPLAIN shows need; skip speculatively.

## Alembic migration constraints
- Use render_as_batch=True (SQLite table-rebuild pattern)
- Migration tested against a 5M-row seeded db before merge (DBA's hard ask)
- Round-trip up/down test (Code Reviewer block-level ask)

## Blocked by
- ADR-007 (retention policy) must land first — informs whether indexes cover rollup access patterns
- Privacy 11a (pre-impl threat model) must land first — may require additional columns or omissions per data-minimization

## Blocks
- BandwidthTracker single-write refactor
- GH-62 backend read API
- All GH-59 impl (deferred to v0.18.0, not in v0.17.0)

## Acceptance
- Schema matches DBA refined design above
- Alembic migration file lands in backend/alembic/versions/
- Up + down migration pass against empty and seeded dbs
- No regression on existing 2503+ tests

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: 2026-04-23: Schema addition for PO override pulling GH-59 into v0.17.0. Add covering index '(provider_id, channel_id, observed_at) INCLUDE (bytes_delta)' for skqln.16 channels-by-provider heatmap query. DBA flagged ~10-15% write overhead per additional index on hot write path — accepted. 5M-row seeded migration test stays unchanged (row count drives the test, not column semantics). NOTE: fixture generator may need update to produce realistic multi-provider distribution — see skqln.10 scope expansion.

title: BandwidthTracker single-write refactor + channel_watch_stats_v compat view
description:
Refactor BandwidthTracker to write telemetry to the new session_telemetry table ONLY (single-write). Ship a SQL VIEW channel_watch_stats_v that presents the old channel_watch_stats shape over session_telemetry for read-compat, so GH-62 and any other existing consumers of channel_watch_stats continue to work without immediate refactoring.

## Why single-write (not dual-write)
Team-plan 2026-04-21: DBA and Engineer both argued (and won, 3-to-1 against Architect) that dual-write creates two write paths that WILL silently diverge. The view approach keeps one source-of-truth while preserving legacy read surface.

## Implementation outline
1. Add append-to-session_telemetry inside BandwidthTracker._collect_stats (or an extracted helper) alongside current writes.
2. Feature-flag the writer (env var or settings flag) so we can disable without reverting code if a latency regression appears — Architect's refinement, accepted.
3. Once the flag is stable on dev for at least one sprint, switch legacy writes (channel_watch_stats) OFF and repoint the read via VIEW channel_watch_stats_v (SELECT ... FROM session_telemetry GROUP BY ...) to preserve the legacy read contract.

## Risk (Engineer flagged as 'hidden work')
- _collect_stats is ~180 lines with 4 dependent tables already. Adding a 5th is measurable correctness risk.
- Hidden work: backfill strategy for historical data, reconciliation job during transition, dual-read feature flag, legacy-write removal plan. Engineer estimated ~2 days of 'nobody scoped' work on top of the core refactor.

## Backfill strategy
Options to discuss with DBA/Architect at impl start:
- (a) Backfill existing channel_watch_stats rows into session_telemetry at migration time (requires synthesizing observed_at and poll_interval_ms — lossy)
- (b) Start session_telemetry at T0; view returns UNION of legacy rows + new rows for a transition window
- (c) Accept a short gap in historical data for GH-62 first ship; backfill only if user complaint arises

## Blocked by
- ADR-007 (retention policy) — informs whether rollup tables exist at refactor time
- session_telemetry schema migration (parent bead child .2)

## Blocks
- GH-62 backend read API (child .4) — depends on stable write path
- Observability instrumentation (child .11) — depends on the writer existing to instrument

## Acceptance
- BandwidthTracker writes session_telemetry per-poll under feature flag
- channel_watch_stats_v view returns data equivalent to channel_watch_stats for GH-62's access patterns
- Dual-read feature flag allows toggle between legacy table and new view for rollback
- QA property-based consistency test (see child .9) green
- No regression on existing stats endpoints during feature-flag-off state

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: 2026-04-23: Scope expansion for PO override pulling GH-59 into v0.17.0. skqln.14 (active-stream → provider resolver) and skqln.15 (buffer-event ingest) now ride on this refactor. Engineer flagged original skqln.3 as ~2 days hidden work; with GH-59 ingest added, hidden work estimate grows to ~3-4 days. This is now the highest-risk bead in the epic. Consider carving skqln.14 and skqln.15 as strictly separate commits to keep blast radius bounded.

title: GH-62: watch-time-by-user backend read API
description:
Backend read API for GH-62 user watch-time stats. Reads from channel_watch_stats_v view (session_telemetry data in legacy shape) so the read surface is stable.

User-facing tracker: enhancedchannelmanager-hl2fm (carries the gh-62 external ref).

## Endpoints (Code Reviewer standards from team-plan)
- GET /api/stats/watch-time — list per-user totals. Params: ?from=&to=&user_id=&group_by=day (snake_case). Response envelope: {data, meta, pagination}. ISO-8601 UTC timestamps with Z.
- GET /api/stats/watch-time/{user_id} — per-user breakdown by channel. Same envelope.

CR note: use existing /api/stats/* namespace in stats.py. No third namespace.

## Performance expectations (QA + SRE)
- p95 < 800ms, p99 < 2s at 3-6 months of data
- Covered by indexes from schema migration
- pytest-benchmark gate lands in child .9

## Blocked by
- child .3 (BandwidthTracker refactor + view)
- (implicit) child .2 schema, child .1 retention ADR

## Blocks

- OpenAPI/Swagger generates cleanly

title: GH-62: Users panel in Stats tab (5th panel, no sub-nav refactor)
description:
New Users panel added as the 5th panel in existing StatsTab. Ships with minimum viable UX per UX Designer consensus in team-plan.

User-facing tracker: enhancedchannelmanager-hl2fm.

## Minimum flow (UX small-scope)
- One daily trend line chart (watch-minutes, last 30 days)
- One table: channel name | total minutes | last watched
- Date range selector (7/30/90-day presets)

Nice-to-have CUT from v1 (per UX scope discipline): heat map by hour-of-day, device breakdown, favorite channel badge.

- Keyboard nav across sub-panel controls (Tab, arrow keys in chart legend)
- Semantic headings (h2/h3)
- Data-table fallback for chart (toggle or visually-hidden) — screen readers cannot read SVG

- NO chart primitives extraction. Reuse existing Recharts setup in StatsTab.tsx. Chart primitives bead deferred (extract when 3+ duplicates force it, not speculatively — Engineer).

- No regression on existing Stats panels (Overview/Channels/Popularity/History)

- enhancedchannelmanager-hl2fm

title: Privacy 11a: pre-implementation threat model + data classification for Stats v2
description:
Security Engineer deliverable: land BEFORE session_telemetry schema merges. Split from the original single privacy bead #11 per Security's team-plan ask (2026-04-21).

## Why pre-impl, not post-impl
'Doing only post-impl review is how you discover the schema needs changing after it's shipped' — Security. Threat model + data classification define retention, access control, and redaction requirements up front so Architect/DBA build to spec, not retrofit.

## Deliverables
- **Data classification** — what fields in session_telemetry are sensitive (user_id, channel viewing habits)? PII-adjacent behavioral data handling.
- **Access control boundary** — can users see only their own watch time, or all users (admin-only)? Shared-household scenario (one Dispatcharr username, multiple viewers)? Default: least-privilege.
- **Retention requirements** — feed into ADR-007 (child .1). Security sets the MINIMUM; DBA/SRE set the operational max.
- **Log redaction requirements** — watch-time data must NOT leak into app logs, support bundles, diagnostic exports, or Discord release-note automation.
- **PII minimization** — do we need raw session rows forever, or only rollups?
- **Consent/visibility UX requirement** — users must be able to see what's recorded about them. Whether opt-out is supported is a PO call; doc the decision.

## Blocks
- child .2 (session_telemetry schema) — schema merge requires 11a sign-off
- child .1 (ADR-007 retention) — informs retention minimum

## Acceptance
- Threat model document (or threat-model section in relevant doc) with DREAD or similar scoring per identified threat
- Data classification table checked into docs/security/
- Retention requirements stated and passed to ADR-007 authors
- Redaction requirements passed to Engineer for implementation
- Security sign-off on the schema + retention policy BEFORE schema bead (.2) ships

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: 2026-04-23: Threat model scope extended for PO override pulling GH-59 into v0.17.0. Data classification must now cover provider attribution as a potentially sensitive dimension: reveals which upstream M3U providers an operator uses + how heavily they're used. Consider whether provider-level data should have same privacy posture as user-level data, or if it's operator-visible-only (no exposure to non-admin users). Blocks skqln.2 schema merge per existing rule.

title: Privacy 11b: post-implementation privacy sign-off for Stats v2
description:
Security Engineer deliverable: post-implementation verification that what was built matches what was specified in 11a. Blocks GH-62 release.

## Verification scope
- Access control tested — users see only their own watch time (or admin-only aggregate, per 11a decision)
- Retention job runs correctly and prunes per ADR-007
- Logs redacted per 11a requirements (no leakage in app logs, support bundles, release notes, etc.)
- No cross-user leakage in API responses
- Consent/visibility UX (if opt-out supported) works as specified

## Deliverables
- Brief post-impl sign-off doc (can be a comment in this bead if small)
- Any exceptions/gaps documented with PO-acknowledged risk acceptance

## Blocked by
- child .6 (GH-62 frontend Users panel) — UI must exist to review
- child .1 (ADR-007) — retention job must be running
- child .7 (Privacy 11a) — can't sign off if the spec doesn't exist

## Blocks
- GH-62 release (i.e., the v0.17.0 release cut that includes GH-62)

## Acceptance
- Security sign-off recorded (in bead, PR, or doc)
- Any follow-on findings filed as their own beads with explicit PO disposition
- PO clear on any accepted risks

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: PR #179 (xq19y) shipped a flake-list PR comment workflow today. The 30-run inspection window predates the JUnit upload step, so every PR currently gets an empty 'no flakes detected' comment that emails the repo owner. This will continue for ~30 future PRs. Per PO directive: only emit a comment when there is actual signal (>=1 flake). Leave previously posted historical comments alone.

title: ADR-005: Code security gating strategy (CodeQL delta-zero on PR-to-dev + PR-to-main)
description:
Architectural follow-on to ADR-001 and PR #82. ADR-001 scoped its PR-to-dev validation gate to DEPENDENCY UPGRADES only (paths-filter on manifest files). It does NOT gate general code-security findings at PR time. PR #82 exposed this gap: 4 HIGH-severity py/path-injection alerts landed on dev (and were only surfaced when the PR-to-main aggregation check ran).

Architectural question: should the CodeQL-delta gate be extended beyond dep-bump PRs to cover ALL PRs that touch Python/JS source?

ADR must cover:
- Problem: current custom CodeQL (.github/workflows/build.yml) only runs on push to main/dev and PR-to-main. PR-to-dev has no CodeQL signal. New HIGH-severity findings can land on dev and only surface on the next dev->main promotion attempt (as they did for PR #82).
- Options:
  (1) Extend custom CodeQL to run on ALL PR-to-dev (not just dep-bump paths-filter scope from ADR-001) — ~2 min CI cost per PR, full coverage.
  (2) Run CodeQL on PR-to-dev but advisory only (don't block merge); block only on PR-to-main. Keeps dev velocity, surfaces findings earlier.
  (3) Keep status quo (CodeQL only on PR-to-main + push) and rely on ADR-002's release-cut gate to catch new findings before they promote.
  (4) Add 'no new HIGH/CRITICAL CodeQL alerts vs. base' as required status check on main branch protection (currently only 'Backend Tests' + 'Frontend Tests' are required — CodeQL failure is visible but non-blocking).
- Decision: TBD — architect leans toward (1)+(4) combination if CI cost is acceptable.
- Consequences: PR latency, dev velocity, how we triage pre-existing vs. new findings, dismiss-with-comment process
- Relationship to ADR-001 (dep-bump-only paths-filter gate) and ADR-002 (dev->main release-cut gate)

Also in scope: decide whether to promote CodeQL failure to a REQUIRED status check on main branch protection. Currently 'CodeQL' check is visible but main protection only requires 'Backend Tests' + 'Frontend Tests' — meaning a PR to main can theoretically merge even with HIGH CodeQL findings as long as the team has admin merge.

Priority P1 because gap is actively biting (PR #82) and low-effort remediation options exist.
notes: Renumbered from ADR-003 → ADR-005 on 2026-04-20 per PO/PM decision. Prior ADR-003 slot is held by enhancedchannelmanager-d42ko (WebSocket introduction & long-running job pattern, parked). Architect filed this bead 2026-04-20 without visibility into the existing parked ADR-002/003.

title: Smart sort: primary criteria not applied within failed-rank buckets (#73)
description:
## Summary
GitHub issue #73 (filed by ferteque, labeled P3 by reporter). PO has elevated to P1 — sprint-blocker for the v0.16.0 re-release. Reporter notes this is a follow-on from issue #58 (the persistence bug, fixed in v0.16.0-0027); #58 was closed today when ferteque agreed this is a distinct defect.

## Observed behavior
With smart sort configured as priority=['resolution','framerate','m3u_priority','bitrate','audio_channels','video_codec'] (all enabled) and deprioritize-failed enabled with failed_stream_sort_order=['black_screen','low_fps','failed'], streams do land in the correct failed-rank buckets — but ordering INSIDE each bucket does not follow the configured primary criteria.

## Hypothesis (for engineer to confirm/refute)
Primary criteria are not being applied within failed-rank buckets. Evidence from the reporter's log (channel-591424, 13 streams):

- Rank 0 (black_screen) bucket: resolutions 1024x576, 1920x1080, 1024x576 and all @ 25fps. If primary criteria were applying, the 1920x1080 stream should sort first inside the bucket. It lands at #2 behind a 1024x576 stream.
- Rank 2 (failed) bucket: 1920x1080 @ 50fps streams land at #8, #9, #11, #12 — behind a 1280x720 @ 25fps stream at #7. Higher resolution + higher framerate should sort ahead.

This points at the bucket secondary-sort not receiving (or not honoring) the active criteria comparator.

## Repro (from reporter)
1. Go to Channel Manager
2. Manually start a stream probe for a channel with known black-screen streams
3. After probe completes, trigger Smart Sort on that channel
4. Expected: streams within each failed-rank bucket sorted by configured primary criteria. Actual: within-bucket order appears random relative to configured criteria.

## Log excerpt (from issue #73)
```
[STREAM-PROBE-SORT] Channel 'channel-591424': Sorting 13 streams
[STREAM-PROBE-SORT] Sort config: priority=['resolution','framerate','m3u_priority','bitrate','audio_channels','video_codec'], enabled={all True}
[STREAM-PROBE-SORT] Active criteria (in order): ['resolution','framerate','m3u_priority','bitrate','audio_channels','video_codec']
[STREAM-PROBE-SORT] Deprioritize failed streams: True
[STREAM-PROBE-SORT] Failed stream sort order: ['black_screen','low_fps','failed']
  Stream 962110 (D.LaLiga2): status=success, res=1024x576, fps=25.0
  Stream 959174 (MOVISTAR LA LIGA 2 UHD): status=success, res=1920x1080, fps=25.0
  Stream 959167 (MOVISTAR LA LIGA 2 SD): status=success, res=1024x576, fps=25.0
  Stream 962109 (K.LaLiga2): status=failed, res=1920x1080, fps=25.0
  Stream 958629 (M.LALIGA 2 RAW): status=success, res=1920x1080, fps=50.0
  Stream 958471 (VO MOVISTAR LALIGA 2 RAW): status=success, res=1920x1080, fps=50.0
  Stream 1465610 (M. La Liga TV 2 HD): status=failed, res=1280x720, fps=25.0
  Stream 1465608 (M. La Liga TV 2 HD 1080): status=failed, res=1920x1080, fps=50.0
  Stream 1465609 (M. La Liga TV 2 HD 1080 B): status=failed, res=1920x1080, fps=50.0
  Stream 1151442 (M. La Liga TV 2 HD): status=failed, res=1280x720, fps=25.0
  Stream 1151440 (M. La Liga TV 2 HD 1080): status=failed, res=1920x1080, fps=50.0
  Stream 1151441 (M. La Liga TV 2 HD 1080 B): status=failed, res=1920x1080, fps=50.0
  Stream 962111 (S.LaLiga2 HD): status=failed, res=1920x1080, fps=25.0
  D.LaLiga2: DEPRIORITIZED (black screen, rank=0)
  MOVISTAR LA LIGA 2 UHD: DEPRIORITIZED (black screen, rank=0)
  MOVISTAR LA LIGA 2 SD: DEPRIORITIZED (black screen, rank=0)
  K.LaLiga2: DEPRIORITIZED (status=failed, rank=2)
  M.LALIGA 2 RAW: DEPRIORITIZED (black screen, rank=0)
  VO MOVISTAR LALIGA 2 RAW: DEPRIORITIZED (black screen, rank=0)
  ... (remaining status=failed deprioritized at rank=2)
Sorted order:
  #1: D.LaLiga2 (success, 1024x576)          <-- rank 0
  #2: MOVISTAR LA LIGA 2 UHD (success, 1920x1080)  <-- rank 0, should be #1 within bucket
  #3: MOVISTAR LA LIGA 2 SD (success, 1024x576)    <-- rank 0
  #4: M.LALIGA 2 RAW (success, 1920x1080)    <-- rank 0
  #5: VO MOVISTAR LALIGA 2 RAW (success, 1920x1080) <-- rank 0
  #6: K.LaLiga2 (failed, 1920x1080)          <-- rank 2
  #7: M. La Liga TV 2 HD (failed, 1280x720)  <-- rank 2, should land BELOW 1920x1080 @50fps
  #8: M. La Liga TV 2 HD 1080 (failed, 1920x1080)  <-- 50fps, should lead bucket
  #9: M. La Liga TV 2 HD 1080 B (failed, 1920x1080)
  #10: M. La Liga TV 2 HD (failed, 1280x720)
  #11: M. La Liga TV 2 HD 1080 (failed, 1920x1080)
  #12: M. La Liga TV 2 HD 1080 B (failed, 1920x1080)
  #13: S.LaLiga2 HD (failed, 1920x1080)
```

## Code location
- Backend Python: `backend/stream_prober.py` (search for `[STREAM-PROBE-SORT]` log tag)
- Likely area: `_smart_sort_streams` / `smart_sort_streams` — specifically the composite key that combines bucket rank with the primary-criteria comparator. The rank prefix is working (buckets are correct); the tiebreaker within the rank is what's misfiring.

## Related history
- Issue #58 (closed): original persistence bug for `failed_stream_sort_order`. Fixed in v0.16.0-0027 (commit 7a5a6203). Ferteque agreed in #58 comment (2026-04-20) that this is a distinct symptom and opened #73.
- Prior PR #72 merged into dev (tip a145250e).
- v0.16.0 was rolled back today (bd-vgm4l) specifically to clear PO-flagged bugs before re-cutting the tag. This bead is one of those bugs.

## Links
- GitHub: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/issues/73
- Prior: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/issues/58

## Sprint context
- Sprint goal: clear PO-flagged bugs, then re-cut v0.16.0.
- main is at v0.15.2 (post-rollback). dev is at a145250e. package.json: 0.16.0-0040.
- Independent of bd-zjge5 (ESLint baseline, in_progress) — no dependency either direction.

## Owner & methodology
- Owner hint: Project Engineer (backend Python).
- TDD required: failing pytest that demonstrates the within-bucket ordering is wrong on current dev, THEN the fix. Do not land a fix without a test that was RED first.
- Deploy-to-container iteration per CLAUDE.md before shipping.

## Out of scope
- Do not touch bucket-assignment logic (ranks are already correct per the log).
- Do not refactor the sort API surface unless the minimal fix requires it — this is a bugfix sprint, not a cleanup sprint.
acceptance_criteria:
- A pytest case reproduces the bug: within a single failed-rank bucket (e.g. rank=0 black_screen, rank=2 failed), streams with higher priority in the configured criteria order (resolution, framerate, m3u_priority, bitrate, audio_channels, video_codec) sort first. Test is RED against current code on dev @ a145250e.
- Test passes GREEN after fix.
- No regression in existing [STREAM-PROBE-SORT] pytest coverage (full suite green).
- Fix is visible in [STREAM-PROBE-SORT] debug log output so reporter (ferteque) can verify visually on re-report — per-bucket ordering should be legible in the log.
- Manual repro from issue #73 (channel-591424 13-stream probe) now orders 1920x1080 streams above 1024x576 inside the black_screen (rank=0) bucket, and orders 1920x1080 @ 50fps streams above 1280x720 @ 25fps inside the failed (rank=2) bucket.
notes: Correction 2026-04-21: my reopen NOTES were based on a misread of the timeline. The fix was NOT rolled back. Fix commits 23a755b6 (sw883, stream probe) and 13dbe496 (bqpq0, auto-create) were authored 2026-04-20 at 17:07 UTC — 29 minutes AFTER reporter filed GH-73 at 16:38 UTC. Reporter's v0.17.0-0000 build was a pre-fix dev tip. Both fix commits are on current dev and present in every :dev GHCR image since ~17:30 UTC 2026-04-20. Project Engineer (subagent ac4d6376) verified: (a) current in-container code returns correct sort order for reporter's exact 13-stream scenario, (b) pre-fix simulation via monkey-patching reproduces the bug, (c) 2487 backend tests pass. Two regression tests added to nail down coverage — landing via PR from worktree-agent-ac4d6376 to dev. Bead closes on regression-test PR merge.

title: ADR-002: DBAS restore engine transaction model & downtime contract
description:
bd-gb5r5.3 (restore engine) requires documented architectural decisions before implementation.

Decisions required:
(a) Transaction model — single transaction spanning all categories vs. savepoint-per-category (partial restore on failure).
(b) Acceptable downtime during the restore window — is the API offline, read-only, or fully available?
(c) Concurrent-API behavior during restore — reject with 503, queue, or allow (with what consistency guarantees)?
(d) Pre-restore snapshot protocol — VACUUM INTO before restore for auto-rollback target; retention, location, cleanup.
(e) Rollback-on-error guarantees — what state is guaranteed if restore fails mid-run?

Required inputs:
- DBA: Alembic migration coupling, FK dependency graph for category ordering.
- SRE: observability during restore (progress, duration, failure telemetry).
- Security: endpoint authz, audit logging for destructive ops.

Stub bead. Content filled by architect + DBA during bd-gb5r5.3 kickoff. Blocks bd-gb5r5.3.
close_reason: Retired 2026-04-21 by PO directive. The original gb5r5 DBAS migration plan was filed 2026-02-18 and the team-plan archaeology (agent a4ca4a38) found it significantly overlapping with already-shipped ECM work (backup.py YAML restore, task_scheduler.py, dispatcharr_client.py, notifications infra). 26 of the 37 tree beads had not been updated in 67 days and lacked acceptance criteria. Rather than salvage, PO chose to kill the existing scope entirely so fresh DBAS planning can start from user need (not from the standalone DBAS product's original shape). Fresh plan to be authored in a future /team-plan or /onboard session. Do NOT reopen these beads — if DBAS work resumes, file new beads with current framing.
