title: Wire tests + typecheck into CI via new test.yml workflow
description:
Onboarding finding (RED). CI runs zero tests today — .github/workflows/build.yml has 11 jobs, all security/build, no pytest/vitest/tsc. ~120 backend tests + 43 vitest + 43 Playwright specs exist but nothing gates PRs on correctness.

Scope (decided with QA + Project Engineer + Code Reviewer 2026-04-19, refined twice after engineer preflight revealed implementation blockers).

FINAL SCOPE (after 2026-04-19 Path C decision): backend + frontend CI gates only. E2E deferred to companion bead enhancedchannelmanager-2lw25.

File layout:
- NEW: .github/workflows/test.yml (backend + frontend jobs only; NO e2e job in this bead)
- EDIT: frontend/package.json — (1) remove stale 'ajv': '>=8.18.0' from overrides block (it breaks ESLint 9 and is not needed for npm audit). (2) add 'typecheck': 'tsc --noEmit' script

test.yml structure:
- Two parallel top-level jobs: backend, frontend
- backend job: pytest on the existing suite. Uses existing backend/tests/conftest.py — no new ci_seed.sql required (pytest fixtures handle test data already)
- frontend job: SEQUENTIAL steps — npm ci → npm run lint (changed-files-only in PR mode; see Lint strategy) → npm run typecheck → npm test (no --coverage)

Lint strategy (Decision 1b):
- Remove the ajv override first (required — lint is currently broken on dev).
- PR mode: lint CHANGED FILES ONLY with --max-warnings 0. Engineer chooses mechanism (simplest: git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD filtered to TS/JS files piped to npx eslint).
- Push mode (dev/main): full lint as NON-BLOCKING informational step (continue-on-error: true). Visibility without blocking.
- Existing 71 errors + 98 warnings are grandfathered; cleanup is bead zjge5.

Coverage strategy (Decision 2f):
- Gate: 'npm test' (no --coverage). DO NOT use 'npm run test:ci' — it fails today because actual coverage (~15%) < 20% threshold in vitest.config.ts.
- DO NOT modify vitest.config.ts. Companion bead nmlxi handles threshold setting from measured data.
- Optionally add non-blocking coverage-report step uploading coverage artifact.

Triggers:
- backend + frontend: push to dev/main AND all PRs

E2E — EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE:
- Engineer discovered that running E2E on GitHub-hosted runners cannot produce a meaningful gate — ECM proxies Dispatcharr; ~15 of 21 modals.spec.ts skips need Dispatcharr-owned entities that don't exist in CI. Real fix requires either a Dispatcharr mock sidecar container (10-14h) or a self-hosted runner with a real Dispatcharr instance. See bead 2lw25 (reframed).
- The prior engineer's work on e2e job YAML (on ci/test-t8xw3 scratch branch) should be REMOVED before this bead closes.

RESUMPTION NOTE FOR ENGINEER:
Scratch branch ci/test-t8xw3 already has commits with:
- test.yml (three-job version including e2e)
- admin user seeding for E2E
- E2E seed email fix
When resuming: remove the e2e job from test.yml, remove any E2E-specific setup that's no longer needed for backend + frontend. Verify backend + frontend still pass (they were already green in the last push). Then PR to dev.

Out of scope:
- E2E job (→ 2lw25)
- Python linting / ruff (separate bead)
- Coverage threshold setting (→ nmlxi)
- Branch protection (→ 8w33i, update to reflect only backend + frontend as required checks)
- Existing 71 lint errors (→ zjge5)
- modals.spec.ts silent skip hygiene (separate small bead if PO chooses)

Success criteria:
- PR with NEW TypeScript error → red backend or frontend check
- PR with NEW ESLint violation on changed file → red frontend check
- PR with failing backend test → red backend check
- 'npm run typecheck' and 'npm run lint' work locally after ajv fix
- No e2e job in test.yml

Flagged during /onboard 2026-04-19 by QA Engineer (RED), Project Engineer, Code Reviewer. Scope refined twice based on engineer preflight findings.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
In channel manager edit mode, check streams under search A, change search to B, check more streams, drag all selected onto an existing channel. Staging adds all IDs correctly, but the expanded channel view shows only streams from the current search because loadStreams resolves staged IDs against the current-search result set plus the backend channel-streams response — streams whose objects only appeared in a prior search aren't in either source.

Fix: App.tsx accumulates a seenStreams Map that is populated every time searchStreams/loadStreamGroup returns results and is never cleared on new search. ChannelManagerTab threads it through as seenStreamsMap. ChannelsPane.loadStreams seeds localStreamMap / combinedMap from seenStreamsMap first so previously-seen streams resolve by ID regardless of the current search filter.

Verified in container: staged streams from prior searches now remain visible after search changes; 1050 frontend tests pass, tsc --noEmit clean.

title: Flake audit & baseline: 3-run cadence to de-flake 2445 BE + 1118 FE suite
description:
**Why:** QA flagged during grooming 2026-04-20: no flake audit cadence exists. 2445 backend + 1118 frontend tests accumulate without pruning. bd-6rrl5 dep bumps need a flake baseline BEFORE upgrades so we can distinguish upgrade-induced flake from pre-existing.

**Scope:**
- Run full BE+FE suites 3 consecutive times on current dev tip; collect any test that fails non-deterministically
- Categorize: true flake (timing, ordering, shared state) vs. environmental (test data, network)
- Fix critical + high-confidence flakes; file follow-up beads for complex ones
- Update docs/testing.md with flake-triage policy (re-run allowed N times before marking fixme; quarterly flake sweep cadence)
- Establish CI baseline: which tests have been seen flaky in the last 30 runs — use as gate in PR reviews

**Acceptance:**
- 3 consecutive green BE+FE runs on dev
- Flake policy documented in docs/testing.md
- Any remaining flakes catalogued in beads with flaky label

**Out of scope:** converting existing test.fixme back to active (that's test authorship, not de-flaking).

**Filed by:** QA Engineer persona, grooming 2026-04-20.
close_reason: Flake policy landed in PR #89 (commit c429189). 3-run baseline on a35d4f5e: FE 1118/1118 green x3; BE 2425 passed x3 (after deselecting 2 real flakes + 3 env-drift, all tracked). Follow-ups: hhsz0 (flaky TestTraceIdMiddleware), 0gcu9 (test-authorship drift), xq19y (CI automation for 30-run list). Unblocks bd-6rrl5 dep-bump train per ADR-001.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Test-quality hygiene fix. Independent of any E2E infrastructure work.

Problem: e2e/modals.spec.ts has 21 conditional test.skip() calls (skip-if-no-orphans-found, skip-if-button-not-present, etc.). These silently pass in Playwright output, making the suite report green while ~21 cases exercised nothing.

Scope:
- Convert every conditional test.skip() in e2e/modals.spec.ts to test.fixme() with a descriptive message naming the missing precondition (e.g., 'fixme: requires Dispatcharr-provided orphaned groups', 'fixme: requires linked M3U accounts with profiles').
- Silent green becomes loud red for any run that reaches those branches. Matches the cataloged 21 states from the 2lw25 evaluation report.
- If any of the 21 are NOT conditional (i.e., legitimate hard skips for unimplemented features), leave them as test.skip() with a TODO comment.

Effort: ~30 minutes. One file, no infrastructure changes.

Prerequisite: none. Can be done any time, independent of t8xw3 and 2lw25.

Benefit: kills the silent-green anti-pattern immediately. When 2lw25 eventually delivers E2E-in-CI, the fixmes become red checks that drive the cataloging of what state to seed. When 2lw25 seeds that state, convert fixme() back to the regular test body.

Flagged during engineer evaluation of 2lw25 on 2026-04-19. Kept separate so the hygiene fix isn't blocked behind a 10-14h sidecar/runner investment.

title: CodeQL cleanup: canonicalization pattern in export.py + export_manager.py (ADR-005 Phase 2 prereq)
description:
Security follow-on to 0a1pr. Six remaining py/path-injection CodeQL findings blocking ADR-005 Phase 2 delta-zero enforcement (kgjci):

- backend/routers/export.py lines 397, 408, 433, 444
- backend/export_manager.py lines 87, 89

Apply the same canonicalization + containment pattern that shipped in 0a1pr for backend/routers/backup.py:

    safe_root = DIR.resolve()
    candidate = (safe_root / user_input).resolve()
    candidate.relative_to(safe_root)  # raises ValueError if outside

CodeQL's dataflow tracker cannot prove anchored-regex containment alone — the explicit resolve() + relative_to() check is what makes it recognize containment.

Each call site needs:
1. Pattern applied
2. Unit test proving ../ traversal → raises, absolute path → raises, URL-encoded → raises

Acceptance:
- 6 findings → 0 on dev tip per: gh api repos/:owner/:repo/code-scanning/alerts --paginate | jq '[.[] | select(.state=="open" and .rule.security_severity_level=="high")] | length'
- kgjci Phase 2 flip unblocks

Size: Small, mechanical — matches established precedent.

LABELS: security, codeql, adr-005

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
Investigation: scope already shipped under sister bead h5rfv (PR #159, commit 099b35b3 "fix(security): canonicalize-and-verify path traversal in export.py + export_manager.py").

Current state on origin/dev:
- backend/export_manager.py: _safe_export_path() helper + ExportPathError class + ExportManager.cleanup() short-circuits on containment failure (alerts 1354, 1355).
- backend/routers/export.py: download_m3u and download_xmltv both canonicalize via _safe_export_path and 400 on ExportPathError (alerts 1356-1359).
- backend/tests/routers/test_export.py::TestExportPathInjection: 11+ tests covering traversal / absolute / null-byte / backslash payloads plus symlink-escape + destructive-rmtree sentinel (mirrors TestSavedBackupsPathInjection from 0a1pr).

CodeQL verification (2026-04-24 via gh api):
- open + high + path~="export": 0 alerts
- open + high (all paths): 0 alerts
Pre-fix count per h5rfv commit message: 6 alerts (1354-1359). Post-fix: 0. Delta: 6 -> 0.

Branch security/codeql-export-canonicalization-tu9v1 created from origin/dev and pushed (empty — zero code delta needed). Can be deleted or left as a marker.

Full-suite baseline in ecm-ecm-1: 23 failures / 2936 passed. All 23 are pre-existing SPA-fallback drift already tracked under vjlzf/pvq6s (6 export-related ones overlap this bead's surface but are test expectation mismatches against the SPA catch-all route, not security regressions — the containment check itself is verified by the 11 other TestExportPathInjection tests that pass). Delta from my branch: 0.

Recommendation: close as duplicate-of-h5rfv. Unblocks kgjci (ADR-005 Phase 2 delta-zero enforcement) immediately — the CodeQL state is already clean on dev tip.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: Closed as duplicate of enhancedchannelmanager-h5rfv (shipped via PR #159, commit 099b35b3, 2026-04-24). All six CodeQL alerts (1354-1359) cleared; 9 tests landed in backend/tests/routers/test_export.py::TestExportPathInjection. Current open HIGH alerts = 0. kgjci (ADR-005 Phase 2 delta-zero enforcement) is effectively unblocked on the ground. Standup synthesis missed h5rfv's prior landing — will verify sister beads before spawning in future.

title: Implement ADR-004: Release-cut discipline (shipping.md rewrite + runbook hardening + PR template)
description:
Implementation of ADR-004 (docs/adr/ADR-004-release-cut-promotion-discipline.md). Documentation + process changes; no code edits to backend/frontend.

## Work items

### 1. Rewrite docs/shipping.md "Release Workflow" section (lines 74-86)
Replace existing "Release Workflow (Merging to Main)" with reference to ADR-004 + the Cut Mechanics shell block from the ADR (steps 0-10). Existing "When User Says 'Ship the Fix'" section (lines 3-72) is UNCHANGED — that covers dev ship cycles, not release cuts. Add a new "Hotfix Path" subsection derived from ADR-004 §4. Add "Pre-Cut Gate Checklist" subsection mirroring the G1a-G7 table.

### 2. Reorder and harden docs/runbooks/v0.16.0-rollback.md
- Reorder Option 1 (admin-bypass) and Option 2 (allow_force_pushes flip) — Option 2 becomes primary since ADR-005 disables admin-bypass. Add one-line note citing ADR-005 as the reason.
- Add post-action verification step to Option 2: "gh api /repos/.../branches/main/protection | jq '.allow_force_pushes.enabled'" must return false before runbook is marked complete.
- Add note considering a short-TTL wrapper script that auto-reverts allow_force_pushes after N minutes regardless of outcome (design discussion; implementation optional).

### 3. (Optional) PR template for release cuts
.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/release.md with the G1a-G7 checklist body. Users invoke via "gh pr create --template release.md". Nice-to-have; not required for ADR-004 to function.

### 4. SRE audit cadence expansion (coordinate with ADR-005 audit bead)
File a subtask against ADR-005's audit-cadence bead (or create if it doesn't exist yet) to absorb two new audit scopes:
- Any PO-downgrade of a P1 bug to P2 within 30 days prior to a release cut (surfaces authorized-but-risky G1a escape-hatch use).
- Any allow_force_pushes flip events in the repo audit log (surfaces unsanctioned rollback attempts).

### 5. Clarify "formally waived" semantics for G1b
Document in shipping.md (or as a Phase 2 ADR addendum) whether a GitHub Security tab dismissal alone satisfies G1b, OR whether a PR-description cross-reference to the dismissal is also required. Recommendation: require BOTH — Security-tab dismissal with rationale AND PR-description line citing the alert number and dismissal category. Belt-and-suspenders; mirrors ADR-005's dismiss-with-comment policy.

### 6. Phase 2 automation (separate follow-on bead; NOT part of this bead)
File a separate bead for mechanical automation of G1a + G1b + G5 + G6 + G7 via a GitHub Action on release-cut PRs. Requires bd available in CI for G1a. Deferred — Phase 1 manual verification is acceptance-sufficient.

## Acceptance
- [ ] shipping.md §Release Workflow rewritten per ADR-004 Cut Mechanics
- [ ] shipping.md §Hotfix Path added per ADR-004 §4
- [ ] docs/runbooks/v0.16.0-rollback.md Option 1/2 reordered with verification step
- [ ] Audit bead subtask filed (item 4)
- [ ] G1b "formally waived" semantics documented (item 5)
- [ ] First release cut under ADR-004 executed as canary; retro bead filed

Owner: Project Engineer (coordinates with SRE for audit cadence and Security for G1b semantics).

Sequencing: Items 1-2 land as a single doc PR. Item 3 is optional follow-up. Items 4-5 can be parallel. Item 6 is a separate bead entirely.
notes: 2026-04-21 progress: items 1 and 2 landed via PR #97 (merged as a2749b70 on dev; shipped at 0.16.0-0044). Done: shipping.md Release Workflow rewrite + Hotfix Path + Pre-Cut Gate Checklist; v0.16.0-rollback.md Option 1/2 reorder + post-action verification + TTL-wrapper design note. Still open: item 3 (PR template, optional), item 4 (SRE audit subtask + allow_force_pushes audit), item 5 (G1b 'formally waived' semantics), item 6 (Phase 2 automation as separate follow-on bead). Bead stays open until remaining items disposition.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #97 (items 1+2: shipping.md Release Workflow rewrite + runbook reorder) and PR #147 (items 3-6 + acceptance carry-forward). Items 3 (release.md PR template), 4 (audit cadence absorbed via bd-se7ay child of bd-kgjci), 5 (G1b 'formally waived' semantics in shipping.md), 6 (Phase 2 automation filed as bd-3d0tv). Acceptance item 'First release cut under ADR-004 executed as canary; retro bead filed' carries forward to whoever cuts the next release.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Fixed: black screen scan task silently overwriting is_black_screen=False on every ffmpeg timeout.

Contract change in stream_prober._detect_black_screen:
- Return type: bool → Optional[bool]. True = black, False = clean, None = indeterminate (timeout / no YAVG samples / abnormal exit).
- Added ffmpeg -timeout 15s network stall guard (microseconds) at the input level.
- Bumped asyncio wait_for grace from sample_duration+15s to sample_duration+30s. Cold-start scans on slow-to-buffer IPTV providers were hitting the 20s wall before the first keyframe decoded.

Call sites updated:
- probe_stream() inline black-screen check now keeps is_black at None when detection is disabled or indeterminate.
- _save_probe_result() signature: is_black_screen: Optional[bool] = None. The success path now writes is_black_screen ONLY when it's True or False — None preserves prior state.
- tasks/black_screen_scan.py::scan_one: None is routed into error_count (surfaced in the TaskResult summary as 'N errors') and the DB row's is_black_screen is left untouched. Previously every timeout wrote False and the stream counted as 'clear' in the task report.

Tests:
- Renamed test_returns_false_on_timeout → test_returns_none_on_timeout; asserts None.
- Renamed test_returns_false_when_no_yavg_data → test_returns_none_when_no_yavg_data; asserts None.
- Added test_preserves_black_screen_when_detection_indeterminate: regression guard that pins None → no DB write.
- Full suite: 2425 passed.

User-observable behavior after fix:
- Scan results now report 'N black screen, N clear, N errors' with real numbers in the errors bucket.
- A scan-task timeout on a stream that a manual probe flagged as black no longer wipes that finding.
- Cold-start timeouts drop substantially thanks to the longer grace and ffmpeg -timeout option, but any remaining timeouts are now visible rather than masquerading as clean streams.

title: GH-104: apply normalization rules to existing channels on refresh/rebuild (P1 bug)
description:
# GH-104: Normalization rules don't apply to existing channels

**Source**: GitHub issue #104 filed by @MotWakorb 2026-04-22 00:08 UTC on ECM v0.16.0-0040
**Debug bundle**: https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26950164/ecm-debug-bundle-20260421-234006.tar.gz

## Symptom
Channel Normalisation rules (regex replace) work at channel-creation time but do NOT apply retroactively to existing channels or during refresh/reconciliation. Reporter added rule `(ᴿᴬᵂ|RAW|UHD|FHD|HD|4K|1080P|720P) → ""` and validated via "Test Rules" that "PROSIEBEN ᴿᴬᵂ" normalizes to "PROSIEBEN". But existing channel "RTL ᴿᴬᵂ" remains untouched, creating duplicate channels "RTL" + "RTL ᴿᴬᵂ".

## Verdict
**Bug / feature gap, not expected-by-design.** The rename-existing code path exists in the auto-creation executor but only fires under very narrow conditions that don't cover the reporter's scenario.

## Root cause

1. **Normalization is invoked ONLY from the auto-creation `create_channel` action**
   - `backend/auto_creation_executor.py:482-493` — normalization applied to the new channel name during rule execution
   - `backend/auto_creation_executor.py:532-555` — existing-channel rename happens ONLY when `_find_channel_by_name(normalized_name)` returns an existing channel AND its stored `existing_core` differs from `channel_name` (specifically handles the number-prefix case like "107 | RTL ᴿᴬᵂ")
   - When the stored name is "RTL ᴿᴬᵂ" and no "RTL" channel exists yet, the lookup at line 514 misses and we fall through to create a brand-new "RTL" channel. The original "RTL ᴿᴬᵂ" is never touched.

2. **Refresh path only runs rules with `run_on_refresh=True`**
   - `backend/tasks/auto_creation.py:205-244` — `run_auto_creation_after_refresh()` filters to `AutoCreationRule.run_on_refresh == True` only
   - `backend/models.py:1424` — `run_on_refresh` defaults to `False`, so most users' rules are never re-run on refresh
   - Even for rules with `run_on_refresh=True`, the pipeline re-evaluates all streams (not existing channels), so it still hits the same gap above

3. **No other entry point re-applies normalization**
   - `backend/routers/normalization.py` exposes only test/migrate/import/export endpoints — no "apply to existing channels" action
   - `backend/routers/channels.py` merge endpoints (`POST /channels/merge`, `POST /channels/bulk-merge` at lines 1745, 2083) are fully manual — user picks channels and target name, no normalization involvement
   - No task in `backend/tasks/` iterates existing channels against normalization rules
   - `run_on_refresh` only runs the auto-creation pipeline — that pipeline is stream-driven, not channel-driven

## Normalization engine architecture (brief)
- `backend/normalization_engine.py` — `NormalizationEngine` class, loads `NormalizationRuleGroup`+`NormalizationRule` from DB, applies rules multi-pass with superscript conversion. Pure text-in/text-out; no channel awareness.
- `NormalizationEngine.normalize(name, group_ids, preserve_superscripts)` — entry point
- `NormalizationEngine.test_rule(...)` at line 1143 — the "Test Rules" UI endpoint. Works correctly because it applies rules to arbitrary text — exactly what reporter confirmed.
- `NormalizationEngine.extract_core_name(name)` at line 1014 — used by `merge_streams` action for reconciliation lookups; already iterates existing channels at executor init (`auto_creation_executor.py:190-225`) to build a `_normalized_name_to_channel` and `_core_name_to_channel` map. **Proof the engine CAN be applied to existing channel names — it's just not used to rename them.**

## Why refresh/reconciliation doesn't apply normalization today
The engine was designed as a stream-name → channel-name transform on creation, not as a channel-maintenance pass. The reporter's hypothesis is correct:
> "normalisation is only applied at channel creation time, not during reconciliation or refresh."

Refresh fetches new streams from the M3U provider and runs auto-creation rules, but auto-creation iterates **streams**, not **channels**. An existing "RTL ᴿᴬᵂ" channel is invisible to the rule engine — only "RTL ᴿᴬᵂ" streams are evaluated, and they match against the existing channel by exact name (`_find_channel_by_name`), so they get re-attached and nothing gets renamed.

## Fix direction comparison

**Option A — Re-normalize existing channels on refresh** (automatic, opt-in)
- New channel-iteration pass after M3U refresh (or after rule save): for each existing channel, apply enabled normalization groups, rename if result differs and no collision
- Effort: **M** (1-2 days) — new pass in `auto_creation_engine.py` or a standalone task; needs collision handling (see below)
- Pro: invisible to user, "just works"
- Con: silently renames channels, hard to undo; needs dry-run preview

**Option B — Auto-merge channels with matching normalized names** (automatic or dedup-driven)
- On refresh or via new action: find all pairs of channels where `normalize(A.name) == normalize(B.name)`, offer merge
- Effort: **L** (3-5 days) — this is a dedup feature, not a rename feature; needs UI for conflict resolution (which streams go where, which metadata wins)
- Pro: solves duplicate problem at the symptom level
- Con: bigger scope, touches merge engine; still leaves renamed-but-collision-free cases unaddressed

**Option C — "Force re-normalize existing channels" action/button** (manual, safe)
- New `POST /api/normalization/apply-to-channels` endpoint that walks all channels, applies rules, returns a preview diff; user confirms, endpoint executes renames + optional merges
- Effort: **S-M** (1-2 days for the preview+execute backend; +1 day for the settings-page UI button/modal)
- Pro: user-initiated, explicit, includes dry-run preview, easy to undo (journal already logs renames)
- Con: manual step — if user forgets, they keep accumulating duplicates

## Data-integrity considerations

When retroactively renaming "RTL ᴿᴬᵂ" → "RTL":
1. **No existing "RTL"** → safe rename (single `update_channel`). Journal entry via existing `channel:rename` pattern.
2. **"RTL" already exists** → collision. Three policies possible:
   - **Skip** (don't rename, flag in preview) — safest default
   - **Merge** — move all streams from "RTL ᴿᴬᵂ" into "RTL", delete the ᴿᴬᵂ channel. Reuses existing `merge_channels` logic. Needs decision on metadata precedence (channel_number, logo_id, epg_data_id, stream_profile_id).
   - **Append suffix** — rename to "RTL (2)" (bad UX, just defers the problem)
3. **Group mismatch** — "RTL ᴿᴬᵂ" and "RTL" may be in different groups. Merge should respect/prompt on target group.
4. **Channel number prefix handling** — existing rename code at `auto_creation_executor.py:535-547` already preserves the "107 | " prefix; a channel-sweep should reuse this pattern.
5. **Streams already assigned** — collision merge must dedupe streams by ID (existing `merge_channels` already does this via `seen_streams`).
6. **Active viewers / sessions** — renaming a live channel may disrupt playback; recommend warning or schedule-during-off-hours (probably out of scope for v1).

## Related UX surfaces

If Option C ships, UI surface is likely **Settings → Normalization Rules → new button**: "Apply to existing channels". Modal shows:
- Current name → normalized name (diff list, only non-empty diffs)
- Collision flag per row (icon + "matches existing channel 'X'")
- Per-row action: Rename / Merge into existing / Skip
- Bulk "accept all non-colliding" button
- Execute button → journal entries + toast

If Option A ships, UI surface is a settings toggle ("Auto-normalize channels on refresh") and a post-refresh notification showing N channels renamed.

## Reporter's 3 asks — mapping to options
1. "Existing channels re-normalized on refresh/rebuild" → Option A
2. "Auto-merge of channels with matching normalized names" → Option B
3. "A 'force re-normalize existing channels' action/button" → Option C

## Recommended direction
**Option C first**, with Option A as a follow-up toggle.

Rationale:
- Option C is the smallest safe change, exposes the diff to the user before mutating anything (matches project conventions — see merge_streams prune `dry_run` pattern)
- Reuses existing `NormalizationEngine.normalize()`, `extract_core_name()`, and the rename/merge code paths in `auto_creation_executor.py:532-555` and `routers/channels.py:merge_channels`
- Lets us discover edge cases (collisions, group mismatches, unicode oddities) on user-initiated runs before making it automatic
- Once stable, Option A becomes "run Option C's execute path on M3U refresh" with a settings toggle

Option B (auto-merge) is not recommended as the first delivery — merging semantics vary (which logo/EPG wins?) and conflating normalization with dedup will confuse users. Leave the existing manual merge UI alone for now.

## Acceptance criteria (proposed — PO to refine)
- [ ] New endpoint `POST /api/normalization/apply-to-channels` with `dry_run: bool` returning per-channel diff + collision flags
- [ ] Execute path renames non-colliding, optionally merges colliding (per-row `action` param), writes journal entries
- [ ] Settings page button + modal showing diff, enables user to accept/reject per row or in bulk
- [ ] Unit tests: normalization application, collision detection, merge-on-collision
- [ ] Integration test: seed DB with "RTL ᴿᴬᵂ" + apply rules + assert renamed to "RTL"
- [ ] No regression: existing `_execute_create_channel` rename path (line 532-555) still works on new-channel creation

## Unexpected findings
- The executor already builds `_normalized_name_to_channel` and `_core_name_to_channel` maps for every run (`auto_creation_executor.py:190-225`), so the cost of iterating channels through normalization is already paid on every auto-creation pipeline run — we just never **write back** the normalized names. Reusing this index for Option C is almost free.
- `run_on_refresh` defaults to `False` (`models.py:1424`). Even users who **have** enabled normalization don't get the existing-channel rename path (line 532-555) triggered on refresh unless they also flip this flag per rule. This is a UX trap — consider surfacing it in the normalization settings (out of scope here, but worth noting).
- GH-92 (`enhancedchannelmanager-r9mtd`) — "separate-not-merge option" — is related conceptually (both touch auto-creation name handling) but solves the inverse problem (user wants explicit separation, not auto-merge). No shared code-change surface expected.

**Do NOT close this bead.** Investigation only — PO reviews findings, picks a direction, then spawn fix bead(s).
notes: 2026-04-22: PO picked combined scope (Option A from revised recommendation) = Option C (force-re-normalize button + dry-run preview + backend endpoint) PLUS the lookup fix at backend/auto_creation_executor.py:514 to consult the already-built _normalized_name_to_channel map. Rationale: Option C alone cleans existing duplicates but doesn't prevent future ones; the lookup fix is 'almost free' (map already built) and prevents new duplicates at creation time. Together = 'just works' without needing Option A's (auto-on-refresh) automation. Delegated to project-engineer subagent 2026-04-22. PO directive: 'do it right away.'

title: Fix article stripping breaking AWE channel matching

comments.author: lecaptainc
comments.text:
Fixed article stripping breaking AWE channel matching.

Root cause: The regex `/^(the|a|an)(?=[a-z])/` was stripping the 'a' from 'awe' because:
- 'a' matches the article pattern
- 'we' follows (a letter follows after 'a')

This turned 'awe' into 'we', which couldn't match the call sign 'awe' (from AWEHD).

Fix: Added minimum length requirement (4+ chars) for the remaining text after stripping an article.
- 'awe' → 'we' (2 chars) - too short, no stripping → remains 'awe' ✓
- 'thebobrosschannel' → 'bobrosschannel' (14 chars) - long enough, strips → 'bobrosschannel' ✓

Version: 0.2.30059

description: Create useBulkOperation hook to eliminate ~400 lines of duplicate code across 3 bulk modals: BulkEPGAssignModal (1,208 lines), BulkLCNFetchModal (491 lines), NormalizeNamesModal. All share: phase management (analyzing→reviewing→applying), progress tracking, conflict resolution UI, batch processing async loops, operation summary display. Hook should provide generic phase/progress/results state management and batch processing utilities.
close_reason: After detailed analysis: NormalizeNamesModal (92 lines) doesn't use phases at all - it's synchronous. BulkEPGAssignModal and BulkLCNFetchModal share only ~25-30 lines of boilerplate each (phase state, run-once ref, reset on close). The rest is domain-specific (EPG matching vs LCN fetching, different result types, different categorization). Creating a generic useBulkOperation hook would require complex generics for phase names, result types, and progress tracking, adding abstraction overhead without proportional benefit. Estimated savings ~50-60 lines, not 400. Closed as low ROI.

title: Amend ADR-001 with scope clarification (dep-bump-only, not general security gating)
description:
ADR-001 was drafted and accepted 2026-04-20. Its scope is PR-to-dev validation gate for DEPENDENCY UPGRADES (paths-filter on manifest files). PR #82 discussion surfaced that a reader could reasonably interpret 'validation gate' more broadly — specifically as also covering general code-security gating. It does not.

Amendment (light — one section, no decision change):
1. Add explicit 'Out of Scope' section that names:
   - General PR-to-dev code-security gating (deferred to ADR-003/bd-sm3n3)
   - Dev->main release-cut promotion discipline (deferred to ADR-002/bd-4lk1q)
   - Two-CodeQL-scan reconciliation (deferred to bd-bsbr3 — likely determined to be a non-issue)
2. Add 'Related ADRs' pointing forward to ADR-002 and ADR-003 once those are drafted.
3. Status unchanged (Accepted). Amendment is clarification, not material change.

P2 because the original decision stands — this is docs hygiene to prevent future confusion. Can be bundled with whichever ADR lands next.
notes: PR #155 opened: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/155 — branch docs/adr-001-scope-clarification. Awaiting review/merge before close.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #155. docs/adr/ADR-001-dependency-upgrade-validation-gate.md +35/-2: added Scope section near top — In Scope (dep-upgrade cadence, paths-filter fresh-image gate, ADR-001 PR-tagging), Out of Scope with forwarding pointers (→ ADR-005 for security gating, → ADR-004 for release-cut, per-PR feature/refactor validation), Relationship to ADR-005 comparison table making the boundary unambiguous (fresh-image smoke vs CodeQL delta-zero, both apply to dep-bump PRs, neither substitutes). Cross-refs added: ADR-005 (sm3n3), ADR-004 (4lk1q), amendment bead (umaop). Decision/constraints/acceptance criteria byte-identical — pure docs hygiene.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Fixed issue where scheduled probes could use stale channel group selections. When users changed which groups to probe in Settings and saved, the prober's internal list wasn't updated - only manual probes (which pass groups from UI) worked correctly, while scheduled probes used the old groups from when the prober was initialized.

title: Bug: ecm-mcp SSE server rejects mcp-remote OAuth discovery → Claude Desktop can't connect (gh #218)
description:
GH #218 (reporter swarthyplacebo, against v0.16.0-0056, ecm-mcp 0.16.0-0075-dev). Claude Desktop via mcp-remote can't connect to ecm-mcp:6101. mcp-remote unconditionally runs OAuth Dynamic Client Registration discovery before connecting; the probe it sends to /sse is rejected by the MCP Python SDK 1.27.0 SSE handler (mcp/server/sse.py:132 connect_sse → ValueError 'Request validation failed' → 421 → disconnect). Direct curl with Authorization: Bearer (or ?api_key=) works — auth is correct; the MCP server itself is fully functional. ecm-mcp uses the DEPRECATED FastMCP.sse_app() transport (mcp-server/server.py:96) on /sse + /messages/, behind an APIKeyAuthMiddleware, SDK pinned at mcp==1.27.0.

Scope/severity: affects ONLY the Claude-Desktop-via-mcp-remote ingress; Claude Code (direct SSE via .mcp.json) works; channel-management core unaffected — P2 'severely degraded' overstated, suggest P3. Reporter found a working workaround (Claude Desktop → mcp-remote → supergateway:6202 (streamableHttp) → mcp-proxy → ecm-mcp:6101/sse?api_key=...) and offered to share their systemd/supergateway config; also noted Authorization:Bearer fails silently through mcp-proxy, must use ?api_key= query param.

Fix options:
1. Modernize transport: migrate ecm-mcp from FastMCP.sse_app() (deprecated) to Streamable HTTP (mcp.streamable_http_app(), endpoint /mcp) + bump mcp SDK. Streamable HTTP is the current standard and mcp-remote handles it cleanly (the reporter's workaround literally translates SSE→streamableHttp). Requires: new endpoint path, update docker-compose.mcp.yml + README + .mcp.json + docs/architecture.md, adapt APIKeyAuthMiddleware path checks, keep SSE as a deprecated alias if backward-compat matters. Medium. ← recommended proper fix.
2. Discovery-fallback route: add /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server + /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource returning a clean 404 / explicit 'no-OAuth, API-key only' so mcp-remote's discovery short-circuits and falls through to direct connect without probing /sse in the 421-ing way. Small — but verify mcp-remote actually falls through after a clean 404 (reporter's analysis suggests the /sse probe is the killer, so this may not be sufficient alone).
3. Investigate the exact 'Request validation failed' (is it the SDK's TransportSecurity Origin/Host DNS-rebinding check at connect_sse?); if so, allow mcp-remote's origin (the server is API-key-gated anyway). Small but security-adjacent.
4. Docs (do regardless): document the working Claude Desktop config (the supergateway/mcp-proxy chain, with the ?api_key= gotcha) OR steer Claude Desktop users to a known-good path; the README currently shows a bare {url:'...:6101/sse?api_key=...'} which doesn't work for mcp-remote-based setups. Small.

Same reporter as gh #217 — credit in the resolution.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.16.0-0078 (PR #232) — migrated ecm-mcp from deprecated FastMCP.sse_app() to Streamable HTTP (/mcp); disabled DNS-rebinding Host allowlist (localhost-only default would 421 remote clients; API key remains the gate); rewrote test_server.py (28 tests); updated .mcp.json/README/architecture/docker-compose/MCPSettingsSection. GH #218 commented & closed. Follow-ups: SDK bump (separate, ADR-001 gate), committed .mcp.json key scrub, '124 tools' wording.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Onboarding finding (RED, security). The fix applied under ppe28.1 was scoped to ffmpeg_builder/probe.py and routers/stream_preview.py — it missed the scheduled probing path. backend/stream_prober.py:903-923 (_run_ffprobe) and :1030-1060 (black-screen ffmpeg scan) invoke ffprobe/ffmpeg against user-controlled URLs without -protocol_whitelist. This is the hottest path (runs continuously) and accepts URLs sourced from Dispatcharr stream rows — exploitable via file://, concat:, subfile:, data:, etc. Remediation: add '-protocol_whitelist', 'http,https,tcp,udp,rtp,rtmp,pipe' to both commands. Consider reopening ppe28.1 or linking this bead as its true closure. Flagged by Security Engineer during /onboard 2026-04-19.

description: Create backend/tests/unit/test_auth.py with tests for: password hashing/verification, JWT creation/validation, token expiry handling, invalid token rejection. Minimum 90% coverage on auth utilities.

title: Write integration tests for auth endpoints
description: Create backend/tests/integration/test_api_auth.py with tests for: login success/failure, logout, token refresh, protected endpoint access, admin-only endpoints. Test cookie handling and error responses.

title: Write frontend auth hook tests
description: Create frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.test.ts and AuthContext tests with: login/logout flow, token refresh, auth state persistence, error handling, loading states. Use MSW for API mocking.

title: Create User and Session database models
description: Add SQLAlchemy models: User (id, username, email, password_hash, auth_provider, external_id, is_active, is_admin, created_at, updated_at), Session (id, user_id, token, refresh_token, expires_at, ip_address, user_agent, created_at). Include Alembic migration.

title: Implement password hashing utilities
description: Create backend/auth/password.py with functions: hash_password(plain) -> hashed, verify_password(plain, hashed) -> bool. Use passlib with bcrypt. Include configurable rounds for testing vs production.

title: Implement JWT token generation and validation
description: Create backend/auth/tokens.py with: create_access_token(user_id, claims) -> token, create_refresh_token(user_id) -> token, decode_token(token) -> claims, validate_token(token) -> user_id. Use python-jose with RS256 or HS256. Support configurable expiry times.

title: Create auth configuration settings
description: Add to config.py: auth_enabled (bool), auth_methods (list), jwt_secret_key, jwt_algorithm, access_token_expire_minutes, refresh_token_expire_days, session_cookie_name, session_cookie_secure, session_cookie_httponly. Generate secret key on first run if not set.

title: Implement auth middleware and dependencies
description: Create backend/auth/middleware.py with FastAPI dependencies: get_current_user(token) -> User, get_current_active_user() -> User, require_admin() -> User. Implement cookie-based token extraction. Handle expired tokens gracefully. Return 401 for unauthenticated, 403 for unauthorized.

title: Create login and logout API endpoints
description: Create backend/auth/routes.py with endpoints: POST /api/auth/login (username, password) -> set cookie + user data, POST /api/auth/logout -> clear cookie, POST /api/auth/refresh -> refresh access token, GET /api/auth/me -> current user info. Add router to main.py.

title: Create AuthContext and useAuth hook
description: Create frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx with: user state, isAuthenticated, isLoading, login(), logout(), refreshToken(). Create useAuth() hook for easy access. Persist auth state across page reloads. Check session validity on app mount.

title: Create Login page component
description: Create frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx with: username/password form, auth method selector (when multiple enabled), error display, loading state, redirect to app after login. Style to match existing dark/light theme. Show ECM branding.

title: Local Authentication
description: Local username/password authentication with user registration, password management, and session handling. The most basic auth method that works standalone.

title: Implement user registration endpoint
description: POST /api/auth/register: username, email, password, confirm_password. Validate password strength, unique username/email, hash password, create user. Option to require admin approval for new users. Rate limit registration attempts.
close_reason: Removing user registration - ECM is admin tool, only admins create accounts

title: Write local auth integration tests
description: Create backend/tests/integration/test_local_auth.py: registration flow, login with local user, password change, password reset flow (mock email), admin user management. Test validation errors and edge cases.

title: Implement password reset flow
description: POST /api/auth/forgot-password: email -> send reset token. POST /api/auth/reset-password: token, new_password -> validate token, update password. Tokens expire in 1 hour. Use existing SMTP settings for email. Create email template.

title: Implement change password endpoint
description: POST /api/auth/change-password (authenticated): current_password, new_password, confirm_password. Verify current password, validate new password strength, update hash. Optionally invalidate other sessions.

title: Create user management admin endpoints
description: Admin-only endpoints: GET /api/admin/users (list users), POST /api/admin/users (create user), PATCH /api/admin/users/{id} (update), DELETE /api/admin/users/{id} (deactivate). Include pagination, search, filter by status.

title: Create registration page component
description: Create frontend/src/pages/RegisterPage.tsx: username, email, password fields, password strength indicator, terms checkbox (optional), submit with loading state. Link from login page. Redirect to login on success.
close_reason: Removing user registration - ECM is admin tool, only admins create accounts

title: Create password reset page components
description: Create ForgotPasswordPage.tsx (email form, success message) and ResetPasswordPage.tsx (new password form with token from URL). Handle expired/invalid tokens gracefully. Link from login page.

title: Create user management admin UI
description: Add Users tab to Settings (admin only): user list table, create user modal, edit user modal, deactivate confirmation. Show auth provider, status, last login. Search and filter controls.

title: Implement first-run setup wizard
description: When no users exist and auth enabled: show setup wizard to create first admin user. Collect username, email, password. Skip Dispatcharr config if not needed. Prevent access to app until complete.

title: Write local auth unit tests
description: Create backend/tests/unit/test_local_auth.py: password validation rules, username validation, email format validation, password reset token generation/validation. Test edge cases and error conditions.

title: Dispatcharr Authentication
description: Authenticate ECM users via Dispatcharr credentials. Allows users who have Dispatcharr accounts to use those same credentials for ECM login, creating a unified experience.

title: Implement Dispatcharr authentication endpoint
description: POST /api/auth/dispatcharr/login: username, password. Forward credentials to Dispatcharr token endpoint, on success create/update local user with auth_provider='dispatcharr', return JWT. Store Dispatcharr user ID as external_id.

title: Create Dispatcharr auth service
description: Create backend/auth/dispatcharr.py: validate_credentials(url, username, password) -> user_info, get_user_profile(url, token) -> profile. Use existing httpx client pattern. Handle connection errors, invalid credentials, timeout.

title: Add Dispatcharr auth configuration
description: Add to config: dispatcharr_auth_enabled (bool), dispatcharr_auth_url (defaults to main url), dispatcharr_auto_create_users (bool), dispatcharr_default_role ('user' or 'admin'). Validate URL on save.

title: Add Dispatcharr login option to Login page
description: Update LoginPage.tsx: when Dispatcharr auth enabled, show 'Login with Dispatcharr' button or tab. Same username/password form but submits to /api/auth/dispatcharr/login. Show Dispatcharr branding/icon.

title: Add Dispatcharr auth settings UI
description: Add Dispatcharr Auth section to Settings: enable/disable toggle, URL override field, auto-create users toggle, default role selector. Test connection button. Show when Dispatcharr is configured.

title: Write Dispatcharr auth tests
description: Create backend/tests/unit/test_dispatcharr_auth.py and integration tests: mock Dispatcharr API responses, test successful auth, invalid credentials, connection errors, user creation/update. Test token handling.

title: OIDC Authentication
description: OpenID Connect authentication supporting providers like Authentik, Keycloak, Auth0, Okta, Google, and Azure AD. Implements authorization code flow with PKCE.

title: Add OIDC dependencies
description: Add to requirements.txt: authlib (for OIDC client), httpx (already present). Authlib provides OAuth2Session and OIDC discovery support.

title: Write OIDC unit tests
description: Create backend/tests/unit/test_oidc.py: test discovery parsing, token validation (mock JWKS), PKCE generation, state/nonce validation, claim extraction. Use fixtures for sample tokens.

title: Write OIDC integration tests
description: Create backend/tests/integration/test_oidc.py: mock OIDC provider endpoints, test full flow (login redirect, callback, user creation), test provider CRUD, test error handling (invalid state, expired token).

title: Create OIDC provider configuration model
description: Add OIDCProvider model: id, name, issuer_url, client_id, client_secret, scopes, auto_discover (bool), authorization_endpoint, token_endpoint, userinfo_endpoint, jwks_uri, enabled, created_at. Support multiple providers.

title: Implement OIDC discovery and metadata
description: Create backend/auth/oidc.py: discover_provider(issuer_url) -> metadata from .well-known/openid-configuration. Cache discovery results. Validate required fields. Support manual endpoint configuration fallback.

title: Implement OIDC authorization flow
description: GET /api/auth/oidc/{provider}/login: generate PKCE code_verifier, state, nonce. Store in session/cookie. Redirect to authorization_endpoint. GET /api/auth/oidc/{provider}/callback: validate state, exchange code for tokens, validate id_token, create/update user, return JWT.

title: Implement OIDC token validation
description: Validate id_token: verify signature using JWKS, check iss, aud, exp, nonce claims. Extract user info (sub, email, name, preferred_username). Support RS256, RS384, RS512 algorithms.

title: Implement OIDC user provisioning
description: On OIDC login: lookup user by provider+external_id (sub claim). If not found and auto_create enabled, create user with email/username from claims. Map groups claim to roles if configured. Update user on each login.

title: Create OIDC provider management endpoints
description: Admin endpoints: GET /api/admin/oidc/providers, POST /api/admin/oidc/providers (with discovery test), PATCH /api/admin/oidc/providers/{id}, DELETE /api/admin/oidc/providers/{id}. Return list of enabled providers for login page.

title: Add OIDC provider buttons to Login page
description: Update LoginPage.tsx: fetch enabled OIDC providers from /api/auth/providers. Show button for each: 'Login with {name}'. Button redirects to /api/auth/oidc/{provider}/login. Handle callback redirect back to app.

title: Create OIDC provider management UI
description: Add OIDC Providers section to Settings (admin): provider list, add provider form (name, issuer URL, client ID, client secret), auto-discover button, manual endpoint fields, scope configuration, test button. Edit/delete providers.

title: SAML Authentication
description: SAML 2.0 Service Provider implementation for enterprise SSO integration. Supports IdPs like Okta, Azure AD, OneLogin, and ADFS.

title: Add SAML dependencies
description: Add to requirements.txt: python3-saml (OneLogin's SAML toolkit), xmlsec (for signature validation). These handle SAML request/response signing and assertion parsing.

title: Add SAML provider buttons to Login page
description: Update LoginPage.tsx: fetch enabled SAML providers. Show 'Login with {name}' button for each. Button redirects to /api/auth/saml/{provider}/login. Handle ACS redirect back to app.

title: Create SAML provider management UI
description: Add SAML Providers section to Settings: provider list, add form (name, metadata URL or upload), attribute mapping UI, SP metadata download button. Show IdP cert expiry warning.

title: Write SAML unit tests
description: Create backend/tests/unit/test_saml.py: test metadata parsing, AuthnRequest generation, Response validation (use sample assertions), signature verification, attribute extraction. Mock xmlsec.

title: Write SAML integration tests
description: Create backend/tests/integration/test_saml.py: test full flow with mock IdP, SP metadata generation, provider CRUD, attribute mapping, error handling (invalid signature, expired assertion).

title: Create SAML provider configuration model
description: Add SAMLProvider model: id, name, entity_id, sso_url, slo_url, x509_cert, metadata_url, auto_discover, attribute_mapping (JSON), enabled, created_at. Store SP certificate/key for signing.

title: Generate SAML SP metadata
description: GET /api/auth/saml/{provider}/metadata: return XML SP metadata with entity ID, ACS URL, SLO URL, signing certificate. Auto-generate SP certificate on first run. IdPs import this to configure trust.

title: Implement SAML IdP metadata import
description: Create backend/auth/saml.py: parse_idp_metadata(xml_or_url) -> extract entity_id, SSO URL, SLO URL, x509 cert. Support metadata URL auto-refresh. Validate certificate and required fields.

title: Implement SAML authentication flow
description: GET /api/auth/saml/{provider}/login: generate AuthnRequest, redirect to IdP SSO URL. POST /api/auth/saml/{provider}/acs: validate Response signature, parse Assertion, extract attributes, create/update user, return JWT.

title: Implement SAML assertion validation
description: Validate SAML Response: verify XML signature using IdP certificate, check Issuer, Audience, NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter conditions, InResponseTo matches request. Reject replayed assertions (track IDs).

title: Implement SAML attribute mapping
description: Map SAML attributes to user fields: configurable mapping from attribute names (e.g., 'urn:oid:0.9.2342.19200300.100.1.1' or 'uid') to user fields (username, email, name). Support group attribute for role mapping.

title: Implement SAML SLO (Single Logout)
description: GET /api/auth/saml/{provider}/logout: generate LogoutRequest, redirect to IdP SLO. Handle IdP-initiated logout: POST /api/auth/saml/{provider}/slo. Clear local session on logout.

title: Create SAML provider management endpoints
description: Admin endpoints: GET /api/admin/saml/providers, POST (with metadata import), PATCH, DELETE. Return SP metadata download link. Test IdP connection option.

title: LDAP Authentication
description: LDAP/Active Directory authentication with bind authentication, user search, attribute mapping, and optional group sync for role-based access.

title: Add LDAP dependencies
description: Add to requirements.txt: ldap3 (pure Python LDAP client, async-compatible). Provides connection pooling, SSL/TLS support, and AD compatibility.

title: Create LDAP configuration UI
description: Add LDAP section to Settings: server URL, SSL/TLS options, bind credentials, search base/filter fields, attribute mapping, admin group DN. Test connection button. Show connection status.

title: Write LDAP unit tests
description: Create backend/tests/unit/test_ldap.py: test filter generation, DN parsing, attribute extraction, group membership check. Mock ldap3 connection. Test AD vs OpenLDAP differences.

title: Write LDAP integration tests
description: Create backend/tests/integration/test_ldap.py: use mock LDAP server (ldap3 mock or testcontainers), test bind authentication, user search, group lookup, config CRUD, error handling.

title: Create LDAP configuration model
description: Add LDAPConfig model: id, name, server_url, use_ssl, use_starttls, bind_dn, bind_password, user_search_base, user_filter, username_attribute, email_attribute, group_search_base, group_filter, admin_group_dn, enabled, created_at.

title: Implement LDAP connection service
description: Create backend/auth/ldap.py: connect(config) -> connection, test_connection(config) -> bool. Support SSL/TLS, StartTLS. Handle connection pooling. Timeout configuration. Error handling for network issues.

title: Implement LDAP bind authentication
description: authenticate(username, password) -> user_dn or None. Two modes: 1) Direct bind with user DN, 2) Service account search then bind. Support AD UPN format (user@domain). Return clear error on invalid credentials vs connection error.

title: Implement LDAP user search
description: search_user(username) -> user_entry. Search user_search_base with user_filter (e.g., '(sAMAccountName={username})'). Extract attributes: username, email, display name. Handle multiple results (error or first match).

title: Implement LDAP group membership check
description: get_user_groups(user_dn) -> list of group DNs. is_admin(user_dn, admin_group_dn) -> bool. Support nested groups (AD memberOf:1.2.840.113556.1.4.1941:). Map groups to roles.

title: Implement LDAP authentication endpoint
description: POST /api/auth/ldap/login: username, password, config_id (optional). Bind to LDAP, search user, check groups, create/update local user with auth_provider='ldap', return JWT. Store LDAP DN as external_id.

title: Create LDAP configuration management endpoints
description: Admin endpoints: GET /api/admin/ldap/configs, POST (with test connection), PATCH, DELETE. Test connection button. Validate bind credentials on save.

title: Add LDAP login option to Login page
description: Update LoginPage.tsx: when LDAP enabled, show 'Login with LDAP' tab or integrate with local login form. Same username/password form submits to /api/auth/ldap/login. Show domain hint if configured.

title: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
description: Granular user and group permissions within ECM. Define roles (admin, operator, viewer), permissions (read/write per feature), and group membership. Control access to channels, settings, M3U accounts, EPG sources, and administrative functions.

title: Create Role and Permission database models
description: Add models: Role (id, name, description, is_system, created_at), Permission (id, resource, action, description), RolePermission (role_id, permission_id), UserRole (user_id, role_id), Group (id, name, description), GroupMember (group_id, user_id), GroupRole (group_id, role_id). Create system roles: admin, operator, viewer.

title: Add role assignment to user management UI
description: Update user edit modal: add role assignment section (multi-select), show group memberships (read-only), show effective permissions summary. Quick role assignment from user list.

title: Implement frontend permission-based UI hiding
description: Create usePermission hook: hasPermission(resource, action). Create PermissionGate component to conditionally render children. Hide/disable UI elements user lacks permission for: edit buttons, delete buttons, settings tabs, admin sections.

title: Add RBAC TypeScript types
description: Add types: Role (id, name, permissions, is_system), Permission (resource, action), Group (id, name, members, roles), UserPermissions (roles, groups, effective_permissions). Resource and Action enums.

title: Write RBAC unit tests
description: Create backend/tests/unit/test_rbac.py: test permission checking logic, role inheritance, group membership, permission caching, edge cases (no roles, multiple groups, overlapping permissions).

title: Write RBAC integration tests
description: Create backend/tests/integration/test_rbac.py: test role/group/permission CRUD, access control on protected endpoints, 403 responses, permission inheritance, role assignment.

title: Define permission resources and actions
description: Define resource types: channels, streams, m3u_accounts, epg_sources, tasks, settings, users, roles. Actions per resource: read, create, update, delete, execute (for tasks). Seed default permissions. Create permission constants/enum.

title: Implement permission checking service
description: Create backend/auth/permissions.py: has_permission(user, resource, action) -> bool, get_user_permissions(user) -> set, check_permission(user, resource, action) raises 403. Cache permissions per session. Support group-based permissions inheritance.

title: Implement permission decorators and dependencies
description: Create FastAPI dependencies: require_permission(resource, action), require_any_permission([...]), require_all_permissions([...]). Apply to existing endpoints. Return 403 Forbidden with clear error message.

title: Create role management API endpoints
description: Admin endpoints: GET /api/admin/roles (list with permissions), POST /api/admin/roles, PATCH /api/admin/roles/{id}, DELETE /api/admin/roles/{id}. Prevent deletion of system roles. Clone role functionality.

title: Create group management API endpoints
description: Admin endpoints: GET /api/admin/groups, POST, PATCH, DELETE. Manage group members: POST/DELETE /api/admin/groups/{id}/members. Assign roles to groups: POST/DELETE /api/admin/groups/{id}/roles.

title: Create user role assignment endpoints
description: Admin endpoints: GET /api/admin/users/{id}/roles, POST /api/admin/users/{id}/roles, DELETE /api/admin/users/{id}/roles/{role_id}. Show effective permissions (user + group roles combined).

title: Create roles management UI
description: Add Roles section to Settings: role list table, create role modal with permission checkboxes (grouped by resource), edit role, delete confirmation. Show system role badge. Clone role button.

title: Create groups management UI
description: Add Groups section to Settings: group list, create group modal, manage members (add/remove users), assign roles to group. Show member count and role badges.

title: Authentication Test Specifications
description: Functional test specifications that define authentication capabilities. Write these tests FIRST - they will fail initially. Implementation work makes them pass. This epic must complete before implementation begins.

title: Test Spec: Core Auth Flow
description:
Write pytest tests that define core auth behavior (all will fail initially):

**Login Flow:**
- POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials returns 200 + JWT cookie + user data
- POST /api/auth/login with invalid credentials returns 401
- POST /api/auth/login with missing fields returns 422

**Session Management:**
- GET /api/auth/me with valid token returns current user
- GET /api/auth/me without token returns 401
- GET /api/auth/me with expired token returns 401
- POST /api/auth/refresh with valid refresh token returns new access token
- POST /api/auth/logout clears session cookie

**Protected Endpoints:**
- All /api/* endpoints (except /auth/login, /health) require authentication
- Requests without token return 401
- Requests with invalid token return 401

title: Test Spec: RBAC Permissions
description:
Write pytest tests for role-based access control:

**System Roles:**
- 'admin' role has all permissions
- 'operator' role has read/write but not user management
- 'viewer' role has read-only access
- System roles cannot be deleted

**Permission Checking:**
- has_permission(user, 'channels', 'read') returns True for viewer
- has_permission(user, 'channels', 'write') returns False for viewer
- has_permission(user, 'users', 'write') returns True only for admin
- Permissions are inherited from groups

**Endpoint Protection:**
- GET endpoints require 'read' permission
- POST/PATCH/DELETE require 'write' permission
- /api/admin/* requires admin role
- 403 returned with clear error message

**Role Management:**
- Admin can create custom roles
- Custom roles can be assigned subset of permissions
- Roles can be assigned to users
- Roles can be assigned to groups

title: Test Spec: Group Management
description:
Write pytest tests for groups:

**Group CRUD:**
- POST /api/admin/groups creates group
- GET /api/admin/groups returns all groups with member count
- PATCH /api/admin/groups/{id} updates group
- DELETE /api/admin/groups/{id} removes group

**Membership:**
- POST /api/admin/groups/{id}/members adds user to group
- DELETE /api/admin/groups/{id}/members/{user_id} removes user
- User inherits all roles assigned to their groups
- Removing user from group removes inherited roles

**Role Assignment:**
- POST /api/admin/groups/{id}/roles assigns role to group
- All group members gain role permissions
- DELETE /api/admin/groups/{id}/roles/{role_id} removes role
- Role removal reflects in member permissions

title: Test Spec: Frontend Auth Flow
description:
Write Vitest/React Testing Library tests:

**AuthContext:**
- useAuth() returns user when authenticated
- useAuth() returns null when not authenticated
- login() calls API and updates state on success
- login() throws on failure, state unchanged
- logout() calls API and clears state
- Auth state persists across page reload

**Protected Routes:**
- Unauthenticated user redirected to /login
- Authenticated user can access protected routes
- Loading spinner shown during auth check

**Login Page:**
- Form validates required fields
- Submit calls login() with credentials
- Error message shown on failure
- Redirect to app on success
- Auth method buttons shown when multiple enabled

title: Test Spec: Frontend Permission UI
description:
Write Vitest/React Testing Library tests:

**Permission Hook:**
- usePermissions() returns user's effective permissions
- hasPermission('channels', 'write') returns boolean
- Permissions refresh on user change

**Permission Gate:**
- <PermissionGate resource='channels' action='write'> renders children when allowed
- <PermissionGate> renders nothing when denied
- <PermissionGate fallback={<Disabled/>}> renders fallback when denied

**UI Elements:**
- Edit buttons hidden for read-only users
- Delete buttons hidden for users without delete permission
- Admin tabs hidden for non-admin users
- Settings restricted fields disabled based on permissions

title: Test Spec: E2E Auth Flows
description:
Write Playwright E2E tests:

**First Run:**
- Fresh install shows setup wizard
- Create admin user completes setup
- Login works after setup

**Local Login:**
- Login page loads with form
- Valid credentials → redirected to app
- Invalid credentials → error message
- Logout → redirected to login

**Session:**
- Refresh page maintains session
- Session expires after timeout
- Expired session → redirected to login

**Admin Functions:**
- Admin can access user management
- Admin can create/edit/delete users
- Admin can manage roles and groups
- Non-admin cannot access admin pages

**OIDC Flow (mock provider):**
- Click OIDC button → redirected to provider
- Complete provider flow → redirected back, logged in

title: Test Spec: Password Security
description:
Write pytest tests for password handling:

**Hashing:**
- hash_password() returns bcrypt hash (starts with $2b$)
- hash_password() with same input returns different hashes (salt)
- verify_password() returns True for correct password
- verify_password() returns False for incorrect password
- Hashing takes >100ms (cost factor protection)

**Validation:**
- Password must be >= 8 characters
- Password must contain uppercase, lowercase, number
- Password cannot be common (password123, qwerty, etc.)
- Password cannot match username

title: Test Spec: JWT Tokens
description:
Write pytest tests for JWT handling:

**Token Creation:**
- create_access_token() returns valid JWT string
- Token contains user_id, exp, iat claims
- Token expires after configured time (default 30 min)
- create_refresh_token() returns longer-lived token (default 7 days)

**Token Validation:**
- decode_token() returns claims for valid token
- decode_token() raises for expired token
- decode_token() raises for tampered token
- decode_token() raises for invalid signature
- decode_token() raises for malformed token

**Token Refresh:**
- Valid refresh token can generate new access token
- Used refresh token is invalidated (one-time use)
- Refresh token rotation creates new refresh token

title: Test Spec: User Management
description:
Write pytest tests for user CRUD:

**Registration:**
- POST /api/auth/register creates new user, returns 201
- Registration with existing username returns 409
- Registration with existing email returns 409
- Registration with weak password returns 422
- New user has is_admin=False by default

**User CRUD (admin only):**
- GET /api/admin/users returns paginated user list
- GET /api/admin/users?search=term filters results
- POST /api/admin/users creates user (admin bypass password rules optional)
- PATCH /api/admin/users/{id} updates user fields
- DELETE /api/admin/users/{id} deactivates (soft delete)
- Non-admin cannot access /api/admin/* endpoints (403)

**Password Management:**
- POST /api/auth/change-password with correct current password succeeds
- POST /api/auth/change-password with wrong current password returns 401
- POST /api/auth/forgot-password sends reset email (mock SMTP)
- POST /api/auth/reset-password with valid token changes password
- POST /api/auth/reset-password with expired token returns 400
- Reset token expires after 1 hour

title: Test Spec: First-Run Setup
description:
Write pytest tests for first-run experience:

**Setup Detection:**
- GET /api/auth/setup-required returns {required: true} when no users exist
- GET /api/auth/setup-required returns {required: false} when users exist
- All protected endpoints redirect to setup when no users exist

**Admin Creation:**
- POST /api/auth/setup creates first admin user
- POST /api/auth/setup only works when no users exist
- POST /api/auth/setup returns 403 if users already exist
- First user is automatically is_admin=True
- After setup, normal auth flow works

title: Test Spec: Dispatcharr Authentication
description:
Write pytest tests for Dispatcharr auth (mock Dispatcharr API):

**Authentication:**
- POST /api/auth/dispatcharr/login with valid creds calls Dispatcharr token endpoint
- Successful Dispatcharr auth creates/updates local user with auth_provider='dispatcharr'
- Dispatcharr user external_id is stored
- Failed Dispatcharr auth returns 401 with clear error
- Dispatcharr connection timeout returns 503

**User Sync:**
- Dispatcharr user info is synced on each login
- Email/username updates from Dispatcharr are reflected locally
- Dispatcharr user can't use local password login

**Configuration:**
- Dispatcharr auth only available when enabled in settings
- GET /api/auth/providers includes dispatcharr when enabled

title: Test Spec: OIDC Authentication
description:
Write pytest tests for OIDC (mock OIDC provider):

**Discovery:**
- Fetching .well-known/openid-configuration extracts endpoints
- Missing required fields in discovery raises error
- Discovery results are cached

**Authorization Flow:**
- GET /api/auth/oidc/{provider}/login redirects to authorization_endpoint
- Redirect includes client_id, redirect_uri, scope, state, nonce, code_challenge
- State and nonce are stored in session

**Callback:**
- GET /api/auth/oidc/{provider}/callback exchanges code for tokens
- Invalid state returns 400
- Token exchange failure returns 401
- id_token signature is validated against JWKS
- id_token claims (iss, aud, exp, nonce) are validated
- Successful callback creates/updates user, returns JWT

**User Provisioning:**
- User created with auth_provider='oidc', external_id=sub claim
- Email extracted from email claim
- Username from preferred_username or email
- Groups claim mapped to roles if configured

title: Test Spec: SAML Authentication
description:
Write pytest tests for SAML (mock IdP):

**SP Metadata:**
- GET /api/auth/saml/{provider}/metadata returns valid XML
- Metadata includes entityID, ACS URL, certificate
- SP certificate is auto-generated if not exists

**IdP Metadata Import:**
- Importing IdP metadata URL fetches and parses XML
- Importing IdP metadata file parses XML

- Expired assertion returns 401
- Assertion InResponseTo matches request ID
- Successful assertion creates/updates user
- Attributes mapped per configuration

title: mcp-server tools __init__.py imports nonexistent ffmpeg/tasks modules
description:
## Context
Discovered during bd-kdsn3 (CodeQL note-level cleanup) when verifying mcp-server module imports after touching tools/{channels,channel_groups,epg,m3u,notifications,profiles,stats}.py.

## Symptom
\`\`\`
$ python -c "from tools import register_all_tools"
ImportError: cannot import name 'ffmpeg' from partially initialized module 'tools'
\`\`\`

## Root Cause
mcp-server/tools/__init__.py lines 4-19 imports modules \`ffmpeg\` and \`tasks\` that do not exist in \`mcp-server/tools/\`:
\`\`\`
$ ls mcp-server/tools/
auto_creation.py  channel_groups.py  channels.py  epg.py  export.py  __init__.py  m3u.py  normalization.py  notifications.py  profiles.py  __pycache__  stats.py  streams.py  system.py  tasks.py
\`\`\`
Wait — \`tasks.py\` exists. \`ffmpeg.py\` does NOT. The error message says ffmpeg.

## Fix Options
1. Create the missing \`tools/ffmpeg.py\` with a stub \`register(mcp)\` function (and remove from __init__.py if no FFmpeg MCP tools are planned).
2. Remove \`ffmpeg\` from both the import and \`_MODULES\` lists in __init__.py.

## Why P2
This means \`register_all_tools()\` is currently broken — the MCP server would fail to start when the registration function runs. Either:
- The MCP server has been broken at startup since this __init__.py was added, OR
- There's another entry point that registers tools individually that I haven't found.

Needs investigation before choosing fix #1 vs #2. Verify whether MCP server is actually starting in production.

Found-via: bd-kdsn3
close_reason: Merged via PR #165 (rolled in alongside 0gcu9) — mcp-server/tools/__init__.py orphan ffmpeg import removed; python -c 'from tools import register_all_tools' now succeeds.
