title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
# Rollback v0.16.0 Release (Hard)

Owner: Project Engineer (same persona that executed bd-mghjm).
Authorized by PO 2026-04-20: user has bugs they want cleared before v0.16.0 ships.
Confirmed no consumers have pulled `:latest` or `:0.16.0` yet, so a destructive hard rollback is safe.

## Rollback Targets

- **main** → reset to `v0.15.2` @ `b277a383b4084b5025c85a8519cd385a046ecd26` (current tip `d2843d54` — the merge commit)
- **dev** → reset to `36108f35` (the [bd-0lvkk] ErrorBoundary #70 merge; package.json was `0.16.0-0040` at this point)
  - Strips these two commits from dev: `e3298a9d` (counter open to 0.17.0-0000) and `42e8c46b` (0.16.0 version bump)
- **GitHub Release** `v0.16.0` → delete (published 2026-04-20T16:00:45Z, currently non-draft/non-prerelease)
- **Git tag** `v0.16.0` → delete local + remote
- **GHCR tags** `0.16.0` and `0.16` → delete
- **GHCR `:latest`** → retag to the v0.15.2 image digest (find via `gh api` or docker registry API — the v0.15.2 release workflow produced a multi-arch manifest; point `:latest` back at it)

## Branch Protection Concern

`main` protection: `allow_force_pushes: false`, `enforce_admins: false`. Force-push needed to reset main. Options:
1. Admin bypass (since enforce_admins is false) — try first
2. If that fails, temporarily flip `allow_force_pushes: true`, do the reset, flip back

Prefer option 1. If option 2, restore protection exactly to the current config (required_status_checks: Backend Tests + Frontend Tests, strict; required_conversation_resolution: true; everything else as-is).

## Execution Order

1. Delete GitHub Release: `gh release delete v0.16.0 --yes --cleanup-tag` (cleanup-tag also removes the v0.16.0 ref)
2. Confirm v0.16.0 git tag is gone locally + on remote. If not, `git push origin :refs/tags/v0.16.0`.
3. Reset `main` to `b277a383b4084b5025c85a8519cd385a046ecd26` via worktree (root stays on dev). If force-push is blocked, follow the branch-protection-toggle approach above.
4. Reset `dev` to `36108f35`. Force-push. (No branch protection on dev per current state — verify.)
5. GHCR cleanup:
   - Find the v0.15.2 image manifest digest (look up via `gh release view v0.15.2` asset metadata, or the build.yml run for v0.15.2, or `docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/motwakorb/enhancedchannelmanager:0.15.2`)
   - Retag `:latest` → v0.15.2 digest (use `docker buildx imagetools create -t ghcr.io/motwakorb/enhancedchannelmanager:latest <v0.15.2-digest>` or equivalent)
   - Delete tags `:0.16.0` and `:0.16` via the GHCR API or `gh api` DELETE on the version IDs. These are the package-version API endpoints; scope may require a PAT with `delete:packages` if `gh auth token` doesn't carry it. If it doesn't, report back — don't improvise.
6. Verify:
   - `git log --oneline origin/main -n 3` tip = `b277a383`
   - `git log --oneline origin/dev -n 3` tip = `36108f35`
   - `gh release list` does NOT include v0.16.0
   - `gh api repos/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/git/refs/tags` does NOT include v0.16.0
   - `docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/motwakorb/enhancedchannelmanager:latest` → points at the v0.15.2 manifest (compare to `:0.15.2`)
   - `docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/motwakorb/enhancedchannelmanager:0.16.0` → Not Found
   - `docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/motwakorb/enhancedchannelmanager:0.16` → Not Found
   - Branch protection on main still at the documented config

## Follow-up After Rollback

- Reopen bd-mghjm or file a new release bead once bugs are cleared. Don't reopen automatically — PO will decide when.
- Root local checkout is on dev; after dev is reset, the root checkout will be ahead of origin/dev (by e3298a9d + 42e8c46b). PE should leave instructions for the user to `git fetch && git reset --hard origin/dev` in the root checkout (not do it from a worktree).
- PR #72 (ESLint, cr/zjge5-eslint-logger-api-any) was based on dev @ e3298a9d. After dev resets, PR #72's base vanishes. PE should comment on PR #72 explaining the rollback and that the branch needs rebase onto new dev tip. Don't close the PR.

## Hard Constraints

- Root checkout MUST stay on dev (use worktrees for any main work)
- No force push to main WITHOUT first trying admin bypass
- If branch protection toggle is needed, it MUST be restored before the task is considered done
- Destructive GHCR deletes are permanent — verify the v0.15.2 digest retagging BEFORE deleting 0.16.0/0.16/latest's current pointers
- If any step fails (e.g., missing scope on gh token), STOP and report — do NOT leave a partial rollback
notes:
[2026-04-20] Resume session 2 (PE) — GHCR cleanup completed.

DONE this session (PAT with write:packages + delete:packages provided):
- docker login ghcr.io as MotWakorb succeeded with provided PAT.
- Retagged :latest to v0.15.2 index via docker buildx imagetools create. Verified :latest raw manifest digest = 906cfdc88516c316829456c0e685ee6e847f3b72332124f946507baf9cf2e623 (matches :0.15.2).
- Deleted GHCR package version 809461924 (sha256:b5be0467...) via DELETE /users/MotWakorb/packages/container/enhancedchannelmanager/versions/809461924. This stripped tags 0.16.0, 0.16, main, main-d2843d5 in one operation (main/main-d2843d5 pointed at a commit no longer on main post-rollback, so their removal is consistent).
- Verified :0.16.0 → manifest unknown, :0.16 → manifest unknown, :0.15.2 → still present at 906cfdc8..., :latest → 906cfdc8...
- Cleanup: docker logout ghcr.io, /tmp/ecm-ghcr-token deleted, /tmp/main-protection-backup.json + /tmp/main-protection-ffp.json deleted.

Rollback complete across git + GitHub release + tag + GHCR. Closing.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Release-scoped. ECM is a public OSS project on GHCR; it has no SECURITY.md and no documented channel for reporting security vulnerabilities. Shipping a release (especially one that contains a security fix, v3xfl) without a vuln-reporting policy is a gap.

Scope:
- Create SECURITY.md at repo root following the GitHub standard template (supported versions, reporting a vulnerability, disclosure policy).
- Explicit vulnerability reporting channel: prefer GitHub Security Advisories (private disclosure via the Security tab) over email. If the maintainer wants an email fallback, use a dedicated alias, not a personal one.
- Brief disclosure timeline expectations (e.g., triage within 7 days, patch within 30 days for critical). Realistic for a solo-maintainer OSS project; don't overpromise.
- List which versions are supported for security fixes (likely just 'latest' since this is a single-maintainer project on a rolling release cadence).

Reference: current practice evidence — the closed OWASP epic (ppe28) and ongoing work on v3xfl suggest the maintainer already acts on security issues, so this is about making the process legible, not creating new process.

Out of scope:
- GitHub Security Advisory workflow setup beyond the doc itself (the maintainer enables it in repo settings).
- Bounty program (N/A for this project).

Flagged by Technical Writer during /onboard 2026-04-19. Promoted to P1 because the project is about to ship a release containing a security fix.
notes: SECURITY.md drafted on docs/release-foundation branch. PO question: do you want an email fallback alongside the GitHub Security Advisories flow? If yes, provide a dedicated alias (NOT a personal email) — e.g., security@<domain>. Current draft uses GHSA as sole reporting channel. If you want email added, tell me the alias and I'll amend the doc.

title: Bug: POST /api/settings drops mcp_api_key when omitted from request body
description:
Surfaced by the i6a1m extension agent (PR #173) while wiring telemetry_client_errors_enabled into SettingsTab. Out of scope for that work — filing here.

Symptom: when a caller POSTs to /api/settings with a partial body (e.g., only telemetry_client_errors_enabled), mcp_api_key is not preserved — it gets overwritten with null/empty instead of keeping the existing stored value. This affects any settings update flow that doesn't resend every field.

Expected: partial update semantics. Fields absent from the request body should retain their current stored value, not be cleared.

Suspected location: backend/routers/settings.py::update_settings. The handler probably does a full overwrite with the Pydantic model's defaults rather than an explicit merge against the existing settings.

Investigation steps:
1. Reproduce: GET /api/settings (record mcp_api_key), POST with just {telemetry_client_errors_enabled: true}, GET again — confirm mcp_api_key is cleared
2. Check if the Pydantic model makes mcp_api_key Optional with a default of None — if so, any partial POST will nuke it
3. Confirm whether this affects other sensitive fields (dispatcharr credentials, Fernet keys, etc.) — if so, this is higher priority than P2

Fix patterns to consider:
- Pydantic model_exclude_unset on incoming body, merge into existing settings dict
- Separate partial-update endpoint (PATCH /api/settings)
- Require full body on POST, deprecate partial updates

Acceptance:
- Reproduction test in backend/tests/routers/test_settings.py demonstrating the bug
- Fix preserves all sensitive fields across partial POSTs
- Test covers at least: mcp_api_key, and one other sensitive field (e.g., dispatcharr password)

LABELS: bug, backend, settings

title: BE test drift (SPA fallback): 9 tests in event_loop_responsiveness + backup path-injection fail in container
description:
Surfaced while clearing enhancedchannelmanager-0gcu9. Root cause is the same as 0gcu9 item #2 (SPA fallback "/{full_path:path}" registers only when backend/static/ exists — present in ecm-ecm-1 container, absent in CI), but it affects 9 additional tests across 2 files that the original bead did not cover.

Failures (all deterministic in ecm-ecm-1, green in CI):

tests/integration/test_event_loop_responsiveness.py::TestRequestTimeoutMiddleware::test_slow_handler_returns_504
- Test dynamically registers /api/_test_stall_for_timeout after app startup. The SPA catch-all registered at import time is earlier in the route table, so it wins the match and returns 200 instead of letting the stalling handler trigger the 504 timeout middleware.

tests/routers/test_backup.py::TestSavedBackupsPathInjection (8 params across test_download_rejects_traversal_payloads + test_delete_rejects_traversal_payloads):
- URL-encoded slash payloads (%2Fetc%2Fpasswd, ..%2F..%2Fetc%2Fpasswd, ...) decode to multi-segment paths that can never match {filename}, so they fall through to the SPA catch-all. Test expects 400/404/422 (router-level rejection) but gets 405 (GET-only catch-all rejects DELETE, and the SPA-path winning the route match blocks the backup router from rejecting at all).
- Security note: the traversal payload IS rejected at the routing layer — never reaches the backup handler. This is not a bypass, just a test expectation mismatch vs. container behavior.

Suggested fixes (all Small):
1. test_event_loop_responsiveness: register the stall route BEFORE the SPA fallback, or register it via monkeypatch.setattr on app.router.routes at index 0 (prepend).
2. test_backup TestSavedBackupsPathInjection: add 405 to the accepted reject codes, or (better) design the backup route so it handles URL-encoded slashes explicitly before the SPA catch-all. The test assertion list should document that 405 is an acceptable rejection.

Acceptance:
- All 9 failures either pass in ecm-ecm-1 container or are restructured to be robust against SPA-fallback presence.
- pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e is fully green in ecm-ecm-1 without --deselect.
- This combined with 0gcu9 closure is the prerequisite QA was asking for to unblock the v0.16.0 dep-bump epic.
close_reason: Implemented and merged via PR #174. Pre-fix: 25 failed / 2934 passed. Post-fix: 11 failed / 2971 passed (-14 targeted failures cleared — 8 backup path-injection, 6 export path-injection variants from same SPA root cause, plus 2 stall-route tests from event_loop_responsiveness). Remaining 11 failures are pre-existing test drift unrelated to SPA — will file separate bead for journal/auto_creation/mcp_status/tags metadata drift.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
Implementation complete in container, full suite green (2945 passed, 0 failed).

Changes:
- backend/main.py: added api_not_found handler at /api/{full_path:path} registered before SPA catch-all. Returns JSON 404 for unmatched /api/* (was: 200 from SPA index.html, or 405 method-not-allowed). Real routers register earlier via include_router so they win by match order.
- backend/tests/integration/test_event_loop_responsiveness.py: prepend stall routes via app.router.routes.insert(0, ...) so they win over both catch-alls (api_not_found + SPA). Same fix applied to test_slow_handler_returns_504 and test_auto_creation_crud_subject_to_timeout.

Pending: ship-the-fix commit + push.

title: MCP↔backend contract enforcement (Plan B): endpoint-contract registry + ECMClient wrapper + OpenAPI contract test
description:
MCP-tool <-> backend-API contract enforcement ("Plan B" from the post-#229 retro). Kills the drift class that was the root cause of 4 of @Koalamanx's 5 bugs (#221 group_id vs channel_group_id; #222 {"rules":[...]} envelope; #223 no real bulk endpoint; #224 swallowed 422 detail). The registry becomes the single source of truth that the tools USE (so a tool physically cannot drift from it), validated against the backend's OpenAPI spec. Plan C (codegen a typed client from OpenAPI) is the heavier alternative — parked; revisit only if B does not hold.

== DESIGN ==

1. mcp-server/_endpoint_contracts.py — the registry
   - frozen dataclass Endpoint: name, method, path (FastAPI-style {param}), request_fields frozenset[str], query_params frozenset[str], response_fields frozenset[str], plus response_is_list bool and an optional "contract-exempt reason" escape hatch.
   - ENDPOINTS dict keyed by a stable id (~tool name; dedupe where multiple tools hit one endpoint).
   - Only names are stored (not full schemas) — that is all the subset checks need. Pure stdlib + dataclasses (must import fine in the backend test env).

2. mcp-server/ecm_client.py — call_endpoint(ep, *, path_args=None, body=None, query=None, timeout=None)
   - formats ep.path from path_args (clear error if a placeholder is missing)
   - asserts set(body or {}) <= ep.request_fields and set(query or {}) <= ep.query_params; raises a developer-facing ContractError on violation — ALWAYS ON (cheap subset check). A tool that sends "group_id" when the registry says "channel_group_id" fails loudly at call time.
   - dispatches to the right httpx verb from ep.method
   - on 4xx/5xx surfaces the response body's "detail" (generalizes the #224 fix; keep the RuntimeError-with-detail behavior the #233 batch added)
   - keep the existing get/post/patch/delete methods (for /health and incremental migration)
   - response-shape validation is left to the contract test (schema-level), not runtime (backend may omit optional fields).

3. Migrate ~112 tools (mcp-server/tools/{channels,channel_groups,streams,m3u,epg,auto_creation,export,tasks,stats,system,notifications,profiles,normalization}.py + resources/overview.py) from hand-rolled client.post("/api/...", json_data={...}) to client.call_endpoint(ENDPOINTS["..."], path_args=..., body=...). Building each ENDPOINTS entry from BOTH the tool code AND the backend route + Pydantic model surfaces any CURRENT drift beyond #221-225 — expect a handful to fix.

4. backend/tests/integration/test_mcp_tool_contracts.py (backend-side: needs FastAPI app importable; adds mcp-server/ to sys.path to import ENDPOINTS). Builds app.openapi(). For each Endpoint asserts: (a) (method, path) in spec["paths"]; (b) request_fields ⊆ the request-body schema's properties (resolving $ref/anyOf/allOf) — the #221 catcher; (c) query_params ⊆ declared query params; (d) response_fields ⊆ the 2xx response schema's properties (resolving $ref) — the #222 catcher. Plus a tool-source guard: regex/AST-scan mcp-server/tools/*.py for client.call_endpoint(ENDPOINTS[...]) refs and any remaining client.<verb>("/api/...") literals; assert every referenced endpoint id is in ENDPOINTS; any unmigrated raw path is a warn (phase 1) / fail (phase 2) unless marked "# contract-exempt: <reason>". Fiddly bits: $ref/anyOf/allOf resolution; the bulk-commit discriminated union (special-case or list the union's superset); GET/DELETE with no body; bare-list responses (response_is_list).

== PHASING (two PRs) ==
Phase 1 (Medium): _endpoint_contracts.py + ECMClient.call_endpoint + the contract test + the tool-source guard in WARN mode + migrate the channels/* and auto_creation/* tool domains (~36 tools — the ones that bit us). Lands the immediate value, proves the design, contract test goes live for the migrated domains. Version bump + CHANGELOG Changed.
Phase 2 (Medium-Large): migrate the remaining ~11 domains; flip the tool-source guard to FAIL on any unmigrated raw /api/ literal; add the "verify the effect" hardening for mutating tools (return the backend's response object in the tool reply / read-back-and-confirm so a 200-with-no-effect like #221 can't fool the calling agent). CHANGELOG entries per the hardening.

== RISKS ==
- OpenAPI $ref/anyOf/discriminated-union resolution in the contract test is the fiddliest part (bulk-commit is the worst case) — may special-case.
- 112-tool migration is mechanical but voluminous — phase 1 de-risks the design first.
- Multi-call / dynamic-path tools (e.g. build_channel_lineup) may not fit one Endpoint — keep them on raw client.<verb> with a "# contract-exempt: <reason>" marker (intentional, not drift).
- sys.path import of mcp-server/_endpoint_contracts.py in the backend CI test env must work (pure stdlib — fine).

Relates to ~/retros/2026-05-11-19_standup-bug-sweep.md §5. P2.
notes: Phase 1 shipped in PR #235 (v0.16.0-0081): _endpoint_contracts.py + ECMClient.call_endpoint + the OpenAPI contract test + the channels/auto_creation domains migrated (28 endpoints; 3 current-drift bugs fixed — group_id→channel_group query param, merge_channels re-routed to /bulk-merge, bulk_commit idMappings→tempIdMap/groupIdMap). Phase 2 remains: migrate the other 11 domains (78 raw client.<verb> calls — channel_groups 9, epg 11, export 6, m3u 7, normalization 2, notifications 5, profiles 2, stats 9, streams 16, system 6, tasks 5) onto call_endpoint; flip the tool-source guard to FAIL on any unmigrated raw /api/ literal; add the verify-the-effect hardening for mutating tools.
close_reason: Complete. Phase 1 (PR #235, v0.16.0-0081): _endpoint_contracts.py + ECMClient.call_endpoint + the OpenAPI contract test + channels/auto_creation migrated. Phase 2 (PR #236, v0.16.0-0082): remaining 11 domains migrated, tool-source guard flipped to FAIL-mode, verify-the-effect hardening for mutating tools. ~99 endpoints in the registry; zero un-exempt raw client.<verb> calls left. Across both phases the registry surfaced 8 silent-drift bugs the test suite never caught (filters ignored: GET /api/streams group/provider_id, GET /api/channels group_id, GET /api/journal limit, GET /api/epg/grid channel_id/limit; tools that 422'd on every call: merge_channels, create_task_schedule; bulk_commit reading idMappings; list_export_profiles/list_task_schedules reading nonexistent response fields) — all fixed. Plan C (codegen typed client) remains parked.

title: Enhanced Dummy EPG v0.14.0
description: Epic: Enhanced template engine with text transforms, conditionals, and lookup tables for dummy EPG system. 8 sub-beads covering backend engine, frontend mirror, lookup table model/API/UI, per-source inline lookups, enhanced preview, backend integration, and comprehensive tests.

title: Backend template engine core
description: Backend template engine (backend/template_engine.py) + 44 unit tests. Supports placeholders, chainable pipe transforms (uppercase, lowercase, titlecase, trim, strip:<chars>, replace:<from>:<to>, normalize, lookup:<table>), conditionals ({if:group}, {if:group=value}, {if:group~regex}, nested), legacy {name_normalize} backwards compat. ReDoS guards: MAX_TEMPLATE_LEN=4096, MAX_INPUT_LEN=1024 (group values truncated), MAX_REGEX_LEN=500 (oversized regex in conditional evaluates false rather than raising).

title: Frontend template engine mirror
description: Frontend template engine mirror (frontend/src/utils/templateEngine.ts) + 44 unit tests that mirror the 44 backend tests one-for-one so preview UI and server rendering stay in sync. Identical syntax (placeholders, pipes, lookups, conditionals, legacy _normalize), identical guards (MAX_TEMPLATE_LEN=4096, MAX_INPUT_LEN=1024, MAX_REGEX_LEN=500). DummyEPGSourceModal's applyTemplate swapped to the new engine with a TemplateSyntaxError fallback so half-typed expressions don't blow up the live preview.

title: Lookup table database model and API
description: LookupTable SQLAlchemy model (id, name unique, description, entries JSON text, timestamps) in backend/models.py; registered in database.py model imports so create_all picks it up. CRUD router backend/routers/lookup_tables.py (GET list + /{id}, POST, PATCH, DELETE) with duplicate-name 409, entry-count cap (10_000), rename clash detection. Registered in routers/__init__.py. Added 'Lookup Tables' to tags_metadata in main.py. Frontend types and API functions in frontend/src/services/api.ts: LookupTable, LookupTableSummary, LookupTableCreateRequest, LookupTableUpdateRequest, listLookupTables, getLookupTable, createLookupTable, updateLookupTable, deleteLookupTable. 14 router tests cover list/get/create/update/delete + name uniqueness + size cap.

title: Lookup table management UI
description: Lookup Tables settings UI: frontend/src/components/settings/LookupTableSection.tsx (+ .css). Wired into Settings > Lookup Tables under the Tags nav entry. SettingsPage union and VALID_SETTINGS_PAGES extended with 'lookup-tables'. Card list with name/description/entry-count. Editor modal supports add/remove/edit row, inline key→value inputs, optional bulk 'key=value per line' import with per-line validation and skip-count feedback. Create + edit share one modal. Confirm before delete. Uses existing ModalBase/ModalOverlay pattern and the existing NotificationContext for success/error toasts.

title: Per-source inline lookups in dummy EPG modal
description:
Per-source inline lookups in DummyEPGSourceModal.

- types/index.ts: DummyEPGCustomProperties extended with inline_lookups (Record<string, Record<string, string>>) and global_lookup_ids (number[]).
- DummyEPGSourceModal.tsx: new collapsible Lookup Tables section, opened automatically when loaded state already has lookups. Global tables shown as a scrollable checkbox list populated from api.listLookupTables() on modal open (refetched each open so newly-created tables appear). Inline tables editor: add table with default 'tableN' name, rename by typing, delete table, add/remove/edit rows. Save path cleans empty tables and empty keys before writing custom_properties.
- Live preview now feeds effective lookups (global-by-id merged with inline, inline wins) into the JS template engine so conditionals/lookups render identically to the backend preview. Note: the summary list endpoint omits entries, so the modal's client-side preview can't resolve global lookup values without a follow-up fetch — documented as a known limitation until .6 preview UI drives the server-side preview endpoint.
- New CSS under DummyEPGSourceModal.css for the global-picker and inline-editor grids.

title: Enhanced preview UI
description:
Enhanced preview UI for dummy EPG.

Backend
- template_engine.TemplateEngine.render_with_trace(template, groups, lookups) -> (output, trace). Trace entries: literal, placeholder (with initial/final + per-pipe input/output), conditional (condition, kind_detail truthy|equality|regex, taken, nested body). Lookup pipes annotated with {source, matched} so the UI distinguishes hits from misses. _evaluate_condition now returns (taken, detail) for richer trace display. +7 unit tests.
- preview_pipeline accepts include_trace; when true, response includes a 'traces' dict keyed by template field name (title_template, description_template, etc.).
- PreviewRequest adds include_trace: bool. +2 integration tests.

Frontend
- templateEngine.ts: renderWithTrace() mirror with identical trace shape + 6 Vitest tests.
- New TemplateHelp.tsx + .css: collapsible syntax reference (placeholders, pipes with each transform listed, lookups, conditionals, worked example).
- Reworked 'Test Your Configuration' section in DummyEPGSourceModal:
  - Kept the fast in-browser live preview (renamed 'Live Preview (browser)').
  - Added 'Run Server Preview' button that POSTs with include_trace=true.
  - New ServerPreviewView component shows match/fallback badge, matched variant, extracted groups grid, and every rendered field with its resolved output.
  - Each field with a non-empty trace gets a 'Show trace' toggle that renders a step viewer: placeholder rows show initial value → each pipe's output; lookup pipes show hit/miss badges; conditionals show taken/skipped + kind detail + value + nested body.
- DummyEPGPreviewResult extended with optional traces dict; DummyEPGPreviewRequest carries inline_lookups, global_lookup_ids, include_trace.

title: Backend processing integration
description:
Backend integration of the template engine + lookup tables.

- backend/dummy_epg_engine.py: render_template now delegates to template_engine.TemplateEngine with an optional lookups dict; TemplateSyntaxError falls back to the raw template so typos in a profile config don't crash an XMLTV refresh. generate_channel_xml accepts lookups and binds it to a local _render helper used by all inner template renders. preview_pipeline and preview_pipeline_batch accept lookups.
- backend/routers/dummy_epg.py: PreviewRequest and BatchPreviewRequest gained inline_lookups (dict[str, dict[str, str]]) and global_lookup_ids (list[int]). New _resolve_lookups helper merges inline tables with globals fetched from lookup_tables by ID; inline tables override same-named globals. Both /api/epg/dummy/preview and /preview/batch now resolve lookups via a DB session and hand them to the engine.
- Updated test_dummy_epg_engine.py test_render_missing_key_renders_empty to reflect the new semantics (missing placeholders render as '' instead of leaving raw {tokens} in output — matches the frontend applyTemplate).
- Added 5 new /preview integration tests covering pipe transforms, conditionals, inline lookups, global lookup IDs, and inline-overrides-global precedence.

Scope notes: the bead called for a separate /api/epg/dummy/process endpoint, but 'process' is effectively /preview/batch with channel-name inputs + lookups — both endpoints now support exactly that. Left as single endpoint to avoid pointless duplication; revisit if .6 preview UI needs a different shape.

Profile-column persistence (inline_lookups / global_lookup_ids on DummyEPGProfile) is deferred to .5 since that bead covers the modal save path that writes them.

title: Tests and documentation
description:
Tests + documentation for the dummy EPG template engine.

Backend tests
- New backend/tests/integration/test_dummy_epg.py (16 tests) covering edge cases through the /api/dummy-epg/preview HTTP path:
  - Nested conditionals (3-level, with middle absent) + trace reports each level.
  - Lookup edge cases: missing key passes value through; unknown table falls back to raw template (not 500, because TemplateSyntaxError is soft-caught in render_template); chained lookup after transform.
  - Invalid regex in conditional: unclosed char class → evaluates false, oversized (>500 char) → false, trace records kind_detail='regex'.
  - Backwards compat: legacy {name_normalize} suffix, legacy inside conditional, pipe on missing group renders empty.
  - Trace shape: literal-only template returns single literal entry; include_trace=false omits traces key.
  - Global lookup resolution: created via /api/lookup-tables then referenced by ID; missing global ID ignored silently.

Frontend tests
- 12 new cases in api.test.ts covering listLookupTables (summaries-only, errors), getLookupTable, createLookupTable (body shape), updateLookupTable (rename + partial entries), deleteLookupTable (204 + 404), and previewDummyEPG (basic render, include_trace flag propagation, inline_lookups + global_lookup_ids forwarded).
- MSW handlers in src/test/mocks/handlers.ts for /api/lookup-tables (GET list/detail, POST, PATCH, DELETE) and /api/dummy-epg/preview (respects include_trace, returns shaped payload).

Documentation
- New docs/template_engine.md: syntax reference (placeholders, pipes, conditionals, lookups), limits, worked examples, trace format, and cross-refs to every related file.
- docs/api.md: added Lookup Tables endpoint table and documented the new inline_lookups / global_lookup_ids / include_trace fields on /preview.
- CLAUDE.md: added 'Dummy EPG Template Engine' → docs/template_engine.md in the Reference Guides table.

Totals: backend 2424 tests (+16), frontend 1112 tests (+12). Typecheck clean.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: Onboarding finding (YELLOW, operability). backend/routers/health.py returns hardcoded 'status: healthy' + version env vars only. Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK will report healthy even if SQLite is locked, Dispatcharr is unreachable, or ffmpeg/ffprobe is missing. Fix: add /api/health/ready that performs DB SELECT 1, cached (30s) Dispatcharr ping, shutil.which('ffprobe') check. Keep /api/health as cheap liveness. Highest-ROI operability improvement per the SRE assessment. Flagged by SRE during /onboard 2026-04-19.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Analyzed drag/drop patterns in ChannelsPane and StreamsPane.

FINDINGS:
- ChannelsPane lines 2462-2524: handlePaneDragOver/Leave/Drop for accepting stream group drops
- StreamsPane lines 345-440: handleDragStart/handleGroupDragStart for INITIATING drags
- These are complementary, not duplicates (one is drag source, one is drop target)
- No actual duplication exists

StreamsPane drag handlers:
- handleDragStart: Sets dataTransfer for individual/multi-stream drags
- handleGroupDragStart: Sets dataTransfer for group drags with custom drag images

ChannelsPane drop handlers:
- handlePaneDragOver: Checks dataTransfer types, sets dropEffect
- handlePaneDragLeave: Clears dragOver state when leaving pane
- handlePaneDrop: Parses dropped data, calls onStreamGroupDrop callback

RECOMMENDATION:
A generic useStreamDragDrop hook would need to handle:
1. Drag source logic (StreamsPane)
2. Drop target logic (ChannelsPane)
3. Application-specific dataTransfer formats
4. Application-specific callbacks (onStreamGroupDrop)

This is too application-specific to be a useful abstraction. The current implementation is clearer and more maintainable than a generic hook would be.

Suggest closing this bead as NOT NEEDED - no duplication exists, patterns are specific to each component's role.

title: Audit sync CPU work in async handlers; thread-pool wrapper + uvicorn concurrency limits
description:
Backlog — discovered during ReDoS spike on GH #104. Not part of normalization-parity epic; general operational weakness.

SRE finding: ECM deploys uvicorn with 1 worker, no --limit-concurrency, no request-timeout middleware, no reverse proxy, and NormalizationEngine.normalize() is a plain sync method called directly inside async FastAPI handlers. Any CPU-heavy sync work blocks the single-worker event loop for every concurrent request.

Scope:
- Audit all async handlers for sync CPU work (regex-heavy, tight loops, large JSON parsing).
- Wrap identified callsites with asyncio.to_thread or run_in_executor so offending requests only block a thread, not the event loop.
- Add uvicorn --limit-concurrency bound in backend/entrypoint.sh:244.
- Consider slowapi rate limits on /api/normalization/* and /api/auto-creation/*.
- Add [SLOW-REQUEST] counter metric; SLO review.

Reference files:
- backend/entrypoint.sh:244 (uvicorn invocation)
- backend/main.py:179-472 (middleware stack — no request-timeout)
- backend/auth/routes.py:46 (slowapi wired but only on two login endpoints)
- backend/normalization_engine.py:865 (sync normalize in async context)
close_reason: Shipped in PR #114 (merge 32067fee). backend/concurrency.py + run_cpu_bound wrapper live; 13 async call sites migrated; request-timeout middleware active; uvicorn --limit-concurrency 100 --timeout-keep-alive 30. Runbook at docs/runbooks/request-timeout.md.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
## Audit — sync CPU work in async FastAPI handlers (user-reachable)

Grep'd backend/routers for sync calls into CPU-heavy modules (normalization_engine, auto_creation_*, dummy_epg_engine, stream_prober, auto_creation_schema). Filtered to **user-reachable async handlers** that call sync code directly without offload.

| file:line | handler | sync call | approx CPU work |
|-|-|-|-|
| routers/normalization.py:497 | POST /api/normalization/test | engine.test_rule(...) | 1 regex/condition eval on 1 text (≤10ms typical, can spike on pathological pattern) |
| routers/normalization.py:528 | POST /api/normalization/test-batch | engine.test_rules_batch(texts) | N rules × M texts, each regex (high — 100-500ms, unbounded on pathological input) |
| routers/normalization.py:559 | POST /api/normalization/normalize | engine.test_rules_batch(texts) | same as test-batch |
| routers/normalization.py:630 | GET /api/normalization/rule-stats | engine._match_condition per rule×stream (loop) | up to 500 streams × 100 rules = 50k regex evals, seconds |
| routers/channels.py:277 | POST /api/channels (create) | engine.normalize(request.name) | 1 full normalize pipeline (5-50ms, spikes on bad regex) |
| routers/channels.py:1349 | POST /api/channels/bulk (createChannel) | engine.normalize(op.name) per op | N × normalize in loop (scales with bulk payload) |
| routers/dummy_epg.py:386 | POST /api/dummy-epg/preview | preview_pipeline(config, sample) | sync template+regex rendering, 10-100ms typical |
| routers/dummy_epg.py:400 | POST /api/dummy-epg/preview/batch | preview_pipeline_batch(config, ≤100) | hundreds of ms |
| routers/dummy_epg.py:515 | GET /api/dummy-epg/xmltv | generate_xmltv(profile_data, channel_map) | XML string build for all profiles — seconds |
| routers/dummy_epg.py:557 | GET /api/dummy-epg/xmltv/{profile_id} | generate_xmltv(...) | single profile — 100s ms |
| routers/dummy_epg.py:598 | POST /api/dummy-epg/generate | generate_xmltv(...) | full regen, seconds |
| routers/dummy_epg.py:608 | POST /api/dummy-epg/generate (per-profile loop) | generate_xmltv([p_dict],...) × P | scales with profile count |
| routers/auto_creation.py:941 | POST /api/auto-creation/validate | validate_rule(conditions, actions) | regex compile checks, ms |

Totals: 13 call sites across 4 router files — under 20-site cutoff, no scope split.

auto_creation_engine.run_pipeline / run_rule is already awaited (async). stream_prober references are in-memory stat reads, no CPU. tags.invalidate_tag_cache is dict.clear — trivial.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
## Implementation complete (not committed; pending user "ship" signal)

### Worktree
- Path: `.claude/worktrees/agent-a05c3731`
- Branch: `worktree-agent-a05c3731`

### Files created
| File | Lines | Purpose |
|-|-|-|
| `backend/concurrency.py` | 85 | `run_cpu_bound` wrapper + bounded ThreadPoolExecutor (env-tunable via `ECM_CPU_POOL_WORKERS`) |
| `backend/tests/unit/test_concurrency.py` | 129 | 11 unit tests for the wrapper (env override, singleton, kwargs, exception propagation, event-loop responsiveness) |
| `backend/tests/integration/test_event_loop_responsiveness.py` | 201 | 4 integration tests: health-during-normalize, health-during-xmltv, timeout passthrough, 504 on stall |
| `docs/runbooks/request-timeout.md` | 164 | SRE runbook: env vars, 504/503 diagnosis, recovery |

### Files modified
- `backend/entrypoint.sh` (+17): uvicorn `--limit-concurrency 100 --timeout-keep-alive 30` (env-override via `ECM_LIMIT_CONCURRENCY`, `ECM_TIMEOUT_KEEP_ALIVE`)
- `backend/main.py` (+59): request-timeout middleware (`ECM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`, default 30s) + CPU-pool shutdown hook. Streaming/ffmpeg/tasks/backup prefixes exempt.
- `backend/routers/normalization.py` (+44/-28): `run_cpu_bound` on `/test`, `/test-batch`, `/normalize`, `/rule-stats` rule×stream loop; `@limiter.limit("30/minute")` on `/test-batch`
- `backend/routers/channels.py` (+7/-4): `run_cpu_bound` on both `engine.normalize()` sites (POST /channels, POST /channels/bulk createChannel)
- `backend/routers/dummy_epg.py` (+22/-13): `run_cpu_bound` on /preview, /preview/batch, /xmltv (×3 generate_xmltv sites), /generate (×2 sites)
- `backend/routers/auto_creation.py` (+4/-2): `run_cpu_bound` on /validate

Net: 10 source files, +128/-25 diff to existing files, +579 new lines.

### Test output
Full suite (`cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ --tb=short --no-header -p no:warnings`): **2278 passed in 46.35s**. No regressions.

Targeted bd-w3z4h tests:
- `tests/unit/test_concurrency.py` — 11 passed (includes `test_does_not_block_event_loop`)
- `tests/integration/test_event_loop_responsiveness.py` — 4 passed

TDD verification: stubbed `run_cpu_bound` to run inline → `test_health_under_500ms_while_normalize_batch_runs` correctly fails with `/api/health took 0.803s` (matches the 800ms blocker). Confirms the test actually exercises event-loop responsiveness and isn't a pass-by-coincidence.

### Container healthcheck verification
Deployed to ecm-ecm-1, restarted. After ~36s the healthcheck reports `(healthy)`. `/proc/1/cmdline` inspection confirms:
```
/usr/local/bin/python /usr/local/bin/uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 6100 --limit-concurrency 100 --timeout-keep-alive 30
```
`curl http://localhost:6100/api/health` returns in ~18ms, well under the 10s HEALTHCHECK timeout.

### Scope changes / deferred
- No scope split needed — 13 offending call sites, under the 20-site cutoff.
- slowapi rate limit: only applied `@limiter.limit("30/minute")` to `/test-batch`. Bead mentioned also applying to `apply-to-channels`, which doesn't exist as an endpoint in current code (bd-eio04.2 frontend reuses bulk/createChannel). Flagging for bd-eio04.12 scope.
- XMLTV endpoints (`/api/dummy-epg/xmltv*`) offloaded but NOT exempted from the 30s timeout. Large catalogs may still see 504. Follow-up recommendation (not blocking this bead): move XMLTV generation to a background task that writes to the cache; endpoint reads cached blob only. Documented in runbook.
- The two channels.py `engine.normalize()` sites referenced in the spec at L287/L1363 are at current L278/L1350 (file now 1972 lines, not 2034 — recent churn). The third site at L2034 doesn't exist — appears to have been removed by earlier work. Verified via grep: only 2 `engine.normalize(` occurrences in routers/channels.py, both offloaded.

### Ready to ship?
Awaiting user signal. All quality gates green: compile, unit, integration, full suite, container healthy, runbook written.

title: UX heuristic pass on PR #163 (scheduled task email recipients UI)
description:
Review gate for PR #163 (contributor: stevencoutts) — scheduled task email alerts + recipient UI.

PR adds 127 lines to frontend/src/components/tabs/SettingsTab.tsx for a multi-recipient email entry flow plus a new Notification Settings panel. Per standup 2026-04-24 decision #3: UX review runs FIRST, Code Reviewer follows — one consolidated round to respect contributor round-count fatigue.

UX scope (heuristic pass, not full critique):
- Nielsen #1 (system status): does "saved" feedback make it visible?
- Nielsen #5 (error prevention): inline validation per recipient (RFC 5322 shape + duplicate detection)
- Nielsen #9 (recover from errors): preserve valid entries when one fails; clear error states
- Recipient-entry pattern: separator convention (comma/semicolon/Enter — pick one, document it), chip/tag vs. plain-text, keyboard affordances (backspace-to-remove, paste-list handling)
- WCAG 2.5.5 touch targets on the recipient entry controls
- Empty-state copy

Acceptance:
- UX review comments posted to PR #163 as inline review
- Pattern decision documented (chip vs. plain-text, separator choice)
- Hand-off to Code Reviewer for the correctness/TDD/API-contract review round

LABELS: ux, pr-review

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
UX heuristic review posted to PR #163: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/163#issuecomment-4316431870

Pattern decision: plain-text comma-separated input, ',' as canonical separator (kept contributor's implementation — chip/tag would be over-engineering for a 1–5 recipient admin screen).

Findings: 3 Blockers, 4 Recommends, 3 Nits.

Blockers:
1. No inline RFC 5322 validation — saves invalid emails silently (Nielsen #5)
2. Save is all-or-nothing — one bad token loses no work but forces user to hunt through a comma-salad (Nielsen #9)
3. No duplicate detection — 'alice@x.com, alice@x.com' double-delivers (Nielsen #5)

Recommends: empty-state copy clarity, sticky 'saved' indicator beyond toast, paste-list normalization (; and newline → , on paste), input loading affordance.

Nits: badge reflects draft not persisted state; WCAG 2.5.5 target-size spot check; label a11y text.

Handed off explicitly to Code Reviewer for correctness/API/test-quality round. Did NOT comment on backend, tests, or API shape per scope.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text: Closed as done. UX heuristic pass posted to PR #163 as consolidated review (3 Blockers, 4 Recommends, 3 Nits, comma-separated plain-text pattern decision documented, Code Reviewer handoff stated). All four bead acceptance criteria met. Code Reviewer round on PR #163 tracked in separate bead.

title: Refactor cyclic-import topology: extract shared Base module + cloud_storage factory split
description:
## Context
Deferred from bd-kdsn3 (CodeQL note-level cleanup). 15 py/cyclic-import findings exist that require non-mechanical refactoring to resolve. Per orchestration discipline, mechanical-only cleanup deferred architectural refactor here.

## Findings (15 total)
### Cluster A: cloud_storage factory pattern (8)
- backend/cloud_storage/base.py:90,93,96,99 — get_adapter() factory imports concrete adapters lazily
- backend/cloud_storage/{s3,gdrive,onedrive,dropbox}_adapter.py — each imports cloud_storage.base for ABC + dataclasses

This is a classic factory cycle: base defines the abstract class + factory; concrete adapters inherit from base. The factory's lazy `from cloud_storage.X import` inside the function body breaks runtime cycle but CodeQL still flags it.

**Fix options:**
1. Extract factory into `cloud_storage/factory.py` so base.py has no cyclic deps.
2. Extract data types (UploadResult, ConnectionTestResult, CloudStorageAdapter ABC) into `cloud_storage/types.py`; let base.py be just the factory.

### Cluster B: database.py <-> models.py / export_models.py / normalization_migration.py (7)
- backend/database.py:129 imports models
- backend/database.py:130 imports export_models
- backend/database.py:315 imports normalization_migration
- backend/models.py:9 imports database (circular)
- backend/export_models.py:9 imports database (circular)
- backend/normalization_migration.py:15,285 imports models (circular)

Models import `Base` from database; database imports models for table registration on init. Standard SQLAlchemy footgun.

**Fix:** Extract the SQLAlchemy `Base = declarative_base()` into `backend/db_base.py`. Both database.py and models.py import from db_base; models no longer needs database, breaking the cycle. Same for export_models.py and normalization_migration.py.

## Out of Scope
- Mechanical CodeQL cleanups (bd-kdsn3, in progress)

## Acceptance
- [ ] All 15 py/cyclic-import findings resolved (verified by next CodeQL run on the affected files)
- [ ] No runtime regression (full backend test suite passes)
- [ ] Consultation with IT Architect before merging — import topology change touches every model
close_reason: Landed via PR #172 (merge commit dabbb65c) on dev. Cluster A: cloud_storage/base.py split into types.py (ABC + dataclasses) and factory.py (get_adapter); external callers import from cloud_storage package root. Cluster B: declarative_base() extracted to backend/db_base.py; database.py re-exports for BC, models.py + export_models.py import from db_base directly. All 15 py/cyclic-import CodeQL findings resolved. CI green: Backend Tests, Frontend Tests, CodeQL Python, CodeQL JS, Semgrep. Rebased twice onto origin/dev (dead-import cleanup conflicts in publish_pipeline.py + 2 test files, resolved by taking dev's cleanup + refactor's new import path). Unblocks 0i2vt.4 and skqln.2.

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
Refactor landed on branch refactor/wlvxh-cyclic-imports (commit 878d6d09).

Cluster A: cloud_storage/base.py deleted; split into types.py (ABC + dataclasses) and factory.py (get_adapter). Concrete adapters and all external callers rewired. No shim.

Cluster B: Base extracted to new db_base.py. database.py re-exports it for backward compat; models.py + export_models.py import from db_base directly.

Verification: 2942 passed / 0 failed in ecm-ecm-1 (baseline pre-refactor was 2945 passed / 0 failed; 3-test delta is test-file drift between the worktree base and the container's existing code, not new failures). Zero new failures introduced by wlvxh. Import-isolation smoke test green — all four Base references are the same declarative_base instance.

grep confirms no remaining 'from cloud_storage.base import' references outside a historical-reference docstring in types.py.

Branch pushed; PR not opened (PO ships via shipping flow). Bead left open pending merge.

title: Update docs/shipping.md §6 — dev branch now requires PR + 5 status checks (no direct push)
description:
docs/shipping.md §6 currently documents direct-push-to-dev (\`git push origin dev\`) as the standard ship flow. This no longer works: the \`dev\` branch protection rule was tightened to require 5 status checks (Backend Tests, Frontend Tests, CodeQL Analysis (python), CodeQL Analysis (javascript-typescript), Semgrep Lint) AND enforce_admins=true. Direct push is now impossible for everyone, including the PO.

Surfaced when bd-i6a1m's tag-stone version bump (commit c479b99a, 0.16.0-0058 → 0.16.0-0059) was rejected on push and had to land via PR chore/bump-dev-0059 → dev instead.

## Required updates

1. **§6 "Commit and Push"** — replace the \`git push origin dev\` example with the PR-driven flow:
   - git push -u origin <feature-or-chore-branch>
   - gh pr create --base dev --head <branch> --title "..." --body "..."
   - wait for the 5 required checks to pass
   - gh pr merge <#> --merge --delete-branch
2. **§3 "Increment the Version"** — note that the version-bump commit also needs to land via PR now (since it touches dev). Or codify a "no-CI-needed for tag-stone bumps" exception via branch protection if desired (separate decision).
3. **"Critical Shipping Rules"** block — replace "Work is NOT complete until \`git push\` succeeds" with "Work is NOT complete until the PR merges into \`dev\`."
4. **CLAUDE.md** — section "Shipping (When User Says 'Ship the Fix')" links to docs/shipping.md; verify no contradictory direct-push language remains in CLAUDE.md itself.

## Acceptance

- §6 reflects PR-driven flow with the exact gh commands
- §3 notes the tag-stone-bump implication
- "Critical Shipping Rules" updated
- CLAUDE.md reviewed for stale direct-push references; updated if any
- No drift from the actual gh API output: `gh api /repos/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/branches/dev/protection` should match the workflow described

## Out of scope

- Whether to relax dev's branch protection (e.g., allow trivial version-bump pushes without CI). That's a security/CI policy decision, not a docs update. File separately if desired.

LABELS: docs, shipping, ci, sre
close_reason: Merged via PR #176 (merge commit 38fb072a) into dev. Updated docs/shipping.md §3 (tag-stone bumps land via PR; cited PR #175 precedent), §6 (replaced direct push with full PR + gh pr merge --merge --delete-branch sequence; listed all 5 required checks per gh api output), and 'Critical Shipping Rules' (Work is NOT complete until the PR merges into dev). CLAUDE.md grep found a stale duplicated rules block at lines 136-139 — updated to match shipping.md and pointed readers to §6 as canonical (no duplicated commands in CLAUDE.md).

title: ADR-001: Dependency upgrade cadence & validation gate (fresh-image smoke requirement)
description:
Establish a fresh-image rebuild + smoke test gate for each dependency upgrade under bd-6rrl5.

Problem: No CI registry push yet. Current 'docker cp + restart' validates against a mutated running container, not a freshly built image from the updated Dockerfile/requirements. This is a silent correctness gap — upgrades can pass in the dev container while a fresh build would fail (missing system deps, version skew, cache bust differences, etc.).

ADR must document:
(a) Problem — fresh-image validation is not currently a gate.
(b) Options considered —
    1. Stand up CI registry push + smoke pipeline,
    2. External/staging smoke environment (docker compose rebuild),
    3. Accept the risk and document it.
(c) Decision — TBD (PO + SRE to fill).
(d) Consequences — cost, pipeline latency, release velocity.
(e) Application — acceptance criteria on each bd-6rrl5 child upgrade bead must reference the chosen gate.

Stub bead. PO/architect fills ADR content later. Blocks bd-6rrl5.
notes: ADR-001 accepted by PO 2026-04-20. PR #82 (dev->main) closed 2026-04-20 per IT Architect Option (b) recommendation — it carried 38 unrelated pre-existing dev commits that shouldn't ride a doc PR into main. Cherry-picked 1b9e2623 onto pe/xnqgo-adr-001-doc-only off main; opened standalone doc-only PR #90: https://github.com/MotWakorb/enhancedchannelmanager/pull/90. Single-file diff (+139 lines, ADR doc only). Awaiting CI + PM merge decision. Dev->main release-cut deferred to ADR-004 / enhancedchannelmanager-4lk1q track. Bead stays in_progress until PR #90 merges — original PO acceptance (2026-04-20) unchanged.

Acceptance captured in docs/adr/ADR-001-dependency-upgrade-validation-gate.md on dev at commit 1b9e2623. No standalone main promotion needed; ADR rides with next release cut via enhancedchannelmanager-4lk1q (ADR-004). title: CI automation: surface 'flagged-in-last-30-runs' flake list in PR reviews (follow-up to tp681)
description:
Follow-up to bead tp681 (flake audit, 2026-04-20). The flake policy in docs/testing.md now specifies a 'flagged-in-last-30-runs' PR-review gate, but that list is manually maintained via bd list --label flaky. This bead captures the automation.

Scope:
- CI artifact capture: persist pytest --junitxml (BE) and vitest JUnit (FE) from every main/dev run.
- 30-run rolling window: a small script or GH Actions job that walks the last 30 artifacts per branch and emits a JSON list of tests that failed in at least one run.
- Surface the list in PR reviews (comment on the PR or a dashboard URL).
- Optional: auto-file/close flaky-labelled beads based on the window data.

Acceptance:
- Reviewer can see the 30-run flaky list for the target branch without running bd list by hand.
- The list updates automatically on every CI run, not on a manual sweep.
- False-positive rate tuned so a test flagged once in 30 runs does not auto-block PRs (only informs the reviewer).
acceptance_criteria: PR review UX shows which tests are in the 30-run flake window; no manual bd list required
notes: Shipped in PR #179 (merge commit 94c12689). Tests workflow now uploads junit-backend / junit-frontend artifacts (30-day retention). New scripts/aggregate_flakes.py walks the last 30 Tests runs on the PR base via GH REST API. New .github/workflows/flake-pr-comment.yml runs on PR open/sync/reopen and posts/updates a single Markdown comment (idempotent via hidden HTML marker). Verified on the shipping PR itself: junit artifacts uploaded successfully (43KB BE, 53KB FE), comment posted on first run, second push updated the same comment in place rather than spawning a new one. Comment is informational only — does not gate merge, per acceptance bar in the bead. docs/testing.md § 'Flake baseline gate for PR reviews' updated to point at the automation.

title: v6dxf.2: Local Authentication - User Management CRUD
description:
Local Authentication - User Management CRUD (COMPLETED)

Implemented user management for local authentication:

1. User Registration (POST /api/auth/register)
   - Creates new users with username, email, password
   - Validates unique username/email (409 conflict)
   - Enforces password strength (422 if weak)
   - New users are non-admin by default

2. Admin User CRUD (/api/admin/users)
   - GET: Paginated user list with search
   - POST: Admin creates user (relaxed password rules)
   - PATCH: Update user fields (email, display_name, is_admin, is_active, password)
   - DELETE: Soft delete (deactivate user)
   - GET /{id}: Get single user
   - Non-admin gets 403 Forbidden

3. Password Management
   - POST /api/auth/change-password - Change own password (requires current)
   - POST /api/auth/forgot-password - Request password reset (always 200 for security)
   - POST /api/auth/reset-password - Reset password with token (1-hour expiry)

Files changed:
- backend/auth/routes.py (registration, password endpoints)
- backend/auth/admin_routes.py (new - admin CRUD endpoints)
- backend/auth/__init__.py (export routers)
- backend/main.py (include admin router)
- backend/requirements.txt (added email-validator)
- backend/tests/conftest.py (fixed async_client fixture)
- backend/tests/integration/test_api_user_management.py (added fixtures)
- backend/tests/integration/test_api_auth.py (added fixtures)

All 67 auth tests passing.

title: Guide tab EPG data limited range
description: Investigated EPG data stopping at 7 PM. Dispatcharr's /api/epg/grid/ endpoint returns a fixed time range (previous hour + next 24 hours from server time) and doesn't accept custom date parameters. If data stops early, it's likely due to limited EPG data in Dispatcharr itself or timezone differences. Added optional start/end params to backend (for future use if Dispatcharr adds support) but reverted frontend to simple call since params aren't honored.

title: Visual FFMPEG Syntax Builder
description: Roadmap 0.12.5: A visual interface for building FFMPEG commands. Users can construct complex FFMPEG pipelines through an intuitive UI - selecting inputs, configuring filters, choosing codecs, and previewing the generated command. Integrates with ECM stream data for source information.

title: FFMPEG Builder Test Specifications
description: Functional test specifications defining FFMPEG builder capabilities. Write these tests FIRST - they define expected behavior. Implementation makes them pass.

title: Spec: Input Source Configuration
description:
Write tests defining input source behavior:

**Source Types:**
- File input: local path validated, file exists check
- URL input: HTTP/HTTPS/RTMP/RTSP URLs accepted
- Stream input: ECM stream ID resolves to URL
- Multiple inputs: support -i for each input

**Source Probing:**
- Probe button fetches stream metadata via ffprobe
- Display: duration, resolution, fps, codecs, bitrate
- Probe errors shown clearly (timeout, invalid source)
- Probe results cached per source

**Input Options:**
- Start time (-ss) with timestamp picker
- Duration (-t) with timestamp picker  
- Loop (-loop) for image/gif inputs
- Input format override (-f)
- Custom input args field

**Generated Syntax:**
- Single input: ffmpeg -i 'source'
- Multiple inputs: ffmpeg -i 'src1' -i 'src2'
- With options: ffmpeg -ss 00:01:00 -i 'source' -t 00:00:30

title: Spec: Command Execution
description:
Write tests defining command execution behavior:

**Execution:**
- Execute button runs FFMPEG command
- Command runs in background (async)
- Multiple jobs can run concurrently
- Cancel button sends SIGTERM/q to stop

**Progress Monitoring:**
- Parse FFMPEG progress output
- Display: frame, fps, bitrate, time, speed
- Progress bar based on duration (if known)
- ETA calculation

**Output Logging:**
- Capture stdout/stderr
- Display in scrollable log view
- Log levels: info, warning, error
- Download/save log option

**Job Status:**
- States: queued, running, completed, failed, cancelled
- Success: show output file path/size
- Failure: show error message prominently

**Job History:**
- List of recent jobs with status
- Re-run previous job
- View job details/log
- Clear history

title: Spec: Configuration Persistence
description:
Write tests defining save/load behavior:

**Save Configuration:**
- Save current builder state as named config
- Config includes: inputs, outputs, codecs, filters, all options
- Stored in database with timestamp
- Overwrite existing config option

**Load Configuration:**
- List saved configurations
- Load populates all builder fields
- Search/filter configurations
- Delete configuration

**Import/Export:**
- Export config as JSON file
- Import config from JSON file
- Share configurations between instances

**Auto-Save:**
- Draft auto-saved periodically
- Restore draft on page load (if exists)
- Clear draft on successful job

**API Endpoints:**
- GET /api/ffmpeg/configs - list configs
- POST /api/ffmpeg/configs - save config
- GET /api/ffmpeg/configs/{id} - load config
- DELETE /api/ffmpeg/configs/{id} - delete config

title: Spec: ECM Integration
description:
Write tests defining ECM integration:

**Stream Selection:**
- Browse ECM streams as input source
- Stream picker modal with search
- Selected stream URL used as input
- Stream probe data pre-populated

**Channel Integration:**
- Select channel to use active stream
- Channel logo as overlay input option
- Channel metadata for output naming

**Probe Data Reuse:**
- Use existing StreamStats if available
- Avoid re-probing already probed streams
- Display probe data in source info

**Quick Actions:**
- 'Test Stream' button on stream list
- 'Record Stream' with duration picker
- 'Transcode' for format conversion
- Actions open builder pre-configured

**Output to ECM:**
- Option to add output to M3U
- Create channel from output stream
- Link output to scheduled task

title: Spec: Frontend Builder UI
description:
Write Vitest/RTL tests for builder UI:

**Layout:**
- Builder accessible from main nav or Tools menu
- Sections: Input, Output, Video, Audio, Filters, Preview
- Collapsible sections with expand/collapse all
- Responsive layout for different screen sizes

**Input Section:**
- Add input button, input list
- Each input: source field, probe button, options toggle
- Remove input button
- Drag to reorder inputs

**Output Section:**
- Output path/URL field
- Format dropdown, codec summaries
- Options panel with common settings

**Preview Section:**
- Command display with syntax highlighting
- Copy button, execute button
- Validation warnings/errors inline

**State Management:**
- All fields update command preview
- Undo/redo for changes
- Reset to defaults button

title: Spec: Backend API Endpoints
description:
Write pytest tests for FFMPEG API:

**Probe Endpoint:**
- POST /api/ffmpeg/probe {url} - returns stream info
- Timeout handling (30s default)
- Error response for invalid source
- Cached results option

**Execute Endpoint:**
- POST /api/ffmpeg/execute {command} - starts job
- Returns job_id immediately
- Validates command safety (no shell injection)
- Rejects dangerous flags (rm, etc.)

**Job Status Endpoint:**
- GET /api/ffmpeg/jobs/{id} - returns job status
- GET /api/ffmpeg/jobs/{id}/log - returns output log
- DELETE /api/ffmpeg/jobs/{id} - cancels job

**Config Endpoints:**
- CRUD for saved configurations
- List with pagination
- Filter by name

**Validation Endpoint:**
- POST /api/ffmpeg/validate {command} - validates syntax
- Returns warnings/errors without executing

title: Spec: Job Queue & Background Processing
description:
Write tests for job processing:

**Job Queue:**
- Jobs queued when max concurrent reached
- Queue order: FIFO by default
- Priority override option
- Queue position shown in UI

**Concurrent Limits:**
- Configurable max concurrent jobs (default: 2)
- CPU/memory considerations
- Per-user limits option

**Process Management:**
- Jobs run as subprocess
- SIGTERM for graceful cancel
- SIGKILL after timeout
- Cleanup temp files on cancel

**Progress Parsing:**
- Parse FFMPEG progress output format
- Handle different output formats (-progress, -stats)
- Calculate percentage from frame/duration

**Persistence:**
- Jobs survive server restart (queued jobs)
- Running jobs marked as failed on crash
- Job history retained configurable days

title: Spec: E2E Builder Flows
description:
Write Playwright E2E tests:

**Basic Transcode:**
- Open builder, add input URL
- Select output format MP4
- Click execute, wait for completion
- Verify output file exists

**Preset Usage:**
- Select 'Web MP4' preset
- Verify fields populated correctly
- Command preview matches expected
- Execute and verify output

**Filter Chain:**
- Add scale filter (720p)
- Add FPS filter (30)
- Verify -vf shows chained filters
- Execute and verify output resolution

**Save/Load Config:**
- Configure complex job
- Save as 'Test Config'
- Clear and load 'Test Config'
- Verify all fields restored

**Cancel Job:**
- Start long-running job
- Click cancel
- Verify job status is 'cancelled'
- Verify process terminated

**Error Handling:**
- Invalid input URL shows error
- Invalid filter syntax shows validation error
- Failed job shows error in UI

title: Spec: Output Configuration
description:
Write tests defining output behavior:

**Output Types:**
- File output: path with extension
- Stream output: RTMP/SRT/UDP URLs
- Pipe output: pipe:1 for stdout
- Multiple outputs: support multiple -f/-o pairs

**Container Formats:**
- Format dropdown: mp4, mkv, webm, ts, flv, mov, avi, gif
- Format inferred from extension if not specified
- -f flag generated when format differs from extension

**Output Options:**
- Overwrite (-y) toggle
- Duration limit (-t)
- Metadata copying (-map_metadata)
- Movflags for streaming (+faststart, +frag_keyframe)
- Custom output args field

**Generated Syntax:**
- Basic: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 output.mkv
- With format: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -f mpegts output.ts
- Streaming: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -f flv rtmp://server/live/key

title: Spec: Video Codec Settings
description:
Write tests defining video codec behavior:

**Codec Selection:**
- Dropdown: libx264, libx265, libvpx-vp9, av1, copy, none
- 'copy' generates -c:v copy (stream copy)
- 'none' generates -vn (disable video)

**H.264/H.265 Options:**
- Preset: ultrafast to veryslow
- CRF: 0-51 slider (default 23)
- Profile: baseline, main, high
- Level: auto, 3.0, 4.0, 4.1, etc.
- Tune: film, animation, grain, etc.

**Quality Modes:**
- CRF mode (constant quality)
- CBR mode (-b:v bitrate)
- VBR mode (-minrate/-maxrate/-bufsize)
- Two-pass encoding option

**Generated Syntax:**
- CRF: -c:v libx264 -preset medium -crf 23
- CBR: -c:v libx264 -b:v 4000k
- Copy: -c:v copy
- Disable: -vn

title: Spec: Audio Codec Settings
description:
Write tests defining audio codec behavior:

**Codec Selection:**
- Dropdown: aac, libmp3lame, libopus, flac, copy, none
- 'copy' generates -c:a copy
- 'none' generates -an (disable audio)

**Common Options:**
- Bitrate: 64k, 128k, 192k, 256k, 320k
- Sample rate: 44100, 48000 Hz
- Channels: mono, stereo, 5.1
- Volume adjustment: -filter:a volume=1.5

**Codec-Specific:**
- AAC: -aac_coder, profile (aac_low, aac_he)
- MP3: quality mode (-q:a 0-9)
- Opus: -application (voip, audio, lowdelay)

**Generated Syntax:**
- Basic: -c:a aac -b:a 128k
- With options: -c:a aac -b:a 192k -ar 48000 -ac 2
- Copy: -c:a copy
- Disable: -an

title: Spec: Video Filters
description:
Write tests defining video filter behavior:

**Scale Filter:**
- Width/height inputs with aspect ratio lock
- Preset sizes: 1080p, 720p, 480p, 360p
- Algorithm: bilinear, bicubic, lanczos
- Generated: -vf scale=1280:720

**Crop Filter:**
- Visual crop area selector (future)
- Width, height, x, y inputs
- Generated: -vf crop=640:480:100:50

**FPS Filter:**
- Target framerate input
- Generated: -vf fps=30

**Deinterlace:**
- Method: yadif, bwdif
- Generated: -vf yadif

**Other Filters:**
- Rotate: transpose=1 (90°CW)
- Flip: hflip, vflip
- Pad: pad=1920:1080:(ow-iw)/2:(oh-ih)/2
- Overlay: overlay=10:10 (for watermarks)

**Filter Chaining:**
- Multiple filters: -vf 'scale=1280:720,fps=30,yadif'
- Order matters - validate chain

title: Spec: Audio Filters
description:
Write tests defining audio filter behavior:

**Volume Filter:**
- Volume multiplier (0.5x, 1x, 2x) or dB
- Generated: -af volume=1.5

**Normalization:**
- Loudnorm filter for broadcast normalization
- Generated: -af loudnorm=I=-16:TP=-1.5:LRA=11

**Channel Manipulation:**
- Downmix to stereo: -af 'pan=stereo|...'
- Extract channel: -af 'channelsplit'
- Mono: -af 'pan=mono|c0=c0+c1'

**Tempo/Speed:**
- Atempo for speed change (0.5x-2x)
- Generated: -af atempo=1.25

**Noise Reduction:**
- Highpass/lowpass filters
- Generated: -af 'highpass=f=200,lowpass=f=3000'

**Filter Chaining:**
- Multiple filters: -af 'volume=1.5,loudnorm,atempo=1.1'
- Validate chain compatibility

title: Spec: Stream Mapping
description:
Write tests defining stream mapping behavior:

**Basic Mapping:**
- Map all streams: -map 0 (default)
- Map specific input: -map 1
- Map stream type: -map 0:v, -map 0:a

**Stream Selection:**
- Display available streams from probe
- Checkbox to include/exclude each stream
- Select specific stream index: -map 0:v:0, -map 0:a:1

**Multi-Input Mapping:**
- Video from input 1, audio from input 2
- Generated: -map 0:v -map 1:a

**Subtitle Handling:**
- Include subtitles: -map 0:s
- Burn-in subtitles: -vf subtitles=input.mkv
- Copy subtitle codec: -c:s copy

**Generated Syntax:**
- Simple: -map 0
- Selective: -map 0:v:0 -map 0:a:0 -map 0:a:1
- Cross-input: -map 0:v -map 1:a

title: Spec: Preset Templates
description:
Write tests defining preset behavior:

**Built-in Presets:**
- 'Web MP4': H.264, AAC, faststart, web-optimized
- 'Archive MKV': High quality H.265, FLAC audio
- 'Stream Copy': -c copy (remux only)
- 'Extract Audio': -vn, audio codec selection
- 'GIF': palette generation, scale, fps
- 'Thumbnail': Single frame extraction
- 'HLS Playlist': Segment generation

**Preset Application:**
- Loading preset populates all relevant fields
- Preset values can be overridden
- 'Custom' preset when values don't match any template

**Custom Presets:**
- Save current configuration as preset
- Name and description for preset
- Presets stored in settings
- Delete/rename presets

**Generated Syntax:**

title: Regression: channel reordering after PR #107 (GH-104)
description: PO-reported regression tonight 2026-04-22. PR #107 changed auto-creation engine init conditions + _find_channel_by_name fallbacks; channels are now being reordered when they shouldn't. Investigate, bisect against #107/#110, report root cause and revert-vs-fix options.
notes:
PO live-test data from 2026-04-22 session — add as Bug 2 regression test cases when the superscript bead is filed:

Streams that created/mapped to 'RTL' channels:
- DE: RTL HEVC          (ASCII, no superscript — baseline)
- DE: RTL ᴿᴬᵂ           (letter-superscript RAW)
- DE: RTL HD (720P)     (ASCII tag with parens)
- AT: RTL ᴿᴬᵂ
- AT: RTL HD
- DE: RTL ᵁᴴᴰ 3840P     ← explicit test case — letter-superscript UHD + digit tag

Confirmed live symptom: channel #45 created with raw name 'RTL ᴿᴬᵂ' (letter-superscripts NOT stripped by _execute_create_channel). Fix plan (split convert_superscripts into numeric vs letter maps) should turn this into 'RTL RAW', which the tag-strip rules then collapse to 'RTL'.

Regression assertions to include in Bug 2's test:
- normalize('DE: RTL ᴿᴬᵂ', preserve_superscripts=True) → strips prefix + converts ᴿᴬᵂ → RAW + strips RAW tag → 'RTL'
- normalize('DE: RTL ᵁᴴᴰ 3840P', preserve_superscripts=True) → 'RTL' (UHD + 3840P both stripped via tag rules)
- normalize('RTL²', preserve_superscripts=True) → 'RTL²' (numeric preserved — PR #61 intent)
- End-to-end: _execute_create_channel with name 'DE: RTL ᴿᴬᵂ' creates a channel named matching the rule's output template, NOT containing ᴿᴬᵂ

title: Auto-creation: letter-superscripts (RAW/HD) not stripped; preserve_superscripts too coarse
description: Reporter: GH-104 thread. Symptom: Settings > Normalization 'Test' strips letter-superscripts (RAW, HD) correctly, but auto-creation rules leave them in created channel names. Root cause: _execute_create_channel at backend/auto_creation_executor.py:485-487 passes preserve_superscripts=True to normalize(), which at backend/normalization_engine.py:818-850 short-circuits convert_superscripts() entirely — preserves BOTH letter-superscripts and numeric. PR #61 (cea3d98b) intended to preserve only numeric superscripts like ESPN-squared. Fix: split convert_superscripts() into two maps — numeric (preserve when flag set) vs letter-superscripts (always convert). Add regression tests for ESPN RAW and ESPN-squared.

title: Test: lock in Alembic 0005 SQLite batch + idempotency paths
description: Follow-up to enhancedchannelmanager-m2k7p / PR #229 (gh stevencoutts). CI didn't catch the original 0005 SQLite CircularDependencyError or the create_all()-already-added-columns idempotency bug, because the in-memory SQLite test setup uses Base.metadata.create_all() + Alembic stamp instead of running upgrades, so batch_alter_table on auto_creation_rules is never exercised. Add targeted tests in backend/tests/integration/test_alembic_smoke.py (or test_database_bootstrap.py): (1) fresh SQLite — alembic upgrade head through 0005 completes without error; (2) drifted SQLite — columns quality_tie_break_order + quality_m3u_tie_break_enabled already present from a prior create_all() while alembic_version stays at 0004, then upgrade head is idempotent (skips the add_columns, still stamps 0005). Small. P3.

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description: PR #99 review flagged: bulk_update_auto_creation_rules calls validate_rule on post-update conditions/actions even when the bulk payload is scalars-only. If any row in auto_creation_rules has stored conditions/actions that fail validate_rule today (schema drift, legacy data, empty conditions, etc.), bulk-toggling enabled on a batch including that row will 400 + rollback the entire batch. Need to verify: run validate_rule(r.get_conditions(), r.get_actions()) against every row and list any invalid ones. If any exist: (a) fix those rows, or (b) change bulk endpoint to skip validation when payload doesn't touch conditions/actions. Container ecm-ecm-1 was not running when the review ran — run this check against the live instance. Single-rule PUT has the same failure mode, but bulk amplifies the blast radius.

title: security: validate tenant_id/drive_id in OneDrive adapter (CodeQL 1361, 1362)
description:
CodeQL alerts 1361 and 1362 flag SSRF risk in the OneDrive adapter via unvalidated tenant_id/drive_id interpolation into Microsoft Graph URLs.

Root cause: user-supplied tenant_id and drive_id flow from CloudTargetCreateRequest/UpdateRequest into Microsoft Graph URL construction without shape validation. Although admin-only, credentials are admin-authored; a malformed or maliciously-crafted identifier could redirect Graph calls to an attacker-controlled endpoint, leaking the client_secret during OAuth token exchange.

Exploitability:
- Admin-only write path, but admin credentials are themselves the secret being protected
- client_secret leak possible if URL is redirected to attacker origin
- Graph URL composition pattern: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/... and https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/drives/{drive_id}/...

Fix direction (from triage):
- Add _TENANT_ID_RE: GUID-or-domain validator (36-char GUID or verified-domain form like contoso.onmicrosoft.com)
- Add _DRIVE_ID_RE: base64url validator for drive IDs
- Apply at Pydantic CloudTargetCreateRequest and CloudTargetUpdateRequest layer (primary gate)
- Apply again at adapter __init__ as defense-in-depth
- Reject with HTTP 422 at API boundary; raise ValueError at adapter construction

Acceptance criteria:
- CodeQL alerts 1361 and 1362 auto-close on next scan after merge
- New tests pass:
  - test_rejects_invalid_tenant_id_shape (reject javascript:, attacker.com, traversal, null-byte, overlong)
  - test_rejects_invalid_drive_id_shape (reject non-b64url characters, path separators, URL schemes)
- Existing OneDrive happy-path tests still pass
- No breaking change for legitimate Azure AD tenants or existing drive IDs

References:
- CodeQL alerts 1361, 1362 (both py/full-server-side-request-forgery or similar)
- Tracked under Bead C audit entry (separate)

title: Improve conflict card UX - scrollable, nav above, search filter

Also added:

- 'No matches found' message when search returns empty
- Search filter state resets when modal closes

title: enhancedchannelmanager
description:
Companion bead to enhancedchannelmanager-t8xw3. Becomes actionable after t8xw3 lands (which fixes the ajv override so lint is runnable).

Background:
- Lint has been broken on dev for ~2 months due to a stale ajv override in frontend/package.json. t8xw3 removes that override.
- Once runnable, 'npm run lint' reports 71 errors and 98 warnings across the codebase. t8xw3 grandfathers these via changed-files-only enforcement; this bead zeroes them.
- Majority of errors are @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any in logger.ts, api.ts, and a handful of other files. Remainder: unused eslint-disable directives, one empty-interface.

Scope:
- Drive the baseline to zero: 0 errors, 0 warnings with --max-warnings 0.
- Once at zero, flip t8xw3's lint strategy from 'changed-files-only block + full-lint informational' to 'full-lint blocking on every run.' Update .github/workflows/test.yml accordingly.
- Document in docs/frontend/ or CLAUDE.md why --max-warnings 0 is enforced.

Approach:
- Fix batches by rule (all no-explicit-any first, then unused directives, etc.) for reviewability.
- Some 'any' usages might be justified — replace with 'unknown' and narrow, or add a targeted eslint-disable-next-line with a comment explaining why.
- Do NOT bulk-disable rules to hit zero; that defeats the purpose.

Expected effort: 1-3 sessions depending on how many 'any' need genuine type-narrowing work vs. rote replacement.

Flagged by Project Engineer during t8xw3 preflight 2026-04-19. Split out so t8xw3 could ship without scope bloat.
notes: Session 3 PR #76 opened 2026-04-20. Lint: 17 errors -> 0 errors, 89 warnings -> 0 warnings. Zero baseline achieved. CI flipped to full-lint blocking (t8xw3 workflow update included). Fixes: setState-in-effect cluster (13) via Outer/Inner modal split pattern + useState initializers + handler-owned transitions; react-compiler memoization (3) via whole-object deps; rules-of-hooks (1) via hook-before-early-return; notifications dep cluster (~48) via memoized context value + dep additions; react-refresh/only-export-components disables for co-location patterns; SORT_MODE_LABELS moved to module scope; autoSyncRelatedGroups/annotations useMemo wrapping. New docs/frontend_lint.md explains the zero-warnings policy and canonical fix patterns. All 1118 unit tests pass, typecheck + build clean. UI smoke test against ecm-ecm-1 confirms React tree mounts cleanly. Ready for close after merge.

title: NIST 800-63B password policy alignment
description:
Modernize password validation to follow NIST 800-63B guidelines:

1. **Drop composition rules** — remove uppercase/lowercase/number requirements (they produce predictable patterns like Password1)
2. **Uniform 8-char minimum** — apply to all users including admin-created (currently 6 in admin_routes.py:160)
3. **Replace 33-word common password list** with a proper breached password check — either bundle a top-100k list or integrate haveibeenpwned k-anonymity API (sends only first 5 chars of SHA-1 hash, no plaintext leaves the server)
4. **Remove starts-with-common-word check** — overly broad, catches legitimate passwords while missing actual breaches
5. **Keep**: no max length penalty, no periodic rotation, allow paste in frontend fields

Files: auth/password.py (validate_password, COMMON_PASSWORDS), auth/admin_routes.py:158-160

title: v0.16.0-0051: auto-creation Bad Gateway + execution history refresh
description:
Root cause: bd-w3z4h introduced a 30s request-timeout middleware in backend/main.py with _TIMEOUT_EXEMPT_PREFIXES = (stream-preview, ffmpeg, tasks, backup). /api/auto-creation/ was not exempt, so pipeline runs exceeding 30s returned 504 Gateway Timeout client-side. Work continued server-side (engine commits as it progresses via run_cpu_bound thread pool), which is why execution history showed up after browser refresh but not in-session (frontend runPipeline only calls fetchExecutions inside the try block — 504 falls into catch).

Fix (this bead): add '/api/auto-creation/' to _TIMEOUT_EXEMPT_PREFIXES in backend/main.py. One-line change, matches the pattern of /api/tasks/ and /api/backup/ which also cover both CRUD and long-running operations under one prefix.

Follow-up (separate bead bd-enfsy, P2): convert /run endpoints to 202 + poll background-task pattern, then remove the exempt prefix to restore defense-in-depth on auto-creation CRUD. Also fix useAutoCreationExecution.runPipeline to call fetchExecutions() in a finally block.

Verified in container: docker cp + docker restart ecm-ecm-1 succeeded, /api/health returns 200, grep on deployed /app/main.py confirms new prefix present. Middleware integration tests (test_event_loop_responsiveness.py) still pass. Full backend suite: 2937 passed, 8 pre-existing alembic env failures unrelated.

title: Create Task Database Models
description: Added ScheduledTask and TaskExecution models to backend/models.py. ScheduledTask stores task configuration (task_id, schedule_type, interval_seconds, cron_expression, schedule_time, timezone, config JSON, timestamps). TaskExecution records execution history (task_id, timing, status, success, counts, details JSON, triggered_by). Updated database.py to import new models. Tables will be auto-created on startup.
